institutions.name: Aberdare Teachers Training College
institutions.profile:
Aberdare Teachers Training College is a public institution located in Nyahururu, within Nyandarua County, Kenya. It falls under the supervision of the Ministry of Education and is dedicated to training primary school teachers. The college plays a vital role in the Kenyan education system by equipping aspiring educators with the necessary skills and knowledge to effectively teach and nurture young learners.

The college provides a conducive learning environment with qualified trainers and resources to facilitate effective teaching and learning. Aberdare TTC is committed to producing highly competent teachers who can contribute significantly to the improvement of primary education standards in Kenya. The college offers a Primary Teacher Education (PTE) program.
institutions.short_description: Aberdare Teachers Training College, a public institution in Nyahururu, Nyandarua County, under the Ministry of Education, specializes in training primary school teachers. Committed to excellence in teacher education.

institutions.name: Africa International University
institutions.profile:
Africa International University (AIU) is a leading Christian chartered private university situated in Karen, Nairobi, Kenya. Established in 1983 as the Nairobi Evangelical Graduate School of Theology (NEGST), AIU has grown from a specialized theological institution into a multidisciplinary university. Today, it attracts students and staff from over 32 countries across five continents, fostering a vibrant, multicultural academic community. The university is guided by a distinguished leadership team, including Chancellor Professor Watson Omulokoli and Vice-Chancellor Professor Dankit Nassiuma, who are committed to nurturing Christ-centered leaders for Africa and beyond.

AIU offers a wide range of undergraduate and postgraduate programs across various disciplines, including theology, business, education, and information technology. The university is committed to providing quality education that is relevant to the needs of the African context. AIU also has a strong focus on research and community engagement, seeking to contribute to the development of society through its scholarly activities and outreach programs.
institutions.short_description: A leading Christian private university in Karen, Nairobi, established in 1983, offering multidisciplinary education and attracting a diverse, multicultural community from over 32 countries, focused on nurturing Christ-centered leaders.

institutions.name: Africa Nazarene University
institutions.profile:
Africa Nazarene University (ANU) is a private Christian liberal arts university that exists to develop transformative leaders for the church and society. The university provides a holistic education that integrates faith and learning, ensuring that students not only acquire academic knowledge but also develop strong moral and ethical values. As one of the top private universities in Kenya, ANU’s mission is to provide a holistic education that develops individuals academically, spiritually, and socially.

Located in Ongata Rongai, ANU offers a wide range of undergraduate and graduate programs across various disciplines, including business, theology, education, and technology. The university is committed to providing a student-centered learning environment with experienced faculty and state-of-the-art facilities. ANU is dedicated to fostering a community of scholars who are committed to academic excellence, spiritual growth, and social responsibility.
institutions.short_description: Africa Nazarene University is a private Christian university located in Ongata Rongai. It offers a holistic education, integrating faith and learning, with a focus on developing ethical leaders.

institutions.name: Ahmed Shahame Mwidani Technical Training Institute
institutions.profile:
Ahmed Shahame Mwidani Technical Training Institute (ASM TTI) is a public tertiary institution located in Likoni Constituency, Mombasa County. The institute is dedicated to providing quality technical and vocational education and training (TVET) to equip students with practical skills and knowledge relevant to the job market. ASM TTI aims to contribute to the socio-economic development of Kenya by producing competent graduates in various technical fields.

The institute offers a range of programs including certificate and diploma courses in fields such as building and construction, electrical engineering, automotive engineering, information technology, business management and hospitality. These programs are designed to meet the demands of various industries and are continuously updated to reflect the latest technological advancements. ASM TTI also focuses on fostering innovation, entrepreneurship, and ethical values among its students to enable them to become responsible and productive members of society.
institutions.short_description: Ahmed Shahame Mwidani Technical Training Institute (ASM TTI) is a public college in Likoni, Mombasa, offering diverse technical and vocational programs to empower students with practical skills for career success.

institutions.name: Aldai Technical Training Institute
institutions.profile:
Aldai Technical Training Institute is a public tertiary institution located in Nandi Hills, Nandi County, Kenya. It operates under the guidance of the Ministry of Education and aims to provide quality technical and vocational education and training (TVET) to students from across the country. The institute is committed to equipping its students with the skills and knowledge necessary to succeed in various technical fields and contribute to the economic development of Kenya.

The institute offers a range of diploma and certificate courses in fields such as engineering, agriculture, business, and ICT. Aldai TTI emphasizes hands-on training and practical experience to ensure that graduates are well-prepared for the demands of the job market. It has established partnerships with industries and organizations to provide students with internship opportunities and exposure to real-world work environments. The institute is recognized for its commitment to innovation, research, and community engagement, striving to be a center of excellence in technical education.
institutions.short_description: Aldai Technical Training Institute, located in Nandi Hills, offers quality TVET programs in engineering, agriculture, business, and ICT, focusing on practical skills and industry connections.

institutions.name: Alupe University
institutions.profile:
Alupe University, established in 2015 and chartered in 2019, is an emerging institution of higher learning in Kenya. Located in Alupe, Teso South Constituency, Busia County, it aims to provide quality education and contribute to the development of the region. The university offers various undergraduate and postgraduate programs across different faculties, including Agriculture, Education, Science, and Business. Alupe University strives to create a conducive learning environment that fosters innovation, research, and community engagement.

Despite being relatively new, Alupe University is committed to becoming a center of excellence in research and innovation. The university actively engages in community outreach programs and collaborates with local and international partners to address societal challenges. With a focus on practical training and skills development, Alupe University prepares its students for the job market and empowers them to become leaders in their respective fields. The university is strategically positioned to serve students from Western Kenya and beyond, offering an alternative to the established universities in the country.
institutions.short_description: Alupe University, a public university located in Busia County, established in 2015 and chartered in 2019, is dedicated to providing quality education, fostering research, and engaging with the community.

institutions.name: Alupe University Tvet Institute
institutions.profile:
Alupe University TVET Institute is a public tertiary institution located in Busia County, Kenya. It is affiliated with Alupe University and focuses on providing technical and vocational education and training (TVET) programs. The institute aims to equip students with practical skills and knowledge relevant to various industries, contributing to the socio-economic development of the region and the country. The institute offers a range of diploma and certificate courses in fields such as agriculture, engineering, business, and information technology. 

The institute is committed to providing quality education and training, with a focus on hands-on experience and industry relevance. Its location in Busia County allows it to serve the local community and contribute to the development of the region's workforce. Alupe University TVET Institute plays a crucial role in providing opportunities for young people to acquire marketable skills and pursue fulfilling careers. It strives to create a conducive learning environment that fosters innovation, creativity, and entrepreneurship.
institutions.short_description: Alupe University TVET Institute, located in Busia County, is a public tertiary institution affiliated with Alupe University, offering diploma and certificate courses focused on technical and vocational skills for various industries.

institutions.name: Amref International University
institutions.profile:
AMREF International University (AMIU) exists to bridge the gap in health education and create a generation of professionals equipped to solve Africa’s health challenges. Established by Amref Health Africa, the university is deeply rooted in public health and community development, with a clear focus on transforming healthcare in Africa through high-quality education and research. As one of the most specialized universities in Kenya, AMIU focuses on building capacity in the health sector by training professionals who are competent, ethical, and service-driven.

Located in Lang'ata, Nairobi, AMIU offers a range of undergraduate and postgraduate programs in areas such as public health, nursing, and community health. The university is committed to providing a conducive learning environment that fosters innovation, critical thinking, and leadership skills. AMIU has gained a reputation for its strong emphasis on research and its commitment to addressing the specific healthcare needs of African communities. Through collaborations with various organizations and institutions, AMIU strives to make a significant impact on healthcare delivery and policy across the continent.
institutions.short_description: Amref International University (AMIU) is a private university located in Nairobi, established by Amref Health Africa. It specializes in health education and research to solve Africa’s healthcare challenges.

institutions.name: Asumbi Teachers Training College
institutions.profile:
Asumbi Teachers Training College is a public institution located in Asumbi, Homa Bay County, Kenya. Established to train primary school teachers, the college plays a crucial role in the development of education in the region and across the country. The college is under the administration of the Ministry of Education.

The college offers various programs focused on teacher education and professional development. Asumbi TTC is known for its commitment to providing quality education and fostering a supportive learning environment. It has established a reputation for producing well-trained teachers who contribute significantly to the education sector in Kenya. The college is situated in a serene environment conducive to learning, allowing students to focus on their studies and develop their teaching skills effectively.
institutions.short_description: Asumbi Teachers Training College, located in Homa Bay County, is a public institution dedicated to training primary school teachers. Committed to quality education and professional development, the college plays a vital role in Kenya's education sector.

institutions.name: Bahati Institute Of Business And Administration Studies
institutions.profile:
Bahati Institute of Business and Administration Studies (BIBAS) is a public tertiary institution located in Bahati Constituency, Nakuru County, Kenya. The institute is dedicated to providing quality education and training in business and administration-related fields. As a public institution under the Ministry of Education, BIBAS aims to equip students with the necessary skills and knowledge to contribute to the economic and social development of the country.

The institute offers a range of diploma and certificate courses designed to meet the evolving demands of the job market. These programs cover various disciplines within business and administration, including business management, accountancy, human resource management, and information technology. BIBAS is committed to fostering a conducive learning environment that encourages innovation, critical thinking, and practical application of knowledge, ensuring its graduates are well-prepared for successful careers.
institutions.short_description: Bahati Institute of Business and Administration Studies (BIBAS) is a public college in Bahati, Nakuru, offering quality education in business and administration-related courses, fostering innovation and practical skills.

institutions.name: Bandari Maritime Academy
institutions.profile:
Bandari Maritime Academy (BMA) is a premier maritime training institution located in Mombasa, Kenya. Established to address the growing demand for skilled maritime professionals in the East African region and beyond, BMA offers a range of courses designed to equip students with the knowledge and practical skills necessary for successful careers at sea and in the broader maritime industry.

The academy's curriculum includes nautical science, marine engineering, and maritime transport logistics, among others. BMA is committed to providing high-quality training that meets international standards, ensuring that its graduates are well-prepared to contribute to the maritime sector. With modern facilities, experienced instructors, and a focus on hands-on learning, Bandari Maritime Academy plays a vital role in developing the next generation of maritime leaders. The academy also prides itself on fostering strong partnerships with shipping companies and other industry stakeholders, providing students with valuable opportunities for internships and employment after graduation.
institutions.short_description: Bandari Maritime Academy in Mombasa offers comprehensive maritime training programs in nautical science, marine engineering, and logistics. It aims to produce highly skilled maritime professionals.

institutions.name: Baringo National Polytechnic
institutions.profile:
Baringo National Polytechnic, located in Kabarnet town, Baringo County, is a public institution dedicated to providing technical and vocational education and training (TVET). The polytechnic aims to equip students with practical skills and knowledge relevant to various industries, contributing to the socio-economic development of the region and the country. The institution offers a range of certificate and diploma courses designed to meet the demands of the Kenyan job market. The polytechnic prides itself on fostering a conducive learning environment that promotes innovation, creativity, and excellence.

The polytechnic's programs include disciplines such as engineering, business studies, information technology, and hospitality. These courses are designed to provide hands-on experience, enabling graduates to be immediately productive in their chosen fields. With a focus on practical training, Baringo National Polytechnic seeks to empower its students to become skilled technicians, entrepreneurs, and leaders in their respective industries. The institution is committed to continuous improvement and strives to stay abreast of the latest technological advancements and industry best practices.
institutions.short_description: Baringo National Polytechnic is a public TVET institution in Kabarnet, Baringo County, offering certificate and diploma courses in engineering, business, IT, and hospitality. Equipping students with practical skills for industry.

institutions.name: Belgut Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Belgut Technical and Vocational College is a public institution located in Belgut Constituency, Kericho County, Kenya. It operates under the Ministry of Education and aims to provide quality technical and vocational training to students, equipping them with practical skills relevant to the job market. The college offers a range of certificate and diploma courses designed to meet the evolving needs of various industries. These programs focus on hands-on training to ensure graduates are well-prepared for employment or entrepreneurship. 

The college is committed to fostering a conducive learning environment with modern workshops and training facilities. Its mission is to empower individuals with technical expertise and contribute to the socio-economic development of the region. Belgut TVC strives to be a center of excellence in technical education, producing highly skilled professionals who can drive innovation and growth in various sectors.
institutions.short_description: Belgut Technical and Vocational College is a public TVC in Belgut, Kericho, offering certificate and diploma courses designed to equip students with practical, marketable skills for various industries.

institutions.name: Bishop Mahon Teachers Training College
institutions.profile:
Bishop Mahon Teachers Training College is a public institution located in Lodwar, Turkana County, Kenya. It is dedicated to training primary school teachers and is approved by the Teachers Service Commission (TSC). The college aims to provide quality teacher education, equipping its graduates with the knowledge, skills, and values necessary to excel in the teaching profession. The college plays a crucial role in boosting education standards in Turkana County and across Kenya by producing competent educators.

The college offers a Diploma in Primary Teacher Education program, focusing on various pedagogical approaches, subject matter expertise, and classroom management techniques. Bishop Mahon TTC is committed to fostering a conducive learning environment with modern facilities and experienced faculty. The institution actively engages with the local community and educational stakeholders to address specific needs and challenges in the region's education sector. Graduates from Bishop Mahon TTC are prepared to make significant contributions to primary education, particularly in underserved areas.
institutions.short_description: Bishop Mahon Teachers Training College, located in Lodwar, Turkana County, is a public institution dedicated to providing quality teacher education and producing competent primary school teachers for Kenya.

institutions.name: Bomet Technical & Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Bomet Technical and Vocational College is a public institution located in Bomet County, Kenya. It aims to provide quality technical and vocational education and training (TVET) to equip students with the skills and knowledge needed for the job market and self-employment. The college offers a range of programs in various fields, including engineering, business, hospitality, and ICT. These programs are designed to meet the demands of the local and national economies, contributing to the socio-economic development of the region.

The college is committed to providing a conducive learning environment with modern facilities and qualified instructors. It strives to instill values of integrity, innovation, and professionalism in its students. Bomet Technical and Vocational College plays a crucial role in empowering the youth of Bomet County and beyond by providing them with relevant skills and opportunities for career advancement. The institution has quickly established itself as a key player in the TVET sector in the region, fostering collaborations with industry partners to ensure its training programs remain relevant and up-to-date.
institutions.short_description: Bomet Technical & Vocational College is a public TVET institution in Bomet County offering diverse programs in engineering, business, hospitality, and ICT, empowering students with practical skills for the job market.

institutions.name: Bomet University College
institutions.profile:
Bomet University College (BUC), located in the heart of Bomet Town, Kenya, is a premier institution dedicated to fostering academic excellence, innovation, and sustainability. Established as a Constituent College of Moi University through Legal Notice No. 145 on July 27, 2017, BUC is the first public university in Bomet County. The institution has quickly gained recognition for its unique niche as a "Green Economy for Sustainability" driver, making it a hub for cutting-edge research and education.

BUC offers a range of programs designed to meet the evolving needs of the job market and contribute to sustainable development. These include degree programs in environmental science, agriculture, business, and education. The university college is committed to providing a conducive learning environment with modern facilities and a dedicated faculty. BUC aims to be a leading center for research and innovation, addressing local and global challenges related to sustainability and economic growth, while maintaining a strong commitment to community engagement and social responsibility.
institutions.short_description: Bomet University College, a public institution in Bomet, established in 2017, is dedicated to academic excellence, innovation, and sustainability, specializing in green economy and sustainability research.

institutions.name: Bondo Teachers Training College
institutions.profile:
Bondo Teachers Training College is a public institution located in Bondo, Siaya County, Kenya. Established to train and develop competent primary school teachers, the college plays a vital role in providing quality education within the region and beyond. The college offers a range of programs focused on teacher education, equipping students with the necessary skills and knowledge to excel in the teaching profession.

The college is situated along Bondo-Usenge Road. Over the years, Bondo Teachers Training College has built a reputation for academic excellence and a commitment to producing highly qualified teachers. It continues to adapt its programs to meet the evolving needs of the education sector, ensuring that its graduates are well-prepared to shape the future of education in Kenya. The college actively engages with the local community and participates in various educational initiatives to promote access to quality education for all.
institutions.short_description: Bondo Teachers Training College, located in Bondo, Siaya County, is a public institution dedicated to training competent primary school teachers and fostering quality education in the region.

institutions.name: Bondo Technical Training Institute
institutions.profile:
Bondo Technical Training Institute is a public tertiary institution located in Bondo, Siaya County, Kenya. It operates under the Ministry of Education and is committed to providing quality technical and vocational education and training (TVET) to students from across the country. The institute aims to equip its graduates with practical skills and knowledge that are highly relevant to the job market, contributing to the socio-economic development of Kenya.

The institute offers a range of certificate and diploma courses in various fields, including engineering, business, ICT, and hospitality. It strives to create a conducive learning environment with modern facilities and experienced instructors. Bondo TTI plays a crucial role in empowering young Kenyans with the skills needed to succeed in various industries and contribute to the nation's workforce.
institutions.short_description: Bondo Technical Training Institute, located in Siaya County, is a public college under the Ministry of Education offering diverse technical and vocational courses for practical skills and workforce development.

institutions.name: Borabu Teachers Training College
institutions.profile:
Borabu Teachers Training College is a public institution located in Nyamira County, Kenya. It is dedicated to providing high-quality teacher education and training programs. The college aims to produce competent and dedicated teachers who can effectively contribute to the development of the education sector in Kenya. The college is under the administration of the Ministry of Education.

Borabu TTC offers a range of programs designed to equip trainees with the knowledge, skills, and attitudes necessary for effective teaching. These programs include Diploma in Primary Teacher Education and other specialized courses aimed at enhancing teaching methodologies. The college is committed to fostering a conducive learning environment that promotes academic excellence and professional growth among its students. Borabu Teachers Training College plays a vital role in shaping the future of education in Kenya by preparing well-trained teachers to serve in primary schools across the country.
institutions.short_description: Borabu Teachers Training College, located in Nyamira County, is a public institution under the Ministry of Education that provides quality teacher education and training programs for primary school teachers.

institutions.name: Borabu Technical And Vocational College
institutions.logo: Borabu Technical And Vocational College Logo
institutions.profile:
Borabu Technical And Vocational College is a public institution located in Borabu Constituency, Nyamira County, Kenya. Established to address the growing need for skilled personnel in various technical and vocational fields, the college operates under the Ministry of Education. Situated along the Kebirigo - Mosobeti Road, adjacent to Riomwamu Market, it serves students from Nyamira and surrounding counties, providing accessible training opportunities in a semi-urban setting. The college aims to empower its graduates with practical skills that are relevant to the job market, contributing to local and national economic development.

The college offers a diverse range of programs across different disciplines, including engineering, information technology, business studies, and hospitality, among others. These courses are designed to provide hands-on experience and theoretical knowledge, preparing students for immediate employment or further education. Borabu TVC is recognized for its commitment to quality technical and vocational training, playing a crucial role in developing the technical workforce in the region and supporting the government's agenda of promoting technical education.
institutions.short_description: Borabu Technical And Vocational College is a public TVC in Borabu, Nyamira County, offering various technical and vocational programs under the Ministry of Education, focused on equipping students with practical skills for employment and regional development.

other_info.address: Along Kebirigo - Mosobeti Road, next to Riomwamu Market, Borabu

institutions.name: Bukura Agricultural College
institutions.profile:
Bukura Agricultural College (BAC) is a prominent public technical and vocational education and training (TVET) institution located in Bukura, Lurambi Constituency, Kakamega County, Kenya. Established in 1970 and officially opened its doors in 1974, the college operates under the Ministry of Agriculture. Its core mandate is to provide specialized training in agriculture and related fields, aiming to equip individuals with the practical skills and theoretical knowledge necessary for sustainable agricultural development and agribusiness success in Kenya.

The college offers a comprehensive range of certificate and diploma programs designed to meet the diverse needs of the agricultural sector and the job market. Key areas of study include General Agriculture, Agricultural Extension, Horticulture, Agribusiness Management, Animal Health, and Community Development. BAC is recognized for its emphasis on hands-on learning, leveraging its extensive farm facilities, modern laboratories, and workshops to provide students with practical experience. Over the decades, Bukura Agricultural College has earned a reputation for producing skilled agricultural professionals who contribute significantly to food security, rural livelihoods, and the overall growth of Kenya's agricultural economy.
institutions.short_description: Bukura Agricultural College is a leading public TVET institution in Kakamega County, Kenya, specializing in agriculture and related sciences. It offers practical-oriented certificate and diploma programs.

institutions.name: Bumbe Technical Training Institute
institutions.profile:
Bumbe Technical Training Institute is a public TVET institution situated in Bumbe area, Butula Constituency, Busia County, Kenya. Established in 2015, the institute is managed by the Ministry of Education and is dedicated to providing quality technical and vocational education and training. It plays a crucial role in equipping young Kenyans with practical skills required for employment and entrepreneurship, contributing significantly to the socio-economic development of the region and the country. The institution is conveniently located off the main Kisumu-Busia highway, ensuring ease of access for students from various parts of the county and beyond.

Bumbe TTI offers a diverse range of market-driven programs across various disciplines, including engineering, business, information communication technology (ICT), agriculture, hospitality, education (ECDE), and health sciences (Nutrition & Dietetics). These programs are meticulously designed to align with current industry demands and are delivered by qualified and experienced instructors. The institute provides hands-on training opportunities, modern facilities, and a conducive learning environment aimed at ensuring students acquire the necessary competencies and confidence. As a registered KUCCPS institution, it attracts students seeking government sponsorship for technical courses, further upholding its commitment to expanding access to vital technical education.
institutions.short_description: Bumbe Technical Training Institute is a public TVET institution located in Busia County, Kenya, established in 2015. It offers a wide array of market-driven technical and vocational programs focusing on providing practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Bungoma North Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Bungoma North Technical And Vocational College (BNTVC) is a public TVET institution located in Tongaren Constituency, Bungoma County, Kenya. Established to provide relevant technical and vocational education and training, BNTVC aims to equip students with practical skills and knowledge essential for employment and entrepreneurship in various sectors. The college plays a crucial role in contributing to the development of the region and the nation by producing competent graduates ready for the job market or to start their own ventures. It operates under the guidance of the Ministry of Education, aligning its curriculum and standards with national TVET policies.

BNTVC is situated in a serene environment conducive to learning and offers a range of courses in technical, business, and applied sciences disciplines. The college is committed to providing quality education through competent trainers and adequate facilities. Its programs are designed to be hands-on, ensuring that students gain practical experience alongside theoretical knowledge. By focusing on market-driven courses, Bungoma North TVC endeavors to address the skills gap in various industries and empower its students to become valuable contributors to the economy. The institution continues to grow, expanding its course offerings and infrastructure to meet the increasing demand for technical and vocational training in the region.
institutions.short_description: Bungoma North Technical And Vocational College (BNTVC) is a public TVET institution in Tongaren, Bungoma County, offering practical technical, business, and applied science courses to equip students with relevant skills for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Bunyala Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Bunyala Technical And Vocational College is a public institution dedicated to providing quality technical and vocational education and training (TVET) in Busia County, Kenya. Established in Budalangi, the college plays a crucial role in the region by offering accessible training opportunities that align with national development goals and market demands. It serves students primarily from Busia and surrounding counties, contributing significantly to capacity building and skill development in diverse technical and business disciplines.

The college offers programs across various departments, including Information Communication Technology (ICT), Building and Civil Engineering, Electrical and Electronic Engineering, Business Studies, Applied Sciences, Cosmetology, Hospitality, and Liberal Studies. These courses are designed to impart hands-on skills and theoretical knowledge, preparing graduates for successful careers or entrepreneurial ventures. Bunyala TVC is committed to fostering innovation, practical expertise, and a strong work ethic among its students, contributing to the socio-economic growth of the Bunyala sub-county and Kenya as a whole.
institutions.short_description: Bunyala Technical And Vocational College (TVC) is a public institution located in Budalangi, Busia County, Kenya. It offers a wide range of technical and business courses at artisan, certificate, and diploma levels, aiming to equip students with practical skills for the job market and self-employment.

institutions.name: Bunyore Teachers Training College
institutions.profile:
Bunyore Teachers Training College (BTTC) is a public institution located in Maseno, Vihiga County, Kenya. Established as a reputable college under the Ministry of Education, BTTC focuses on training qualified primary school teachers, contributing significantly to the educational sector in Kenya. The college offers various teacher education programs accredited by relevant bodies, equipping trainees with the necessary pedagogical skills, subject knowledge, and professional ethics required to excel in the teaching profession. Its serene location provides a conducive environment for learning and personal development.

The college is known for its commitment to producing competent and dedicated educators. It provides facilities and resources aimed at enhancing the learning experience, including libraries, laboratories, and ICT centres. Bunyore TTC plays a crucial role in implementing the national education curriculum changes, ensuring its graduates are well-prepared for the evolving demands of the classroom, such as the competency-based curriculum (CBC). The institution maintains connections with schools for teaching practice, allowing trainees to gain hands-on experience under supervision, solidifying its reputation as a key player in teacher training within the region.
institutions.short_description: Bunyore Teachers Training College is a public college in Maseno, Vihiga County, Kenya, dedicated to training competent primary school teachers. It offers accredited programs focusing on pedagogical skills and subject knowledge.

institutions.name: Bureti Technical Training Institute
institutions.profile:
Bureti Technical Training Institute (BTTI) is a public TVET institution located in Kabianga, near Kericho Town, within Belgut Constituency, Kericho County, Kenya. Established to enhance access to technical and vocational training in the region, BTTI plays a crucial role in equipping Kenyan youth with essential skills for the modern job market. The institute is registered by TVETA and offers a diverse range of courses designed to meet industry demands.

The institute provides training across various disciplines, including business, information communication technology, engineering, and agriculture, among others. BTTI is committed to fostering innovation and entrepreneurship among its students, preparing them not only for employment but also for creating their own opportunities. Its strategic location makes it accessible to students from Kericho and neighboring counties, contributing significantly to the development of a skilled workforce in Kenya.
institutions.short_description: Bureti Technical Training Institute (BTTI) is a public TVET institution located in Kabianga, Kericho County, Kenya. It provides a wide range of technical, engineering, and business programs to equip students with practical skills for the job market and self-employment, contributing to national development.

other_info.address: Kabianga, near Kericho Town, Belgut Constituency, Kericho County, Kenya P.O. Box P.O. Box – 20200 Kericho, Kenya

institutions.name: Bushiangala Technical Training Institute
institutions.profile:
Bushiangala Technical Training Institute (TTI) is a public institution located in Bushiangala, Ikolomani Constituency, Kakamega County, Kenya. Established under the Ministry of Education, the institute is dedicated to providing quality technical and vocational education and training (TVET). It offers a diverse range of courses at certificate and diploma levels across various fields including engineering, building technology, business studies, information communication technology, and hospitality. The curriculum is designed to be hands-on and industry-relevant, preparing students for employment and entrepreneurship upon graduation.

Bushiangala TTI serves students from Kakamega and the wider Western region, contributing significantly to the development of a skilled workforce in Kenya. The institute is equipped with workshops, laboratories, and learning facilities aimed at facilitating practical learning. As a recognized TVET center, it is a popular choice for students seeking post-secondary technical training, contributing to the national agenda of increasing technical skills and promoting self-reliance among the youth. It is listed by KUCCPS for placement of government-sponsored students and is committed to fostering innovation and excellence in technical education.
institutions.short_description: Bushiangala Technical Training Institute is a public TVET college in Kakamega County, Kenya, offering diverse certificate and diploma programs in technical, business, and ICT fields to equip students with practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Butere Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Butere Technical And Vocational College (BUTERE TVC) is a prominent public technical and vocational institution situated in Butere Town, Kakamega County, Kenya. Established under the Kenyan Ministry of Education, the college plays a crucial role in providing accessible and relevant technical training to students from the surrounding areas and beyond. Its mission is to equip learners with practical skills and knowledge essential for contributing to the nation's workforce and fostering self-employment through entrepreneurship.

The college offers a wide array of programs across various disciplines, including engineering technology, building and construction, business studies, information technology, and hospitality. These courses are designed to meet the demands of the local and national economy, providing students with hands-on experience and theoretical understanding. BUTERE TVC is committed to nurturing a learning environment that promotes innovation, skill development, and character building, aiming to produce graduates who are competent, ethical, and ready to tackle real-world challenges.
institutions.short_description: Butere Technical and Vocational College is a public TVC in Butere, Kakamega County, offering diverse technical and vocational training programs to empower students with practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Butula Technical & Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Butula Technical & Vocational College (Butula TVC) is a public tertiary institution situated in Butula town, Butula Constituency, within Busia County, Kenya. Operating under the Ministry of Education, the college is mandated to provide accessible and quality technical and vocational education and training (TVET) programs. Established to serve the training needs of the western region of Kenya, Butula TVC plays a crucial role in developing a skilled workforce capable of contributing to national development and meeting the demands of various industries. Its location makes it a key educational hub for students from Busia and neighboring counties seeking practical, hands-on training.

The college offers a diverse range of programs across various disciplines, including engineering technology, business studies, information and communication technology (ICT), hospitality, and agriculture, among others. These courses are structured to provide students with both theoretical knowledge and practical skills, enhancing their employability and entrepreneurial potential. Butula TVC is equipped with workshops, laboratories, and learning resources necessary to facilitate effective technical training. By focusing on demand-driven courses and maintaining close links with industry, Butula TVC has built a reputation as a reliable provider of market-ready graduates, significantly contributing to the socio-economic empowerment of the youth in the region.
institutions.short_description: Butula Technical & Vocational College is a public TVET institution located in Butula town, Busia County. It offers quality technical and vocational training programs approved by KUCCPS, equipping students with essential skills for employment and entrepreneurship in various technical fields.

institutions.name: Cardinal Maurice Otunga Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Cardinal Maurice Otunga Technical and Vocational College (CMOTVC) is a public TVC located in Rongo Sub-County, Migori County, Kenya. The institution is named in honor of the late Cardinal Maurice Otunga. Established under the Ministry of Education, its primary mission is to provide accessible and quality technical and vocational education and training (TVET) to equip students with practical skills needed for the job market and self-employment. Situated strategically in the South Nyanza region, CMOTVC serves students from the surrounding counties and beyond, aiming to contribute significantly to national development through skilled human capital.

The college offers a diverse range of courses across various disciplines, including engineering technologies, business studies, information communication technology, hospitality, and agricultural sciences. These programs are available at certificate, diploma, and artisan levels, tailored to meet industry demands and technological advancements. CMOTVC is listed under the Kenya Universities and Colleges Central Placement Service (KUCCPS), allowing government-sponsored students to enroll. The institution is dedicated to fostering a conducive learning environment, emphasizing hands-on training, innovation, and entrepreneurship, which helps students gain the competence and confidence required to succeed in their chosen fields.
institutions.short_description: Cardinal Maurice Otunga Technical and Vocational College is a public TVC in Rongo, Migori County, offering certificate, diploma, and artisan courses in various technical and business fields. It provides quality hands-on training for career development.

institutions.name: Catholic University Of Eastern Africa
institutions.profile:
The Catholic University of Eastern Africa (CUEA) is a private university in Nairobi, Kenya, founded in 1984 by the Association of Member Episcopal Conferences in Eastern Africa (AMECEA). It began as a graduate school of theology and was granted university status in 1992 by the Commission for Higher Education in Kenya. CUEA is committed to providing integral education through research and training, aiming to mould students into competent, ethical, and responsible citizens.

Located in the serene environment of Karen, Nairobi, CUEA offers a wide array of academic programs across various faculties, including theology, philosophy, arts, social sciences, education, science, and business. The university attracts a diverse student body from Eastern Africa and beyond, fostering a rich multicultural learning environment. With its strong Catholic identity, CUEA emphasizes moral and spiritual development alongside academic excellence, contributing significantly to higher education in the region.
institutions.short_description: The Catholic University of Eastern Africa (CUEA) is a private Nairobi-based university established in 1984 by AMECEA. Initially a theology school, it expanded into a full university in 1992, offering diverse programs in arts, sciences, and business with a strong emphasis on holistic education.

institutions.name: Centre For Tourism Training And Research
programs: Certificate in Food and Beverage Service
institutions.profile:
The Centre For Tourism Training And Research (CTTR) is a renowned public college in Kenya, established in 1997 and affiliated with the prestigious Kenya Utalii College. Located on the expansive Kenya Utalii College campus along Thika Road in Nairobi, CTTR serves as a premier training institution dedicated to developing skilled professionals for the dynamic tourism and hospitality sectors in Kenya and the wider East African region. As a public entity, it operates under the purview of the Ministry of Tourism and Wildlife, playing a critical role in capacity building for the national tourism industry.

CTTR offers a diverse range of programs designed to meet the evolving needs of the industry, including diplomas and certificates. These courses cover key areas such as Hospitality Management, Tourism Management, Tour Guiding, Accommodation Operations, Food Production, Food and Beverage Service, and various languages essential for customer service. The institution is recognized for its practical, industry-oriented training approach, leveraging the facilities and expertise available at Kenya Utalii College to provide students with hands-on experience. Its strategic location in Nairobi, a major hub for tourism and business, further enhances learning opportunities and industry connections for its graduates.
institutions.short_description: Centre For Tourism Training And Research (CTTR) is a public college affiliated with Kenya Utalii College, located on Thika Road, Nairobi. Established in 1997, it's a premier training centre offering diplomas and certificates in tourism and hospitality.

other_info.location: Kenya Utalii College, Thika Road, Nairobi

institutions.name: Chamasiri Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Chamasiri Technical and Vocational College (TVC) is a public TVET institution located in Chamasiri, Nambale Constituency, Busia County, Kenya. Established to address the growing need for skilled manpower in the region, the college is mandated to provide technical and vocational training that is relevant to the job market and national development goals. It offers a range of courses accredited by various examination bodies in Kenya, including KNEC (Kenya National Examinations Council) and CDACC (Curriculum Development, Assessment and Certification Council).

The college plays a crucial role in equipping young people with practical skills and knowledge that enhance their employability and entrepreneurial capabilities. Its programs span diverse fields such as engineering, building technology, information technology, business studies, and applied sciences, catering to different levels of training from artisan to diploma. Chamasiri TVC aims to be a center of excellence in technical and vocational training, contributing significantly to the socio-economic transformation of Busia County and Kenya at large by producing competent and innovative graduates.
institutions.short_description: Chamasiri Technical and Vocational College is a public TVET institution in Busia County, Kenya, offering a diverse range of technical, vocational, and business courses to equip students with practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Chanzeywe Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Chanzeywe Technical And Vocational College (CHANZEYWE TVC) is a public technical and vocational education and training institution located near Chanzeywe Market along the Vihiga - Kapsabet Road in Vihiga County, Kenya. Established in 2016 and registered in 2017, the college operates under the Ministry of Education. Situated in a rural setting, it plays a crucial role in providing accessible TVET opportunities to the youth in Vihiga and surrounding counties, contributing to the national agenda of skills development and capacity building.

The college is dedicated to providing market-driven technical, industrial, technological, and entrepreneurial skills. It offers a diverse range of programs across various departments including Building & Civil Engineering, Electrical & Electronics Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Information Communication Technology, Business Studies, Hospitality & Liberal Studies, and Health Sciences. CHANZEYWE TVC aims to empower its graduates with practical competencies and innovative thinking necessary for employment, self-employment, and further education, thereby contributing to socio-economic growth and poverty reduction in the region and beyond.
institutions.short_description: Chanzeywe Technical And Vocational College is a public TVET institution located in Vihiga County, Kenya, established in 2016. It offers market-driven technical, business, and ICT programs to equip youth with practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Chepalungu Technical Training Institute
institutions.profile:
Chepalungu Technical Training Institute (CHEPALUNGU TTI) is a public technical and vocational education and training (TVET) institution located in Chepalungu Constituency, Bomet County, Kenya. Established to provide essential technical skills and knowledge, the institute plays a crucial role in empowering youth and adults with competencies relevant to the job market and entrepreneurial pursuits. It offers a range of programs aimed at producing skilled graduates capable of contributing to national development, focusing on practical learning and industry-aligned training.

The institute is committed to fostering a learning environment that encourages innovation, creativity, and excellence. CHEPALUNGU TTI's curriculum covers various technical and business fields, preparing students for diverse careers. Its location in a rural setting underscores its mission to bring technical education closer to communities outside major urban centers, thereby promoting equitable access to training opportunities and contributing to regional economic growth. The institution is registered under the Ministry of Education, ensuring adherence to national education standards and quality assurance.
institutions.short_description: Chepalungu Technical Training Institute is a public TVET institution located in Chepalungu, Bomet County. It offers various technical and business courses aimed at equipping students with practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship, contributing to regional development.

institutions.name: Chepsirei Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Chepsirei Technical And Vocational College (Chepsirei TVC) is a public Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) institution located in Chepsirei, Keiyo South Constituency, Elgeyo Marakwet County, Kenya. The college was established to provide accessible and relevant skills training to the youth and community in the region, contributing to socio-economic development. It operates under the Ministry of Education and is a registered TVET institution, admitting students through the Kenya Universities and Colleges Central Placement Service (KUCCPS) and walk-ins.

Chepsirei TVC offers a diverse range of market-driven diploma and certificate programs across various disciplines, including Agriculture, Business, Information Technology, Engineering, and Applied Sciences. The college is committed to equipping students with practical skills and knowledge through competent trainers and appropriate infrastructure, preparing them for employment or entrepreneurship. Situated in a rural setting, the college serves as a vital center for technical education, playing a key role in enhancing the skills capacity of the local population and beyond.
institutions.short_description: Chepsirei Technical And Vocational College is a public TVET institution in Chepsirei, Elgeyo Marakwet County, Kenya. It offers diverse technical and vocational programs to equip students with practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Cherangany Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Cherangany Technical and Vocational College (CTVC) is a public TVET institution situated in Cherangany Constituency, Trans Nzoia County. Established with the aim of providing quality technical and vocational training, the college plays a crucial role in equipping students with practical skills and knowledge relevant to various industries. It is strategically located to serve the community and surrounding regions, contributing to local development through accessible education and skill development programs.

CTVC offers a diverse range of market-driven courses across various disciplines, including engineering, business, hospitality, agriculture, and ICT. The college focuses on hands-on training and industry exposure to prepare graduates for employment or entrepreneurship. As a public institution under the Ministry of Education, it adheres to national standards for technical training and is committed to nurturing a skilled workforce for Kenya's economic growth. Its reputation is built on its commitment to practical education and community engagement.
institutions.short_description: Cherangany Technical And Vocational College is a public TVET institution in Trans Nzoia County, offering practical technical and vocational training across diverse fields like engineering, business, and agriculture to empower students with employable skills.

institutions.name: Chesta Teachers Training College
programs: Diploma in Early Childhood Development Education (DECTE)
institutions.profile:
Chesta Teachers Training College is a public teacher training institution located in Chesta area, West Pokot County, Kenya. Established to address the growing demand for qualified teachers in the region and the country, the college plays a crucial role in shaping the future of primary education. Situated in a serene environment, it provides a conducive learning atmosphere for its students. The college is registered and regulated by the Ministry of Education, ensuring that its curriculum and standards align with national educational policies and requirements.

The college primarily focuses on training teachers for the primary education sector, offering programmes like the Diploma in Primary Teacher Education (DPTE) and the Diploma in Early Childhood Development Education (DECTE). Chesta TTC is committed to producing competent, disciplined, and resourceful teachers equipped with the necessary pedagogical skills and knowledge to serve in diverse educational settings, particularly in rural and underserved areas. Its location in West Pokot underscores its importance in expanding access to teacher training opportunities within the Rift Valley region.
institutions.short_description: Chesta Teachers Training College is a public institution in West Pokot County, Kenya, dedicated to training qualified teachers for primary education, offering Diploma programs like DPTE and DECTE to meet national educational needs.

institutions.name: Chevaywa Technical And Vocational College
institutions.location: Near Chevaywa Market, along Kakamega-Webuye Road
institutions.profile:
Chevaywa Technical And Vocational College (CTVC) is a public TVET institution located in Chevaywa, Lugari Constituency, Kakamega County, Kenya. Established under the Ministry of Education, the college plays a crucial role in providing technical and vocational skills training to young Kenyans. Situated along the busy Kakamega-Webuye Road, CTVC is strategically positioned to serve students from Kakamega and surrounding counties, contributing to human capital development in the region.

CTVC offers a diverse range of courses across various disciplines, including business, engineering, ICT, and hospitality, among others. These programs are designed to equip students with practical, hands-on skills that are highly relevant to the current job market and entrepreneurial ventures. The college is committed to fostering a conducive learning environment that promotes innovation, skill acquisition, and holistic student development, preparing graduates for successful careers and contributions to national growth.
institutions.short_description: Chevaywa Technical And Vocational College (CTVC) is a public TVET institution in Chevaywa, Lugari, Kakamega County. It provides practical technical and vocational training across diverse fields, empowering students with job-ready skills for employment and entrepreneurship.

other_info.address: Along Kakamega-Webuye Road, Near Chevaywa Market

institutions.name: Chuka Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Chuka Technical And Vocational College is a public institution established under the Ministry of Education, Science and Technology. Located in Chuka Town, Tharaka-Nithi County, along the Chuka-Nkubu Road, the college plays a crucial role in providing accessible technical and vocational education and training (TVET) to students from the region and beyond. It is committed to offering practical, industry-relevant courses aimed at addressing the skills gap in various sectors of the Kenyan economy. The college strives to be a center of excellence in TVET, contributing to national development through skills development and innovation.

The college offers diverse programs across departments such as Building & Civil Engineering, Electrical & Electronics Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Business Studies, and Applied Sciences, among others. These programs are designed to cater to different academic levels, from artisan and certificate to diploma. Chuka TVC is known for its focus on hands-on training, utilizing workshops and laboratories to provide students with practical experience. It aims to produce competent graduates who can contribute effectively to the workforce or become successful entrepreneurs, fostering economic growth in the community and the country.
institutions.short_description: Chuka Technical And Vocational College (Chuka TVC) is a public TVET institution in Chuka town, Tharaka-Nithi County, offering a wide range of technical, vocational, and business programs focused on practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Chuka University
institutions.profile:
Chuka University, situated in the serene Chuka town in Tharaka Nithi County, Kenya, is a public university established in 2004 as a constituent college of Egerton University. It was granted its charter in 2013, becoming a fully-fledged university. Nestled at the foot of Mount Kenya, the university's location provides a conducive environment for learning and research. Over the years, Chuka University has experienced significant growth in infrastructure, student population, and academic programs, establishing itself as a key institution of higher learning in the Eastern region of Kenya.

The university offers a wide array of programs across various disciplines, including Agriculture and Environmental Sciences, Business and Economics, Education, Humanities and Social Sciences, and Pure and Applied Sciences. It is dedicated to fostering academic excellence, promoting research and innovation, and producing graduates who are equipped to address societal challenges. Chuka University emphasizes holistic development, providing students with opportunities for co-curricular activities and community engagement, contributing positively to the local community and the nation at large.
institutions.short_description: Chuka University is a leading public university located in Chuka Town, Tharaka Nithi County, Kenya. Established in 2004 as a constituent college and chartered in 2013, it offers diverse academic programs, promotes research, and fosters innovation.

institutions.name: Chuka University Tvet - Igembe Campus
institutions.profile:
Chuka University is a public university located in Chuka, Tharaka Nithi County, Kenya. Established initially as a campus of Egerton University in 2004, it was granted constituent college status in 2007 and awarded a full charter in 2013. The university has expanded its reach by establishing various campuses across the region to make higher education and TVET accessible to diverse communities. These campuses operate under the university's charter and academic standards, ensuring a consistent quality of education across all locations.

The Igembe Campus, located in Maua, Igembe South Constituency, Meru County, is one such extension focusing on providing Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) programs. This campus plays a vital role in bringing skills development closer to the people of Igembe and surrounding areas. It offers a range of technical and vocational courses designed to equip students with practical skills relevant to the job market and local industries. By being situated in Maua, the campus serves as a key educational hub, enhancing access to training and contributing to the socio-economic development of the upper Eastern region by producing skilled graduates ready to contribute to various sectors.
institutions.short_description: Chuka University TVET - Igembe Campus is a public technical and vocational training institution located in Maua, Meru County. As a campus of Chuka University, it provides essential technical and vocational education programs to students in the region, contributing to skills development and workforce readiness.

institutions.name: Co-Operative University Of Kenya
institutions.profile:
The Co-operative University of Kenya (CUK) is a public university situated in Karen, Nairobi. It originated from the Co-operative College of Kenya, which was established in 1947 and later transitioned into a university college in 2011 before receiving its charter in 2013. CUK is uniquely positioned as a leading institution in cooperative education, finance, and related business fields, playing a crucial role in the development of the cooperative sector in Kenya and beyond. The university is committed to providing relevant, high-quality education, training, research, and innovation.

The university's main campus in Karen provides a serene and conducive environment for learning. CUK offers a diverse range of academic programs across various faculties and schools, including cooperative development, business, information science, and social sciences, at certificate, diploma, undergraduate, and postgraduate levels. It attracts students seeking specialized knowledge in areas vital for economic development and community empowerment. The university is recognized for its strong links with the cooperative movement and industry, ensuring its curriculum remains current and relevant to the needs of the job market and societal development.
institutions.short_description: The Co-operative University of Kenya (CUK) is a leading public university in Karen, Nairobi, specializing in cooperative education, finance, and business. It evolved from the Co-operative College of Kenya and focuses on high-quality, relevant programs.

institutions.name: David M Wambuli Technical Vocational College
institutions.profile:
David M Wambuli Technical Vocational College (DMWTVC) is a public technical and vocational education and training institution located in Wote Town, Makueni County, Kenya. The college is strategically positioned in the county headquarters, serving the educational needs of students from Makueni and neighbouring counties. Established as part of the government's initiative to enhance TVET training across the country, DMWTVC is registered and regulated by the Technical and Vocational Education and Training Authority (TVETA). It plays a crucial role in providing market-relevant skills and knowledge to empower the youth for employment and entrepreneurship, contributing significantly to the socio-economic development of the region.

DMWTVC offers a diverse range of technical, vocational, and business-related courses at certificate and diploma levels. The college boasts qualified trainers and facilities aimed at providing hands-on practical training alongside theoretical knowledge. The programs cover various fields, including engineering, building technology, business studies, information communication technology, and more. As a KUCCPS-accredited institution, it attracts students placed by the central body as well as self-sponsored students. The institution is committed to producing competent and skilled graduates who can meet the demands of the evolving job market and drive innovation within their respective industries.
institutions.short_description: David M Wambuli Technical Vocational College (DMWTVC) is a public TVET institution in Wote, Makueni County, offering diverse technical and vocational programs to equip students with practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship opportunities.

institutions.name: Daystar University
institutions.profile:
Daystar University is a private Christian university in Kenya, founded in 1974 in Nairobi by American missionaries Donald and Faye Smith. Originally established as Daystar Communications, an interdenominational organization focused on media and communication training, it evolved into a college offering various programs. In 1984, it became Daystar University College and was granted a charter in 1998 by the Government of Kenya, becoming the first private university in East Africa to receive one. The main campus is situated in a serene environment in Athi River, Machakos County, with a satellite campus located along Valley Road in Nairobi. The university is accredited by the Commission for University Education (CUE) in Kenya and holds memberships in various international education associations, including the International Association of Universities (IAU) and the Council for Christian Colleges and Universities (CCCU).

Daystar University is known for its wide range of programs across disciplines such as Communication, Business, Theology, Education, and Humanities, offered at undergraduate and postgraduate levels. The curriculum integrates Christian faith and values with academic rigor, aiming to develop holistic individuals prepared for servant leadership. The university has built a reputation for producing graduates with strong ethical foundations and professional competence, particularly in fields like communication and media. Its commitment to Christian principles and academic excellence attracts students from across Africa and beyond, fostering a diverse and vibrant community. The institution actively engages in research and community service, contributing to societal development from a faith-based perspective.
institutions.short_description: Daystar University is a leading private Christian university in Kenya with campuses in Athi River and Nairobi. Founded in 1974, it offers a wide range of accredited undergraduate and postgraduate programs rooted in Christian values, focusing on developing holistic servant leaders for Africa.

institutions.name: Dedan Kimathi University Of Technology
institutions.profile:
Dedan Kimathi University of Technology (DKUT) is a public university located in Nyeri, Kenya. It was chartered as a fully-fledged university in 2012, building upon its foundation as the Kimathi University College of Technology (KUCT), a constituent college of Jomo Kenyatta University of Agriculture and Technology (JKUAT) established in 2007. Originally founded as Kimathi Institute of Technology in 1972, the institution has a long history of providing technical education in the region. Located along the Nyeri-Mweiga Road, DKUT is strategically positioned to serve students from across the country.

DKUT is renowned for its strong focus on science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM) programs. The university offers a wide array of courses at certificate, diploma, undergraduate, and postgraduate levels across various schools and institutes, including Engineering, Technology, Computer Science, Business Management, and Health Sciences. The university emphasizes practical training, innovation, research, and community engagement, aiming to produce skilled and innovative graduates who can contribute significantly to national development. DKUT is increasingly recognized for its research output and initiatives aimed at linking academia with industry.
institutions.short_description: Dedan Kimathi University of Technology (DKUT) is a public university in Nyeri, Kenya, specializing in STEM fields. Chartered in 2012, it offers practical-oriented programs from diploma to postgraduate levels, focusing on innovation and industry linkages.

institutions.name: Dr. Daniel Wako Murende Technical & Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Dr. Daniel Wako Murende Technical & Vocational College (DWM T&VC) is a prominent public TVET institution located in Nambale Town, Nambale Constituency, Busia County, Kenya. Established to contribute to Kenya's skilled workforce, the college operates under the Ministry of Education and is registered by the Technical and Vocational Education and Training Authority (TVETA). DWM T&VC is committed to providing accessible and relevant technical and vocational education and training that empowers individuals with practical skills, fosters innovation, and promotes self-reliance. The institution plays a vital role in developing human capacity in the region by offering competency-based training aligned with industry demands.

The college offers a wide array of programs at diploma, certificate, and artisan levels across disciplines such as Information Communication Technology, Business Studies, Building & Civil Engineering, Electrical & Electronic Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, and Hospitality & Tourism. These programs are delivered through a blend of theoretical instruction and extensive hands-on practical sessions conducted in modern workshops and laboratories. DWM T&VC strives to create a conducive learning environment that supports student success, encourages entrepreneurial spirit, and prepares graduates for successful careers or business ventures in a rapidly evolving economy.
institutions.short_description: Dr. Daniel Wako Murende TVC is a public technical and vocational college in Nambale, Busia County, Kenya. It provides diverse market-relevant diploma, certificate, and artisan programs across engineering, business, hospitality, and IT fields, focusing on practical skills and competency-based training for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: East African School Of Aviation
institutions.parent_ministry: Ministry of Transport, Infrastructure, Housing, Urban Development and Public Works
institutions.profile:
The East African School of Aviation (EASA) is a public institution located in Embakasi, Nairobi, Kenya. Established under the Kenya Civil Aviation Authority (KCAA), EASA serves as a premier training ground for aviation professionals across the East African region and beyond. It offers a wide range of specialized courses accredited by international bodies like the International Civil Aviation Organization (ICAO) and the International Air Transport Association (IATA), covering critical areas such as aircraft maintenance, air traffic control, aviation management, cabin crew training, and flight operations. The school is equipped with modern facilities, including hangars, simulation labs, and training aircraft, providing hands-on experience essential for the demanding aviation industry.

EASA plays a crucial role in developing skilled personnel to meet the growing demands of the aviation sector in Africa. Its reputation is built on providing high-quality, practical training aligned with international standards, making its graduates highly sought after by airlines, airports, and other aviation organizations. The institution's curriculum is designed to adapt to technological advancements and regulatory changes in the industry, ensuring that students receive relevant and up-to-date knowledge. Beyond technical training, EASA also focuses on instilling professionalism, safety culture, and ethical conduct, contributing significantly to enhancing aviation safety and efficiency in the region.
institutions.short_description: The East African School of Aviation (EASA) is a leading public aviation training institution in Nairobi, Kenya, offering specialized programs in various fields like aircraft maintenance, air traffic control, and aviation management, accredited by international bodies.

other_info.address: East African School of Aviation, JKIA Complex, Embakasi

institutions.name: Ebukanga Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Ebukanga Technical And Vocational College is a public TVET institution located in Ebusakami, Emuhaya Constituency, Vihiga County, Kenya. Established and registered in 2018 under the Ministry of Education, the college is dedicated to providing accessible and quality technical and vocational training. Situated in a rural setting, it serves students primarily from Vihiga and surrounding counties, aiming to equip them with practical skills and knowledge relevant to the current job market and for entrepreneurial ventures. The college plays a crucial role in enhancing technical capacity within the region, contributing to national development goals through skills-based education.

The college offers a diverse range of programs at Artisan, Certificate, and Diploma levels across various disciplines. These include courses in key areas such as Building Technology, Electrical & Electronics Engineering, Information Communication Technology (ICT), Business Studies, Hospitality, Agriculture, and Social Sciences. With modern workshops, laboratories, and qualified instructors, Ebukanga TVC strives to create a conducive learning environment that fosters innovation and practical competence. Its curriculum is designed to meet industry standards and national training requirements, preparing graduates for successful careers and self-sufficiency.
institutions.short_description: Ebukanga Technical And Vocational College is a public TVC in Emuhaya, Vihiga County, Kenya. Established in 2018, it offers diverse technical and vocational training programs at Artisan, Certificate, and Diploma levels, empowering youth with practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Egerton University
institutions.profile:
Egerton University, situated in the scenic town of Njoro, Nakuru County, is a cornerstone of higher education in Kenya with a rich history dating back to 1939. Initially founded as a Farm School by Lord Maurice Egerton of Tatton, it was established to train European settlers' sons in agriculture. Over the decades, it expanded its scope and evolved, becoming Egerton College in 1955 and eventually a constituent college of the University of Nairobi in 1986. In 1987, it attained full university status, becoming Egerton University, the first university chartered under the Universities Act, 1985. Its growth reflects Kenya's educational development and commitment to expanding access to higher learning beyond the capital.

Today, Egerton University is a comprehensive public university offering a wide array of academic programs across diverse fields including Agriculture, Arts & Social Sciences, Education, Engineering, Environment & Resources, Health Sciences, Science, Veterinary Medicine, and Commerce. Located primarily at its expansive main campus in Njoro, the university also operates a Town Campus in Nakuru city, catering to different student needs and program formats. It is renowned for its strong foundation in agricultural sciences, leveraging its historical roots and the surrounding fertile lands, while concurrently developing strong programs in science, technology, and humanities, contributing significantly to research, innovation, and human capital development in Kenya and the wider region. Its reputation is built on a legacy of practical education and academic expansion.
institutions.short_description: Egerton University, located in Njoro, Nakuru County, is Kenya's oldest institution of higher learning, founded in 1939 and chartered as a university in 1987. It offers a diverse range of academic programs across multiple disciplines.

institutions.name: Egoji Teachers Training College
programs: Diploma in Early Childhood Teacher Education (DECTE)
institutions.profile:
Egoji Teachers Training College is a prominent public teacher training institution located in Egoji, Meru County, Kenya. Established initially as a primary school in 1958, it underwent significant transformations, becoming a girls' boarding primary school and later a Harambee secondary school, before being handed over to the Catholic Church in 1972. Its conversion to a Teachers' College in 1978 marked its pivot towards training primary school teachers, a role it has fulfilled for over four decades.

Situated along the Meru-Nkubu road in Imenti South Constituency, Egoji TTC offers government-approved programs aimed at producing qualified educators. Currently, the college focuses on the Diploma in Primary Teacher Education (DPTE) and Diploma in Early Childhood Teacher Education (DECTE), aligning with the Ministry of Education's curriculum reforms. As a public institution under the Ministry of Education and regulated by the Teachers Service Commission, Egoji TTC maintains a reputation for providing structured pedagogical training to meet the educational needs of the nation.
institutions.short_description: Egoji Teachers Training College is a public institution in Egoji, Meru County, established in 1978. It specializes in training primary school teachers through Diploma programs like DPTE and DECTE, playing a key role in Kenya's education sector.

institutions.name: Eldama Ravine Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Eldama Ravine Technical and Vocational College (ERTVC) is a public institution dedicated to providing technical and vocational education and training (TVET) in Kenya. Established relatively recently, the college was founded in 2014 and officially opened its doors in 2016, operating under the Ministry of Education. Situated along the Eldama Ravine - Nakuru Highway in Eldama Ravine town, Baringo County, the college plays a vital role in equipping students from the region and beyond with practical skills essential for various industries.

ERTVC offers a diverse range of programs across several departments, including engineering (civil, electrical), ICT, business studies, hospitality, and applied sciences.
