The curriculum is designed to meet the demands of the modern job market, focusing on hands-on training and industry relevance. As a government TVET institution, ERTVC aims to contribute to national development by producing competent and skilled graduates prepared for employment or entrepreneurship, thereby enhancing human capital in the Rift Valley region.
institutions.short_description: Eldama Ravine Technical and Vocational College is a public TVET institution in Eldama Ravine, Baringo County. Established in 2014, it offers technical, vocational, business, and hospitality programs.

institutions.name: Eldoret Polytechnic
institutions.profile:
Eldoret Polytechnic is a prominent public Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) institution located in Eldoret, Uasin Gishu County, Kenya. Established in 1988 as Eldoret Technical Training Institute, it was officially upgraded to a national polytechnic in 2012. Situated in the heart of Eldoret town, the polytechnic plays a crucial role in providing accessible technical and business education to students from the North Rift region and beyond, contributing significantly to the development of a skilled workforce in Kenya.

The institution offers a wide array of programs across diverse fields, including various engineering disciplines (Mechanical, Electrical, Civil), Building & Civil Engineering, Business Studies, Information Communication Technology (ICT), Hospitality & Tourism, Health Sciences, and Applied Sciences. These programs are offered at different levels, including Diploma, Certificate, and Artisan courses, designed to equip students with practical skills and theoretical knowledge demanded by the industry. Eldoret Polytechnic is recognized for its commitment to quality training, state-of-the-art workshops and laboratories, and fostering innovation among its students.
institutions.short_description: Eldoret Polytechnic is a leading public TVET institution in Eldoret, Uasin Gishu County, offering a diverse range of practical-oriented Diploma, Certificate, and Artisan programs in engineering, business, ICT, and applied sciences.

institutions.name: Elwak Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Elwak Technical And Vocational College is a public institution situated in Elwak town, Mandera County, Kenya. Established as part of the Kenyan government's broader initiative to expand access to technical and vocational education and training (TVET) across the country, particularly in underserved regions, the college plays a crucial role in providing essential skills to the local youth. Its establishment reflects a commitment to decentralizing educational opportunities and addressing the specific needs of communities in arid and semi-arid lands (ASALs) like Mandera. By offering practical, skill-based training, Elwak TVC aims to empower its students for gainful employment and entrepreneurship, contributing significantly to the socio-economic development of the region.

The college offers a range of technical and business programs designed to equip students with competencies relevant to the local and national job markets. While specific program details may vary, TVET institutions typically provide courses in areas such as building technology, electrical engineering, automotive engineering, business management, accounting, and information technology at artisan, craft, and diploma levels. Elwak TVC serves as a vital educational hub in Mandera South Constituency, providing an accessible pathway to technical careers and business ventures for students who might otherwise face barriers to accessing post-secondary education. Its presence underscores the government's effort to enhance equitable access to quality education and training across all parts of Kenya.
institutions.short_description: Elwak Technical And Vocational College is a public TVET institution in Elwak, Mandera County, providing essential technical and vocational skills to local youth. It aims to empower students for employment and entrepreneurship, contributing to regional development through accessible, practical training.

institutions.name: Emgwen Technical & Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Emgwen Technical & Vocational College is a public TVET institution located in Kapsabet town, Emgwen Constituency, Nandi County, Kenya. The college is dedicated to providing quality technical and vocational training to equip students with practical skills relevant to the job market and entrepreneurship. Established to meet the growing demand for skilled manpower in the region and nationally, Emgwen TVC plays a crucial role in the development of technical expertise, offering a wide range of certificate and diploma programs across various disciplines. Its strategic location in Nandi County makes it accessible to students from the surrounding areas and beyond.

The college is committed to academic excellence, innovation, and community service. It offers programs in areas such as Building & Civil Engineering, Electrical & Electronics Engineering, Business Studies, ICT, Hospitality, and Agriculture, among others. Emgwen TVC aims to produce competent graduates who can contribute effectively to the socio-economic development of Kenya. The institution is regulated by the Ministry of Education and is a recognized institution for KUCCPS placements, attracting students seeking hands-on training and technical careers. With modern facilities and qualified instructors, Emgwen TVC strives to create a conducive learning environment that fosters skills development and holistic student growth.
institutions.short_description: Emgwen Technical & Vocational College in Kapsabet, Nandi County, is a public TVET institution offering diverse technical and vocational programs at certificate and diploma levels. It focuses on providing practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship, contributing to Kenya's skilled workforce development.

institutions.name: Emining Technical Training Institute
institutions.profile:
Emining Technical Training Institute (ETTI) is a prominent public Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) institution situated in Emining Town, Mogotio Constituency, Baringo County, Kenya. Established under the Ministry of Education, the institute plays a crucial role in the government's strategy to increase access to quality technical education and training. Located in the semi-arid region, ETTI serves students from Baringo and surrounding counties, aiming to equip them with practical, industry-relevant skills essential for employment and entrepreneurship. The serene environment of Emining provides a conducive learning atmosphere for students pursuing technical careers.

ETTI offers a diverse range of programs across various disciplines, including electrical and electronics engineering, building and civil engineering, information communication technology, business studies, and hospitality. These courses are designed to meet the demands of the current job market, providing graduates with competencies needed to contribute to national development. As a public institution, ETTI adheres to national TVET standards and regulations, ensuring the quality and relevance of its training programs. Its mission is centered on fostering innovation and producing highly skilled technical personnel capable of driving economic growth and improving livelihoods in the region and beyond.
institutions.short_description: Emining Technical Training Institute is a public TVET institution located in Emining, Baringo County, Kenya. It offers various technical and vocational courses aimed at equipping students with practical skills for the job market and fostering regional development.

institutions.name: Emsos Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Emsos Technical And Vocational College (TVC) is a public institution located in the Emsoo area, near the town of Eldoret, in Uasin Gishu County, Kenya. Established with the aim of providing accessible and quality technical and vocational training, the college operates under the Ministry of Education. Since its inception, Emsos TVC has been dedicated to equipping students with practical skills and knowledge necessary for gainful employment and entrepreneurship in various sectors. The college's location in a rapidly developing region of Kenya allows it to serve a significant population seeking technical skills.

The college offers a diverse range of programs at Artisan, Certificate, and Diploma levels, covering fields vital to the economy. These programs are designed to be hands-on and industry-relevant, ensuring that graduates are well-prepared for the workforce. While its reputation is still growing as a relatively new institution, Emsos TVC is committed to academic excellence and providing a supportive learning environment. Its focus on practical training aligns with the government's initiative to boost technical education and address skill gaps in the Kenyan job market, making it a key player in the regional education landscape.
institutions.short_description: Emsos Technical And Vocational College is a public institution in Uasin Gishu County, Kenya, offering technical and vocational programs from Artisan to Diploma levels, focusing on practical skills development for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Emurua Dikirr Technical Training Institute
institutions.profile:
Emurua Dikirr Technical Training Institute (EDTTI) is a public TVET institution located in Emurua Dikirr Town, Narok County, Kenya. Established to address the growing demand for technical and vocational skills in the region and the country at large, the institute plays a crucial role in providing accessible and quality training to young Kenyans. It operates under the Ministry of Education and is committed to empowering its students with relevant skills that meet industry standards and contribute to national development.

EDTTI offers a diverse range of programs across various disciplines, including business, ICT, engineering fields (civil, electrical, mechanical, automotive), agriculture, health sciences, hospitality, and liberal studies. The institute aims to provide practical, hands-on training complemented by theoretical knowledge, preparing graduates for employment, entrepreneurship, and further education. Its location in Narok County serves the local community and attracts students from surrounding areas, contributing to the socio-economic growth of the region.
institutions.short_description: Emurua Dikirr Technical Training Institute is a public TVET institution in Emurua Dikirr Town, Narok County, Kenya. It offers diverse technical and vocational programs across various disciplines, aiming to equip students with essential skills for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Endebess Technical Training Institute
institutions.profile:
Endebess Technical Training Institute (ETTI) is a public TVET institution located in Endebess Constituency, Trans Nzoia County, Kenya. Established to contribute to the country's development goals, ETTI focuses on providing relevant, quality technical and vocational education and training to equip students with practical skills needed for employment and entrepreneurship. The institute offers a range of programs across various disciplines, aimed at meeting the demands of the local and national job markets. Its location in Trans Nzoia provides accessibility for students from the surrounding agricultural region and beyond.

The institute is committed to fostering a conducive learning environment supported by qualified trainers and necessary infrastructure. ETTI plays a crucial role in bridging the skills gap by producing competent graduates ready to contribute to various sectors of the economy, including engineering, business, and ICT. Through partnerships and community engagement, ETTI strives to be a center of excellence in technical training, empowering youth and adults to improve their livelihoods and drive economic growth in the region.
institutions.short_description: Endebess Technical Training Institute (ETTI) is a public TVET college in Trans Nzoia County, Kenya, offering practical technical and vocational courses across various fields to equip students with essential skills for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Fayya Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Fayya Technical And Vocational College is a public institution located in Bura, Tana River County, Kenya. Established under the Ministry of Education, the college is dedicated to providing relevant and market-driven technical and vocational education and training (TVET) to young Kenyans. It plays a crucial role in enhancing access to post-secondary education and skill development opportunities in the Tana River region, which is essential for socio-economic growth and poverty reduction. The college is registered and regulated by the Technical and Vocational Education and Training Authority (TVETA) and participates in the Kenya Universities and Colleges Central Placement Service (KUCCPS) system, admitting students from across the country for various diploma, certificate, and artisan courses.

The college offers a diverse range of programs spanning multiple disciplines designed to meet the current demands of industry and the economy. Key areas of study often include various engineering trades (such as Building Technology, Electrical and Electronic Engineering), business studies, information communication technology (ICT), agriculture, fashion design, hospitality, and other technical courses like plumbing and welding. By focusing on practical training and hands-on skills, Fayya TVC aims to equip its graduates with the competencies needed for direct employment or entrepreneurship, contributing to the workforce in Tana River and beyond. The institution strives to be a center of excellence in TVET within the arid and semi-arid lands (ASALs) region it serves.
institutions.short_description: Fayya Technical And Vocational College is a public TVET institution situated in Bura, Tana River County. It offers a wide array of practical technical, vocational, and business programs aimed at empowering students with skills for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Friends College Kaimosi
institutions.profile:
Friends College Kaimosi (FCK) is a public Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) institution located in Kaimosi, Hamisi Constituency, Vihiga County, Kenya. Established under the TVET Act of 2013, the college is a constituent college of The Technical University of Kenya. Its roots trace back to vocational training initiatives championed by the Friends Church in the area, aiming to provide practical skills and technical knowledge to young Kenyans. Over the years, FCK has developed into a reputable institution, playing a crucial role in manpower development within the Western region by offering a diverse array of programs aligned with national development goals.

The college is situated in a serene environment conducive to learning. It offers numerous diploma, certificate, and artisan courses across various disciplines including Business Studies, ICT, Engineering, Applied Sciences, Health Sciences, Agriculture, Teacher Education, and Hospitality. FCK is committed to producing competent graduates equipped with industry-relevant skills, fostering innovation, and promoting research. Its strategic location makes it accessible to students from Vihiga and surrounding counties, contributing significantly to expanding access to technical and vocational training and empowering individuals for employment and entrepreneurship in the Kenyan economy.
institutions.short_description: Friends College Kaimosi is a public TVET college in Vihiga County, offering diverse diploma, certificate, and artisan programs. It focuses on practical, industry-relevant skills for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Galana Teachers Training College
programs: Diploma in Early Childhood Development Education (DECE)
Certificate in Early Childhood Development Education (CECE)
institutions.profile:
Galana Teachers Training College (GTTC) is a public teacher training institution located in Kilifi County, Kenya. Established in 2016, it was founded to address the growing need for qualified and competent teachers in the region and the country at large. The college is situated strategically along the Malindi-Mombasa Highway, specifically near the notable Sabaki Bridge in Malindi. As a public entity, GTTC operates under the Ministry of Education, aligning its curriculum and standards with national educational policies and the Teachers Service Commission (TSC).

GTTC is mandated to train teachers for various levels of the Kenyan education system. Its core programs include the Diploma in Primary Teacher Education (DPTE), the Diploma in Early Childhood Development Education (DECE), and the Certificate in Early Childhood Development Education (CECE). These programs are designed to equip trainees with the pedagogical skills, subject knowledge, and professional values necessary for effective teaching. The college aims to provide a conducive learning environment and contribute significantly to the development of quality education by producing well-trained and dedicated teachers.
institutions.short_description: Galana Teachers Training College is a public institution located near Sabaki Bridge, Malindi, Kilifi County. Established in 2016 under the Ministry of Education, it specializes in training teachers, offering programs like Diploma in Primary Teacher Education (DPTE) and Early Childhood Development Education (ECDE).

other_info.address: Along Malindi - Mombasa Highway, Sabaki Bridge, Malindi

institutions.name: Garissa Teachers Training College
programs: Diploma in Early Childhood Development Education
Certificate in Early Childhood Development Education
institutions.profile:
Garissa Teachers Training College (TTC) is a prominent public institution situated in Garissa Town, Garissa County, Kenya. Established in 1984, it initially operated as a day college before transitioning into a boarding institution. It falls under the Ministry of Education and plays a critical role in providing quality teacher education, particularly serving the unique needs of the arid and semi-arid lands (ASALs) in Kenya's North Eastern region. The college is committed to training competent and dedicated teachers capable of serving in diverse educational settings, contributing significantly to the development of human capital in the region.

The college offers various programs certified by the Kenya National Examinations Council (KNEC), including the Diploma in Primary Teacher Education (DPTE), Diploma in Early Childhood Development Education (DECE), and Certificate in Early Childhood Development Education, among other short courses and CBET programs. Despite facing regional challenges, Garissa TTC has maintained its commitment to academic excellence and professional development, producing qualified teachers who are essential for staffing schools in Garissa and surrounding counties. Its strategic location makes it a vital centre for teacher training in a region that historically faced educational disparities.
institutions.short_description: Garissa Teachers Training College is a public institution in Garissa Town, Garissa County, established in 1984. It specializes in training primary and early childhood development teachers, serving Kenya's North Eastern region. The college offers key diploma and certificate programs, contributing significantly to teacher supply in ASAL areas.

institutions.name: Garissa University
institutions.profile:
Garissa University (GaU) is a public university located in Garissa, Kenya. Established as a constituent college of Moi University in 2011, it was chartered as a full-fledged university in 2016. Situated in the arid and semi-arid region of Northern Kenya, the university plays a crucial role in expanding access to higher education in a historically underserved area. Despite facing significant challenges, including a tragic terrorist attack in 2015, the institution has demonstrated remarkable resilience, rebuilding its infrastructure and academic programs to continue serving the community and the nation.

GaU offers a diverse range of academic programs across various faculties and schools, including Education, Arts and Social Sciences, Business and Economics, and Science and Technology. The curriculum is designed to address the specific needs and challenges of the region while also providing nationally and internationally recognized qualifications. Committed to academic excellence, research, and community service, Garissa University aims to contribute significantly to the social and economic development of Northern Kenya and beyond through quality education, innovative research, and community engagement initiatives.
institutions.short_description: Garissa University is a public university located in Garissa, Kenya. Established as a constituent college and chartered in 2016, it offers diverse programs in Education, Arts, Business, and Science, focusing on expanding access to higher education in Northern Kenya and contributing to regional development.

institutions.name: Garsen Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Garsen Technical And Vocational College (Garsen TVC) is a public technical and vocational training institution located in Garsen town, Tana River County, Kenya. Established by the government as part of its initiative to expand access to technical education across the country, especially in marginalized regions, the college plays a crucial role in providing relevant skills training to the youth in the lower Tana region. Its strategic location along the Garsen-Witu road makes it accessible to students from within the county and surrounding areas, aiming to address the shortage of skilled labor and promote economic development through vocational training.

The college offers a diverse range of artisan, certificate, and diploma courses accredited by relevant national bodies. These programs span various fields including engineering disciplines (such as electrical and building technology), information and communication technology, business studies, agriculture, hospitality, and technical trades like hairdressing and tailoring. Garsen TVC is committed to equipping its graduates with practical, market-ready skills that enable them to gain meaningful employment, pursue further education, or start their own businesses, thereby contributing to improved livelihoods and socio-economic growth in the community.
institutions.short_description: Garsen Technical And Vocational College is a public TVC in Garsen, Tana River County, offering diverse artisan, certificate, and diploma programs in technical and vocational fields to equip local youth with essential skills.

other_info.address: P.O Box 66-80201 Garsen, Along Garsen-Witu Road, Garsen Town

institutions.name: Gatanga Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Gatanga Technical And Vocational College is a public TVET institution situated in Gatanga, Murang'a County, Kenya. It was established under the government's initiative to expand access to technical and vocational education across the country, aiming to provide essential skills training to young Kenyans. The college is strategically located to serve students from Murang'a and surrounding regions, offering them opportunities to acquire practical knowledge and technical expertise vital for the job market and entrepreneurial ventures. It plays a crucial role in contributing to local and national development by fostering a skilled workforce.

The college offers a diverse range of programs across various technical and business disciplines. These include courses in engineering technologies, information communication technology (ICT), business studies, agriculture, hospitality, and applied sciences. Gatanga TVC is committed to providing quality education and training through competent instructors and relevant practical facilities. It is registered and regulated by the Technical and Vocational Education and Training Authority (TVETA) and participates in the KUCCPS central placement system, making its programs accessible to a wide pool of eligible students seeking technical training.
institutions.short_description: Gatanga Technical And Vocational College is a public TVET institution in Gatanga, Murang'a County. It offers diverse technical and vocational programs across fields like engineering, ICT, business, and agriculture, aimed at equipping students with practical skills for employment and self-reliance.

institutions.name: Gatundu South Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Gatundu South Technical and Vocational College (GSTVC) is a public TVET institution located in Gatundu Town, Gatundu South Constituency, Kiambu County, Kenya. Established to address the growing need for skilled manpower in the region and the country, GSTVC plays a crucial role in providing technical and vocational training programs. The college is registered and regulated by the Technical and Vocational Education and Training Authority (TVETA) and is affiliated with the Kenya National Examinations Council (KNEC) and other relevant examination bodies for certification of its graduates. It falls under the purview of the Ministry of Education.

The college offers a diverse range of market-driven programs across various disciplines including business, engineering, ICT, and hospitality. These courses are designed to equip students with practical skills and knowledge, enhancing their employability and enabling them to contribute to national development. GSTVC is committed to fostering a conducive learning environment and strives to maintain high standards of education and training. Its location makes it accessible to students from Kiambu County and surrounding areas, positioning it as a key institution for TVET education in the central region of Kenya.
institutions.short_description: Gatundu South Technical and Vocational College, located in Gatundu Town, Kiambu County, is a public TVET institution providing market-driven technical and vocational training programs across various sectors like business, engineering, and hospitality to equip students with practical skills for employment and national development.

institutions.name: Gichugu Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Gichugu Technical and Vocational College (GTVC) is a public institution operating under the Kenyan Ministry of Education. Strategically located in Wang'uru, Gichugu Constituency, within Kirinyaga County, the college is dedicated to providing accessible and market-driven technical and vocational education and training (TVET). Established to meet the increasing demand for skilled professionals, GTVC offers a diverse portfolio of programs across various technical and business disciplines, including engineering, building technology, business studies, ICT, and applied sciences. These programs are designed to equip students with the practical skills and theoretical knowledge essential for successful careers at certificate and diploma levels.

The college is focused on enhancing employability and fostering entrepreneurship among its graduates. By emphasizing hands-on training and maintaining relevance with industry needs, GTVC contributes significantly to the national goal of developing a competent workforce capable of driving economic growth. The institution is duly registered and regulated by the Technical and Vocational Education and Training Authority (TVETA), ensuring the quality and standards of its educational offerings. Its location makes it a key educational resource, serving students not only from Kirinyaga County but also from neighboring regions.
institutions.short_description: Gichugu Technical and Vocational College, located in Wang'uru, Kirinyaga County, is a public TVET institution offering market-driven certificate and diploma programs across engineering, business, and technical fields to equip students with practical skills.

institutions.name: Gilgil Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Gilgil Technical And Vocational College (GTVC) is a public tertiary institution situated in Gilgil, Nakuru County, Kenya. Established under the Ministry of Education, the college plays a crucial role in providing technical and vocational training to equip young Kenyans with practical skills essential for employment and entrepreneurship. Located strategically along the Gilgil-Olkalou road, GTVC serves students from the surrounding region and beyond, offering a conducive learning environment for hands-on training.

The college offers a diverse range of diploma and certificate programs across various disciplines, including electrical and electronics engineering, building and civil engineering, information communication technology, business studies, hospitality, and applied sciences. These programs are designed to meet the demands of the current job market and contribute to national development goals by producing skilled manpower. GTVC is known for its commitment to practical learning, state-of-the-art workshops (where applicable), and qualified instructors, fostering a reputation as a reliable institution for technical education in the Rift Valley region. It is registered by the Technical and Vocational Education and Training Authority (TVETA) and its programs are recognized by relevant examination bodies like KNEC.
institutions.short_description: Gilgil Technical And Vocational College is a public TVET institution in Gilgil, Nakuru County, offering diverse technical, hospitality, and business diploma/certificate programs.

other_info.address: Along Gilgil-Olkalou road opposite Gilgil prison, P.O Box 132-20116, Gilgil

institutions.name: Githunguri Technical & Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Githunguri Technical & Vocational College (Githunguri TVC) is a public technical and vocational training institution located in Githunguri, Kiambu County, Kenya. Established to provide accessible and quality technical education, the college plays a crucial role in equipping students with practical skills and knowledge essential for various industries. It offers a diverse range of certificate and diploma programs aligned with market demands, focusing on technical, vocational, and business-related fields. The institution aims to contribute to the socio-economic development of the region and the nation by producing competent and skilled graduates.

The college is situated within the Githunguri area, making it easily accessible to students from the surrounding region. Its programs are designed to meet the needs of both entry-level students seeking foundational skills and those looking to advance their careers. Githunguri TVC is recognized for its commitment to practical training, providing students with hands-on experience alongside theoretical knowledge. As a public institution under the Ministry of Education, it adheres to national standards for TVET education and often features in the KUCCPS selection list, attracting students from across the country.
institutions.short_description: Githunguri Technical & Vocational College is a public TVET institution in Kiambu County, Kenya, offering market-driven certificate and diploma programs with a focus on practical skills for national development.

other_info.address: Along Githunguri-Ndumberi Road, opposite Githunguri Police Station

institutions.name: Gitwebe Technical Training Institute
programs: Diploma in Food and Beverage Management
Certificate in Food and Beverage Management
institutions.profile:
Gitwebe Technical Training Institute (GTTI) is a prominent public Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) institution situated in the Gitwebe area, along the Keroka-Nyamira road in Nyamira County, Kenya. Established under the Ministry of Education, GTTI plays a crucial role in contributing to Kenya's national development by providing accessible, relevant, and quality technical and business training. The institute offers a diverse portfolio of programs at certificate and diploma levels across various disciplines, including engineering (automotive, building, civil, electrical, mechanical), business, hospitality, health sciences, and information communication technology (ICT). These programs are designed to meet the demands of the modern job market, focusing on practical skills and hands-on experience.

GTTI is dedicated to nurturing skilled manpower capable of driving innovation and economic growth. Its curriculum is aligned with national development goals, aiming to produce competent graduates ready for employment or self-employment. The institute provides a conducive learning environment with qualified faculty and necessary infrastructure to support effective training. By offering a wide array of technical and vocational courses, Gitwebe TTI serves the community in Nyamira and surrounding regions, providing pathways for career development and contributing to the national agenda of expanding TVET opportunities. Its commitment to practical education makes it a valuable institution for students seeking industry-relevant skills.
institutions.short_description: Gitwebe Technical Training Institute is a public TVET institution in Gitwebe, Nyamira County, offering diverse technical, vocational, and business programs focused on practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Godoma Technical Training Institute
institutions.profile:
Godoma Technical Training Institute (Godoma TTI) is a public Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) institution located in Hola, Tana River County, Kenya. Established in 2014, the institute operates under the Ministry of Education and is dedicated to providing relevant skills and knowledge necessary for national development and individual empowerment. Situated along the Garsen-Hola Road, Godoma TTI serves the local community and surrounding regions, offering a range of technical and business programs designed to meet the current and future demands of the labor market.

The institute is committed to fostering a learning environment that promotes innovation, practical skills acquisition, and entrepreneurial thinking. It offers courses across various disciplines including engineering, business, and ICT. As a TVET institution, Godoma TTI plays a crucial role in bridging the skills gap in the region, providing young people with opportunities for employment and self-reliance. Its location in Tana River County makes it a vital educational hub, contributing to socio-economic growth by producing skilled graduates ready to contribute to various sectors.
institutions.short_description: Godoma Technical Training Institute, located in Hola, Tana River County, is a public TVET institution established in 2014. It offers a variety of technical, business, and ICT programs, aiming to equip students with practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Great Lakes University Of Kisumu
institutions.profile:
Great Lakes University of Kisumu (GLUK) is a private university in Kenya, located in Kisumu. Founded in 1998 by the Tropical Institute of Community Health (TICH) as a constituent college, it received its charter in 2007, becoming a fully-fledged university. The institution was established with a strong focus on community service, research, and addressing development challenges relevant to Africa. GLUK offers a range of programs across various faculties, including Public Health, Environmental Science, Business, Education, Information Sciences, and Social Sciences, emphasizing interdisciplinary approaches to learning and problem-solving.

The main campus of GLUK is situated in Mamboleo along the Kisumu-Kakamega Highway, providing a serene environment conducive to learning. The university is recognized for its commitment to producing graduates who are not only academically competent but also socially responsible and ethically grounded. Through its various outreach programs and research initiatives, GLUK actively engages with local communities, contributing to regional development and public health improvements. Its reputation is built on its strong foundation in community-oriented education and its role in expanding access to higher education in the western Kenya region.
institutions.short_description: Great Lakes University of Kisumu (GLUK) is a private chartered university located in Kisumu, Kenya. Established with a strong community service ethos, it offers diverse programs aimed at producing ethical professionals capable of addressing development challenges through transformative education and research.

institutions.name: Gretsa University
institutions.profile:
Gretsa University, located in Thika, Kiambu County, is a private institution of higher learning in Kenya that received its charter in 2008. Established with a vision to provide quality education and training, the university is situated along the busy Thika-Garissa Road, making it accessible. It offers a diverse range of academic programs spanning across various disciplines including Business, Information Technology, Education, Social Sciences, and Hospitality. The university emphasizes a blend of theoretical knowledge and practical skills, aiming to produce graduates who are not only academically competent but also ready for the professional world and entrepreneurial ventures.

The university campus in Thika provides a conducive learning environment with facilities designed to support student life and academic pursuits. Gretsa University is recognized by the Commission for University Education (CUE) in Kenya, ensuring its programs meet national and international standards. It attracts students from different parts of Kenya and the East African region, contributing to a vibrant and multicultural community. The institution's commitment to innovation, research, and community engagement plays a significant role in shaping its reputation as a growing force in the Kenyan higher education landscape, focusing on holistic development and societal impact.
institutions.short_description: Gretsa University is a chartered private university situated in Thika, Kiambu County, Kenya. It offers diverse programs in business, IT, education, and social sciences, emphasizing practical skills, entrepreneurship, and community engagement for holistic student development.

institutions.name: Heroes Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Heroes Technical And Vocational College (TVC) is a public institution located in Nakuru Town West Constituency, Nakuru County, Kenya. Established in 2017 under the Ministry of Education, it plays a vital role in providing accessible and quality technical and vocational education and training (TVET) to Kenyan youth. The college is situated along Mburu Gichua Road, making it easily accessible within Nakuru town. It is registered by TVETA and aims to equip students with practical skills for various industries, contributing to national development through a skilled workforce.

Heroes TVC offers a diverse range of diploma, certificate, and artisan courses across various fields including Business Studies, Hospitality, ICT, Engineering, Building Technology, and Applied Sciences. The institution is committed to fostering innovation, entrepreneurship, and research among its students. Through its programs and industry linkages, Heroes TVC strives to produce competent graduates who can contribute effectively to the job market and entrepreneurial ventures. Its reputation is growing as a key TVET provider in the Rift Valley region.
institutions.short_description: Heroes Technical And Vocational College (TVC) is a public institution in Nakuru, Kenya, offering diverse technical and vocational training programs. Established in 2017, it equips students with practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship, contributing to national development.

institutions.name: Ijara Technical & Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Ijara Technical & Vocational College is a public institution located in Ijara Town, Garissa County, in the arid and semi-arid lands (ASAL) region of Kenya. Established under the Ministry of Education, the college plays a crucial role in expanding access to technical and vocational education and training (TVET) in an area historically underserved by such facilities. It aims to equip local youth and residents with practical skills and knowledge relevant to the economic needs and opportunities of the region, contributing directly to community development and empowerment.

The college offers a range of diploma, certificate, and artisan programs across various disciplines, including business studies, information communication technology, engineering fields like electrical and building technology, fashion design, food and beverage, and social work. By providing these diverse courses, Ijara TVC endeavors to address skills gaps, promote self-employment, and improve the employability of its graduates. Its establishment is a key step towards enhancing educational equity and fostering socio-economic growth in Garissa County by making technical skills training accessible locally.
institutions.short_description: Ijara Technical & Vocational College is a public TVET institution in Ijara Town, Garissa County. It provides vital technical and vocational skills training in various disciplines to empower youth in the ASAL region and support local development.

institutions.name: Ikutha Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Ikutha Technical and Vocational College (ITVC) is a public institution located in Ikutha Town, Kitui South Constituency, Kitui County, Kenya. Established under the Kenyan Ministry of Education, ITVC aims to provide accessible and quality technical and vocational education and training (TVET) to youth and members of the local community, particularly in the semi-arid region it serves. The college is dedicated to equipping students with practical skills and knowledge relevant to industry demands and entrepreneurial ventures, contributing to human capital development and socio-economic empowerment in the area.

The college offers a diverse range of programs across various departments, including Business Studies, Building & Civil Engineering, Electrical & Electronic Engineering, Information Communication Technology (ICT), and Health & Applied Sciences. These programs are offered at diploma, certificate, and artisan levels, catering to different academic backgrounds and career aspirations. By focusing on hands-on training and competency-based education, Ikutha TVC strives to produce graduates who are not only employable but also capable of creating their own employment opportunities, addressing the skills gap in the region and contributing to national development goals.
institutions.short_description: Ikutha Technical and Vocational College (ITVC) is a public TVET institution in Ikutha, Kitui County, offering diverse diploma, certificate, and artisan programs in fields like engineering, business, and ICT. It provides practical skills training to empower youth for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: International Leadership University
institutions.profile:
International Leadership University (ILU), formerly known as Nairobi International School of Theology (NIST), was founded in 1994 with a vision to develop transformative leaders for Africa. Accredited by the Commission for University Education (CUE) in Kenya, ILU is committed to integrating biblical principles with academic excellence, equipping students not just with professional skills but also with a strong ethical foundation and a passion for service in both church and society. The university offers a range of programs across disciplines like Theology, Business, Education, and Leadership, attracting a diverse student body from Kenya and other African nations.

Located in the serene and conducive environment of Karen in Nairobi, the International Leadership University provides a unique learning atmosphere that fosters spiritual growth, academic rigor, and community engagement. Its reputation is built on its distinct approach to leadership development, emphasizing integrity, vision, and servant leadership. The university's faculty are dedicated scholars and practitioners who guide students in their intellectual and personal formation. ILU plays a significant role in shaping leaders who are prepared to address complex challenges and contribute positively to their communities and the wider world.
institutions.short_description: International Leadership University (ILU), formerly NIST, is a private university in Karen, Nairobi, accredited by CUE. It is dedicated to equipping transformative leaders with biblical integration for service in church and society.

other_info.address: Mokoyeti Road East, off Magadi Road, Karen, P.O. Box 24680-00502 Nairobi, Kenya

institutions.name: Islamic University Of Kenya
institutions.profile:
The Islamic University of Kenya (IUK) is a distinguished private institution of higher learning situated in Kitengela, Kajiado County, Kenya. Founded with a core mission to integrate academic excellence with Islamic ethical principles, IUK aims to cultivate knowledgeable, moral, and socially responsible leaders who can contribute positively to society. The university offers a range of programs designed to meet contemporary needs while upholding values rooted in faith and community service.

IUK's campus in Kitengela provides a conducive environment for learning and personal growth. The university is committed to delivering quality education across various disciplines, fostering critical thinking, and promoting research that addresses societal challenges. Through its unique approach that blends academic rigor with spiritual development, IUK strives to empower its students to become agents of positive change, embodying the principles of knowledge, piety, and beneficial contribution to their communities and the world at large.
institutions.short_description: Islamic University of Kenya, located in Kitengela, Kajiado County, is a private institution integrating academic excellence with Islamic values, focusing on ethical leadership and community service through quality education and spiritual development.

other_info.address: P.O Box 1033-00204, Kitengela. Kitengela Campus along Kitengela-Namanga Road.

institutions.name: Jaramogi Oginga Odinga University Of Science And Technology
institutions.profile:
Jaramogi Oginga Odinga University of Science and Technology (JOOUST) is a public university situated in Bondo, Siaya County, Kenya. It commenced its operations in 2009 as a constituent college of Maseno University and was awarded its charter on February 13, 2013, becoming a fully-fledged university. Located on a serene campus along the shores of Lake Victoria, JOOUST is strategically positioned to serve the Nyanza region and contribute to national development, with a strong emphasis on science, technology, and innovation.

JOOUST offers a wide array of academic programs across various faculties and schools, including Agriculture, Education, Pure and Applied Sciences, Health Sciences, Engineering, Informatics and Innovative Systems, and Business and Economics. The university is committed to providing quality education, fostering research and innovation relevant to societal needs, promoting community engagement, and developing graduates equipped with skills for national and global challenges. It is recognized as a rapidly growing institution contributing significantly to higher education access in western Kenya.
institutions.short_description: Jaramogi Oginga Odinga University of Science and Technology (JOOUST) is a public university located in Bondo, Siaya County. Established in 2009 and chartered in 2013, it is renowned for its focus on science, technology, and innovation.

other_info.address: P. O. BOX 210 - 40601 BONDO, Bondo Town, Along Bondo - Usenge Road, Siaya County
other_info.location: Bondo Town, Along Bondo - Usenge Road

institutions.name: Jeremiah Nyagah Technical Institute
institutions.profile:
Jeremiah Nyagah Technical Institute, located in Embu, Kenya, is a public tertiary institution dedicated to providing technical and vocational education and training (TVET). The institute aims to equip students with practical skills and knowledge relevant to various industries, contributing to the socio-economic development of the region and the country. The institute offers a range of diploma and certificate courses designed to meet the demands of the job market. These programs cover diverse fields such as engineering, agriculture, business, and ICT. 

The institute is situated in Embu County, making it accessible to students from the surrounding areas. Jeremiah Nyagah Technical Institute strives to maintain a positive reputation by fostering a conducive learning environment and emphasizing practical skills. Through its commitment to quality TVET, the institute plays a crucial role in empowering individuals with the competencies needed for successful careers and entrepreneurship, thereby supporting Kenya's industrial growth and economic development.
institutions.short_description: Jeremiah Nyagah Technical Institute is a public college in Embu, Kenya, offering diverse technical and vocational programs. It focuses on practical skills for various industries and contributes to regional socio-economic development.

institutions.name: Jomo Kenyatta University Of Agriculture And Technology
institutions.profile:
Jomo Kenyatta University of Agriculture and Technology (JKUAT) is a leading public university in Kenya, established in 1981 as a middle-level college and chartered in 1994. Located in Juja, Kiambu County, JKUAT is renowned for its focus on agriculture, engineering, technology, and innovation. The university offers a wide range of undergraduate, postgraduate, and doctoral programs, emphasizing research, entrepreneurship, and industry collaboration. With multiple campuses across Kenya, JKUAT is committed to producing globally competitive graduates and advancing sustainable development through cutting-edge research and technology transfer.

JKUAT's main campus in Juja provides a vibrant academic environment with modern facilities, research centers, and a dedicated faculty. The university has gained recognition for its contributions to agricultural research, technological advancements, and training of skilled professionals. JKUAT also plays a significant role in community outreach programs, promoting sustainable practices and empowering local communities. Its reputation as a center of excellence in science and technology makes it a preferred choice for students seeking quality higher education in Kenya and beyond.
institutions.short_description: Jomo Kenyatta University of Agriculture and Technology (JKUAT) is a premier public university located in Juja, Kenya, renowned for its programs in agriculture, engineering, science, and technology, and its commitment to innovation.

institutions.name: Jomo Kenyatta University Of Agriculture And Technology Tvet Institute
institutions.profile:
The Jomo Kenyatta University of Agriculture and Technology (JKUAT) TVET Institute is a constituent college of JKUAT, dedicated to providing high-quality technical and vocational education and training. Located in Juja, Kiambu County, the institute offers a range of diploma and certificate programs designed to equip students with practical skills and knowledge relevant to various industries. The institute aims to address the growing demand for skilled technicians and professionals in Kenya and the broader East African region.

The TVET Institute focuses on hands-on training and industry-relevant curricula. Programs cover fields such as engineering, agriculture, information technology, and business. JKUAT TVET Institute is committed to fostering innovation, entrepreneurship, and sustainable development through its training programs, contributing significantly to the nation's economic growth and development. The institute benefits from the resources and expertise of the main JKUAT campus, ensuring students receive a well-rounded and comprehensive education.
institutions.short_description: Jomo Kenyatta University of Agriculture and Technology TVET Institute, located in Juja, Kiambu, offers technical and vocational training programs aimed at providing practical skills in various fields. It is a constituent college of JKUAT.

institutions.name: Jooust Centre Of Technical Vocational And Education Training
institutions.profile:
The Jooust Centre of Technical Vocational and Education Training (TVET) is a public institution located in Bondo, Siaya County, Kenya. As a TVET center affiliated with Jaramogi Oginga Odinga University of Science and Technology (JOOUST), it focuses on providing technical and vocational skills training to equip students with practical knowledge for various industries. The center offers a range of programs designed to meet the demands of the job market and promote self-employment among its graduates.

JOOUST TVET aims to be a leading institution in technical and vocational education in the region. It strives to offer high-quality training, foster innovation, and contribute to the socio-economic development of Siaya County and beyond. The center's programs are designed to provide students with hands-on experience and prepare them for successful careers in their chosen fields. The institution is committed to providing accessible and affordable education to all students, regardless of their background.
institutions.short_description: JOOUST Centre of Technical Vocational and Education Training is a public TVET college in Bondo, Siaya, affiliated with JOOUST, offering practical skills training for various industries and promoting regional socio-economic development.

institutions.name: Kabarak University
institutions.profile:
Kabarak University is one of the leading private universities in Kenya, founded with a mission to provide holistic quality education based on Christian values. Established by the late President Daniel T. arap Moi, the university stands as a testament to the power of education to transform lives, societies, and the nation at large.

Located in Nakuru County, Kabarak University offers a wide range of undergraduate and postgraduate programs across various disciplines, including business, law, engineering, health sciences, and theology. The university is renowned for its commitment to academic excellence, research, and community service. Its serene environment provides a conducive atmosphere for learning and personal growth. Kabarak University is also known for its strong emphasis on ethics and integrity, shaping students into responsible and ethical leaders.
institutions.short_description: A leading private university in Nakuru, founded by Daniel T. arap Moi, providing holistic education based on Christian values. Kabarak University is committed to academic excellence and ethical leadership.

institutions.name: Kabete National Polytechnic
institutions.profile:
Kabete National Polytechnic, located in Kabete Constituency, Nairobi County, is a leading public technical training institution in Kenya. Established to provide high-quality technical and vocational education and training (TVET), the polytechnic offers a wide range of courses designed to equip students with practical skills and knowledge relevant to various industries. The institution is committed to fostering innovation, entrepreneurship, and sustainable development through its diverse academic programs and research activities.

Kabete National Polytechnic has a long-standing reputation for excellence in TVET. The institution continually updates its curriculum to meet the changing needs of the job market, ensuring that graduates are well-prepared for employment and self-employment opportunities. The polytechnic also focuses on collaborations with industry partners to provide students with internships, apprenticeships, and other practical experiences. Its strategic location in Kabete provides a conducive environment for learning and innovation, attracting students from across the country.
institutions.short_description: Kabete National Polytechnic is a public TVET institution in Kabete, Nairobi, offering diverse technical courses. It aims to provide practical skills and knowledge for various industries, fostering innovation and entrepreneurship among students.

institutions.name: Kaelo Technical Training Institute
institutions.profile:
Kaelo Technical Training Institute is a public tertiary institution located in Maua, Igembe South Constituency, Meru County. It operates under the Kenyan Ministry of Education, dedicated to providing quality technical and vocational education and training (TVET). The institute aims to equip students with practical skills and knowledge relevant to various industries, fostering self-reliance and contributing to national development.

The institute offers a range of certificate and diploma courses in fields such as building and construction, electrical engineering, automotive engineering, business management, information technology, and agricultural studies. These programs are designed to meet the demands of the job market and prepare graduates for employment or entrepreneurship. Kaelo TTI is committed to providing a conducive learning environment with well-equipped workshops, laboratories, and qualified instructors to ensure students receive hands-on training and theoretical knowledge.
institutions.short_description: Kaelo Technical Training Institute, located in Maua, Meru County, is a public TVET institution offering certificate and diploma courses in various technical and vocational fields, fostering practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Kagumo Teachers Training College
institutions.profile:
Kagumo Teachers Training College is a public institution located in the Kagumo area of Nyeri County, Kenya. Established to train and develop primary school teachers, the college has a long history of contributing to the education sector in the country. Kagumo TTC offers various programs focused on teacher education, equipping students with the pedagogical skills and knowledge necessary for effective teaching. The college is under the supervision of the Ministry of Education.

Located in a serene environment conducive to learning, Kagumo TTC aims to provide quality education and training to its students. With experienced faculty and modern facilities, the college is committed to producing competent and dedicated teachers who can positively impact the lives of their students and contribute to the overall development of the education system in Kenya. Kagumo Teachers Training College has a strong reputation for academic excellence and a commitment to serving the community through education.
institutions.short_description: Kagumo Teachers Training College, located in Nyeri, is a public institution dedicated to training competent and dedicated primary school teachers, contributing significantly to Kenya's education sector.

institutions.name: Kaiboi National Polytechnic
institutions.profile:
Kaiboi National Polytechnic is a leading public technical institution located in Kaiboi, Moiben Constituency, Uasin Gishu County, Kenya. Established to provide high-quality technical and vocational education and training (TVET), the polytechnic plays a crucial role in equipping students with practical skills and knowledge that are essential for the job market and self-employment. The institution is committed to fostering innovation, entrepreneurship, and sustainable development through its various programs and initiatives.

Kaiboi National Polytechnic offers a wide range of courses across various disciplines, including engineering, business, information technology, agriculture, and hospitality. These programs are designed to meet the evolving needs of the industry and are regularly updated to incorporate the latest technological advancements and best practices. The polytechnic also focuses on research and development, collaborating with industry partners to develop innovative solutions to real-world challenges. With a focus on hands-on training and practical application, Kaiboi National Polytechnic aims to produce graduates who are highly skilled, employable, and ready to contribute to the economic growth of the region and the country as a whole.
institutions.short_description: Kaiboi National Polytechnic is a public TVET institution in Uasin Gishu County, offering diverse programs in engineering, business, and IT. Committed to providing practical skills and fostering innovation for economic growth.

institutions.name: Kaimosi Friends University
institutions.profile:
Kaimosi Friends University (KAFU) is a public university situated in Kaimosi, Vihiga County, Kenya. Established in 2015 as a constituent college of Masinde Muliro University of Science and Technology, KAFU was chartered as a full-fledged university in 2022. Its establishment was deeply influenced by the Friends Church (Quakers), emphasizing values of integrity, simplicity, and community service. The university aims to provide quality education rooted in ethical principles, fostering a conducive environment for research, innovation, and holistic development.

KAFU offers a range of undergraduate and postgraduate programs across various disciplines, including education, business, science, and technology. The university is dedicated to addressing the educational and developmental needs of Western Kenya and beyond. Its serene location in Kaimosi provides a conducive atmosphere for learning and personal growth. Kaimosi Friends University is committed to producing graduates who are not only academically competent but also ethically grounded and ready to contribute positively to society.
institutions.short_description: Kaimosi Friends University, located in Vihiga County, is a public university founded on Quaker values, offering diverse programs in education, business, sciences, and technology since 2015.

institutions.name: Kaimosi Teachers Training College
programs: Diploma in Early Childhood Development Education (DECDE)
institutions.profile:
Kaimosi Teachers Training College (Kaimosi TTC) is a prominent public institution situated in Kaimosi, Hamisi Constituency, Vihiga County, Kenya. Established to cater to the growing demand for qualified teachers, the college plays a crucial role in the Ministry of Education's efforts to enhance the quality of primary and early childhood education across the nation. Its strategic location in Western Kenya makes it accessible to students from various parts of the region and beyond, fostering a diverse learning environment.

The college offers a range of teacher training programs, including the Diploma in Primary Teacher Education (DPTE) and Diploma in Early Childhood Development Education (DECDE), aligned with the current Competency Based Curriculum (CBC). Kaimosi TTC is recognized for its commitment to academic excellence, practical training, and holistic student development. It boasts qualified faculty, adequate training facilities, and a conducive learning atmosphere designed to equip trainee teachers with the necessary pedagogical skills, subject knowledge, and professional ethics required for effective teaching in Kenyan schools.
institutions.short_description: Kaimosi Teachers Training College is a public institution in Vihiga County, Kenya, offering Diploma programs in Primary Teacher Education and Early Childhood Development Education. It focuses on producing competent teachers for the Kenyan education system through quality training.

institutions.name: Kajiado East Technical & Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Kajiado East Technical & Vocational College (KETVC) is a dynamic public TVET institution situated in Mashuru, Kajiado East Constituency, Kajiado County, Kenya. Established to address the growing demand for skilled manpower in the region and nationally, KETVC plays a crucial role in providing accessible and relevant technical and vocational training. The college is dedicated to offering a wide array of courses across various disciplines, designed to meet the needs of both students seeking employment and industries requiring competent professionals. Its curriculum is structured to provide hands-on experience and practical knowledge, preparing graduates for immediate entry into the workforce or for further education.

KETVC operates under the Ministry of Education and is a registered institution under TVETA (Technical and Vocational Education and Training Authority). It is also listed among the institutions whose programs are accessible through the Kenya Universities and Colleges Central Placement Service (KUCCPS), facilitating student admissions from across the country. Located in a semi-arid region, the college not only serves the local community but also attracts students from beyond, contributing to the socio-economic development of Kajiado County. The institution is committed to fostering innovation, entrepreneurship, and community engagement through its training programs and activities, aiming to be a leading center for technical and vocational excellence in Kenya.
institutions.short_description: Kajiado East Technical & Vocational College (KETVC) is a public TVET institution located in Mashuru, Kajiado County, Kenya. It offers diverse technical and vocational programs aimed at equipping students with practical skills for various industries, contributing to national development.

institutions.name: Kajiado West Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Kajiado West Technical and Vocational College (KWTVC) is a public TVET institution located in Kajiado West Sub-County, Kajiado County. Situated in Oloitokitok Centre along the Kiserian-Isinya road, the college aims to equip the youth with essential technical and vocational skills to meet the demands of the job market and promote self-employment. Established under the Ministry of Education, KWTVC plays a crucial role in expanding access to quality technical education in the Kajiado region and beyond, contributing to national development goals through skilled manpower.

The college offers a diverse range of programs at Diploma and Certificate levels across various disciplines. Key departments include Building & Civil Engineering, Information Communication Technology (ICT), Electrical & Electronics Engineering, Mechanical & Automotive Engineering, Business Studies, and Liberal Studies. These programs are designed to provide hands-on training and theoretical knowledge, preparing students for careers in technical fields, entrepreneurship, and further education. KWTVC is committed to fostering a conducive learning environment and contributing to the socio-economic empowerment of its students and the local community.
institutions.short_description: Kajiado West Technical And Vocational College is a public TVET institution in Kajiado County, offering diverse Diploma and Certificate programs in technical, vocational, business, and engineering fields to equip youth with practical skills.

institutions.name: Kakrao Technical & Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Kakrao Technical & Vocational College is a public TVET institution located in the Kakrao area, near Rongo Town, within Rongo Constituency, Migori County, Kenya. Established as part of the Kenyan government's initiative to expand access to technical and vocational training, the college plays a crucial role in equipping the youth with practical skills essential for employment and entrepreneurship. Its strategic location in Migori County allows it to serve students from the surrounding rural and urban areas, contributing to local economic development through skill building.

The college offers a diverse range of programs across various fields, including building technology, electrical and electronic engineering, information communication technology (ICT), business studies, fashion design, hairdressing and cosmetology, and food technology. These courses are designed to meet industry demands and prepare graduates for the job market or to start their own businesses. Kakrao TVC is committed to providing quality technical education, focusing on hands-on training and practical application of knowledge, thereby building a reputation as a key provider of skilled labour in the region.
institutions.short_description: Kakrao Technical & Vocational College is a public TVET institution in Rongo, Migori County, Kenya. It provides a wide array of technical, vocational, and business programs aimed at equipping students with practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Kaloleni Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Kaloleni Technical And Vocational College (TVC) is a public institution located in Kaloleni, Kilifi County, Kenya. Established under the Kenyan government's initiative to expand access to technical and vocational education and training, the college aims to equip learners with practical skills necessary for both formal employment and entrepreneurship. It offers a wide range of courses in various fields, including engineering, business, hospitality, and ICT, designed to meet the growing demand for skilled labor in the region and beyond. The college is registered by the Technical and Vocational Education and Training Authority (TVETA) and accredited by the Kenya National Qualifications Authority (KNQA), ensuring its programs adhere to national standards.

Situated in the coastal region, Kaloleni TVC serves students from Kilifi County and neighboring areas, contributing significantly to human capital development in the region. The institution is committed to providing quality education through competent trainers and relevant practical training facilities. By focusing on hands-on learning and industry-relevant curricula, Kaloleni TVC prepares its graduates to be competent professionals and innovators capable of contributing effectively to the economy and society. Its strategic location makes it an accessible hub for TVET in Kilifi, playing a vital role in the government's plan to vocationalize education and reduce youth unemployment.
institutions.short_description: Kaloleni Technical And Vocational College is a public TVET institution in Kaloleni, Kilifi County, Kenya. It offers diverse technical and vocational courses approved by TVETA, focusing on practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship, serving students in the coastal region and beyond.

institutions.name: Kamukunji Technical Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Kamukunji Technical Vocational College (KTVC) is a public TVC located in Nairobi, Kenya. Established under the Ministry of Education, the college is strategically situated in the Kamukunji constituency, aiming to provide accessible technical and vocational education and training (TVET) to the youth and adults in the surrounding areas and beyond. KTVC plays a crucial role in bridging the skills gap in various sectors of the economy by offering competency-based training programs aligned with industry needs. The college is committed to fostering innovation, entrepreneurship, and practical skills development, preparing graduates for employment and self-employment opportunities.

The institution offers a wide array of programs at different levels, including Artisan, Certificate, and Diploma, across diverse fields such as Business, ICT, Engineering, Fashion Design, and Hospitality. With modern workshops, laboratories, and experienced trainers, KTVC provides a conducive learning environment that emphasizes hands-on experience. The college is recognized for its contribution to national development through the production of skilled manpower. Its reputation is built on a foundation of practical training, industry partnerships, and a focus on producing job-ready graduates who can contribute effectively to the Kenyan economy.
institutions.short_description: Kamukunji Technical Vocational College (KTVC) is a public TVET institution located in Nairobi, Kenya, offering a diverse range of technical and vocational programs at Artisan, Certificate, and Diploma levels to equip students with practical, job-ready skills for various industries.

institutions.name: Kamwenja Teachers Training College
programs: Diploma in Early Childhood Development Education (DECTE)

Kamwenja Teachers Training College is a prominent public educational institution situated along the Nyeri-Nyahururu Road in Nyeri, Nyeri County, Kenya. Established as one of the country's key teacher training colleges, it operates under the purview of the Ministry of Education. With a rich history, Kamwenja TTC has played a vital role in the development of Kenya's primary education sector by consistently producing qualified and competent teaching professionals.

The college is dedicated to offering comprehensive teacher training programs designed to meet the evolving needs of the education system. Currently, its core offerings include the Diploma in Primary Teacher Education (DPTE) and the Diploma in Early Childhood Development Education (DECTE), aligning with the Competency-Based Curriculum (CBC). These programs are designed to equip prospective teachers with the necessary pedagogical skills, subject knowledge, and professional values required to effectively educate young learners across the nation.
institutions.short_description: Kamwenja Teachers Training College is a public institution in Nyeri, Kenya, specializing in teacher education under the Ministry of Education. It offers Diploma programs like DPTE and DECTE to train primary and ECDE teachers.

institutions.name: Kandara Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Kandara Technical And Vocational College (Kandara TVC) is a public institution situated in Kandara Constituency, Murang'a County, Kenya. Established under the Ministry of Education, the college plays a crucial role in the Kenyan government's initiative to expand access to technical and vocational education and training (TVET). Kandara TVC is dedicated to providing quality, demand-driven training that equips students with practical skills and knowledge necessary for employment, entrepreneurship, and lifelong learning in various technical and business fields.

The college offers a diverse range of programs across departments such as Electrical & Electronics Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Building & Civil Engineering, ICT, Business Studies, Hospitality, and Agriculture. These programs are designed to meet the needs of the local and national job markets, fostering innovation and contributing to economic development. Kandara TVC is committed to nurturing skilled manpower and promoting technical excellence among its students, contributing significantly to the TVET sector in Murang'a County and beyond.
institutions.short_description: Kandara Technical And Vocational College is a public TVET institution in Kandara, Murang'a County, Kenya, offering practical skills training in engineering, business, ICT, hospitality, and agriculture to empower students for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Kapchepkor Technical Training Institute
institutions.profile:
Kapchepkor Technical Training Institute is a public TVET institution located in Baringo County, Kenya. It operates under the Ministry of Education and is registered by TVETA. The institute offers a range of technical and vocational programs designed to equip students with practical skills for various industries. As a public institution, it plays a key role in the government's initiative to enhance technical and vocational education and training accessibility across the country.

The institute is situated in the Eldama Ravine area, serving students from the surrounding communities in Baringo and neighboring counties. It focuses on delivering quality hands-on training to prepare graduates for employment or entrepreneurship. While specific historical details are not readily available publicly, its establishment aligns with the national effort to strengthen technical training infrastructure. The institution contributes to meeting the demand for skilled labor in Kenya through its accredited programs.
institutions.short_description: Kapchepkor Technical Training Institute is a public TVET college in Eldama Ravine, Baringo County, offering practical technical and vocational training under the Ministry of Education to equip students with industry-relevant skills.

institutions.name: Kapcherop Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Kapcherop Technical And Vocational College (KTVC) is a public tertiary institution located in Kapcherop Town, Marakwet West Constituency, Elgeyo Marakwet County, Kenya. Established to provide accessible and quality technical and vocational education and training (TVET), KTVC plays a crucial role in equipping local youth and residents with practical skills necessary for employment, entrepreneurship, and national development. The college is registered and regulated by the Technical and Vocational Education and Training Authority (TVETA) in Kenya.

KTVC offers a diverse range of programs across various departments, including Engineering, Building Technology, ICT, Hospitality, Business Studies, and Social Sciences. These courses are designed to meet industry demands and provide students with hands-on experience. The institution aims to foster a conducive learning environment that promotes innovation, critical thinking, and ethical values. By focusing on practical training and competency-based education, Kapcherop TVC contributes significantly to building a skilled workforce in the region and beyond, enhancing livelihood opportunities for its graduates.
