institutions.name: Karatina University
institutions.profile:
Karatina University, located in Nyeri County, Kenya, is a public institution of higher learning that traces its roots back to 2008 when it was established as a constituent college of Moi University. Situated on its main campus in Kagochi, Karatina, the university officially received its charter in 2013, becoming an independent university. It is recognized for its serene environment conducive to learning and research, attracting students from across the country and beyond. The university plays a significant role in expanding access to quality university education in the Central Kenya region.

Karatina University offers a diverse range of academic programs spanning various fields including Agriculture, Education, Pure and Applied Sciences, Social Sciences, and Business. The curriculum is designed to be relevant to national development goals, with a strong emphasis on research, innovation, community engagement, and sustainable development practices, particularly in agriculture, given the region's economic backbone. With additional campuses extending its reach, Karatina University is committed to producing skilled graduates equipped to address contemporary challenges and contribute meaningfully to society, fostering a reputation for academic rigor and practical training.
institutions.short_description: Karatina University is a public university chartered in 2013, located in Karatina, Nyeri County. It offers diverse programs with a focus on research, innovation, and sustainable development.

institutions.name: Karen Technical Training Institute For The Deaf
institutions.profile:
Karen Technical Training Institute For The Deaf (KTTID) is a distinguished public technical training institution situated in the serene suburb of Karen, Nairobi. Established initially in 1990 by the Africa Inland Church (AIC) Kenya and later becoming a government institution in 1992, KTTID holds the unique mandate of providing specialized technical and vocational education and training (TVET) to deaf youth across Kenya. Its establishment was a significant step towards inclusivity in education, ensuring that deaf individuals have access to quality technical skills necessary for their integration into the workforce and society. The institute is located on Langata South Road, providing a conducive learning environment away from the city bustle.

KTTID offers a diverse range of programs tailored to meet the needs and abilities of its deaf learners, delivered through accessible methods like Kenyan Sign Language. Courses span various technical fields including Masonry, Carpentry, Tailoring, Electrical Installation, ICT, Motor Vehicle Mechanics, Hairdressing and Beauty Therapy, and Sign Language Interpretation. The curriculum is designed not only to impart technical skills but also to foster entrepreneurship, communication, and life skills. As a public institution under the Ministry of Education, KTTID plays a crucial role in empowering the deaf community by equipping them with practical trades that enhance their employability and ability to contribute meaningfully to the economy. Its reputation is built on its specialized focus, dedicated staff, and successful graduates who have made strides in various sectors.
institutions.short_description: Karen Technical Training Institute For The Deaf is a unique public college in Karen, Nairobi, dedicated to providing technical and vocational training to young deaf individuals. It offers specialized programs equipping students with practical skills for employment and self-reliance.

other_info.address: Langata South Road, P.O. Box 24805, 00502 Karen, Nairobi

institutions.name: Karumo Technical Training Institute
institutions.profile:
Karumo Technical Training Institute (KTTI) is a public Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) institution located in Muriri, Tigania West Constituency, Meru County, Kenya. Established under the Ministry of Education, KTTI plays a crucial role in the region by providing accessible and market-relevant technical, vocational, and business training programs. Its mandate is to empower the youth and other segments of the population with essential skills and knowledge required for national development and individual prosperity, aligning with Kenya's Vision 2030 goals of creating a globally competitive and prosperous nation.

The institute offers a wide array of courses at certificate and diploma levels across various disciplines including engineering, building technology, business studies, information communication technology (ICT), hospitality, and agriculture. KTTI is committed to delivering quality education through practical-oriented training, modern facilities, and qualified faculty. The programs are designed to meet industry demands, preparing graduates for immediate employment or entrepreneurial ventures. By fostering a conducive learning environment, KTTI aims to nurture skilled professionals and innovators contributing positively to the economy and society.
institutions.short_description: Karumo Technical Training Institute is a public TVET college in Muriri, Tigania West, Meru County, Kenya. It offers diverse technical, vocational, and business programs at certificate and diploma levels, focusing on practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Kasarani Technical And Vocational College
programs: Diploma in Electrical and Electronic Engineering (Power Option)
Craft Certificate in Electrical and Electronic Engineering (Power Option)
institutions.profile:
Kasarani Technical And Vocational College is a prominent public TVET institution situated in the Kasarani area of Nairobi County, Kenya. Established under the Ministry of Education, its primary mission is to contribute to national development by providing accessible and quality technical and vocational training. The college is strategically located to serve the growing population of Nairobi and surrounding regions, offering a conducive environment for learning and skill acquisition in various fields. It plays a crucial role in bridging the skills gap in the Kenyan workforce, aligning its programs with the country's Vision 2030 development goals.

The college offers a wide array of courses at diploma and certificate levels, covering disciplines such as engineering, building technology, business studies, information communication technology (ICT), hospitality, and applied sciences. These programs are designed to be practical and market-oriented, incorporating hands-on training and industry exposure to ensure graduates are well-prepared for the demands of the labor market or for starting their own ventures. Kasarani TVC is recognized by relevant accreditation bodies and is a key player in the government's initiative to enhance technical skills development across the country, fostering innovation and economic growth through its diverse and relevant curriculum.
institutions.short_description: Kasarani Technical And Vocational College (Kasarani TVC) is a public institution under Kenya's Ministry of Education. Located in Nairobi, it offers diverse technical and business programs aimed at equipping students with practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship in various industries.

institutions.name: Kasneb
programs: Certificate in Accounting and Management Skills (CAMS)
institutions.profile:
Kasneb (Kenya Accountants and Secretaries National Examinations Board) is a national examination body in Kenya, established by an Act of Parliament (the KASNEB Act, Cap 286 of the Laws of Kenya) in 1969. Its primary mandate is to develop syllabuses, conduct examinations, and issue certificates to candidates in accountancy, finance, investment, and other related disciplines. Kasneb plays a pivotal role in ensuring the quality and standards of professional qualifications in these fields within Kenya and potentially the East African region. It is overseen by the Ministry of Finance, highlighting its significance in the country's economic and financial sector.

Located in Kasneb Towers, Hospital Road, Upper Hill, Nairobi, Kasneb is situated in a key financial and administrative hub of the city. While Kasneb itself is an examination body and not a teaching institution, its certifications are prerequisites for professional practice in various fields. The institution is highly regarded for its rigorous examination standards, which contribute to the development of competent professionals. Its qualifications, such as CPA, CS, and CIFA, are widely recognized by employers and professional bodies, making them highly sought after by students aiming for careers in accounting, corporate governance, finance, and investment.
institutions.short_description: Kasneb is Kenya's national examination body responsible for setting and administering professional examinations in accountancy, finance, and related fields. It awards certificates and plays a crucial role in developing qualified professionals for the Kenyan economy.

other_info.address: Kasneb Towers, Hospital Road, Upper Hill, P.O Box 41362 – 00100 Nairobi

institutions.name: Katine Technical Training Institute
institutions.profile:
Katine Technical Training Institute (KATTI) is a public TVET institution located in Tala, Machakos County, Kenya. Established under the Ministry of Education, KATTI is dedicated to providing technical and vocational training aimed at contributing to national development goals. The institute offers a wide array of programs designed to impart practical skills and relevant knowledge across various technical and business disciplines. Focusing on competence-based education and training (CBET), KATTI plays a significant role in producing skilled manpower capable of meeting the demands of the job market and fostering entrepreneurship in Kenya.

Situated in Tala, KATTI serves students from Machakos and surrounding regions, ensuring access to quality technical education. The institute is committed to academic excellence, innovation, and the holistic development of its students, providing a conducive environment for learning and skill acquisition. KATTI's curriculum is structured to align with industry needs, ensuring that graduates are well-prepared and competitive. The institution is recognized for its emphasis on hands-on training, qualified faculty, and its positive impact on the local and national economy through the successful careers of its alumni.
institutions.short_description: Katine Technical Training Institute (KATTI) is a public TVET college in Tala, Machakos County, offering diverse technical and vocational programs. It focuses on competence-based training in fields like engineering, business, and ICT to equip students with practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: KCA University
institutions.profile:
KCA University is a prominent private university in Kenya, founded in 1989 by the Institute of Certified Public Accountants of Kenya (ICPAK) as the Kenya College of Accountancy (KCA). Initially established to provide education in accounting and finance, it has since expanded its scope significantly. It was awarded its university charter in 2013, marking its transition to a fully-fledged university and allowing it to offer a wider range of degree programs. The university is recognized for its strong links to professional bodies and its commitment to producing market-ready graduates, particularly in business, information technology, and related fields.

Headquartered on Thika Road in Nairobi, KCA University also operates satellite campuses in Kisumu, Kitale, and Mombasa, extending its reach across Kenya. The university offers programs from certificate to doctoral levels, focusing on practical skills, research, and ethical leadership. Its reputation is particularly strong in accounting and business studies, building on its foundation as a premier accountancy college. The university strives to provide accessible, quality education that addresses the needs of the evolving global economy and contributes positively to society.
institutions.short_description: KCA University is a reputable private university in Nairobi, Kenya, established by ICPAK. It offers a wide range of business, IT, and related programs across multiple campuses, focusing on quality education and professional development for market-ready graduates.

other_info.address: Thika Road, Opposite Safari Park Hotel

institutions.name: Kendege Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Kendege Technical And Vocational College is a public TVET institution located in the Kendege area, Uriri Constituency, Migori County, Kenya. Established in 2018, the college is dedicated to providing quality technical and vocational education and training to Kenyan youth. Situated in a rural setting, it plays a crucial role in expanding access to higher education and practical skills development in the South Nyanza region, contributing significantly to local and national development by producing competent and skilled graduates.

The college offers a diverse range of programs across various fields, including engineering (civil, electrical, automotive), information and communication technology, business studies, agriculture, health sciences, and creative arts like fashion design and cosmetology. These courses are designed to meet the demands of the current job market and equip students with hands-on experience and theoretical knowledge. Kendege TVC aims to empower its students to become innovators, entrepreneurs, and valuable contributors to the economy, fostering a culture of practical learning and skill acquisition that is essential for socio-economic growth.
institutions.short_description: Kendege Technical And Vocational College is a public TVET institution in Migori County, Kenya, established in 2018. It offers diverse technical and vocational programs aimed at empowering youth with practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship, contributing to regional development.

institutions.name: Kengen Geothermal Training Centre
institutions.profile:
Kengen Geothermal Training Centre is a specialized technical institution operated by the Kenya Electricity Generating Company (Kengen). Located within Kengen's extensive geothermal power complex in Oserian, Naivasha, the centre plays a crucial role in developing skilled manpower for the energy sector, particularly in geothermal technology. Established to provide technical training and capacity building, it serves both Kengen employees and external trainees, contributing significantly to the localization of expertise in geothermal power generation, which is a cornerstone of Kenya's energy strategy. Its unique location within an active geothermal field offers trainees unparalleled access to practical learning environments and state-of-the-art power plant facilities.

The centre offers a range of programs tailored to the needs of the energy industry, focusing on areas such as geothermal plant operations, maintenance, and related engineering disciplines like electrical, mechanical, and instrumentation technology. By leveraging Kengen's vast experience and infrastructure, the training centre provides hands-on experience alongside theoretical knowledge, preparing graduates for careers in power generation and other industrial sectors. Its programs are designed to meet industry standards and contribute to enhancing technical skills essential for the sustainable development and operation of energy infrastructure in Kenya and the East African region. As a public institution under the umbrella of Kengen, it is a vital resource for capacity building within the nation's energy sector.
institutions.short_description: Kengen Geothermal Training Centre in Naivasha is a specialized institution operated by Kengen, offering technical training in geothermal technology and power plant operations. Located within Kengen's Oserian complex, it provides hands-on experience for careers in Kenya's energy sector.

institutions.name: Kenya Assemblies of God University
institutions.profile:
Kenya Assemblies of God University (KAG-U), formerly known as East Africa School of Theology (EAST), is a private Christian university located in Elementeita, Nakuru County, Kenya. Established to provide theological education and other academic disciplines, the institution has grown from its origins as a Bible school into a fully-fledged university. It is affiliated with the Kenya Assemblies of God Church and aims to integrate Christian faith and values with academic excellence across its programs.

Situated along the Nakuru-Nairobi Highway, KAG-U provides a serene and conducive environment for learning. The university offers a range of programs across various faculties including Theology, Education, Business, and Arts and Social Sciences. It attracts students from Kenya and other parts of East Africa, fostering a diverse learning community. The university is recognized by the Commission for University Education (CUE) in Kenya and participates in the KUCCPS placement system, admitting government-sponsored students alongside self-sponsored ones. Its curriculum is designed to equip students with both academic knowledge and practical skills grounded in a biblical worldview, preparing them for leadership and service in various sectors.
institutions.short_description: Kenya Assemblies of God University (KAG-U) is a private Christian university in Elementeita, Nakuru County, offering programs in theology, education, business, and arts, integrating faith with academic pursuits.

other_info.address: Along Nakuru – Nairobi Highway, Next to CADAC, Elementeita

institutions.name: Kenya Coast Polytechnic
institutions.profile:
Kenya Coast Polytechnic (KCP) is a public technical and vocational education and training (TVET) institution located in Mombasa, Kenya. Established under the Technical and Vocational Education and Training Act, 2013, KCP plays a crucial role in providing skilled manpower for the coastal region and the nation. It offers a wide range of certificate, diploma, and higher diploma programs across various disciplines, including engineering, applied sciences, business, hospitality, and information technology, catering to diverse student aspirations and industry needs.

Situated along Kurasini Road in Mombasa, Kenya Coast Polytechnic is strategically positioned to serve students from the coastal counties and beyond. The institution is recognized for its commitment to practical, hands-on training and its efforts to align its curriculum with current market demands. It boasts modern workshops, laboratories, and lecture halls designed to facilitate effective learning and skill development, contributing significantly to the national agenda of developing a competent workforce for socio-economic growth. KCP is a significant institution in the region, known for producing graduates ready for the job market.
institutions.short_description: Kenya Coast Polytechnic (KCP) is a leading public TVET institution in Mombasa, Kenya, offering diverse technical and vocational programs aimed at producing skilled and competent graduates for various industries and national development.

other_info.address: Along Kurasini Road, Off Moi Avenue, Mombasa

institutions.name: Kenya Forestry College
institutions.parent_ministry: Ministry of Environment, Climate Change and Forestry
institutions.profile:
The Kenya Forestry College (KFC) is a premier public institution dedicated to forestry training in Kenya. Established in 1947 as the Forest Training School, it was upgraded to a college in 2002. It serves as a key training arm of the Kenya Forest Service (KFS) and is strategically located in Londiani, Kericho County. This region, known for its significant forest cover and natural beauty, provides an ideal living laboratory and environment for hands-on forestry education and practical experience for students.

KFC offers a comprehensive range of programs designed to equip students with the necessary skills and knowledge for sustainable forest management, conservation, and utilization. These include accredited Diploma and Certificate courses in Forestry, as well as various specialized short courses tailored to specific industry needs and emerging trends in environmental conservation. With several decades of experience in forestry education, the college has built a strong national reputation for producing competent, well-trained forestry professionals who play a crucial role in the management, protection, and development of Kenya's vital forest resources and ecosystems.
institutions.short_description: Kenya Forestry College (KFC) in Londiani, Kericho, is a public institution established in 1947. As the training arm of Kenya Forest Service, it offers Diploma, Certificate, and short courses in forestry, producing skilled professionals for sustainable forest management and conservation.

institutions.name: Kenya Highlands Evangelical University
programs: Bachelor of Science in Computer Science
institutions.profile:
Kenya Highlands Evangelical University (KHEU) is a private Christian university situated in Kericho, Kenya. Established in 1932 by the Africa Inland Church (AIC) as a modest Bible school, its roots trace back to a strong tradition of theological education. Over the decades, the institution has grown and evolved significantly, transitioning from Kenya Highlands Bible College to a college offering diplomas and degrees, and ultimately receiving its university charter from the Kenyan government in 2011. KHEU is dedicated to providing quality higher education rooted in Christian principles, aiming to produce individuals who are not only academically competent but also morally upright and capable of leading transformative change within the church and wider society. Its academic offerings have expanded over time to include diverse fields such as education, business, social sciences, and computing, alongside its foundational theological programs.

The university's campus is located in the scenic surroundings of Kericho town, offering a peaceful and conducive atmosphere for learning, spiritual development, and research. KHEU maintains a close relationship with the Africa Inland Church and other evangelical denominations, which deeply influences its curriculum, campus life, and student body composition. As an institution recognized and accredited by the Commission for University Education (CUE) in Kenya, KHEU adheres to national standards of quality education. It has built a reputation for its commitment to holistic student development, emphasizing spiritual formation alongside academic rigor. Graduates of KHEU are known for their strong ethical grounding and are active contributors in various sectors, including church leadership, educational institutions, non-governmental organizations, and the business world. The university continues to develop its infrastructure and program portfolio to meet the evolving educational needs and challenges of the region.
institutions.short_description: Kenya Highlands Evangelical University (KHEU) is a private Christian university in Kericho, Kenya, founded in 1932 by the Africa Inland Church. It offers accredited programs in theology, education, business, and more, focusing on integrating faith and learning to produce transformative leaders for society.

institutions.name: Kenya Industrial Training Institute
institutions.parent_ministry: Ministry of Industrialization, Trade and Enterprise Development
institutions.profile:
The Kenya Industrial Training Institute (KITI) is a prominent public Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) institution located in Nakuru, Kenya. Established in 1965 through a collaboration between the Governments of Kenya and Japan, KITI has a long history of contributing to the development of skilled industrial manpower in the country. Situated conveniently off the Nakuru-Nairobi Highway, the institute provides practical, hands-on training in a wide range of technical disciplines.

KITI operates under the Ministry of Industrialization, Trade and Enterprise Development and is recognized for its role in equipping Kenyan youth with relevant skills for the job market and entrepreneurship. Its programs span areas such as Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Building Technology, Automotive Engineering, Business Studies, and Information and Communication Technology. The institute is committed to fostering innovation and quality in technical education, aligning its curriculum with industry needs to produce competent graduates ready to contribute to Kenya's industrial growth.
institutions.short_description: Kenya Industrial Training Institute (KITI) is a public TVET college in Nakuru, Kenya, established in 1965. It provides practical training in various technical and industrial fields like engineering, building, and ICT, aiming to produce skilled personnel for the industrial sector.

other_info.location: Off Nakuru-Nairobi Highway, Opposite Nakuru Showground, Nakuru

institutions.name: Kenya Institute Of Highways And Building Technology
institutions.parent_ministry: Ministry of Transport, Infrastructure, Housing, Urban Development, and Public Works
institutions.profile:
The Kenya Institute of Highways and Building Technology (KIHBT) is a leading public institution established under Legal Notice No. 181 of 1986, operating under the Ministry of Transport, Infrastructure, Housing, Urban Development and Public Works. Its primary mandate is to provide specialized training, research, and consultancy services in the fields of highways, building technology, and related engineering disciplines. Located strategically in Nairobi's Industrial Area with regional centres, KIHBT plays a crucial role in capacity building for the infrastructure sector in Kenya and the East African region.

KIHBT offers a wide range of programs from artisan to diploma levels, targeting both entry-level trainees and experienced professionals seeking to upgrade their skills. The institution is recognized for its practical-oriented approach, equipping graduates with hands-on expertise essential for designing, constructing, and maintaining critical infrastructure projects. With state-of-the-art workshops, laboratories, and experienced faculty, KIHBT has built a strong reputation for producing competent technicians and technologists who contribute significantly to the development of roads, bridges, buildings, and other vital structures across the nation.
institutions.short_description: The Kenya Institute of Highways and Building Technology (KIHBT) is a public institution under the Ministry of Transport, specializing in training, research, and consultancy for the highways, building, and infrastructure sectors in Kenya and the region.

other_info.address: Commercial Street, Industrial Area, P.O Box 57271-00200

institutions.name: Kenya Institute Of Mass Communication
institutions.parent_ministry: Ministry of Information, Communications and The Digital Economy
institutions.profile:
The Kenya Institute of Mass Communication (KIMC) is a leading public media training institution located in Nairobi, Kenya. Established in 1961 as a training school for the Kenya Broadcasting Corporation (KBC), it was later elevated to the status of a State Corporation in 2001 through Legal Notice No.123 of 2001. KIMC plays a crucial role in shaping the media landscape in Kenya and East Africa by providing comprehensive training in various fields of communication and media technology.

KIMC offers a wide range of programs including certificates, diplomas, and degrees in disciplines such as Journalism, Film and Television Production, Broadcast Engineering, Radio Production, Print Technology, and Mass Communication. Situated in South B, Nairobi, the institution is known for its practical-oriented approach to training, equipped with modern studios, labs, and production facilities that simulate real-world media environments. It attracts students from across the region and has produced many notable media professionals who have significantly contributed to the growth of the media industry.
institutions.short_description: Kenya Institute of Mass Communication (KIMC) is a premier public media training institution in Kenya, offering diverse programs in journalism, film, broadcast engineering, and related fields, known for practical training and producing skilled media professionals.

other_info.address: Ufangamano Drive, Off Mombasa Road, South B, Nairobi, Kenya

institutions.name: Kenya Institute Of Surveying And Mapping
institutions.parent_ministry: Ministry of Lands and Physical Planning
programs: Diploma in Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing
Short Courses in GIS, Remote Sensing, GPS, etc.
institutions.profile:
The Kenya Institute of Surveying and Mapping (KISM) is a premier public institution in Kenya dedicated to providing specialized training in the fields of surveying, mapping, geoinformation management, and related disciplines. Established to cater to the growing demand for skilled professionals in these technical areas, KISM plays a crucial role in supporting land administration, infrastructure development, and environmental management within the country. Located along Thika Road in the Ruaraka area of Nairobi, the institute operates under the Ministry of Lands and Physical Planning, ensuring its curriculum and training standards align with national development priorities and industry needs.

KISM offers a range of programs from certificate to diploma levels, focusing on practical skills and theoretical knowledge essential for modern surveying and mapping practices. Its courses cover diverse areas such as land surveying, cartography, photogrammetry, remote sensing, and geographical information systems (GIS). The institute is recognized for its hands-on training approach and state-of-the-art equipment, preparing graduates for careers in both the public and private sectors. Over the years, KISM has built a reputation as a center of excellence, contributing significantly to the professional workforce in land surveying and geomatics in Kenya and the East African region.
institutions.short_description: The Kenya Institute of Surveying and Mapping (KISM) is a public college under the Ministry of Lands, specializing in training professionals in surveying, mapping, GIS, and related geomatics fields.

institutions.name: Kenya Medical Training College - Awendo Campus
institutions.profile:
Kenya Medical Training College (KMTC) Awendo Campus is one of the constituent campuses of the national KMTC institution, a prominent public college providing healthcare training in Kenya. Established as part of KMTC's expansion strategy to decentralize medical education, the Awendo campus serves the needs of Migori County and the surrounding regions. It offers a range of medical and health-related diploma and certificate programs, contributing significantly to the development of a skilled healthcare workforce in the area. Being part of the larger KMTC network, the Awendo campus benefits from standardized curriculum, resources, and national recognition.

Located strategically in Awendo town, Migori County, the campus plays a vital role in increasing access to medical training for students from the western and Nyanza regions of Kenya. The college is known for its practical-oriented training and partnership with local healthcare facilities for clinical experience, adhering to the high standards set by the national KMTC body. Its graduates are equipped with the necessary skills to work in various healthcare settings, reinforcing the healthcare system at the county and national levels. KMTC's reputation as a leading provider of middle-level medical training in Kenya extends to its Awendo campus, making it a respected institution for healthcare education in the area.
institutions.short_description: KMTC Awendo Campus is a public medical training college located in Awendo, Migori County. As a campus of the national Kenya Medical Training College, it offers various diploma and certificate programs in medical and health sciences.

other_info.address: Along Awendo - Ranen Road, next to Awendo Health Centre

institutions.name: Kenya Medical Training College - Bomet Campus
programs: Diploma in Kenya Registered Community Health Nursing
Diploma in Clinical Medicine and Surgery
Diploma in Health Records and Information Technology
institutions.profile:
The Kenya Medical Training College (KMTC) Bomet Campus is one of the regional branches of the esteemed national institution dedicated to training healthcare professionals in Kenya. Established to extend specialized medical training opportunities closer to communities, the Bomet campus serves the South Rift region and surrounding areas. Like other KMTC campuses, it operates under the Ministry of Health and adheres to the high standards set by the KMTC national board, ensuring quality education and practical training in various medical disciplines. The campus plays a crucial role in addressing the healthcare worker shortage in the region and providing accessible training for local students.

KMTC Bomet Campus offers a range of certificate and diploma programs accredited by relevant professional bodies in the medical field. These programs include popular courses such as Nursing, Clinical Medicine, Medical Laboratory Sciences, Nutrition and Dietetics, and Health Records and Information Technology. The curriculum combines theoretical knowledge with extensive practical exposure through skills laboratories and clinical attachments in local healthcare facilities, preparing graduates for immediate integration into the healthcare workforce. The campus is strategically located in Bomet Town, making it a vital educational hub for medical training in Bomet County.
institutions.short_description: KMTC Bomet Campus is a public medical training college in Bomet County, Kenya. It offers various certificate and diploma programs in healthcare disciplines under the Ministry of Health, serving the South Rift region.

institutions.name: Kenya Medical Training College - Bondo Campus
programs: Diploma in Clinical Medicine and Surgery
institutions.profile:
KMTC Bondo Campus is one of the constituent campuses of the Kenya Medical Training College, a prominent public institution for health professional training in Kenya. Located in Bondo Town, Siaya County, the campus was established to decentralize medical training and make it accessible to students from the Nyanza region and beyond. Initially operating from temporary premises, the campus moved to its permanent location adjacent to the Bondo Sub-County Hospital in 2008, benefiting from proximity to a clinical training environment.

The campus offers diploma programs in key health disciplines such as Nursing and Clinical Medicine and Surgery. These programs are designed to equip students with the necessary theoretical knowledge and practical skills required for middle-level healthcare provision. As part of the national KMTC network, the Bondo campus adheres to the college's high standards of training and contributes significantly to producing qualified healthcare workers who serve in various health facilities across the country.
institutions.short_description: Kenya Medical Training College (KMTC) Bondo Campus is a public health training institution located in Bondo, Siaya County. It offers accredited diploma programs in nursing and clinical medicine, contributing to the training of skilled healthcare professionals for Kenya.

institutions.name: Kenya Medical Training College - Bungoma Campus
institutions.profile:
The Kenya Medical Training College (KMTC) Bungoma Campus is a vital institution within the national KMTC network, established to provide quality medical training in the Western region of Kenya. As part of a state corporation under the Ministry of Health, KMTC Bungoma adheres to national standards for health training, contributing significantly to the healthcare workforce in Bungoma County and beyond. It offers a range of programs designed to equip students with practical skills and theoretical knowledge necessary for various healthcare roles, helping to address the demand for qualified health professionals in the country.

Located in Bungoma town, the campus provides a conducive learning environment for students. KMTC campuses are known for their rigorous practical training components, often leveraging partnerships with local healthcare facilities for student attachments. The institution's reputation is built on the quality of its graduates who serve in public and private healthcare sectors across Kenya, making KMTC Bungoma a respected center for medical education in the region.
institutions.short_description: Kenya Medical Training College (KMTC) Bungoma Campus is a public medical training institution located in Bungoma County, part of the national KMTC system dedicated to training healthcare professionals.

institutions.name: Kenya Medical Training College - Burnt Forest Campus
institutions.profile:
Kenya Medical Training College (KMTC) is a state corporation under the Ministry of Health in Kenya, established to train various health professionals. The Burnt Forest Campus is one of its decentralized branches, strategically located in Burnt Forest town, Uasin Gishu County, to enhance access to medical training in the Rift Valley region and surrounding areas. The campus upholds the KMTC mandate of producing competent and qualified healthcare workers to meet the country's health needs.

The Burnt Forest campus provides training for several health science disciplines, though specific programs may vary depending on the campus facilities and current demand. Like other KMTC campuses, it follows a standardized curriculum approved by relevant professional bodies, ensuring quality education and practical skills development. The institution contributes significantly to local employment and the healthcare workforce, playing a vital role in community health services through its graduates. Its public reputation is tied to the larger KMTC brand, known for providing accessible and relevant medical training in Kenya.
institutions.short_description: Kenya Medical Training College (KMTC) Burnt Forest Campus is a public medical college in Uasin Gishu County, offering various diploma and certificate programs in health sciences to train competent healthcare professionals for Kenya.

institutions.name: Kenya Medical Training College - Busia Campus
programs: Diploma in Clinical Medicine and Surgery
institutions.profile:
Kenya Medical Training College (KMTC) Busia Campus is a key public health training institution situated in Busia Town, Busia County, Kenya. As a constituent campus of the national Kenya Medical Training College, it operates under the Ministry of Health and plays a vital role in expanding access to quality medical education in the western region. The campus is dedicated to training competent and skilled healthcare professionals who are prepared to meet the evolving health needs of the population. It is an integral part of KMTC's broader mission to contribute significantly to the nation's healthcare workforce capacity.

The Busia campus offers various medical programs primarily at the Diploma and Certificate levels across several health disciplines. These programs are designed to equip students with the necessary theoretical knowledge and practical skills through structured classroom learning and clinical rotations. The campus provides an environment conducive to learning, preparing graduates for impactful careers in diverse healthcare settings, including public and private hospitals, clinics, and community health programs. Its location in Busia Town makes it an accessible training center for students from Busia and neighboring counties, reinforcing its importance in regional healthcare development.
institutions.short_description: KMTC Busia Campus is a public medical training college located in Busia Town, offering Diploma and Certificate programs in various health sciences. It trains healthcare professionals to serve communities in Busia and the wider Kenyan population.

institutions.name: Kenya Medical Training College - Butula Campus
institutions.profile:
The Kenya Medical Training College (KMTC) is a state corporation under the Ministry of Health in Kenya, renowned for training middle-level human resources for health since its inception in 1927. With numerous campuses spread across the country, KMTC plays a crucial role in developing competent healthcare professionals to serve both public and private health sectors. The Butula Campus is one of its regional centers, strategically located in Busia County to expand access to quality medical training in the western region of Kenya and contribute to strengthening the local healthcare system.

Kenya Medical Training College (KMTC) is a premier government-owned medical training institution in Kenya, established in 1927. Operating under the Ministry of Health, KMTC is dedicated to training a competent and motivated health workforce to contribute towards the provision of quality healthcare services in the country. With its headquarters in Nairobi, KMTC has expanded its reach across Kenya through numerous campuses, strategically located to enhance access to medical education and training, including the Rachuonyo Campus in Homa Bay County.

The Rachuonyo Campus, situated near Oyugis town in Rachuonyo South Constituency, plays a vital role in decentralizing medical training and serving the educational needs of students in the Nyanza region and beyond. The campus offers various certificate and diploma programs in critical medical fields such as Nursing (Community Health) and Medical Laboratory Sciences. By providing quality training and practical experience, KMTC Rachuonyo equips graduates with the necessary skills to address healthcare challenges at the community level and contribute meaningfully to Kenya's health sector, maintaining the high standards associated with the KMTC brand.
institutions.short_description: Kenya Medical Training College (KMTC) Rachuonyo Campus is a public medical training institution located near Oyugis town in Homa Bay County. It offers accredited certificate and diploma programs in vital healthcare fields, contributing to Kenya's health workforce development.

institutions.name: Kenya Medical Training College - Rera Campus
programs: Diploma in Clinical Medicine and Surgery
institutions.profile:
The Kenya Medical Training College (KMTC) is a state corporation under the Ministry of Health, mandated to train health professionals in Kenya. Established in 1927, KMTC has grown into a vital institution with numerous campuses across the country, playing a crucial role in developing the healthcare workforce. KMTC Rera Campus is one of these strategic branches, located in Rera, Siaya County, aimed at decentralizing medical training and making it accessible to students from the Lake Victoria region and beyond. The campus operates under the strict quality standards and curriculum set by the main KMTC body and relevant regulatory bodies, ensuring graduates are well-prepared to serve in various healthcare settings.

KMTC Rera Campus offers various certificate and diploma programs in health sciences, tailored to meet the healthcare needs of the community and the nation. Typical programs offered at KMTC campuses include disciplines such as Nursing, Clinical Medicine, and Medical Laboratory Sciences, among others. By providing training in these essential fields, the campus contributes significantly to bridging the gap in healthcare professional shortages, particularly in rural and underserved areas. As part of the larger KMTC network, the Rera campus upholds a reputation for producing competent and dedicated healthcare workers who are instrumental in improving health outcomes across Kenya.
institutions.short_description: Kenya Medical Training College (KMTC) - Rera Campus is a public health training institution located in Rera, Siaya County, offering certificate and diploma programs to train qualified healthcare professionals for service in Kenya.

institutions.name: Kenya Medical Training College - Shianda - Mumias Campus
institutions.profile:
Kenya Medical Training College (KMTC) - Shianda Campus is one of the constituent campuses of the national Kenya Medical Training College, a premier institution for health training in Kenya. Located in Shianda, Mumias East Constituency, Kakamega County, the campus plays a crucial role in expanding access to quality medical education in the Western region of Kenya. Established as part of KMTC's decentralization strategy, the Shianda campus aims to train healthcare professionals closer to their home communities, addressing the healthcare worker shortage and improving service delivery in rural and underserved areas.

The campus offers various medical courses at diploma and certificate levels, aligned with the curriculum set by KMTC headquarters and relevant regulatory bodies. It provides a conducive learning environment with necessary facilities to support theoretical and practical training for aspiring nurses, clinical officers, public health officers, and other essential healthcare cadres. KMTC Shianda is recognized for contributing skilled health workers to both public and private healthcare sectors, upholding the college's reputation for producing competent and ethical professionals ready to serve the health needs of Kenyans.
institutions.short_description: KMTC Shianda - Mumias Campus is a public medical training college located in Shianda, Kakamega County. It is part of the national KMTC institution, offering diploma and certificate courses in various medical fields to train healthcare professionals for the Western region.

institutions.name: Kenya Medical Training College - Siaya Campus
institutions.profile:
The Kenya Medical Training College (KMTC) Siaya Campus is one of the regional branches of the national KMTC institution, established under the Ministry of Health in Kenya. KMTC is the leading institution in Kenya for training middle-level human resources for health. The establishment of campuses like Siaya across the country is part of KMTC's strategic plan to decentralize medical training, making it accessible to a wider population and ensuring equitable distribution of healthcare professionals throughout Kenya. KMTC Siaya plays a vital role in contributing to the healthcare workforce in Siaya County and the surrounding Lake Region, producing graduates who serve in various health facilities.

Located strategically in Siaya Town, near the Siaya County Referral Hospital, the campus benefits from proximity to a major healthcare facility, providing students with valuable opportunities for practical training and clinical experience. While the specific programs offered can vary or change, KMTC campuses typically offer certificate and diploma courses in high-demand health disciplines such as Nursing, Clinical Medicine, Medical Laboratory Technology, Community Health, and others. The campus is equipped with necessary infrastructure and qualified faculty to deliver quality training aligned with national health sector needs and standards set by relevant professional bodies. KMTC Siaya is recognized for its contribution to health education and its graduates are integral to the public and private healthcare systems in Kenya.
institutions.short_description: Kenya Medical Training College (KMTC) Siaya Campus is a public institution under the Ministry of Health, dedicated to training competent healthcare professionals. Located in Siaya Town, it offers various medical courses crucial for strengthening Kenya's health sector.

other_info.location: Near Siaya County Referral Hospital, Siaya Town

institutions.name: Kenya Medical Training College - Sigowet Campus
institutions.profile:
Kenya Medical Training College (KMTC) is a state corporation under the Ministry of Health, mandated to train diverse health professionals. The Sigowet Campus is one of the many campuses established across Kenya to decentralize medical training and increase access to quality healthcare education in various regions. Located in Sigowet, Sigowet-Soin Constituency within Kericho County, the campus plays a crucial role in equipping local youth and others from the surrounding areas with the skills needed to serve in the healthcare sector.

The campus offers various health science programs, aligning with KMTC's national curriculum and standards. These programs are designed to meet the current and future healthcare needs of the country, producing competent and ethical healthcare professionals. The establishment of the Sigowet campus reflects the government's commitment to strengthening the healthcare system by ensuring a steady supply of well-trained personnel across all counties, including rural and semi-urban areas like Sigowet.

institutions.name: Kenya Medical Training College - Tana River Campus
institutions.profile:
The Kenya Medical Training College (KMTC) is a vital state corporation under the Ministry of Health in Kenya, mandated to train various disciplines in the health sector to serve hospitals and health facilities in Kenya. With numerous campuses spread across the country, KMTC plays a crucial role in producing competent healthcare professionals and enhancing access to medical training opportunities for Kenyans, especially in underserved regions. The establishment of campuses like the one in Tana River is part of KMTC's strategic vision to decentralize training and address regional healthcare human resource needs.

The KMTC Tana River Campus is strategically located in Hola, Tana River County, bringing essential medical training closer to the communities in this region. It primarily focuses on programs that directly impact local healthcare delivery. Currently, the campus is known for offering a Certificate in Community Health Nursing, a program designed to equip students with the skills necessary to provide essential nursing care and promote health within communities. The campus serves as a critical institution for residents of Tana River and surrounding areas, contributing significantly to improving healthcare services and fostering local development through education.
institutions.short_description: Kenya Medical Training College (KMTC) Tana River Campus is a public medical training institution located in Hola, Tana River County. Part of the national KMTC network, it offers healthcare programs like Community Health Nursing to train professionals for local health facilities and serve the regional community's needs.

institutions.name: Kenya Medical Training College - Taveta Campus
institutions.profile:
The Kenya Medical Training College (KMTC) - Taveta Campus is a vital constituent college of the national KMTC institution, strategically located in Taveta Town, Taita Taveta County. Established to decentralize medical training and bring healthcare education closer to the communities it serves, the Taveta campus plays a crucial role in addressing the healthcare professional deficit in the region and across Kenya. As a public institution under the Ministry of Health, KMTC Taveta upholds the high standards and curriculum set by the main KMTC body, ensuring quality training for its students.

KMTC Taveta Campus offers various diploma and certificate programs in medical sciences, equipping students with the necessary skills and knowledge to serve in diverse healthcare settings. Common programs available include Community Health Nursing, Clinical Medicine, Medical Laboratory Sciences, Health Records and Information Technology, and Nutrition & Dietetics, among others. The campus contributes significantly to the local economy and community by training healthcare workers who often go on to serve within Taita Taveta and neighbouring counties, thereby improving access to quality healthcare in underserved areas. Its location in Taveta town makes it accessible to students from the coastal region and the interior, solidifying its reputation as a key medical training hub in Southern Kenya.
institutions.short_description: Kenya Medical Training College - Taveta Campus is a public medical college in Taveta Town, Taita Taveta County. Part of the national KMTC network, it provides essential healthcare training programs to address regional and national health professional needs.

other_info.address: Off Taveta-Namanga Rd, Near Taveta Sub-County Hospital

institutions.profile:
Kenya Medical Training College (KMTC) is a state corporation under the Ministry of Health, mandated to train healthcare professionals in Kenya. Established in 1927, KMTC has grown into a major provider of health education with numerous campuses spread across the country. The establishment of campuses like KMTC Teso aims to decentralize training opportunities and bring medical education closer to various communities, addressing regional healthcare needs and promoting equitable access to training. The Teso campus is part of this broader initiative to increase the output of qualified health personnel in diverse fields, contributing significantly to the national healthcare workforce.

Located in Amagoro, Teso North Constituency, Busia County, KMTC Teso Campus serves students primarily from the western parts of Kenya and beyond. The campus offers a range of medical training programs at certificate and diploma levels, covering disciplines essential for the healthcare sector. The campus adheres to the established curriculum and rigorous standards of the national KMTC institution, playing a crucial role in equipping students with the skills required to serve in hospitals and clinics, thereby supporting healthcare delivery in the region and the country.

institutions.name: Kenya Medical Training College - Thika Campus

Kenya Medical Training College (KMTC) is a state corporation under the Ministry of Health, mandated to train various health professionals at certificate, diploma, and higher diploma levels. Established in 1927, KMTC has grown significantly and now operates numerous campuses across Kenya, including the Thika Campus. The college's mission is to produce competent health professionals who contribute effectively to the health sector and national development. KMTC is highly regarded for its quality of training and its vital role in addressing healthcare workforce needs in Kenya.

KMTC Thika Campus, situated off Thika-Garissa Road next to Thika Level 5 Hospital, is a key training centre in Kiambu County. It offers a range of medical programs designed to equip students with practical skills and theoretical knowledge required in the healthcare field. The campus benefits from its proximity to a major hospital, providing students with valuable clinical experience opportunities. It is committed to maintaining high academic standards and contributing skilled health professionals to serve communities locally and nationally.

institutions.name: Kenya Medical Training College - Trans Mara Campus

Kenya Medical Training College (KMTC) Trans Mara Campus is one of the constituent colleges of the national KMTC institution, which is a state corporation under the Ministry of Health in Kenya. Established to train middle-level medical personnel, KMTC plays a crucial role in the country's healthcare system. The Trans Mara Campus, located in Kilgoris town, serves the vast Trans Mara region and surrounding areas, contributing significantly to the availability of skilled healthcare professionals in Narok County and beyond. The campus aims to provide quality training that meets national and international standards, equipping students with the necessary knowledge and skills to address the health challenges facing communities.

KMTC Trans Mara Campus offers a range of programs in various medical fields, focusing primarily on nursing and medical laboratory sciences. These programs are designed to produce competent graduates capable of working in diverse healthcare settings, including hospitals, clinics, and community health centers. The campus is committed to fostering an environment conducive to learning and professional growth, utilizing modern teaching methods and practical training opportunities. By extending its reach to areas like Trans Mara, KMTC continues its mission of decentralizing medical training and making it accessible to students from all parts of Kenya, thereby improving healthcare service delivery across the nation.
institutions.short_description: Kenya Medical Training College (KMTC) Trans Mara Campus, located in Kilgoris, Narok County, is a public institution training medical professionals, primarily in nursing and laboratory sciences, to serve local healthcare needs.

It plays a crucial role in producing competent and skilled healthcare workers for both public and private sectors, contributing significantly to the nation's health service delivery.

Kenya Medical Training College (KMTC) Ugunja Campus is one of the decentralized training institutions under the national Kenya Medical Training College umbrella, strategically located in Ugunja, Siaya County. It serves the healthcare needs of Siaya County and the larger Nyanza region by producing competent mid-level healthcare professionals. The campus is situated in Ugunja town, contributing significantly to the local economy and providing essential educational opportunities for residents.

The campus focuses on training healthcare professionals critical for grassroots service delivery. Currently, it offers programs such as the Certificate in Community Health Nursing and the Diploma in Community Health Nursing. These programs are designed to equip students with the practical skills and theoretical knowledge required to provide essential nursing care in diverse community settings. By training nurses locally, KMTC Ugunja helps to bridge the gap in healthcare workforce distribution and improve health outcomes in rural and underserved areas, upholding the reputation of KMTC as a leading provider of medical training in Kenya.

Situated in Vihiga Town, Vihiga County, the campus plays a crucial role in extending quality medical education to students in the Western region, contributing significantly to the local and national healthcare system by producing skilled and competent health workers.

Established as part of KMTC's strategic plan to decentralize training opportunities and increase accessibility, the Vihiga Campus adheres to the high standards set by the parent institution. The campus is equipped with necessary facilities for theoretical learning and practical training, providing students with the foundational knowledge and hands-on skills required for their future careers in clinics, hospitals, and public health settings across Kenya. Its establishment in Vihiga underscores KMTC's commitment to developing human resources for health across the nation.

The Kenya Medical Training College (KMTC) is a prominent public institution in Kenya renowned for training highly skilled healthcare professionals. Established in 1927, KMTC has expanded its reach across the country through numerous campuses, including the Webuye Campus located in Bungoma County. The Webuye Campus serves the Western region of Kenya, providing accessible medical education and contributing significantly to the local and national healthcare workforce. Like other KMTC campuses, it operates under the Ministry of Health, adhering to national standards for medical training.

KMTC Webuye Campus offers a range of programs in various health disciplines, preparing students for careers in critical healthcare roles. These programs are designed to equip graduates with the practical skills and theoretical knowledge necessary to work in hospitals, clinics, and community health settings. The campus benefits from the overall strong reputation of KMTC as a key producer of mid-level healthcare cadres in Kenya. institutions.short_description: Kenya Medical Training College (KMTC) Webuye Campus is a public medical training institution located in Webuye, Bungoma County, Kenya. It is part of the national KMTC network, dedicated to training competent health professionals for the Kenyan healthcare sector.
