Kenya Medical Training College (KMTC) - Yatta Campus is a constituent college of the national Kenya Medical Training College, a body corporate established by an Act of Parliament (Cap 261) of the Laws of Kenya. As a public institution under the Ministry of Health, KMTC is mandated to train qualified health professionals for both public and private sectors in Kenya. The Yatta Campus, located in Kithimani, Yatta Constituency, Machakos County, is one of the numerous campuses strategically spread across the country to enhance accessibility to medical training opportunities and address the healthcare human resource needs in diverse regions.

The campus offers a variety of medical and health-related certificate and diploma courses approved by the relevant regulatory bodies. It aims to provide quality education and practical skills necessary for students to excel in their chosen fields. Like other KMTC campuses, Yatta maintains the high standards set by the parent institution and contributes significantly to the healthcare workforce in Machakos County and the surrounding areas. Its presence in the region is vital for training local talent and supporting healthcare delivery systems.
institutions.short_description: Kenya Medical Training College (KMTC) - Yatta Campus is a public institution located in Kithimani, Machakos County, offering accredited medical and health-related certificate and diploma programs under the Ministry of Health.

institutions.name: Kenya Methodist University
institutions.profile:
Kenya Methodist University (KeMU) is a private Christian university situated primarily in Meru, Kenya. Established by the Methodist Church in Kenya, KeMU received its charter in 2006, becoming a fully-fledged university after operating as a constituent college since 1997. Its main campus is located along the Meru-Nairobi Highway, south of Meru town. KeMU is recognized for its commitment to providing quality education grounded in Christian principles, aiming to produce graduates who are not only academically proficient but also ethically sound and service-oriented. The university operates several faculties and schools, offering a wide range of programs from certificate to doctoral levels across disciplines like Business, Education, Health Sciences, Pure and Applied Sciences, and Social Sciences.

KeMU has played a significant role in expanding access to higher education in the Eastern region of Kenya and beyond. While its main campus in Meru remains its largest and most established, the university has historically maintained a presence in other urban centers such as Nairobi and Mombasa, extending its reach and programs to a broader population. The university emphasizes research, innovation, and community engagement as core components of its mission. Over the years, KeMU has built a reputation as a reputable institution known for its structured programs, focus on holistic development, and contribution to national development through trained professionals and research output, attracting students from various parts of Kenya and East Africa.
institutions.short_description: A leading private Christian university in Meru, Kenya, Kenya Methodist University offers a wide array of programs integrating faith and learning, focusing on ethical leadership, innovation, and community service for holistic student development and national contribution.

institutions.name: Kenya School Of Agriculture
institutions.parent_ministry: Ministry of Agriculture, Livestock, Fisheries and Co-operatives
programs: Diploma in Agricultural Education and Extension
Diploma in Agricultural Economics and Resource Management
Certificate in Agricultural Education and Extension
Certificate in Agricultural Economics and Resource Management
institutions.profile:
The Kenya School of Agriculture (KSA) is a prominent public agricultural training institution located in Wote, Makueni County, Kenya. Established in 1949, it stands as one of the oldest institutions dedicated to agricultural training in the country, playing a significant role in developing human resources for the agricultural sector. KSA is known for its practical-oriented training approach, focusing on producing competent agriculturalists equipped with the skills and knowledge necessary to contribute to food security and rural development in Kenya and beyond. The school offers a range of programs from certificate to diploma levels, covering various specializations within agriculture.

KSA's curriculum is designed to meet the evolving needs of the agricultural industry, incorporating modern farming techniques, sustainable practices, and agricultural extension methodologies. Its location in a predominantly agricultural region provides students with ample opportunities for field-based learning and interaction with farming communities. As a public institution under the Ministry of Agriculture, Livestock, Fisheries and Co-operatives, KSA is committed to upholding standards of excellence in agricultural education and research. Its alumni have contributed significantly to the growth and modernization of Kenya's agricultural landscape in various capacities, including extension officers, farm managers, researchers, and entrepreneurs.
institutions.short_description: The Kenya School of Agriculture (KSA) is a historic public institution in Wote, Makueni County, established in 1949. It specializes in practical-oriented agricultural training, offering certificate and diploma programs designed to equip students with skills for the dynamic agricultural sector in Kenya.

institutions.name: Kenya School Of Agriculture Songa Mbele Campus
institutions.parent_ministry: Ministry of Agriculture and Livestock Development
institutions.profile:
The Kenya School of Agriculture (KSA) is a premier public agricultural training institution located in Njoro, Nakuru County. Established in 1960 as Egerton Agricultural College, it played a crucial role in agricultural education before Egerton University was founded on the same grounds. As a State Corporation currently operating under the Ministry of Agriculture and Livestock Development, KSA is dedicated to providing specialized, practical-oriented training in various fields of agriculture and related sciences. The institution focuses on equipping students with the knowledge and skills necessary for sustainable agricultural practices, agribusiness management, research, and extension services, aiming to support the growth and modernization of Kenya's agricultural sector.

Situated in the agricultural heartland of Nakuru, KSA provides an ideal environment for learning and research with access to diverse farming activities and ecological zones. It offers a range of certificate and diploma programs that are designed to be relevant and responsive to the current needs of the agricultural industry in Kenya and the East African region. The curriculum emphasizes hands-on learning, extensive field practicals, and the adoption of modern farming techniques and technologies. KSA is recognized for its contribution to capacity building for the agricultural sector, training professionals who play a vital role in enhancing food security, promoting economic development, and driving innovation in agricultural practices.
institutions.short_description: Kenya School of Agriculture (KSA) is a public agricultural college in Njoro, Nakuru, Kenya, established in 1960. It specializes in providing practical-oriented certificate and diploma programs in agriculture and related fields to support the national agricultural sector.

institutions.name: Kenya School Of Law
institutions.parent_ministry: Office of the Attorney General and Department of Justice
institutions.profile:
The Kenya School of Law (KSL) is the sole provider of the Advocates Training Programme (ATP) in Kenya, established under the Council of Legal Education Act. Founded in 1963, its primary mandate is to provide practical legal training necessary for admission to the Kenyan bar. KSL plays a critical role in bridging the gap between theoretical university education and the practical demands of legal practice, ensuring that aspiring advocates are equipped with the skills and knowledge required to uphold justice and professionalism in the legal field.

Located in the serene Karen area of Nairobi, KSL's campus offers a conducive learning environment. Beyond the mandatory ATP, the institution provides a range of specialised courses and continuous professional development (CPD) programs for legal practitioners, facilitating lifelong learning and specialisation. KSL holds a strong reputation within the legal fraternity as the essential final step in the journey to becoming a practicing lawyer in Kenya, maintaining high standards for professional conduct and competence.
institutions.short_description: The Kenya School of Law (KSL) is Kenya's mandatory institution for professional legal training, offering the essential Advocates Training Programme (ATP) required for admission to the bar. Located in Karen, Nairobi, it bridges theoretical knowledge with practical skills, also providing continuous professional development for legal practitioners.

institutions.name: Kenya School Of Revenue Administration
institutions.profile:
The Kenya School of Revenue Administration (KESRA) is the corporate academy of the Kenya Revenue Authority (KRA), established under the KRA Act, Cap 469 of the Laws of Kenya. Its primary mandate is to build capacity for revenue administration within KRA, the wider public sector, and the private sector. KESRA achieves this by offering a diverse range of specialized training programs, including certificates, diplomas, higher diplomas, postgraduate diplomas, and executive courses in areas such as Tax Administration, Customs Administration, Fiscal Studies, and various management disciplines. KESRA is recognized both regionally and internationally, serving as a World Customs Organization (WCO) Regional Training Centre and being a member of the International Association of Customs and Tax Academies (IACTA), underscoring its commitment to international standards and collaboration.

KESRA's main campus is located in Nairobi at Times Tower, but it also operates other training facilities. The institution plays a critical role in developing skilled professionals capable of effectively managing revenue collection processes and contributing to national economic development. Its curriculum is designed to be responsive to the evolving landscape of tax and customs administration, incorporating current legal frameworks, technological advancements, and international best practices. By training both KRA staff and external stakeholders, KESRA aims to foster a better understanding of tax and customs laws, enhance compliance, and improve the overall efficiency and effectiveness of revenue administration in Kenya and the region.
institutions.short_description: The Kenya School of Revenue Administration (KESRA) is the training arm of the Kenya Revenue Authority (KRA), offering specialized courses in tax, customs, fiscal studies, and management to build capacity in revenue administration for KRA staff, the private sector, and the public.

other_info.address: Times Tower - 8th Floor, P.O. Box 48240, NAIROBI

institutions.name: Kenya Technical Trainers College
institutions.profile:
Kenya Technical Trainers College (KTTC) is a leading public institution in Kenya dedicated to training technical and vocational education and training (TVET) trainers, instructors, and technologists. Established in 1976, its primary mission is to produce competent professionals who can effectively impart technical and vocational skills across various sectors of the economy. Located in Kasarani, Nairobi, off the Thika Super Highway, KTTC plays a critical role in shaping the future of technical education in the country by providing comprehensive training programs that align with national development goals and industry demands.

The college offers a wide range of programs at diploma and certificate levels, including Diploma in Technical Education, Diploma in Instructor Training, Diploma in Business Education, and various programs in engineering, applied sciences, and information technology. KTTC is recognized for its practical-oriented training approach, state-of-the-art facilities, and experienced faculty. It has a strong reputation for producing highly skilled trainers and technologists who contribute significantly to the TVET sector and the broader workforce in Kenya and beyond.
institutions.short_description: Kenya Technical Trainers College (KTTC) is a premier public TVET institution in Nairobi, Kenya, dedicated to training technical and vocational education and training (TVET) trainers, instructors, and technologists across diverse technical and business fields.

other_info.address: Kasarani-Mwiki Rd, Off Thika Super Highway, P.O Box 44600-00200 Nairobi

institutions.name: Kenya Water Institute
institutions.parent_ministry: Ministry of Water, Sanitation and Irrigation
programs: Higher Diploma in Water Resources Management
institutions.profile:
Kenya Water Institute (KEWI) is a public National Technical Training Institute established by the Government of Kenya under the Ministry of Water, Sanitation and Irrigation. Founded under Legal Notice No. 195 of November 2002, it was later elevated to a State Corporation status in 2007 via the KEWI Order 2007 (Legal Notice No. 119). KEWI holds a significant role as the leading institution in Kenya dedicated to providing specialized training, research, and consultancy services in the water and sanitation sector. Its mandate is crucial for building capacity within the sector and ensuring sustainable management of water resources in the country.

The Institute offers a diverse range of academic programs tailored to meet the needs of the water and sanitation industry. These programs include Certificate, Diploma, and Higher Diploma courses spanning various disciplines such as Water Engineering, Water Technology, Waste Water Technology, Water Resources Management, Irrigation and Drainage, and Environmental Management. Located primarily on Enterprise Road in Industrial Area, Nairobi, KEWI also operates satellite campuses in Kitui and Chiakariga, Tharaka Nithi County, extending its reach and accessibility across different regions of Kenya. As the national training hub, KEWI plays a vital role in producing qualified professionals essential for the development and maintenance of Kenya's water infrastructure and services.
institutions.short_description: Kenya Water Institute (KEWI) is the national technical training institute in Kenya specializing in water and sanitation education and research. Established under the Ministry of Water, Sanitation and Irrigation, it offers certificate, diploma, and higher diploma programs in various water-related fields.

institutions.name: Kenya Wildlife Service Training Institute
institutions.parent_ministry: Ministry of Tourism, Wildlife and Heritage
institutions.profile:
The Kenya Wildlife Service Training Institute (KWSTI) is a public TVET institution located in Naivasha, Nakuru County. Established in 1987 under African Tours and Hotels (AT&H), it was later taken over by the Kenya Wildlife Service (KWS) in 1991, becoming the principal training arm for KWS personnel. Situated in a unique ecological setting near Lake Naivasha, the institute provides an ideal environment for studying wildlife, conservation, and tourism.

KWSTI offers a diverse range of programs, including diplomas and certificates, tailored for both KWS staff and the general public. The curriculum focuses on critical areas such as wildlife management, environmental conservation, tourism, and hospitality. The institute plays a vital role in capacity building for the conservation and tourism sectors in Kenya and the wider East African region, equipping graduates with the necessary skills for careers in park management, research, tourism guiding, and environmental protection. Its close ties with KWS ensure practical, relevant training aligned with industry needs.
institutions.short_description: The Kenya Wildlife Service Training Institute (KWSTI) is a public college in Naivasha, Nakuru County, specializing in wildlife management, conservation, tourism, and hospitality training for KWS staff and the public, serving as the primary training arm for Kenya Wildlife Service.

institutions.name: Kenyatta University
institutions.profile:
Kenyatta University (KU) is a major public research university located in Nairobi, Kenya. Established originally in 1965 as Kenyatta College, a teacher training institution, it became a constituent college of the University of Nairobi in 1970 and was chartered as a full-fledged university on 15th March 1985. Situated along the Thika Superhighway, its main campus is one of the largest in East Africa. KU is renowned for its diverse academic programs spanning various disciplines, including education, health sciences, engineering, business, humanities, and social sciences. The university boasts state-of-the-art facilities, including a teaching and referral hospital, extensive libraries, and research centers, supporting a large student body undertaking undergraduate and postgraduate studies.

KU is committed to producing globally competitive graduates through quality teaching, research, and innovation. It plays a significant role in national development by addressing societal challenges through relevant training, research output, and community engagement initiatives. The university has cultivated a strong reputation for academic excellence and has expanded its reach through several satellite campuses across the country, enhancing access to higher education. Its emphasis on research and partnerships with both local and international institutions solidifies its position as a leading institution of higher learning in Kenya and the East African region.
institutions.short_description: Kenyatta University is a leading public research university in Nairobi, Kenya, chartered in 1985, known for its extensive academic programs and commitment to research, innovation, and community development.

other_info.address: Kenyatta University Main Campus, Along Thika Superhighway

institutions.name: Kenyatta University - Mama Ngina University College
institutions.profile:
Mama Ngina University College (MNUC) is a public constituent college of Kenyatta University, located in Mutomo area, Gatundu South Constituency, Kiambu County, Kenya. The institution was officially inaugurated in 2022, marking a significant step in expanding access to higher education in the region. Situated in a serene environment outside the bustling city center, MNUC aims to provide a conducive learning atmosphere for its students while contributing to the socio-economic development of the surrounding community and the nation at large.

As a constituent college of one of Kenya's leading public universities, Mama Ngina University College benefits from Kenyatta University's established academic framework and reputation. MNUC offers programs across various disciplines through its Schools, including Education, Business & Economics, Science & Technology, and Humanities & Social Sciences. These programs are designed to equip students with the knowledge, skills, and values necessary to excel in their chosen fields and contribute positively to society. The college is committed to upholding high standards of academic excellence, research, and community engagement.
institutions.short_description: Mama Ngina University College is a public constituent college of Kenyatta University, inaugurated in 2022 and situated in Mutomo, Gatundu South, Kiambu County. It offers diverse programs in education, business, science, and humanities, providing accessible higher education in a conducive environment.

institutions.name: Kenyatta University TVET - Kitui
institutions.profile:
Kenyatta University TVET - Kitui is a training centre operating under the umbrella of Kenyatta University, one of Kenya's leading public universities. Located in Kitui Town along Kitui - Mutomo Road, this centre extends Kenyatta University's commitment to providing accessible and quality education, specifically focusing on Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET). It serves the Kitui region and surrounding areas, offering hands-on, skills-based training designed to meet the demands of various industries and contribute to national development goals through practical expertise.

The centre plays a crucial role in decentralizing educational opportunities, bringing TVET programs closer to students in Kitui County. By leveraging Kenyatta University's resources and academic standards, the Kitui TVET Centre aims to equip learners with marketable skills, fostering entrepreneurship, and enhancing employability. Its programs likely cover a range of technical fields, providing students with the competencies needed to excel in vocational careers.
institutions.short_description: Kenyatta University TVET - Kitui is a training centre under Kenyatta University, offering technical and vocational education in Kitui Town. It provides practical skills to meet regional industry needs and enhance employability.

institutions.name: Kenyatta University Tvet - Main Campus
institutions.location: Kenyatta University Main Campus, Thika Road
programs: Diploma in Criminology and Security Studies
institutions.profile:
Kenyatta University offers TVET programs through its integrated structure at the Main Campus. As a leading public university in Kenya, KU extends its educational mandate to provide technical and vocational education, catering to students seeking practical skills and specialized knowledge. These programs are designed to meet the demands of various industries, offering diplomas and certificates that bridge academic learning with real-world application. Students benefit from the university's extensive resources, including modern facilities, experienced faculty, and a vibrant campus environment.

The TVET programs at Kenyatta University cover a diverse range of fields, from business and social sciences to applied technology. Situated on the expansive Main Campus along Thika Road in Ruiru, Kiambu County, the institution provides a conducive learning environment accessible from Nairobi and surrounding areas. By integrating TVET within the university framework, Kenyatta University aims to enhance career prospects for graduates and contribute to the national development goals through skills-based education. The institution maintains a strong reputation for quality education, attracting students from across the region.
institutions.short_description: Kenyatta University TVET programs are offered at the main campus, providing diploma and certificate courses in various technical and vocational fields. It is a public institution known for quality skills-based education.

institutions.name: Kenyatta University Tvet - Mombasa
institutions.profile:
Kenyatta University TVET - Mombasa represents the Technical and Vocational Education and Training programs offered by Kenyatta University at its Mombasa Campus. As one of the major public universities in Kenya, Kenyatta University has expanded its reach and academic offerings through various satellite campuses, including the one in Mombasa. The Mombasa Campus is situated in the Tudor area, serving the coastal region with a range of academic programs, including those specifically tailored for the TVET sector. This allows students in the region to access quality university-level and TVET education without needing to relocate to the main campus in Nairobi.

The TVET programs at Kenyatta University Mombasa Campus are designed to equip students with practical skills and technical knowledge relevant to various industries, addressing the growing demand for skilled professionals in Kenya's economy. Leveraging the extensive resources, experienced faculty, and established curriculum development capabilities of Kenyatta University, the Mombasa TVET offerings maintain high standards of quality and relevance. The campus environment provides students with access to necessary facilities and a conducive learning atmosphere, contributing to their overall educational experience and preparing them for successful careers or further studies.
institutions.short_description: Kenyatta University TVET - Mombasa offers practical-oriented technical and vocational education programs through Kenyatta University's Mombasa Campus, providing accessible and quality skills training in the coastal region.

institutions.name: Kenyatta University Tvet - Nakuru
institutions.profile:
Kenyatta University (KU) is a leading public university in Kenya, established in 1985. It has expanded its reach through various campuses and specialized centers across the country to make higher education and skills development more accessible. The Kenyatta University TVET Centre in Nakuru operates under this mandate, providing technical and vocational education and training programs aligned with national development goals and industry needs. Located within the Kenyatta University Nakuru Campus, the centre offers a range of courses designed to equip students with practical skills and knowledge for various trades and professions.

The Nakuru Campus itself is situated along the Nakuru-Eldoret Highway, serving students from Nakuru County and the surrounding Rift Valley region. It provides a conducive learning environment with facilities supporting academic and technical training. By integrating TVET within the university structure, KU aims to bridge the gap between theoretical knowledge and practical application, offering alternative pathways for students seeking market-ready skills. The centre leverages the university's resources and academic standards to ensure quality training, contributing to the local workforce and economy.
institutions.short_description: Kenyatta University TVET - Nakuru offers technical and vocational education and training programs within the framework of Kenyatta University, providing accessible skills-based learning opportunities in the Rift Valley region.

other_info.address: Along Nakuru-Eldoret Highway, Next to Rift Valley ASK Showground

institutions.name: Kenyenya Teachers Training College
institutions.profile:
Kenyenya Teachers Training College (KTTC) is a public institution located in Kenyenya town, Bomachoge Borabu Constituency, Kisii County, Kenya. Established with the primary objective of training qualified teachers for primary schools in the country, KTTC plays a crucial role in shaping the educational landscape of the region and beyond. The college is known for its commitment to providing quality teacher education, adhering to the curriculum set by the Ministry of Education and the Teachers Service Commission (TSC). It offers various programs, including the flagship Primary Teacher Education (PTE) certificate course, as well as Diploma courses in Education and Early Childhood Development Education (ECDE), catering to different levels and specializations within the teaching profession.

KTTC is situated in a serene environment conducive to learning, providing students with the necessary facilities for academic and personal development. The college boasts experienced faculty members dedicated to imparting both theoretical knowledge and practical skills essential for effective teaching. Over the years, Kenyenya TTC has produced numerous competent teachers who have contributed significantly to the growth and improvement of primary education in Kenya. Its reputation is built on a foundation of academic excellence, discipline, and a focus on producing well-rounded educators prepared to meet the challenges of the modern classroom. The college continues to adapt its programs and infrastructure to meet the evolving demands of the education sector in Kenya.
institutions.short_description: Kenyenya Teachers Training College is a public institution in Kisii County, Kenya, dedicated to training qualified primary school teachers. It offers PTE certificate, Diploma in Education, and ECDE programs, focusing on quality education and producing competent educators for Kenya's primary schools.

institutions.name: Kericho Teachers' College
institutions.profile:
Kericho Teachers' College (KTC) is a prominent public teacher training institution located in Kericho, Kenya. Established in 1963, the college has a long-standing history of contributing to the education sector by training qualified teachers for basic education in Kenya. Located along Hospital Road in Kericho Town, KTC serves students primarily from the Rift Valley region and across the country, offering a conducive learning environment for aspiring educators.

The college is currently mandated to train teachers for the Competency-Based Curriculum (CBC) under the new framework introduced by the Ministry of Education. It offers Diploma in Primary Teacher Education (DPTE) and Diploma in Early Childhood Development Education (DECDE) programs. KTC is known for its structured curriculum, experienced faculty, and practical training opportunities, preparing graduates to meet the evolving demands of the teaching profession. Its reputation is built on decades of service and a commitment to producing competent and disciplined teachers.
institutions.short_description: Kericho Teachers' College is a public teacher training institution in Kericho, Kenya, established in 1963. Located in Kericho Town, it offers Diploma in Primary Teacher Education and Early Childhood Development Education programs, playing a vital role in training qualified teachers for the Kenyan education system.

institutions.name: Kericho Township Technical & Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Kericho Township Technical & Vocational College (KTTVC) is a public institution established in 2015 under the Ministry of Education in Kenya. Located strategically in Kericho Town, the college plays a crucial role in providing accessible and affordable technical and vocational education and training (TVET) to students from the surrounding regions and beyond. Its establishment was part of the Kenyan government's initiative to increase the number of TVET institutions across the country, aiming to equip the youth with market-relevant skills for employment and entrepreneurship.

KTTVC offers a diverse range of courses across various disciplines including Business Studies, Information Communication Technology, Building and Civil Engineering, Electrical and Electronic Engineering, Automotive Engineering, Agriculture, and Health Sciences, among others. These programs are offered at artisan, certificate, and diploma levels, designed to meet the growing demands of the modern workforce. The college is committed to providing practical-oriented training, fostering innovation, and contributing to the socio-economic development of the community through skill development and knowledge transfer.
institutions.short_description: Kericho Township Technical & Vocational College is a public TVET institution located in Kericho Town, Kenya. It offers various artisan, certificate, and diploma programs in technical and business fields, aiming to equip students with practical skills for employment and self-reliance.

institutions.name: Kerio Valley Technical & Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Kerio Valley Technical & Vocational College (KVTVC) is a public TVET institution situated in Kabartonjo, Baringo County, Kenya. Established as part of the Kenyan government's commitment to expanding access to technical and vocational education and training across the nation, KVTVC plays a vital role in empowering the youth of the Kerio Valley region and surrounding areas with practical, market-driven skills. The college aims to equip students with competencies that are essential for personal development, gainful employment, and contribution to the country's economic growth. Its strategic location addresses the historical imbalance in access to quality TVET programs in rural and semi-urban parts of Kenya.

KVTVC offers a diverse portfolio of courses accredited by recognized bodies such as the Technical and Vocational Education and Training Authority (TVETA) and the Kenya National Examinations Council (KNEC). Programs typically span key sectors including Building and Civil Engineering, Electrical and Electronic Engineering, Information Communication Technology, Business Studies, and Agricultural Sciences. By providing these courses, the college serves as a crucial resource for students seeking hands-on skills and technical expertise. Located within Baringo North Constituency, KVTVC is well-positioned to support local and regional development initiatives by producing a skilled workforce tailored to the needs of various industries and the specific economic activities prevalent in the Kerio Valley area. The institution contributes significantly to enhancing the educational landscape and creating pathways to employment and entrepreneurship for the local population.
institutions.short_description: Kerio Valley Technical & Vocational College (KVTVC) is a public TVET institution located in Kabartonjo, Baringo County. It offers a range of accredited technical, vocational, and business courses aimed at equipping students with practical, market-relevant skills for national development and personal empowerment.

other_info.address: Along Kabartonjo - Tenges Rd, Kabartonjo Town, Baringo North Constituency

institutions.name: Keroka Technical Training Institute
institutions.profile:
Keroka Technical Training Institute (KTTI) is a public institution established under the Ministry of Education, State Department for Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET). Located in Keroka Town, Kisii County, the institute plays a crucial role in providing technical, industrial, vocational, and entrepreneurial training. Its mission is to equip trainees with relevant skills and knowledge to meet the demands of the dynamic labor market and foster self-employment opportunities. KTTI offers a wide range of programs across various disciplines, including engineering, business, ICT, and applied sciences.

Since its inception, Keroka TTI has grown to become a reputable TVET institution in the region, known for its commitment to quality training and practical-oriented learning. The institute is strategically situated to serve students from Kisii and neighboring counties, contributing significantly to human resource development. With modern facilities and experienced faculty, KTTI aims to produce competent graduates who can contribute effectively to national development and innovation.
institutions.short_description: Keroka Technical Training Institute (KTTI) is a public TVET institution in Keroka Town, Kisii County, offering diverse technical, industrial, vocational, and entrepreneurial programs aimed at equipping students with relevant skills for the job market and self-employment.

institutions.name: Khwisero Technical & Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Khwisero Technical & Vocational College is a public TVET institution located in Khwisero Constituency, Kakamega County, Kenya. Established under the Ministry of Education, the college plays a crucial role in expanding access to technical and vocational training in Western Kenya. Situated along the Khwisero-Musoli Road, it serves students from the surrounding rural areas and beyond, providing essential skills development aligned with national development goals.

The college offers a range of diploma and certificate courses across various disciplines, including business studies, information and communication technology (ICT), electrical and electronics engineering, building and civil engineering, and mechanical engineering. These programs are designed to meet the demands of the job market and empower graduates with practical expertise for employment or entrepreneurship. Khwisero TVC contributes significantly to the socio-economic upliftment of the region by providing relevant training opportunities for the youth.
institutions.short_description: Khwisero Technical & Vocational College (TVC) is a public institution in Khwisero, Kakamega County, offering technical and vocational education and training (TVET) programs aimed at equipping students with practical skills for various industries and self-employment, contributing to regional development.

institutions.name: Kiambu Institute Of Science And Technology
institutions.profile:
Kiambu Institute of Science and Technology (KIST) is a renowned public Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) institution located in Kiambu County, Kenya. Established in 1971 through the collaborative efforts of the local community and the German Government, KIST has a rich history of providing technical and vocational training. As one of the pioneering TVET institutions in Kenya, it falls under the purview of the Ministry of Education, playing a crucial role in the country's technical workforce development.

Situated just outside Kiambu town, the institute offers a wide array of diploma and certificate programs designed to equip students with practical skills relevant to the modern job market. Its academic disciplines span across applied sciences, various engineering fields, business studies, hospitality, and information technology. KIST is committed to fostering innovation and producing graduates who are ready to contribute meaningfully to industrial growth and national development, maintaining a strong reputation for quality technical training.
institutions.short_description: Kiambu Institute of Science and Technology (KIST) is a public TVET institution in Kiambu, established in 1971. It offers diverse diploma and certificate programs in science, tech, business, hospitality, and IT, focusing on practical skills for industry and development.

institutions.name: Kibabii Diploma Teachers Training College
programs: Diploma in Early Childhood Development Education (DECDE)
institutions.profile:
Kibabii Diploma Teachers Training College (KIBABII TTC) is a public institution in Kenya dedicated to training qualified teachers for the primary school level. Located in Bungoma County, near the town of Bungoma and in close proximity to Kibabii University, it plays a crucial role in the region's educational landscape. As a government-managed college under the Ministry of Education, KIBABII TTC adheres to national curriculum standards, particularly focusing on the Diploma in Primary Teacher Education (DPTE) and Diploma in Early Childhood Development Education (DECDE) programs, which are essential for the implementation of the Competency-Based Curriculum (CBC).

The college is recognized for its contribution to teacher training, providing students with the pedagogical skills, subject knowledge, and practical experience needed to excel in primary education. Its location in a vibrant regional center offers students access to necessary resources and practice teaching opportunities in local schools. While specific historical details and a separate public profile are less prominent than those of its university neighbor, KIBABII TTC maintains its standing as a key institution for moulding the next generation of primary school educators in western Kenya.
institutions.short_description: Kibabii Diploma Teachers Training College is a public institution in Bungoma County, Kenya, training primary school teachers. It offers Diploma in Primary Teacher Education (DPTE) and Early Childhood Development Education (DECDE) programs, aligning with the national curriculum to produce competent educators for the CBC era.

institutions.name: Kibabii University
institutions.profile: Kibabii University College (KIBU) is a premier institution of higher learning located in Bungoma County, Kenya. Established as a constituent college of Masinde Muliro University of Science and Technology (MMUST) in 2015, KIBU has since grown into a fully-fledged university, offering a wide range of academic programs. The institution is committed to excellence in teaching, research, and innovation, making it a key player in Kenya’s education sector.
institutions.short_description: A premier public university in Bungoma, established in 2015, offering diverse academic programs with a focus on teaching, research, and innovation.

institutions.name: Kibabii University Directorate Of Tvet College
institutions.profile:
Kibabii University, situated in Bungoma County, Kenya, was established in 2015, having been elevated from a constituent college of Masinde Muliro University of Science and Technology. Located along the Chwele-Bungoma road, approximately 10 kilometers from Bungoma town, KIBU serves as a key higher education institution in the Western region, offering a wide range of academic programs from certificates to doctoral levels in various disciplines.

The Directorate of Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) within Kibabii University plays a crucial role in expanding the institution's offerings to include practical, skill-based training. This directorate is dedicated to providing accessible TVET programs that equip students with the competencies required for employment and entrepreneurship. By integrating TVET into the university structure, Kibabii aims to address the growing demand for skilled professionals and contribute significantly to national development through technical and vocational education alongside its traditional university functions.
institutions.short_description: The Kibabii University Directorate of TVET College is a public institution in Bungoma County, Kenya, dedicated to offering quality technical and vocational education and training programs. It operates under Kibabii University, providing students with practical skills for various industries and contributing to Kenya's workforce development.

institutions.name: Kibra Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Kibra Technical And Vocational College (Kibra TVC) is a public technical and vocational education and training (TVET) institution located in the bustling Kibera area of Nairobi County, Kenya. Established by the Government of Kenya under the Ministry of Education, the college plays a crucial role in providing accessible, relevant, and practical technical training to the youth. Its strategic location within one of Nairobi's largest informal settlements underscores its commitment to empowering the local community by equipping young individuals with essential skills needed for sustainable livelihoods, employment, and entrepreneurship.

The college offers a diverse array of market-driven programs across various disciplines, including engineering technologies such as Building Technology and Electrical & Electronics Engineering, Information Communication Technology, Business Studies, and specialized vocational trades like Fashion Design & Garment Making, Hair Dressing & Beauty Therapy, Food & Beverage Production, and Hospitality. These programs are designed to meet the current demands of industries and the job market, ensuring graduates are well-prepared to contribute to national development. Kibra TVC is dedicated to fostering a learning environment that emphasizes hands-on training and practical application, aiming to produce competent and skilled professionals.
institutions.short_description: Kibra Technical And Vocational College is a public TVET institution in Kibra, Nairobi, offering a wide range of technical and vocational programs including engineering, IT, business, and trades, aimed at empowering youth with practical skills for the job market.

institutions.name: Kibwezi Technical & Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Kibwezi Technical & Vocational College is a leading public Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) institution situated in Kibwezi, Makueni County, Kenya. Established under the Ministry of Education, the college plays a crucial role in advancing technical skills and knowledge aligned with national development goals. It is strategically located off the bustling Mombasa-Nairobi Highway, making it accessible to students from across the region. The college is committed to fostering a conducive learning environment where students can acquire hands-on experience and theoretical understanding in diverse technical disciplines. Its programs are designed to meet the current demands of the job market, preparing graduates for successful careers or entrepreneurial ventures.

The college offers a wide array of programs ranging from diploma to certificate levels in fields such as Building & Civil Engineering, Electrical & Electronic Engineering, Business Studies, ICT, and hospitality, among others. Kibwezi TVC is dedicated to producing competent and skilled manpower essential for driving industrial growth and economic development in Kenya. The institution emphasizes practical training, industry linkages, and continuous improvement of its curriculum and facilities to ensure relevance and quality in technical education. It serves as a vital resource for the local community and the broader Kenyan economy by empowering youth with practical, marketable skills.
institutions.short_description: Kibwezi Technical & Vocational College is a public TVET institution in Kibwezi, Makueni County, Kenya. It offers market-driven technical and vocational training programs to equip students with practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship.

other_info.location: Off Mombasa-Nairobi Highway, near Muthingini Shopping Centre, Kibwezi

institutions.name: Kieni Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Kieni Technical and Vocational College (TVC) is a public institution located in Mweiga, Kieni East Constituency, Nyeri County. Established under the Kenyan Ministry of Education, Science, and Technology, the college serves the region by providing essential technical and vocational education and training. Its mission is centered on equipping students with practical, hands-on skills across various disciplines, preparing them for the demands of the job market and contributing to national development goals. The college aims to be a centre of excellence in technical training within the central Kenya region, fostering innovation and self-reliance among its graduates.

The college offers a diverse range of courses under various departments, including Building & Civil Engineering, Business Studies, ICT, Mechanical & Automotive Engineering, Electrical & Electronics Engineering, Applied Sciences, Agriculture, and Hospitality & Tourism. These programs are designed to cater to different skill levels, from certificate to diploma courses. Situated in a rural setting, Kieni TVC plays a crucial role in making post-secondary technical training accessible to students in the area and beyond. Its focus on practical skills and industry relevance helps bridge the gap between education and employment, making it a vital institution for community empowerment and economic growth.
institutions.short_description: Kieni Technical and Vocational College is a public TVC in Mweiga, Nyeri County. It provides practical technical and vocational education across various fields, preparing students for employment and contributing to national development through skills training.

institutions.name: Kigumo Technical Training Institute
institutions.profile:
Kigumo Technical Training Institute is a public technical and vocational education and training (TVET) institution situated in Kigumo Sub-county, Murang'a County, Kenya. Established under the Ministry of Education, the institute plays a crucial role in providing accessible and quality technical skills development to the youth and adults in the region and beyond. It is strategically located to serve the local community while attracting students from across the country, contributing significantly to the government's agenda of promoting technical skills for national development and job creation. The institute is mandated to offer diverse programs aligned with industry needs, fostering practical competence and innovation among its graduates.

The institute offers a broad spectrum of courses spanning various disciplines, including engineering, business, information communication technology, building and civil engineering, and hospitality, among others. These programs are designed to meet the demands of the modern labor market, emphasizing hands-on training and practical application of knowledge. Kigumo TTI is recognized for its commitment to providing relevant, industry-focused education, aiming to produce skilled professionals and entrepreneurs capable of contributing effectively to the Kenyan economy. The institution strives to maintain high standards of training and infrastructure to support a conducive learning environment for all its students.
institutions.short_description: Kigumo Technical Training Institute is a public TVET institution located in Murang'a County, Kenya. It offers a wide range of technical and vocational programs aimed at equipping students with practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship in various fields.

institutions.name: Kiharu Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Kiharu Technical And Vocational College (Kiharu TVC) is a public institution located in Murang'a County, Kenya. Established under the government's initiative to increase access to technical and vocational education and training (TVET), the college plays a crucial role in equipping young Kenyans with practical skills essential for employment and entrepreneurship. Situated in the heart of Murang'a, specifically within the Kiharu Constituency, the college serves students from the local region and beyond, contributing to the development of a skilled workforce in various technical disciplines.

Kiharu TVC offers a range of certificate and diploma programs accredited by relevant authorities such as the Kenya National Examinations Council (KNEC) and the Curriculum Development, Assessment and Certification Council (CDACC). These programs cover diverse fields including but not limited to engineering, building technology, business studies, and information communication technology (ICT). The college is committed to providing quality training through competent instructors and well-equipped workshops and laboratories. Its focus is on producing graduates who are not only technically proficient but also possess the necessary soft skills and entrepreneurial spirit to thrive in the dynamic job market and contribute positively to society. The college continues to expand its program offerings and infrastructure to meet the growing demand for TVET in the country.
institutions.short_description: Kiharu Technical And Vocational College is a public TVET institution in Murang'a County, offering accredited certificate and diploma programs in technical, business, and IT fields to empower students with practical skills.

other_info.address: Kiharu Technical and Vocational College, Muranga

institutions.name: Kiirua Technical Training Institute
institutions.logo: Logo available on institution website and social media
institutions.profile:
Kiirua Technical Training Institute (KIIRUA TTI) is a prominent public Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) institution located in Kiirua town, Imenti Central Constituency, Meru County, Kenya. Operating under the Ministry of Education, the institute became operational around 2016/2017, marking its entry into Kenya's TVET sector with a mission to provide accessible and quality technical training. Strategically situated off the busy Meru-Nanyuki Highway, KIIRUA TTI serves students from Meru and surrounding regions, contributing to skills development in the area.

The institute offers a diverse portfolio of programs across various departments, including Building & Civil Engineering, Electrical & Electronics Engineering, Mechanical & Automotive Engineering, Business Studies, ICT, Applied Sciences, and Hospitality & Tourism. These programs are offered at different levels, including Diploma, Certificate, and Artisan, catering to a broad range of academic backgrounds and career aspirations. KIIRUA TTI focuses on providing practical, industry-relevant training, equipped with workshops, labs, and qualified instructors, aiming to produce competent graduates ready for employment or entrepreneurship, thereby supporting national development goals.
institutions.short_description: Kiirua Technical Training Institute is a public TVET institution situated in Kiirua Town, Meru County, Kenya. It offers a wide range of technical and vocational programs across various disciplines, including engineering, business, and ICT, aimed at equipping students with practical skills for the job market and entrepreneurship, contributing to national development.

institutions.name: Kilgoris Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Kilgoris Technical And Vocational College is a public Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) institution located in Kilgoris, Narok County. Established under the Ministry of Education, the college is dedicated to providing quality technical and vocational skills training to students. It plays a crucial role in enhancing the skilled workforce in the region and contributing to national development through practical, industry-relevant education.

The college offers a diverse range of certificate and diploma courses across various disciplines, including engineering technology, business and finance, information communication technology, and hospitality and tourism, among others. Situated in the heart of Kilgoris town, it serves students from the surrounding areas and other parts of Kenya, aiming to equip them with the competencies needed for employment, entrepreneurship, and further education. The institution is recognized for its commitment to practical learning and aims to foster innovation and self-reliance among its graduates.
institutions.short_description: Kilgoris Technical And Vocational College is a public TVET institution in Kilgoris, Narok County, offering a wide array of certificate and diploma programs in technical, vocational, and business fields to equip students with practical skills for the job market and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Kimasian Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Kimasian Technical And Vocational College (Kimasian TVC) is a public institution located in Kimasian, Belgut Constituency, within Kericho County, Kenya. It operates under the Ministry of Education, State Department of Vocational and Technical Training, and is recognized by the Technical and Vocational Education and Training Authority (TVETA). The college is dedicated to providing accessible and quality technical and vocational training to equip students with relevant skills for national development and global competitiveness.

Established to address the growing need for skilled manpower in various sectors, Kimasian TVC offers a diverse range of programs at diploma, certificate, and artisan levels. These programs cover fields such as engineering, building technology, business, ICT, hospitality, and agriculture. The college aims to foster innovation, practical competence, and entrepreneurial skills among its graduates, preparing them for employment, further education, or self-employment. Its strategic location in Kericho County allows it to serve the local community and surrounding regions, contributing significantly to the socio-economic development of the area by producing work-ready professionals.
institutions.short_description: Kimasian Technical And Vocational College is a public TVC in Kimasian, Belgut Constituency, Kericho County. It offers diverse technical and vocational programs at diploma, certificate, and artisan levels, aiming to equip students with practical skills for employment and self-reliance under the Ministry of Education.

institutions.name: Kiminini Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Kiminini Technical and Vocational College (KTVC) is a public Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) institution located in Kiminini Town, Trans-Nzoia County. Established with the aim of providing accessible and quality technical and vocational training, KTVC is registered by the Technical and Vocational Education and Training Authority (TVETA) and operates under the Ministry of Education in Kenya. The college plays a crucial role in empowering the youth in the region and beyond with practical skills necessary for employment, entrepreneurship, and national development.

KTVC offers a diverse range of programs across various disciplines, including engineering, information technology, business, and applied sciences, among others. The curriculum is designed to be industry-relevant, emphasizing hands-on training and practical exposure. The institution is known for its commitment to producing competent graduates who can contribute effectively to the economy. Its location in Kiminini, a growing urban centre, makes it a vital educational hub for the surrounding agricultural and business communities.
institutions.short_description: Kiminini Technical And Vocational College is a public TVET institution in Kiminini, Trans-Nzoia County, registered by TVETA. It offers diverse technical, vocational, and business programs to equip students with practical skills for industry and self-employment.

institutions.profile:
Kinango Technical And Vocational College (KINANGO TVC) is a public technical and vocational education and training (TVET) institution located in Kinango, Kwale County, Kenya. Established in 2017, the college is strategically positioned approximately 2km off the Mombasa – Lunga Lunga Highway along the Samburu – Kinango Road, opposite the Kinango Police Station. Its establishment was part of the Kenyan government's broader initiative to expand access to TVET opportunities across the country, particularly in previously underserved regions, thereby contributing to national development through skills training.

KINANGO TVC offers a diverse range of diploma and certificate programs designed to meet the evolving demands of the job market. These programs span various disciplines, including engineering (such as Building & Civil Engineering and Electrical Engineering), Business Studies, Information Communication Technology (ICT), Hospitality and Tourism, Fashion Design, and Agriculture. The college aims to provide quality, practical-oriented training that equips students with the necessary competencies for employment or entrepreneurship, fostering self-reliance and contributing to socio-economic growth in the region and beyond. The college is registered by TVETA and its programs are recognized by bodies like KUCCPS, facilitating student admission.
institutions.short_description: Kinango Technical And Vocational College is a public TVET institution in Kinango, Kwale County, Kenya. Established in 2017, it offers various diploma and certificate programs in technical, business, and hospitality fields, aimed at equipping youth with practical skills.

other_info.address: P.O. Box 188 – 80204, Kinango. Physical Address: Opposite Kinango Police Station.

institutions.name: Kinangop Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Kinangop Technical And Vocational College (KTVC) is a public TVC situated in Kinangop, Nyandarua County, Kenya. The college was established to provide accessible technical and vocational education and training to students in the region and beyond, equipping them with practical skills necessary for employment and entrepreneurship. It operates under the Ministry of Education and is a recognized institution by the Kenya National Examinations Council (KNEC) and other relevant bodies, offering various programs at diploma and certificate levels.

KTVC focuses on offering market-driven courses aligned with the country's development goals. Its curriculum covers a range of technical and business fields, designed to meet the needs of various industries. The college aims to foster innovation, creativity, and competence among its trainees through practical-oriented learning and dedicated staff. It plays a significant role in contributing to the human resource capacity of Nyandarua County and the broader Kenyan economy.
institutions.short_description: Kinangop Technical And Vocational College (KTVC) is a public TVC in Nyandarua County, offering market-driven technical and business programs to equip students with practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Kipipiri Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Kipipiri Technical and Vocational College (TVC) is a public institution under the Ministry of Education in Kenya, dedicated to providing quality technical and vocational education and training (TVET). Located in Kipipiri town within Kipipiri Constituency, Nyandarua County, the college plays a crucial role in enhancing access to post-secondary education and skills development in the region. Since its establishment, Kipipiri TVC has focused on empowering youth with practical, industry-relevant skills necessary for employment and self-reliance, contributing significantly to the socio-economic growth of Nyandarua and surrounding areas.

The college offers a wide array of programs across various disciplines, including Engineering (Electrical, Mechanical, Building & Civil), Information Technology, Business Studies, Hospitality, Fashion Design, Hairdressing, and Agriculture. These programs are designed to meet the demands of the current job market, combining theoretical knowledge with hands-on training. Kipipiri TVC is committed to fostering a learning environment that promotes innovation, creativity, and technical excellence, preparing graduates to become competent professionals and entrepreneurs. Its strategic location makes it accessible to students from across Nyandarua and beyond, serving as a vital center for technical skills acquisition in the region.
institutions.short_description: Kipipiri Technical and Vocational College is a public TVC located in Kipipiri, Nyandarua County, Kenya. Established by the Ministry of Education, it offers diverse technical and vocational programs aimed at equipping students with practical skills for the job market and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Kipkabus Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Kipkabus Technical And Vocational College (Kipkabus TVC) is a public technical and vocational education and training (TVET) institution located in Kipkabus town, Uasin Gishu County, Kenya. Situated strategically along the busy Eldoret-Nakuru Highway, the college was established under the Ministry of Education to expand access to quality technical and vocational skills development in the region and contribute to national development goals. It aims to provide relevant, industry-aligned training to empower young Kenyans with practical competencies.

The college offers a diverse range of diploma, certificate, and artisan programs across various disciplines, including engineering technology, building and construction, business studies, information communication technology, fashion design, hospitality, and more. Kipkabus TVC is dedicated to fostering innovation and entrepreneurship among its students, equipping them with the skills needed for successful employment or self-employment in the dynamic job market. Its location makes it accessible to students from Uasin Gishu and neighbouring counties, serving as a vital centre for skills acquisition.
institutions.short_description: Kipkabus Technical And Vocational College is a public TVET institution in Kipkabus, Uasin Gishu County, offering accessible and quality technical and vocational training across various disciplines to equip students with practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship.

other_info.address: P.O. Box 423 - 30100, Eldoret. Physical: Along Eldoret-Nakuru Highway, Kipkabus Town

institutions.name: Kipsinende Technical Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Kipsinende Technical Vocational College (TVC) is a public institution situated in Kipsinende village, Belgut Constituency, Kericho County, Kenya. Established to provide accessible technical and vocational education and training (TVET) to the youth, the college plays a crucial role in equipping students with practical skills necessary for the job market and entrepreneurship. It is regulated by the TVET Authority (TVETA) and offers a variety of artisan, certificate, and diploma courses across various fields, aligning with the government's initiative to promote technical skills development.

The college is committed to delivering quality education and training that meets industry standards. While a comprehensive list of all programs is best obtained directly from the institution or its official website, TVCs like Kipsinende typically offer courses in areas such as engineering, building and construction, business studies, ICT, hospitality, and applied sciences. Its location in a rural setting makes it a vital resource for students from the surrounding areas, providing them with opportunities to gain skills without needing to relocate to larger urban centers. Kipsinende TVC aims to foster innovation and self-reliance among its graduates.
institutions.short_description: Kipsinende Technical Vocational College is a public TVET institution in Kericho County, Kenya. It provides practical skills training through artisan, certificate, and diploma programs to empower youth for employment and entrepreneurship opportunities.

institutions.name: Kipsoen Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Kipsoen Technical and Vocational College is a public TVET institution located in Iten, Elgeyo-Marakwet County, Kenya. Situated along the Iten-Kapsowar Road, the college plays a crucial role in providing technical and vocational training to the youth in the North Rift region and beyond. Established under the Ministry of Education, Kipsoen TVC is committed to addressing the skills gap in various sectors of the economy by offering hands-on training and practical knowledge designed to meet industry demands.

The college offers a diverse range of diploma, certificate, and artisan courses across disciplines such as engineering, building and construction, information technology, business, hospitality, and applied sciences. Its curriculum is aligned with the Technical and Vocational Education and Training Authority (TVETA) standards, focusing on competency-based education (CBE). Kipsoen TVC aims to empower its graduates with the necessary skills and entrepreneurial mindset to secure employment, pursue further education, or create their own job opportunities, contributing to national development.
institutions.short_description: Kipsoen Technical and Vocational College is a public TVET institution in Iten, Elgeyo-Marakwet County, Kenya, offering diverse diploma, certificate, and artisan programs focused on practical skills and competency-based education to empower youth for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Kiptaragon Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Kiptaragon Technical And Vocational College (TVC) is a public institution situated in Kiptaragon, Molo Constituency, within Nakuru County, Kenya. Established to address the growing need for skilled labor, the college plays a crucial role in providing technical and vocational education and training (TVET) to the youth. Located along the Molo-Kiptaragon Road, the college serves students from Nakuru County and surrounding regions, offering a conducive learning environment for practical skill acquisition. As part of the Ministry of Education's efforts to expand TVET access, Kiptaragon TVC is committed to delivering quality training programs that align with industry demands and national development priorities, fostering innovation and entrepreneurship among its graduates.

The college offers a range of courses across various technical disciplines, designed to equip students with hands-on expertise and theoretical knowledge. These programs are tailored to meet the needs of different sectors, preparing students for direct entry into the workforce or for further education. Kiptaragon TVC is recognized for its focus on practical learning and its contribution to developing a competent and skilled workforce. Through its dedicated faculty and vocational workshops, the institution aims to empower students with the necessary skills to thrive in a competitive job market or establish their own ventures, thereby contributing significantly to the socio-economic growth of the region and the nation.
institutions.short_description: Kiptaragon Technical And Vocational College is a public TVC located in Kiptaragon, Molo Constituency, Nakuru County. It offers diverse technical and vocational training programs aimed at equipping students with practical skills for various industries and self-employment, contributing to national development goals.

institutions.name: Kirinyaga Central Technical& Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Kirinyaga Central Technical and Vocational College (KCTV&C) is a public technical institution located in Kerugoya, Kirinyaga County, Kenya. Established as part of the Kenyan government's initiative to expand access to technical and vocational education and training (TVET), the college plays a crucial role in developing skilled human resources for national growth. It offers a wide range of programs at diploma, certificate, and artisan levels across various disciplines including engineering, building technology, business studies, hospitality, fashion design, and information communication technology, among others.

The institution is situated conveniently near Kerugoya town along the Kerugoya-Kutus road, making it accessible to students from the surrounding areas and beyond. Committed to providing quality hands-on training, Kirinyaga Central TVC aims to equip students with practical skills and knowledge relevant to the current job market and entrepreneurial opportunities. It operates under the Ministry of Education and is registered and regulated by the TVET Authority (TVETA). The college contributes significantly to increasing TVET enrollment and fostering technical expertise in the region, aligning with Kenya's vision for industrialization and economic development.
institutions.short_description: Kirinyaga Central Technical and Vocational College (KCTV&C) is a public TVET institution in Kerugoya, Kirinyaga County, offering diploma, certificate, and artisan programs in various technical and business fields to equip students with practical skills.

institutions.name: Kirinyaga University
institutions.profile:
Kirinyaga University (KYU) is a public chartered university situated in Kutus, Kirinyaga County, Kenya. Its origins trace back to 2011 when it was established as a constituent college of Jomo Kenyatta University of Agriculture and Technology (JKUAT). This foundational phase allowed the institution to develop its academic structures and programs. In October 2016, it was officially granted a charter, elevating its status to a fully-fledged university, enabling it to independently award its degrees and diplomas. Located strategically along the Kutus-Kerugoya road, the university serves the educational needs of Kirinyaga County and the wider central Kenya region.

KYU is dedicated to providing accessible and quality higher education across a diverse range of fields. The university offers programs in key areas such as Engineering, Computing and IT, Health Sciences, Pure and Applied Sciences, Business, and Education. It emphasizes practical skills, research, and innovation, aiming to produce graduates equipped to address societal challenges and contribute to national development. KYU is rapidly growing and strives to foster an environment of academic excellence, integrity, and community engagement, positioning itself as a significant contributor to Kenya's human capital development and research landscape.
institutions.short_description: A dynamic public university in Kirinyaga, established in 2011 and chartered in 2016, committed to quality education, research, and community engagement.

institutions.name: Kiriri Womens University Of Science And Technology
institutions.profile:
Kiriri Women's University of Science and Technology (KWUST) is a pioneering private institution chartered in 2007, having been founded in 1999. It stands out among Kenyan universities by focusing exclusively on empowering women through specialized education in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM), alongside related fields like business and education. Located strategically in Ruiru, Kiambu County, just off the bustling Thika Superhighway, KWUST provides an accessible and conducive environment for learning. Its establishment was driven by the mission to bridge the gender gap in technical fields and produce confident, skilled female professionals ready to contribute to national development.

The university offers a range of degree and diploma programs designed to meet industry demands, encouraging innovation and research among its students. KWUST is recognized for its commitment to academic excellence and the holistic development of its students, fostering leadership qualities and a strong ethical foundation. The campus, situated along Kiriri Road near major landmarks, provides necessary facilities to support rigorous academic pursuits and student life. KWUST holds a unique position in Kenya's higher education landscape, dedicated to nurturing a new generation of female leaders and innovators in critical sectors.
institutions.short_description: Kiriri Women's University of Science and Technology (KWUST) is a private university in Ruiru, Kiambu County, uniquely dedicated to providing quality higher education exclusively for women, particularly in the fields of science, technology, and related disciplines, aiming to empower female leaders and innovators.

other_info.location: Along Thika Superhighway, exit 13, Kiriri Road, Ruiru, Kiambu

institutions.name: Kisii National Polytechnic
institutions.profile:
Kisii National Polytechnic is a leading public Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) institution located in Kisii County, Kenya. Established with a mandate to provide technical, industrial, technological, and entrepreneurial training, the polytechnic plays a crucial role in producing skilled manpower for national development. Situated approximately 5 kilometers from Kisii town along the Kisii-Kilgoris highway, the institution is easily accessible and serves students from across the country. It offers a wide range of programs across diverse fields including engineering, building and civil technology, business studies, applied sciences, health sciences, and information communication technology, among others.

The institution is known for its commitment to quality education and practical training, equipping students with hands-on skills relevant to the current job market. It boasts modern workshops, laboratories, and qualified faculty dedicated to nurturing talent and innovation. As a national polytechnic, it has grown significantly in infrastructure and program offerings since its inception, becoming a preferred destination for students seeking technical and vocational skills. The polytechnic collaborates with various industries and organizations to ensure its curriculum remains relevant and its graduates are well-prepared for employment or entrepreneurship. It also engages in community outreach programs and research activities that contribute to societal development.
institutions.short_description: Kisii National Polytechnic is a premier public TVET institution in Kisii, Kenya, offering a wide array of technical, industrial, technological, and business programs. Located near Kisii town, it focuses on practical skills training for national development.

institutions.name: Kisii University
institutions.profile: Kisii University stands as a beacon of higher learning in Kenya, offering a transformative educational experience that blends academic excellence with community engagement. Established in 1965 and elevated to university status in 2013, it has grown into a dynamic institution committed to fostering knowledge, innovation, and societal development.
institutions.short_description: A public university in Kisii, established in 1965 and chartered in 2013, offering transformative education with a focus on innovation and community engagement.

institutions.name: Kisii University Tvet Institute
institutions.profile:
Kisii University TVET Institute operates as part of the larger Kisii University, a public institution located in Kisii Town, Kisii County, Kenya. The university traces its origins back to 1994 as a campus of Egerton University, later becoming a constituent college in 2007 before being awarded its charter in August 2013. The establishment of the TVET institute aligns with the university's mandate to contribute to national development through providing relevant technical and vocational education and training.

The institute offers a range of practical, hands-on courses designed to equip students with skills needed in various sectors of the economy. As an integrated part of the university, it benefits from the existing infrastructure, resources, and academic environment. Situated along the Kisii-Eldoret Highway, it is accessible to students from the immediate region and beyond, contributing significantly to skills development and employability among the youth in Western Kenya.
institutions.short_description: Kisii University TVET Institute is a public technical and vocational training institution located in Kisii Town, Kisii County, operating under the umbrella of Kisii University. It offers practical skills-based programs aimed at preparing students for the workforce.

institutions.name: Kisiwa Technical Training Institute
institutions.profile:
Kisiwa Technical Training Institute (KISTTI) is a public Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) institution located in Bungoma County, Kenya. Established to provide accessible and quality technical education, the institute operates under the Ministry of Education and is situated conveniently off the busy Webuye-Bungoma highway. KISTTI is committed to equipping trainees with the necessary skills and knowledge to meet the demands of the labor market and contribute effectively to national development through practical, hands-on training and entrepreneurship development.

The institute offers a wide range of programs across various disciplines, including Electrical and Electronic Engineering, Building Technology, ICT, Business Studies, Hospitality, and Agriculture, among others. With modern workshops and laboratories, KISTTI provides a conducive learning environment for trainees pursuing certificate, diploma, and artisan courses. It plays a crucial role in the Western Kenya region by providing relevant TVET programs that foster innovation, self-reliance, and employability, contributing significantly to the socio-economic empowerment of its graduates and the community.
institutions.short_description: Kisiwa Technical Training Institute (KISTTI) is a public TVET college in Bungoma, Kenya, offering diverse certificate, diploma, and artisan programs in engineering, ICT, business, hospitality, and agriculture to equip students with practical skills for employment and national development.

institutions.name: Kisumu Institute Of Community Development Training
institutions.profile:
Kisumu National Polytechnic (KNP) is a prominent public TVET institution located in Kisumu, Kenya.
