Established with the mission to provide quality technical and vocational education and training, KNP plays a crucial role in equipping students with practical skills demanded by various industries. The polytechnic is situated along the Kisumu-Kakamega Road, making it accessible within the urban center of Kisumu Town. Over the years, KNP has grown to offer a diverse range of programs, contributing significantly to the development of skilled manpower in the Western region of Kenya and beyond.

The institution boasts a wide array of departments including Engineering, Business Studies, Information Communication Technology, Hospitality and Tourism, Applied Sciences, and Health Sciences. These departments offer certificate, diploma, and higher diploma courses across various disciplines, catering to a broad spectrum of career interests and national development needs. KNP is recognized for its commitment to academic excellence and practical training, providing students with hands-on experience and fostering innovation. Its reputation is built on producing graduates who are well-prepared for the job market and capable of contributing positively to the economy.
institutions.short_description: A leading public TVET institution in Kisumu, Kisumu National Polytechnic offers a diverse range of technical and vocational programs across engineering, business, ICT, hospitality, and sciences, equipping students with essential practical skills for industry relevance and national development.

institutions.name: Kisumu Polytechnic
institutions.profile:
Kisumu Polytechnic is a prominent public Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) institution located in Kisumu City, Kenya. Established in 1967 as a technical high school, it was later upgraded to a technical college and eventually became a national polytechnic. Situated strategically along the busy Kisumu-Kakamega Highway, the polytechnic serves a large catchment area in Western Kenya and beyond, playing a crucial role in the region's technical and human resource development. It is registered under the TVET Act 2013 and operates under the Ministry of Education.

The institution is renowned for offering a wide array of courses across various disciplines including Engineering, Information Communication Technology, Business Studies, Institutional Management, Liberal Studies, and Health Sciences. Programs are offered at Diploma, Certificate, and Artisan levels, designed to equip students with practical skills and knowledge relevant to the current job market and entrepreneurial ventures. Kisumu Polytechnic boasts modern facilities, including workshops, laboratories, libraries, and lecture halls, supporting its commitment to quality technical education and skills development. Its reputation is built on years of providing hands-on training and contributing significantly to Kenya's skilled workforce.
institutions.short_description: Kisumu Polytechnic is a leading public TVET institution in Kisumu City, Kenya, offering diverse Diploma, Certificate, and Artisan programs in engineering, ICT, business, and more, dedicated to providing practical skills training.

institutions.name: Kitale National Polytechnic
institutions.profile:
Kitale National Polytechnic, located in Kitale, Trans Nzoia County, is a prominent public technical and vocational education and training (TVET) institution in Western Kenya. Established initially as Kitale Technical Training Institute, it was upgraded to a National Polytechnic in 2016, a significant milestone that enhanced its capacity and broadened its mandate in offering higher-level technical courses. Situated along the Kitale-Cherangani Road, the polytechnic serves students from the surrounding counties and across Kenya, playing a crucial role in providing skills training essential for national development.

The polytechnic offers a diverse range of diploma, certificate, and artisan programs across various disciplines, including Engineering (Electrical, Automotive, Civil, Mechanical), Business Studies, Hospitality, ICT, Agriculture, and Applied Sciences. These programs are designed to equip students with practical, hands-on skills demanded by the industry and labor market. Kitale National Polytechnic is recognized for its commitment to quality education, modern facilities, and fostering an environment conducive to technical innovation and entrepreneurship, contributing significantly to the socio-economic growth of the region.
institutions.short_description: Kitale National Polytechnic is a leading public TVET institution in Kitale, Trans Nzoia County, offering diverse diploma, certificate, and artisan programs in engineering, business, hospitality, ICT, and more, focused on practical skills development.

institutions.name: Kitelakapel Technical Training Institute
institutions.profile:
Kitelakapel Technical Training Institute (TTI) is a public tertiary institution situated in Kitelakapel area, Kapenguria Constituency, West Pokot County. Established under the Ministry of Education, it serves the region by providing technical and vocational education and training (TVET) programs. The institute plays a crucial role in equipping students with practical skills essential for employment and entrepreneurship, contributing to the socio-economic development of West Pokot and surrounding areas.

The institute offers a diverse range of courses across various technical and business disciplines, including programs in Information Communication Technology, Electrical and Electronic Engineering, Building Technology, Fashion Design, Business Management, and Accounting. These programs are designed to meet the current demands of the job market and provide students with hands-on experience and theoretical knowledge. Kitelakapel TTI is accessible to students through the Kenya Universities and Colleges Central Placement Service (KUCCPS), reinforcing its commitment to providing quality and accessible technical education to Kenyan youth.
institutions.short_description: Kitelakapel Technical Training Institute is a public TVET institution in Kitelakapel, West Pokot County, Kenya. It offers a range of technical and business programs aimed at imparting practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Kitui East Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Kitui East Technical And Vocational College is a public technical institution located in the Endau area of Kitui East Constituency, Kitui County. As part of the Ministry of Education's initiative to expand access to technical and vocational training across the country, the college serves the local community and surrounding regions by offering relevant post-secondary education opportunities. The institution focuses on equipping students with practical skills and knowledge essential for various trades and industries, contributing to local economic development and empowering youth with employable skills.

The college provides training in a range of disciplines, including technical courses like electrical engineering and building technology, as well as business-related programs. By offering these diverse courses, Kitui East TVC aims to meet the varied career aspirations of its students and address the demand for skilled labor in different sectors. Its establishment in a rural constituency highlights the government's commitment to decentralizing educational resources and making TVET accessible to students who may not have the means or opportunity to attend institutions in larger urban centers.
institutions.short_description: Kitui East Technical And Vocational College is a public TVET institution located in Endau, Kitui East Constituency, Kitui County. It offers a variety of technical and business programs to provide practical skills and enhance employability for students in the region.

institutions.name: Kitui Teachers Technical College
institutions.profile:
Kitui Teachers Technical College (KTTC) is a prominent public Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) institution located in Kitui County, Kenya. Situated along the busy Kitui-Thika Highway near Kitui Town, the college plays a vital role in providing technical and professional skills to students from the region and beyond. Accredited by the Technical and Vocational Education and Training Authority (TVETA), KTTC offers a diverse range of programs designed to meet the demands of various industries and the education sector.

The college is particularly known for its programs in Teacher Education, including Early Childhood Development Education (ECDE) and Primary Teacher Education (P1), preparing qualified teachers for basic education levels. In addition to education courses, KTTC provides training in business studies, information communication technology (ICT), hospitality, and various technical fields. This broad spectrum of courses aims to equip graduates with practical skills and knowledge, enhancing their employability and contribution to national development. The institution is committed to fostering a conducive learning environment and upholding standards of excellence in technical and vocational training.
institutions.short_description: Kitui Teachers Technical College is a public TVET institution in Kitui, Kenya, offering accredited programs in teacher education (ECDE, P1), business, ICT, hospitality, and technical fields. Located near Kitui Town, it trains students for various sectors, focusing on practical skills and professional development.

institutions.name: Kitui Teachers Training College
institutions.profile:
Kitui Teachers Training College (KTTC) is a renowned public institution situated in Kitui Town, Kitui County, Kenya. Established in 1962, it has a long history of contributing to the education sector through the training of qualified teachers. Located strategically along the Kitui-Thika Highway, KTTC operates under the Ministry of Education and is recognized as a key player in teacher education within the country, adapting its curriculum over the decades to meet national educational needs and standards.

KTTC offers various teacher training programs accredited by relevant education bodies, primarily focusing on the Diploma in Primary Teacher Education (DPTE) and Diploma in Early Childhood Development Education (DECDE). These programs are designed to equip prospective teachers with the necessary pedagogical skills, subject knowledge, and professional ethics required for effective teaching in primary schools and early childhood settings. The college provides a conducive learning environment with facilities aimed at supporting comprehensive academic and practical training for its diverse student body.
institutions.short_description: Kitui Teachers Training College is a prominent public institution located in Kitui Town, Kenya, under the Ministry of Education. It specializes in training teachers, offering various programs including Diploma in Primary Teacher Education (DPTE) and Diploma in Early Childhood Development Education (DECDE).

institutions.name: Kitutu Chache Technical & Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Kitutu Chache Technical & Vocational College (KCTVC) is a public technical and vocational training institution situated in Kiogoro Town, Kitutu Chache South Constituency, Kisii County, Kenya. Established to provide accessible and quality technical education, the college plays a vital role in bridging the skills gap in the region and the country. It operates under the Ministry of Education and is recognized by the Kenya Universities and Colleges Central Placement Service (KUCCPS), enrolling students in diverse diploma and certificate programs across various disciplines. KCTVC is dedicated to fostering a learning environment that promotes innovation, practical skills development, and entrepreneurial thinking.

The college offers a range of programs designed to meet the demands of the modern job market, spanning fields such as Building and Civil Engineering, Electrical and Electronic Engineering, Information Communication Technology (ICT), Business Studies, Hospitality and Tourism, and Liberal Studies. With a focus on hands-on training and industry relevance, Kitutu Chache TVC aims to produce competent graduates capable of contributing effectively to the economy. Its location in Kisii County makes it a key educational hub for students from the surrounding areas, providing opportunities for technical and vocational advancement within a supportive institutional framework. The college is committed to upholding high standards of training and contributing positively to community development through skills empowerment.
institutions.short_description: Kitutu Chache Technical & Vocational College is a public TVC in Kiogoro, Kisii County. It offers various technical and business programs, equipping trainees with practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship and contributing to national development.

institutions.name: Kitutu Masaba Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Kitutu Masaba Technical and Vocational College, situated in Ekerenyo, Kitutu Masaba Constituency, Nyamira County, is a government-sponsored institution dedicated to providing accessible and relevant technical and vocational training. Established to meet the growing demand for skilled labour in Kenya, KMTVC operates under the Ministry of Education and plays a crucial role in the region's educational landscape by offering diploma, certificate, and artisan courses across various disciplines, including engineering, building technology, business studies, and information communication technology. The college focuses on hands-on training and practical application, ensuring graduates are well-prepared for the job market or starting their own ventures.

KMTVC is committed to fostering innovation, excellence, and holistic development among its students. The college boasts workshops, laboratories, and classrooms designed to facilitate effective learning. Its curriculum is regularly reviewed to align with industry needs and technological advancements, ensuring the relevance of its programs. Beyond academics, KMTVC actively engages with the local community and industry partners, creating opportunities for student internships, research, and community service. Its reputation is built on its commitment to quality education, student support services, and contributing to national development through skills empowerment.
institutions.short_description: Kitutu Masaba Technical and Vocational College (KMTVC) is a public TVC located in Ekerenyo, Nyamira County, Kenya. It offers a wide range of technical, vocational, and business programs focused on practical skills.

institutions.name: Koitaleel Samoei University College
institutions.profile:
Koitaleel Samoei University College (KSUC) is a public constituent college of the University of Nairobi, located in Mosoriot, Nandi County, Kenya. The establishment of KSUC was a significant development for the region, aimed at expanding access to higher education and promoting socio-economic development in the North Rift area. Initially envisioned for Kaimosi, the college was eventually established on the grounds of the former Mosoriot Teachers Training College, leveraging existing infrastructure to quickly begin operations.

KSUC offers a range of academic programs across various disciplines, including education, business, and sciences, catering to both undergraduate and postgraduate students. As a constituent college of a premier university like the University of Nairobi, KSUC benefits from established academic standards and resources, providing quality education focused on research, innovation, and community engagement. The college is committed to producing graduates who are well-equipped to contribute to national development and address contemporary challenges.
institutions.short_description: Koitaleel Samoei University College (KSUC) is a public constituent college of the University of Nairobi, situated in Mosoriot, Nandi County. It provides diverse academic programs including education, business, and sciences, aiming to expand access to quality higher education in the region.

institutions.name: Kongoni Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Kongoni Technical And Vocational College (KONGONI TVC) is a public TVET institution situated in Kakamega County, Kenya. Established to contribute to national development through skills training, the college is under the purview of the Ministry of Education. It is located along the Kakamega-Mumias Highway, making it accessible to students from the surrounding regions and beyond. The college is committed to providing relevant, industry-driven training programs that equip students with the practical skills and knowledge necessary for employment and entrepreneurship in various technical and vocational fields.

KONGONI TVC offers a diverse range of courses spanning engineering technology, building technology, business studies, computing, and hospitality, among others. The curriculum is designed in line with national TVET standards and aims to foster innovation, critical thinking, and ethical conduct. With a focus on hands-on learning and practical application, the college strives to be a leading center of excellence in technical and vocational training in the Western region of Kenya, contributing significantly to the nation's skilled workforce.
institutions.short_description: Kongoni Technical And Vocational College is a public TVET institution in Kakamega, Kenya, offering a wide range of technical, vocational, and business programs designed to equip students with practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship. Situated along the Kakamega-Mumias Highway, it plays a vital role in regional workforce development.

institutions.name: Konoin Technical Training Institute
institutions.profile:
Konoin Technical Training Institute (KTTI) is a public TVET institution located in Mogogosiek Town, Konoin Constituency, Bomet County, Kenya. Established to provide technical and vocational skills, KTTI plays a crucial role in empowering youth and contributing to national development through practical education and training. It operates under the Ministry of Education and is recognized by regulatory bodies like TVETA and KNEC. The institute is committed to offering market-driven courses that equip students with relevant competencies for employment and entrepreneurship. KTTI strives to be a center of excellence in technical education, fostering innovation, creativity, and ethical values among its trainees.

The institute offers a wide range of programs across various disciplines, including Engineering, Building & Construction, ICT, Business Studies, Agriculture, Health Sciences, and Hospitality. These programs are offered at diploma, certificate, and artisan levels, catering to diverse academic backgrounds and career aspirations. KTTI focuses on hands-on training, leveraging well-equipped workshops and laboratories to ensure students gain practical experience. The institute also emphasizes mentorship, career guidance, and industrial linkages to enhance graduate employability. Its strategic location serves students from Bomet and neighboring counties, making technical education accessible to the region's youth.
institutions.short_description: Konoin Technical Training Institute is a public TVET institution in Bomet County, Kenya, offering diverse technical and vocational programs at diploma, certificate, and artisan levels to equip students with practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship and contribute to national development.

institutions.name: Koshin Technical Training Institute
institutions.profile:
Koshin Technical Training Institute (Koshin TTI) is a prominent public Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) institution situated in Koshin, Turbo Constituency, Uasin Gishu County, Kenya. Established in 2017 under the Ministry of Education, the institute is part of the Kenyan government's initiative to expand access to technical education and skills development across the country. Located near the bustling town of Eldoret, Koshin TTI serves a diverse student population from the surrounding rural and urban areas, providing essential training needed for various industries.

The institute offers a wide array of programs at diploma, certificate, and artisan levels across disciplines such as engineering, building and construction, business, information technology, and hospitality. Koshin TTI is committed to providing quality, hands-on training that aligns with industry needs, preparing graduates for successful careers and entrepreneurial ventures. Its strategic location in the North Rift region positions it as a key institution for driving socio-economic development through skills empowerment.
institutions.short_description: Koshin Technical Training Institute is a public TVET institution located in Koshin, Turbo Constituency, Uasin Gishu County, Kenya. Established in 2017, it offers a range of technical and vocational programs designed to equip students with practical skills for the job market and self-employment.

other_info.address: The PrincipalKoshin Technical Training InstituteP.O. Box 4040 – 30100, EldoretKenya

institutions.name: Kwale Teachers Training College
programs: Diploma in Early Childhood Teacher Education (DECTE)
institutions.profile:
Kwale Teachers Training College (Kwale TTC) is a public institution located near Kwale Town in Kwale County, Kenya. Established in 1982, it has served for decades as a key training ground for primary and early childhood education teachers in the coastal region and beyond. The college operates under the Ministry of Education and is dedicated to producing qualified and competent teachers who can contribute effectively to the education sector in Kenya.

The college offers pre-service and in-service training programs tailored to meet the evolving demands of the Kenyan curriculum. Its core programs include the Diploma in Primary Teacher Education (DPTE) and the Diploma in Early Childhood Teacher Education (DECTE). Located in a serene environment outside Kwale Town, the college provides a conducive atmosphere for learning and professional development, attracting students from various parts of the country.
institutions.short_description: Kwale Teachers Training College is a public institution in Kwale County, Kenya, established in 1982. It specializes in training teachers for primary and early childhood education levels, offering programs like DPTE and DECTE under the Ministry of Education.

institutions.name: Laikipia East Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Laikipia East Technical And Vocational College is a public institution situated in Nanyuki, Laikipia County, within the Laikipia East Constituency of Kenya. As part of the government's initiative to enhance technical and vocational education and training (TVET), the college is dedicated to providing market-driven skills and knowledge to the youth. It plays a crucial role in capacity building, offering hands-on training and technical expertise across various disciplines to prepare students for employment opportunities and entrepreneurial ventures in a rapidly evolving economy.

Located strategically, the college serves students from Laikipia County and beyond, contributing to regional development by producing a skilled workforce. Its programs are designed to align with national development goals, focusing on practical application and industry relevance. Through modern workshops, qualified instructors, and a conducive learning environment, Laikipia East TVC aims to be a leading TVET institution fostering innovation, creativity, and self-reliance among its graduates, thereby addressing the demand for skilled professionals in Kenya.
institutions.short_description: Laikipia East Technical And Vocational College (TVC) is a public TVET institution in Nanyuki, Laikipia County. It focuses on providing practical, market-relevant technical and vocational skills training to empower youth for employment and entrepreneurship opportunities.

institutions.name: Laikipia North Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Laikipia North Technical And Vocational College (TVC) is a public tertiary institution located in Rumuruti town, within Laikipia West Constituency, Laikipia County. Established as part of the Kenyan government's initiative to expand access to technical and vocational training, the college plays a crucial role in providing practical skills and knowledge to young people in the region and beyond. Its strategic location aims to serve the training needs of the semi-arid Laikipia North area, contributing to local economic development and empowering students with employable skills.

The college offers a diverse range of technical, vocational, and business programs designed to meet the demands of various industries. These programs cover disciplines such as Building Technology, Electrical and Electronic Technology, Automotive Technology, Information Communication Technology (ICT), Business Management, Supply Chain Management, and Social Work and Community Development. By focusing on hands-on training and industry-relevant curricula, Laikipia North TVC prepares graduates for the job market or for entrepreneurship. The institution is committed to fostering a conducive learning environment that promotes innovation, creativity, and excellence among its students.
institutions.short_description: Laikipia North Technical And Vocational College is a public TVC located in Rumuruti, Laikipia County. It offers a range of technical, vocational, and business programs to equip students with practical, industry-relevant skills for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Laikipia University
institutions.profile:
Laikipia University is a public institution situated in Nyahururu, Laikipia County, Kenya. Its origins can be traced back to 1965, evolving from a primary school to a teacher training college, and eventually becoming a constituent college of Egerton University in 1990. The university was granted a charter in 2013, marking its elevation to a fully-fledged university. Located approximately 11 kilometers from Nyahururu town along the Nyahururu-Nakuru road, the main campus is set in a serene environment conducive to learning and research. Laikipia University is mandated to provide education, training, and research in various fields, contributing to national development.

The university offers a diverse range of academic programs across disciplines such as Education, Arts and Humanities, Natural Sciences, and Business and Economics, from certificate to doctoral levels. Known for its focus on quality education and research, Laikipia University is committed to community engagement and sustainable development, particularly emphasizing environmental conservation due to its location in a region of ecological significance. It strives to produce graduates equipped with relevant skills and knowledge to address contemporary challenges and contribute positively to society, fostering a reputation as a growing centre of academic excellence in Kenya.
institutions.short_description: Laikipia University, chartered in 2013 and located in Nyahururu, Laikipia County, is a public university that originated from a teacher training college established in 1965, offering diverse programs across various disciplines.

institutions.name: Laikipia University Tvet Institute
institutions.profile:
Laikipia University TVET Institute is a specialized branch operating under the umbrella of Laikipia University, a renowned public institution situated in Kenya. Located in the town of Nyahururu, within Laikipia County, the institute is dedicated to providing high-quality technical and vocational education and training. Its core mission revolves around equipping students with practical, market-relevant skills and knowledge, directly addressing the demand for skilled manpower across various sectors of the economy and contributing significantly to national development goals.

The institute offers a range of programs designed to be hands-on and industry-focused. Being part of Laikipia University, it benefits from access to the university's infrastructure, resources, and academic standards, ensuring a conducive learning environment. Laikipia University TVET Institute is recognized for its commitment to delivering accessible and effective TVET programs, playing a vital role in expanding educational opportunities in the region and preparing graduates for successful careers.
institutions.short_description: Laikipia University TVET Institute, located in Nyahururu, Laikipia County, is a public institution offering hands-on technical and vocational programs. It aims to equip students with practical skills for employment and national development.

institutions.name: Laikipia West Technical & Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Laikipia West Technical & Vocational College (LWTVC) is a public TVET institution located in the Kinamba area of Laikipia West Constituency, Laikipia County, Kenya. Established to meet the growing demand for skilled manpower, the college provides comprehensive technical and vocational training aligned with national development objectives. Operating under the Ministry of Education, LWTVC is recognized by the Technical and Vocational Education and Training Authority (TVETA) and is listed by the Kenya Universities and Colleges Central Placement Service (KUCCPS). The college plays a significant role in equipping youth and adults with practical skills essential for employment, entrepreneurship, and opportunities for further education.

LWTVC offers a diverse array of programs spanning various technical and business fields. These include, but are not limited to, courses in Electrical and Electronic Engineering, Building Technology, Information Communication Technology (ICT), Business Management, Secretarial Studies, and Tourism & Hospitality. The curriculum emphasizes competency-based training, ensuring that graduates are well-prepared to meet the demands of the modern job market and contribute effectively to the economy. The institution is dedicated to delivering quality education, fostering innovation, and actively participating in the socio-economic advancement of the local community and the nation at large.
institutions.short_description: Laikipia West Technical & Vocational College is a public TVET institution situated in Kinamba, Laikipia West, Kenya. It provides a range of technical and business courses focused on developing practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Laisamis Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Laisamis Technical And Vocational College (TVC) is a public technical and vocational training institution located in Laisamis Town, Marsabit County, Kenya. Established under the Ministry of Education, the college plays a crucial role in expanding access to TVET programs in the northern part of Kenya. Its strategic location in Laisamis aims to serve the educational needs of the local community and surrounding areas, providing essential technical and vocational skills in a region historically underserved by higher education institutions. The college is seen as a key driver for local development and empowering the youth with practical skills.

The college offers a diverse range of programs covering various technical and vocational fields, including engineering disciplines, building technology, Information and Communication Technology (ICT), business studies, agriculture, and fashion design. These programs are specifically designed to equip students with hands-on competencies that are relevant to the current job market demands in Kenya and also foster entrepreneurship. By providing quality, practical training, Laisamis TVC endeavors to prepare its graduates for successful careers or for starting their own businesses, thereby contributing significantly to the socio-economic growth and development of Marsabit County and the wider nation.
institutions.short_description: Laisamis Technical And Vocational College is a public TVC in Laisamis Town, Marsabit County, offering diverse technical and business programs. It aims to equip youth with practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Lamu East Techchnical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Lamu East Technical And Vocational College is a public tertiary institution situated in Hindi, Lamu East Constituency, Lamu County. Operating under the Ministry of Education in Kenya, the college is dedicated to providing accessible and quality technical and vocational training programs. Its establishment is part of the government's broader initiative to expand TVET opportunities across the country, aiming to equip young Kenyans with practical skills that are relevant to the job market and can foster self-employment and entrepreneurship.

The college plays a crucial role in addressing the demand for skilled labour within Lamu County and the wider region. By offering diverse courses in technical and business fields, Lamu East TVC contributes significantly to capacity building and socio-economic development. It focuses on providing hands-on training and competency-based education to ensure graduates are well-prepared for the challenges and opportunities in various sectors, supporting local industries and empowering the community.
institutions.short_description: Lamu East Technical And Vocational College is a public TVET institution located in Hindi, Lamu East Constituency, Lamu County, offering vital technical and vocational training under the Ministry of Education to empower local youth with essential practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Langata Technical & Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Langata Technical & Vocational College is a public TVET institution located in the heart of Langata Constituency, Nairobi County. Established under the Ministry of Education, the college plays a crucial role in enhancing access to technical and vocational education and training for young Kenyans. Situated along Kitengela Road, it serves the local community and beyond, providing a conducive environment for learning practical skills essential for national development.

The college offers a diverse range of market-driven courses at diploma, certificate, and artisan levels. These programs span various fields including Engineering Technology, Information Communication Technology, Business and Development Studies, Hospitality and Tourism, Fashion and Beauty, and Electrical and Electronics Technology. The curriculum is designed to be competency-based, aligning with the demands of the modern job market and promoting entrepreneurship among its graduates. Langata TVC is dedicated to empowering youth with the skills needed to secure employment, create businesses, and contribute effectively to the Kenyan economy.
institutions.short_description: Langata Technical & Vocational College is a public TVET institution in Langata, Nairobi, offering a wide range of technical and vocational programs approved by CDACC and KNEC to equip youth with practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Lari Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Lari Technical And Vocational College is a government-sponsored institution of higher learning situated in Kimende area, Lari Constituency, Kiambu County. Established under the Ministry of Education, the college plays a vital role in enhancing technical and vocational skills development within the region and nationally. Its strategic location off the Nairobi-Nakuru highway makes it accessible to students from various parts of the country. The college is dedicated to providing quality, accessible, and relevant technical training that addresses the demands of the modern job market and promotes self-employment opportunities among Kenyan youth.

Lari TVC offers a diverse range of courses across various disciplines including Engineering, ICT, Business Studies, Applied Sciences, and Hospitality. These programs are designed to be hands-on and practical, ensuring graduates are well-prepared for industry employment or entrepreneurship. As a public institution, Lari TVC adheres to national educational standards and is registered by TVETA. It is a recognized institution for KUCCPS placement, attracting students pursuing technical careers. The college aims to foster innovation, creativity, and a strong work ethic among its students, contributing significantly to the socio-economic growth of Kenya through skilled manpower development.
institutions.short_description: Lari Technical And Vocational College (Lari TVC) is a public institution in Kimende, Lari Constituency, Kiambu County, under the Ministry of Education, offering technical, vocational, and entrepreneurial training programs for national development.

institutions.name: Likoni Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Likoni Technical And Vocational College (Likoni TVC) is a public technical and vocational education and training institution situated in Likoni, Mombasa County, Kenya. Established under the Ministry of Education, the college serves the coastal region by providing essential technical and business skills. It is recognized by KUCCPS and plays a crucial role in expanding access to TVET programs in the area, contributing to the government's agenda of skills development and youth empowerment.

The college offers a diverse range of certificate and diploma courses designed to meet the demands of various industries. Key program areas include engineering disciplines like Automotive, Building, and Electrical, as well as Business Studies, ICT, Hospitality, Fashion Design, and Applied Sciences. Likoni TVC focuses on providing practical, hands-on training to equip students with the competencies needed for employment, entrepreneurship, and further education, thus fostering economic growth and self-reliance among its graduates.
institutions.short_description: Likoni Technical And Vocational College is a public TVET institution in Likoni, Mombasa, offering a wide range of technical and business courses. It provides practical skills training to equip students for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Limuru Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Limuru Technical And Vocational College (Limuru TVC) is a prominent public Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) institution situated in Limuru, Kiambu County, Kenya. Established under the Ministry of Education, the college plays a crucial role in providing accessible, high-quality technical and vocational training to Kenyan youth. Located strategically off Limuru - Tigoni Road, Limuru TVC serves students from Kiambu and surrounding counties, contributing significantly to the development of a skilled workforce aligned with national development goals, particularly Vision 2030.

The college is committed to offering competency-based education and training (CBET) across various disciplines, including engineering technology, information and communication technology (ICT), business studies, applied sciences, and hospitality. Limuru TVC provides programs at artisan, certificate, and diploma levels, catering to diverse educational backgrounds and career aspirations. The institution focuses on practical learning, equipping graduates with hands-on skills and entrepreneurial knowledge necessary for both formal employment and self-employment, thereby addressing unemployment challenges and fostering economic growth in the region.
institutions.short_description: Limuru Technical And Vocational College is a public TVET institution located off Limuru - Tigoni Road in Limuru, Kiambu County. It offers a range of technical, business, and ICT programs designed to equip students with practical skills for employment and self-reliance.

institutions.name: Lodwar Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Lodwar Technical And Vocational College (Lodwar TVC) is a prominent public technical and vocational training institution situated in Lodwar town, Turkana County, Kenya. Established under the Ministry of Education, Lodwar TVC plays a crucial role in providing accessible and relevant technical education to the youth in the arid and semi-arid region of Turkana and surrounding areas. Its establishment was a key step in addressing the need for skilled labor and empowering local communities through practical training opportunities that align with regional development priorities. The college is dedicated to equipping students with hands-on skills necessary for both formal employment and self-employment.

The college offers a diverse range of programs across various technical and business disciplines, including engineering trades (electrical, mechanical, automotive, civil), building technology, information communication technology (ICT), applied sciences, business studies, and fashion & design, among others. These courses are designed to meet the demands of the local and national job markets, focusing heavily on practical application and industry-relevant skills. Lodwar TVC is recognized as a vital institution for fostering economic empowerment and reducing unemployment in the region by producing graduates who are competent and ready to contribute to the workforce.
institutions.short_description: Lodwar Technical And Vocational College (Lodwar TVC) is a public TVC in Lodwar, Turkana County, Kenya, offering technical and vocational training programs to equip students with practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Loima Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Loima Technical And Vocational College is a public technical and vocational training institution situated in Loima, Turkana County, Kenya. Located strategically along the Kitale-Lodwar Highway, the college serves the community of Loima Sub-County and the wider Turkana region. As part of the Ministry of Education's initiative to expand TVET access across the country, Loima TVC was established to provide essential skills training and empower local youth for economic participation. Its establishment reflects the government's commitment to promoting technical skills development as a pathway to employment and entrepreneurship in semi-arid areas like Turkana.

The college offers a variety of technical and vocational programs aligned with industry needs, covering disciplines designed to equip students with practical, hands-on competencies. These programs are crucial for addressing local skill gaps and contributing to regional development. By focusing on market-driven courses, Loima TVC aims to produce graduates who are readily employable or capable of starting their own businesses, thereby enhancing the socio-economic well-being of the community. Its presence provides accessible post-secondary training opportunities for students in Turkana County.
institutions.short_description: Loima Technical And Vocational College is a public TVET institution located in Loima, Turkana County. It offers essential technical and vocational training programs aimed at equipping students with practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship, contributing to regional development.

other_info.location: Loima along Kitale-Lodwar Highway, Loima Sub-County

institutions.name: Lugari Diploma Teachers Training College
programs: Diploma in Early Childhood Development Education (DECE)
institutions.profile:
Lugari Diploma Teachers Training College is a public educational institution situated in Lugari, Kakamega County, Kenya. Established under the Ministry of Education, the college plays a crucial role in training competent primary school teachers for the country's education system. It is one of the many government-run TTCs mandated to implement the new curriculum teacher training programs, focusing on equipping prospective educators with the necessary pedagogical skills, subject content knowledge, and professional ethics required for teaching in primary schools.

The college primarily offers the Diploma in Primary Teacher Education (DPTE) and Diploma in Early Childhood Development Education (DECE) programs. These programs are designed to meet the evolving demands of the Competency-Based Curriculum (CBC) in Kenya. Admissions are primarily facilitated through the Kenya Universities and Colleges Central Placement Service (KUCCPS). Located in a semi-rural setting, Lugari TTC provides a conducive learning environment for students, contributing significantly to the local community and the national effort in developing a qualified teaching workforce.
institutions.short_description: Lugari Diploma Teachers Training College is a public college in Lugari, Kakamega County, Kenya, focused on training primary school teachers. It offers Diploma in Primary Teacher Education and Early Childhood Development Education programs through KUCCPS.

institutions.name: Lukenya University
institutions.profile:
Lukenya University is a private chartered university located near Makutano junction along the Machakos-Kitui road in Kalulu, Kathiani Constituency, Machakos County, Kenya. Founded by Prof. Arthur Mutambuki, the institution officially commenced operations and received its Charter in 2016, signifying its full accreditation and recognition as a degree-awarding institution by the Commission for University Education (CUE). Situated on expansive grounds, the university boasts a serene and conducive environment for learning, research, and innovation, strategically leveraging its rural setting to foster programs related to agriculture and environmental sustainability alongside traditional disciplines.

The university offers a wide range of academic programs across various faculties and schools, including Business, Education, Social Sciences, Health Sciences, Pure & Applied Sciences, and Agriculture. These programs span certificate, diploma, undergraduate, and postgraduate levels (Masters and PhD). Lukenya University places a strong emphasis on research, technology, and entrepreneurship, aiming to produce graduates who are not only academically proficient but also equipped to contribute meaningfully to societal development and economic growth. Its curriculum and research focus are often tailored to address regional needs, particularly in areas like dryland agriculture and sustainable resource management, building a reputation as a growing center for applied research and community engagement in the region.
institutions.short_description: Lukenya University is a private chartered university located in Kalulu, Machakos County, offering a diverse range of academic programs from certificate to PhD levels. Established in 2016, it focuses on research, innovation, and technology in a serene rural environment.

other_info.location: Kalulu, Makutano, Along Machakos - Kitui Road

institutions.name: Lunga Lunga Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Lunga Lunga Technical And Vocational College (TVC) is a public institution situated in Lunga Lunga town, Kwale County, Kenya. Commissioned in 2019, it was established to address the critical need for technical and vocational skills development among the youth in the South Coast region. The college operates under the Ministry of Education and is dedicated to providing accessible and quality technical education that empowers students with practical skills relevant to the current job market and entrepreneurial ventures.

The college offers a diverse range of programs across various departments, including Engineering (Building, Civil, Electrical, Electronics), Information Communication Technology (ICT), Business Studies, Hospitality & Tourism, and Liberal Studies. These courses are designed to equip graduates with hands-on expertise, making them competitive in various sectors. Lunga Lunga TVC plays a vital role in contributing to local economic development by training a skilled workforce and promoting technical proficiency in the region.
institutions.short_description: Lunga Lunga Technical And Vocational College is a public TVC in Lunga Lunga, Kwale County, commissioned in 2019. It offers diverse technical and vocational programs aimed at empowering youth with practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship in various fields.

institutions.name: Maasai Mara Technical Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Maasai Mara Technical Vocational College (MMTVC) is a public tertiary institution situated in Narok Town, Narok County, Kenya. Established to provide accessible and quality technical and vocational education and training (TVET) to the youth in the region and beyond, the college plays a crucial role in developing skills essential for industrial growth and self-employment. It operates under the Ministry of Education and is a registered institution, recognized for its commitment to practical, hands-on training.

The college offers a diverse range of programs across various disciplines, including engineering, building technology, information technology, business, and hospitality. MMTVC aims to equip students with the necessary competencies and entrepreneurial skills to meet the demands of the job market and contribute positively to the economy. Its location in Narok serves the educational needs of a largely agricultural and tourism-dependent region, providing opportunities for local residents to acquire technical expertise relevant to the area's economic activities.
institutions.short_description: Maasai Mara Technical Vocational College is a public TVET institution in Narok Town, offering diverse technical, vocational, and business programs to equip students with practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Maasai Mara University
institutions.profile:
Maasai Mara University (MMU) is a public institution of higher learning located approximately 3 kilometers from Narok Town along the Narok-Bomet highway in Narok County, Kenya. Established in 2008 as a constituent college of Egerton University, it was granted its Charter in 2013, officially becoming a fully-fledged university. Situated near the world-famous Maasai Mara National Reserve, the university leverages its unique location, integrating environmental conservation and tourism studies into its academic framework alongside a broad range of other disciplines. MMU is committed to providing quality education, fostering research, and engaging in community service, playing a significant role in regional development.

MMU offers a diverse portfolio of academic programs through its various schools, including Science, Health Sciences, Education, Business and Economics, Arts and Social Sciences, and Tourism and Natural Resources. The university emphasizes innovative teaching methods, research projects addressing local and national challenges, and initiatives aimed at community empowerment. With a vision to be a leading university in fostering teaching, learning, research, innovation, and community service, Maasai Mara University strives to produce graduates equipped with the knowledge, skills, and values necessary to contribute to sustainable national and global development, while upholding core values like integrity, professionalism, and environmental conservation.
institutions.short_description: Maasai Mara University is a public university in Narok, Kenya, established in 2013. Located near the Maasai Mara Reserve, it offers diverse programs including science, business, education, health sciences, and tourism, focusing on research and community service for sustainable development.

other_info.location: Along Narok-Bomet Highway, 3km from Narok Town

institutions.name: Maasai Mara University Tvet Institute
institutions.profile:
Maasai Mara University was chartered in 2013, evolving from a university college established in 2006. Located in Narok Town, Narok County, it serves the Maasai community and the wider region. The university focuses on various fields including applied sciences, education, business, and social sciences. The TVET Institute is a crucial component, established under the Directorate of TVET within the university structure. Its role is to offer technical and vocational training programs, aligning with Kenya's vision for skills development and middle-level manpower.

The TVET institute provides practical, hands-on training aimed at equipping students with marketable skills for employment and entrepreneurship. It leverages the university's resources and infrastructure while maintaining a distinct focus on technical education. The establishment of the TVET directorate reflects the university's commitment to providing diverse educational pathways, catering to students who seek vocational skills rather than traditional university degrees.
institutions.short_description: Maasai Mara University TVET Institute, part of Maasai Mara University in Narok, offers diverse technical and vocational education and training programs focused on practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship, contributing to Kenya's middle-level workforce development.

institutions.name: Mabera Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Mabera Technical And Vocational College is a public technical institution located in Mabera, Kuria West Constituency, Migori County, Kenya. Established to address the growing demand for technical and vocational skills in the region, the college plays a crucial role in providing accessible and affordable training opportunities to the youth and local community. It is part of the Kenyan government's initiative to expand TVET institutions across the country, aiming to equip learners with practical, industry-relevant competencies essential for employment and entrepreneurship in various sectors.

The college offers a diverse range of programs under various departments, including Information Communication Technology, Building & Civil Engineering, Electrical & Electronics Engineering, Business Studies, and Health Sciences, among others. These courses are designed to meet the standards set by regulatory bodies like TVETA and KNEC, leading to qualifications such as National Certificates, Diplomas, and Craft Certificates. By focusing on hands-on training and theoretical knowledge, Mabera TVC contributes significantly to human capital development and supports the economic growth of Migori County and surrounding areas, fostering self-reliance and innovation among its graduates.
institutions.short_description: Mabera Technical And Vocational College is a public TVET institution in Mabera, Kuria West, Migori County, Kenya. It provides practical, industry-relevant training in diverse fields like ICT, Engineering, Business, and Health Sciences to empower youth for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Machakos Teachers Training College
institutions.profile:
Machakos Teachers Training College (MATTC) is a prominent public institution located in Machakos Town, Machakos County, Kenya. Established with the core mandate of producing highly qualified and competent teachers, the college plays a crucial role in Kenya's education sector. It is one of the well-regarded public teacher training colleges under the Ministry of Education, known for its structured programs and contribution to shaping the teaching profession in the country. The college campus is situated along Kangundo Road and provides a conducive environment for learning and professional development.

MATTC offers various diploma programs aligned with the current educational curriculum and needs in Kenya, including the Diploma in Primary Teacher Education (DPTE), Diploma in Secondary Teacher Education (DSTE), Diploma in Special Needs Education (SNE), and Early Childhood Development Education (ECDE). These programs are designed to equip prospective teachers with pedagogical skills, subject knowledge, and professional ethics required for effective teaching practice at different levels of education. The college attracts students from across the nation and maintains a reputation for academic rigor and commitment to producing educators who can contribute positively to the national education system.
institutions.short_description: Machakos Teachers Training College (MATTC) is a leading public institution in Machakos, Kenya, dedicated to training competent teachers for primary, secondary, ECDE, and special needs education, serving the national education sector.

institutions.name: Machakos Technical Institute For The Blind
institutions.profile:
Machakos Technical Institute For The Blind (MTIB) is a unique public technical and vocational education and training (TVET) institution in Kenya, established in 1989. It operates under the Ministry of Education and is dedicated to providing specialized technical training for visually impaired individuals. Located in Machakos Town, Machakos County, along the Machakos-Wote Road near Machakos Prison, the institute plays a crucial role in empowering its students with practical skills necessary for gainful employment and independent living. It is recognized nationally for its focused approach to inclusive education in the TVET sector.

MTIB offers a diverse range of programs tailored to the needs and capabilities of visually impaired learners, including courses in ICT, Business Studies, Electrical and Electronic Technology, Media Studies, and Rehabilitation Studies. These programs are designed to equip students with marketable skills, fostering self-reliance and integration into society. The institute's curriculum and training methodologies are adapted to support students with visual impairments, utilizing assistive technologies and specialized teaching techniques. MTIB is committed to providing a supportive learning environment that enables students to overcome challenges and achieve their full potential, contributing significantly to the accessibility of technical education for persons with disabilities in Kenya.
institutions.short_description: Machakos Technical Institute For The Blind is a pioneering public TVET institution in Kenya dedicated to providing specialized technical and vocational training for visually impaired students. Located in Machakos Town, it offers diverse programs like ICT, Business Studies, and technical courses, empowering learners with skills for employment and independence in a supportive environment.

other_info.address: Along Machakos-Wote Road near Machakos Prison, P.O. Box 530-90100, Machakos

institutions.name: Machakos Town Technical & Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Machakos Town Technical & Vocational College (MTTVC) is a public technical and vocational training institution located in Machakos Town, Machakos County, Kenya. Established by the Government of Kenya, the college operates under the Ministry of Education and is a key component in the nation's strategy to enhance TVET education. Situated along the Machakos-Kitui Road, it serves students from the surrounding region and beyond, providing accessible technical training opportunities. The college is committed to offering market-driven courses designed to equip learners with practical skills and knowledge essential for employment and entrepreneurship in various sectors.

MTTVC offers a diverse range of programs across different disciplines including Engineering, Business Studies, Information Communication Technology (ICT), Building Technology, and Applied Sciences. The institution emphasizes hands-on training and industry collaboration to ensure graduates are competent and ready for the workforce. As a recognized TVET center, it plays a significant role in contributing to Kenya's economic development by producing skilled personnel capable of driving innovation and productivity. The college's infrastructure includes workshops, laboratories, and classrooms tailored to support technical and vocational learning, aiming to foster a conducive environment for academic and practical excellence.
institutions.short_description: Machakos Town Technical & Vocational College is a public TVET institution located in Machakos Town, Machakos County. It offers diverse technical, vocational, and business programs, focusing on providing practical, market-relevant skills to prepare students for employment and entrepreneurship in Kenya.

other_info.address: Along Machakos-Kitui Road, Opposite Kenya Orchards Limited

institutions.name: Machakos University
institutions.profile:
Machakos University is a distinguished public institution of higher learning located in Machakos County, Kenya. It was formally accredited as a full-fledged university on October 5, 2017, having previously operated as a constituent college of the University of Nairobi from 2011. Situated along the Machakos-Wote road, the university plays a pivotal role in expanding access to quality university education in the lower eastern region and beyond.

Committed to academic excellence, research, and innovation, Machakos University offers a wide array of programs across various disciplines including Science, Technology, Engineering, Arts, Business, and Education. The university is dedicated to producing skilled graduates equipped to contribute meaningfully to national development. Its strategic location provides a conducive learning environment while fostering community engagement and research initiatives relevant to regional needs.
institutions.short_description: Machakos University is a public university in Machakos, Kenya, offering diverse programs in Science, Arts, Business, and Education, known for its commitment to academic excellence, research, and community engagement.

other_info.location: Along Machakos-Wote road, next to Machakos Level 5 Hospital

institutions.name: Machakos University Tvet Institute
institutions.profile:
Machakos University TVET Institute stands as a public technical and vocational education and training institution located in Machakos Town, Machakos County. It holds significant historical importance as the precursor to the current Machakos University, having originated as Machakos Technical Training Institute before being elevated to a university college and subsequently a fully-fledged university. As part of Machakos University, the institute benefits from the university's infrastructure and academic environment while maintaining a focus on practical, skills-based training aligned with national development goals. It operates under the Ministry of Education's University TVET category and is regulated by the Technical and Vocational Education and Training Authority (TVETA).

The institute is dedicated to equipping students with hands-on expertise and theoretical knowledge in various technical and vocational disciplines. It plays a crucial role in bridging the gap between academic learning and industry demands by offering programs designed to produce competent and readily employable graduates. Situated conveniently within or adjacent to the main university campus along Machakos-Wote Road, it is accessible and serves students from Machakos County and beyond. Its reputation is tied to the larger university's standing, known for contributing to Kenya's human resource development through quality education and training.
institutions.short_description: Machakos University TVET Institute is a public technical and vocational training institution in Machakos Town, Machakos County. Historically the root of Machakos University, it offers practical, skills-based programs under the Ministry of Education, focusing on equipping students for various technical professions.

institutions.name: Mama Ngina University College
institutions.profile:
Mama Ngina University College (MNUC), located in Gatundu South, Kiambu County, is a public constituent college of Kenyatta University. Established with the aim of expanding access to higher education in the region, MNUC began its operations and has since grown, attracting students from various parts of the country. The college is committed to providing quality education and fostering research and innovation, contributing to national development. It was officially opened in 2022, marking a significant milestone in its development.

MNUC offers a range of academic programs across various disciplines, including Education, Science, and Humanities, designed to meet the needs of the job market and societal challenges. The institution is situated on a spacious campus, providing a conducive learning environment with modern facilities. As a constituent college of Kenyatta University, it benefits from the established academic standards and resources of its parent institution while developing its own unique identity and academic strengths. The college aims to be a leading center of academic excellence and research in Kenya and the East African region.
institutions.short_description: Mama Ngina University College is a public constituent college of Kenyatta University located in Gatundu South, Kiambu County. It offers diverse academic programs across various fields and is committed to providing quality education and fostering research.

institutions.name: Management University Of Africa
programs: Bachelor of Procurement and Supplies Management
institutions.profile:
The Management University of Africa (MUA) is a premier private chartered university in Kenya, renowned for its specialization in Management, Leadership, Governance, and Entrepreneurship. Established in 1997 as the Kenya Institute of Management (KIM) School of Management, it was granted university accreditation in 2005 and received its charter in 2011. Located in Nairobi, MUA is dedicated to producing graduates who are not only academically proficient but also possess a strong entrepreneurial spirit, ready to contribute effectively to the global business landscape. The university's curriculum is designed in collaboration with industry experts to ensure relevance and practical applicability.

MUA offers a wide range of programs from certificate and diploma levels to undergraduate and postgraduate degrees, including popular programs like the Master of Business Administration (MBA) and Bachelor of Commerce (BCom), alongside specialized degrees in areas like Human Resource Management, Finance, and Marketing. The university is situated in South C, Nairobi, providing accessible learning opportunities. With a focus on research, innovation, and community engagement, MUA has established itself as a significant institution in the field of business and management education in East Africa, known for its quality teaching and development of skilled professionals.
institutions.short_description: The Management University of Africa (MUA) is a private chartered university in Nairobi, Kenya, specializing in Management, Leadership, Governance, and Entrepreneurship. It offers various programs from certificates to postgraduate degrees, focusing on developing industry-ready professionals with an entrepreneurial mindset.

other_info.address: MUA Towers, Popo Road, South C, Nairobi

institutions.name: Mandera Teachers Training College
programs: Diploma in Early Childhood Development Education (DECDE)
institutions.profile:
Mandera Teachers Training College (MTTC) is a public teacher training institution located in Mandera County, North Eastern Kenya. Established to address the critical need for trained teachers in the arid and semi-arid lands (ASALs) region, the college plays a vital role in the development of education in the area. It is managed by the Ministry of Education and is registered and accredited by the relevant government bodies to offer teacher training programs.

The college is dedicated to providing quality education and training to aspiring teachers, equipping them with the necessary pedagogical skills, knowledge, and professional ethics required to teach effectively in primary schools and early childhood development centres. MTTC offers key programs such as the Diploma in Primary Teacher Education (DPTE) and the Diploma in Early Childhood Development Education (DECDE). Situated in Mandera town, the college serves students predominantly from the local community and surrounding areas, contributing significantly to local human resource capacity building and promoting educational standards in a region facing unique challenges.
institutions.short_description: Mandera Teachers Training College is a public institution in Mandera, Kenya, dedicated to training qualified teachers for primary and early childhood education, serving the educational needs of the North Eastern region.

institutions.name: Mandera Technical Training Institute
institutions.profile:
Mandera Technical Training Institute (MTTI) is a public technical and vocational training institution located in Mandera Town, Mandera County, Kenya. Established with the aim of providing accessible and relevant technical education, the institute plays a crucial role in empowering youth and residents of the arid and semi-arid region with practical skills. It operates under the Ministry of Education and is registered and regulated by TVETA (Technical and Vocational Education and Training Authority), ensuring adherence to national standards for TVET provision.

MTTI offers a diverse range of courses across various disciplines, including engineering, business, ICT, and hospitality. These programs are designed to meet the demands of the local and national job markets, equipping students with skills for employment or entrepreneurship. The institute is committed to fostering a conducive learning environment and contributing to the socio-economic development of the region by producing competent and skilled graduates. Its focus on providing quality TVET is vital for capacity building and improving livelihoods in Northern Kenya.
institutions.short_description: Mandera Technical Training Institute is a public TVET institution located in Mandera Town, Kenya. It offers a variety of technical, business, and ICT programs aimed at equipping students with practical skills for employment and self-reliance in the region.

institutions.name: Manyatta Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Manyatta Technical And Vocational College (Manyatta TVC) is a public technical institution situated in Manyatta Constituency, Embu County, Kenya. Established under the Ministry of Education, Science and Technology, the college plays a crucial role in enhancing technical and vocational education and training (TVET) in the region. It is strategically located to serve students from Embu and surrounding counties, providing accessible and affordable technical skills vital for national development and individual empowerment. The college is committed to producing competent graduates equipped with practical skills and knowledge relevant to the current industry demands, contributing significantly to the country's workforce.

Manyatta TVC offers a diverse range of diploma, certificate, and artisan courses across various disciplines, including engineering, business, ICT, and hospitality. The curriculum is designed to blend theoretical knowledge with hands-on practical training, facilitated by qualified instructors and workshops. The institution aims to foster innovation, entrepreneurship, and self-reliance among its students, preparing them for employment or self-employment opportunities. Manyatta TVC is registered by the Technical and Vocational Education and Training Authority (TVETA) and participates in KUCCPS selection, making it a recognized institution for post-secondary technical education in Kenya.
institutions.short_description: Manyatta Technical And Vocational College is a public TVET institution in Manyatta, Embu County, offering diverse diploma, certificate, and artisan programs in engineering, business, ICT, and hospitality to equip students with practical skills.

institutions.name: Marist International University College
institutions.profile:
Marist International University College (MIUC) is a premier private university college located in Nairobi, Kenya. Founded by the Marist Brothers, a Catholic religious congregation, MIUC is rooted in a rich tradition of education spanning over 200 years worldwide. The institution was established in Kenya to provide holistic and value-based education aligned with Christian principles and the Marist charism of service and excellence. Situated in the serene Karen area of Nairobi, MIUC provides a conducive learning environment that fosters academic growth, personal development, and spiritual formation.

MIUC offers a diverse range of programs across various disciplines, including Education, Business, Information Technology, and Social Sciences. These programs are designed to equip students with the knowledge, skills, and ethical values necessary to become competent professionals and transformative leaders in their respective fields. The university college is known for its commitment to quality teaching, relevant research, and community engagement. Its location in Karen, a vibrant suburb of Nairobi, allows students access to urban amenities while providing a tranquil setting for study and reflection. MIUC maintains a reputation for producing graduates who are not only academically proficient but also socially responsible and ethically grounded, contributing positively to the development of Kenya and beyond.
institutions.short_description: Marist International University College (MIUC) is a private higher education institution in Nairobi, Kenya, offering holistic, value-based programs rooted in the Marist tradition to develop competent professionals and transformative leaders across various disciplines.

other_info.address: Marist International University College, Marist Road, Karen, Nairobi

institutions.name: Masai Technical Training Institute
institutions.profile:
Masai Technical Training Institute (MTTI) is a public technical training institute located in Kajiado Town, Kajiado County, Kenya. Established to provide technical and vocational education and training (TVET), MTTI plays a crucial role in equipping students with practical skills demanded by various industries. The institution is recognized for its commitment to offering quality education that aligns with national development goals, focusing on producing skilled manpower capable of contributing to the economy. As a government institution under the Ministry of Education, MTTI adheres to national education standards and frameworks.

MTTI offers a diverse range of technical courses across various disciplines, including engineering, building technology, business studies, hospitality, and information communication technology. These programs are designed to cater to different academic levels, offering certificates and diplomas. The institute emphasizes hands-on training, providing students with opportunities to gain real-world experience through workshops, laboratories, and potential industrial attachments. With a focus on practical learning and a location within the rapidly developing Kajiado region, Masai Technical Training Institute serves as a vital educational hub for local and national students seeking technical and vocational skills for career advancement and entrepreneurship.
institutions.short_description: Masai Technical Training Institute (MTTI) is a public TVET institution in Kajiado, offering diverse certificate and diploma programs in technical, vocational, and business fields. It focuses on practical skills for industry relevance.

institutions.name: Maseno University
programs: Bachelor of Science in Computer Science
Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery
institutions.profile:
Maseno University holds a significant place in Kenya's higher education landscape, having been established as a constituent college of Moi University in 1990 and gaining full university charter status in 2001. It is strategically situated in Maseno town, approximately 25 kilometers west of Kisumu city, along the busy Kisumu-Busia highway. The university operates through several campuses, including the main campus in Maseno, a vibrant city campus in Kisumu, and a campus in Homa Bay, extending its reach and accessibility. Maseno University distinguished itself early on by being the first in East Africa to offer a degree program in Computer Science, reflecting its pioneering spirit in technology and innovation.

The institution is renowned for its strong academic programs across a wide array of disciplines, including Education, Pure and Applied Sciences, Computing and Informatics, Health Sciences, Arts and Social Sciences, Business and Economics, and Environment and Earth Sciences. Maseno University is committed to academic excellence, research, and community service, fostering an environment that encourages critical thinking, innovation, and social responsibility among its students and staff. With a reputation for producing skilled graduates and contributing significantly to research and development, Maseno University remains a vital public institution attracting students from across Kenya and the East African region.
institutions.short_description: Maseno University is a leading public university in Kenya, located in Maseno town near Kisumu. Established in 2001, it is recognized for its pioneering role in Computer Science education in East Africa, strong research focus, and diverse programs across various faculties.

institutions.name: Masinde Muliro University Of Science & Technology
institutions.profile:
Masinde Muliro University of Science and Technology (MMUST) is a public university located in Kakamega Town, Kakamega County, Kenya. It was established as a constituent college of Moi University in 2002 and was chartered as a full-fledged university on December 1, 2007. MMUST is the first university in Western Kenya, mandated to provide academic programs and research focused on science, technology, and innovation. Situated approximately 3 kilometers from Kakamega town centre along the Kakamega-Webuye Highway, the university serves students from the surrounding region and beyond, offering a wide range of courses across various faculties and schools, including Science, Engineering, Education, Health Sciences, and Business.

MMUST plays a crucial role in the socio-economic development of the Western Kenya region through its research activities, community engagement programs, and by producing graduates equipped with the skills required for the job market, particularly in technical fields. Over the years, the university has grown in size and reputation, attracting students and faculty interested in its specialized programs and research opportunities. It continues to expand its infrastructure and academic offerings to meet the evolving educational needs of Kenya and contribute to national development goals through science and technology.
institutions.short_description: Masinde Muliro University of Science & Technology (MMUST) is a public university in Kakamega, Kenya, chartered in 2007. Specializing in science, technology, and innovation, it offers diverse programs and plays a vital role in regional development through education and research.

institutions.name: Masinde Muliro University Of Science And Technology Tvet Institute
institutions.profile:
Masinde Muliro University of Science and Technology (MMUST) TVET Institute operates under the umbrella of the renowned public university, MMUST, located in Kakamega, Kenya. Established to complement the university's academic offerings, the TVET Institute focuses specifically on providing competency-based technical and vocational education and training.
