Situated within or adjacent to the main university campus along Kakamega-Webuye Road, it benefits from shared resources, infrastructure, and the academic environment of a leading science and technology institution, aiming to produce skilled graduates ready for the job market.

The institute offers diverse programs designed to equip students with practical skills and knowledge relevant to various industries. These programs often involve hands-on training, workshops, and practical attachments, aligning with the national agenda to promote technical education as a pathway to employment and entrepreneurship. By operating within a university setting, the MMUST TVET Institute provides a unique blend of academic rigor and vocational focus, contributing significantly to the development of a technically proficient workforce in the region and Kenya at large. Its strong link to the main university enhances its reputation and resource base.
institutions.short_description: Masinde Muliro University of Science and Technology (MMUST) TVET Institute provides technical and vocational education and training, leveraging the resources of the main university to offer practical skills programs for career readiness in various technical fields.

institutions.name: Masinga Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Masinga Technical and Vocational College (MTVC) is a public TVET institution situated in Kithyoko Market, Masinga Sub-County, within Machakos County. Established to address the growing demand for skilled personnel, the college is dedicated to providing accessible and quality technical and vocational training. It aims to equip students with practical, hands-on skills that are directly relevant to the current job market and also foster an entrepreneurial spirit to encourage self-employment. The college offers a diverse range of certificate and diploma programs across various disciplines including Business Studies, Information Communication Technology (ICT), Engineering, Building and Civil Engineering, Electrical and Electronics Engineering, Motor Vehicle Technology, and Hospitality. It primarily serves students from Machakos County and the surrounding regions, contributing significantly to the development of a skilled workforce in the area.

As a registered and regulated institution under the Technical and Vocational Education and Training Authority (TVETA) and operating under the Ministry of Education, MTVC plays a crucial role in supporting the Kenyan government's agenda of promoting TVET education as a pathway to economic development and youth empowerment. Strategically located along the Masinga-Thika road, the college is easily accessible to students and staff. Since its establishment, Masinga Technical and Vocational College has continuously worked towards enhancing its infrastructure, expanding its program offerings, and improving the quality of training provided. The institution is committed to producing competent graduates who can contribute effectively to national development and compete successfully in the dynamic global economy.
institutions.short_description: Masinga Technical And Vocational College (MTVC) is a public TVET college in Masinga, Machakos County, offering diverse certificate and diploma programs in technical and business fields focused on practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Mathenge Technical Training Institute
institutions.profile:
Mathenge Technical Training Institute (MTTI) is a public Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) institution situated in Kiganjo town, Kieni Constituency, Nyeri County, Kenya. Established through a community-driven Harambee initiative in 1986 and later upgraded, MTTI plays a crucial role in the provision of technical skills training in the region. It operates under the Ministry of Education, offering courses accredited by relevant TVET bodies and facilitating student admissions through the Kenya Universities and Colleges Central Placement Service (KUCCPS).

The institute is dedicated to equipping learners with practical, hands-on skills across a diverse range of disciplines, including engineering, building technology, business studies, hospitality, and information communication technology. Its programs are designed to meet the demands of the labor market and promote self-employment opportunities among graduates. Mathenge TTI is recognized for its contribution to technical education in Nyeri and the surrounding areas, providing accessible and relevant training pathways for Kenyan youth.
institutions.short_description: Mathenge Technical Training Institute (TTI) is a public TVET institution located in Kiganjo, Nyeri County. Established in 1986, it offers diverse technical, vocational, and business programs aimed at equipping students with practical skills for various industries and self-employment, contributing significantly to the local economy.

institutions.name: Mathioya Technical Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Mathioya Technical Vocational College (MTVC) is a public institution located in Mathioya Constituency, Murang'a County, Kenya. Established under the Ministry of Education, Science, and Technology, the college plays a crucial role in providing accessible technical and vocational education and training (TVET) to students in the region and beyond. Its mission is to equip trainees with essential practical skills and knowledge that are relevant to the job market and support entrepreneurship. The college is situated along the Kinyona-Handege Road, providing a conducive learning environment away from urban distractions.

MTVC offers a diverse range of programs across various technical fields, including engineering, business, ICT, and hospitality. These courses are designed with a strong emphasis on hands-on training and practical application, preparing graduates for immediate employment or self-employment opportunities. The institution is committed to fostering innovation and skill development, contributing significantly to the national agenda of developing a technically competent workforce. Its reputation is built on its dedication to quality training and its contribution to improving the livelihoods of its graduates and the community.
institutions.short_description: Mathioya Technical Vocational College is a public TVET institution in Murang'a County, Kenya, offering diverse practical courses in engineering, business, and ICT. It focuses on hands-on training to equip students with job-ready skills for employment and entrepreneurship, contributing to national development.

institutions.name: Mathira Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Mathira Technical And Vocational College (Mathira TVC) is a public TVET institution situated in the serene environment of Kagochi area, Mathira Constituency, Nyeri County, Kenya. Established in 2015 and fully registered in 2016, the college falls under the Ministry of Education and is mandated to provide accessible and quality technical and vocational training. Its location in a rural setting makes it a vital institution for skill development and empowerment within the local community and the broader Central Kenya region.

The college is dedicated to equipping students with market-relevant skills through a diverse range of programs offered across various departments, including Engineering, ICT, Business, Building & Civil Engineering, Hospitality, and Health Sciences. Mathira TVC emphasizes practical learning and aims to produce competent graduates ready to contribute to national development. The institution is accredited by TVETA and its programs are available for selection through the Kenya Universities and Colleges Central Placement Service (KUCCPS), solidifying its reputation as a growing and impactful player in Kenya's TVET sector.
institutions.short_description: Mathira Technical And Vocational College (Mathira TVC) is a public TVET institution in Kagochi, Karatina, Nyeri County, offering a wide array of technical and vocational programs aimed at equipping students with practical, market-ready skills for national development.

institutions.name: Matili Technical Training Institute
institutions.profile:
Matili Technical Training Institute (TTI) is a public technical and vocational education and training (TVET) institution located in Kimilili Constituency, Bungoma County, Kenya. Established to provide accessible and quality technical skills, Matili TTI aims to equip students with practical knowledge and competencies essential for employment and entrepreneurship in various sectors. The institute is registered and regulated by the Technical and Vocational Education and Training Authority (TVETA) and is a key player in the Kenyan government's initiative to promote technical training as a pathway to national development and job creation. It offers a range of diploma, certificate, and artisan courses tailored to meet the demands of the local and regional job market.

The institute is situated in a conducive learning environment in Kimilili, making it accessible to students from Bungoma County and surrounding regions. Matili TTI is committed to fostering innovation, practical skills, and ethical values among its students. Its curriculum is designed to be hands-on, often incorporating workshops, industrial attachments, and projects that provide real-world experience. The institution plays a vital role in enhancing the socio-economic status of its community by producing skilled graduates who contribute to various industries. Through its dedication to quality training and student support, Matili TTI has built a reputation as a reliable provider of technical education in Western Kenya.
institutions.short_description: Matili Technical Training Institute (TTI) is a public TVET institution in Kimilili, Bungoma County, offering practical diploma, certificate, and artisan courses to equip students with skills for employment and entrepreneurship, contributing to regional development.

other_info.address: Along Bungoma - Kitale Highway, Kimilili Town

institutions.name: Matuga Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Matuga Technical And Vocational College (MTVC) is a public TVET institution located in Matuga Constituency, Kwale County, Kenya. The college was established to provide technical and vocational training to young people in the region and beyond, equipping them with practical skills necessary for employment and entrepreneurship. It offers a diverse range of courses tailored to meet the demands of various industries, playing a crucial role in national development by contributing to a skilled workforce.

The college is situated in a serene environment in Matuga, making it accessible to students from different parts of the county and neighboring areas. MTVC is dedicated to fostering a learning environment that encourages innovation, creativity, and hands-on experience. It is committed to providing quality education and training that transforms lives and contributes positively to the socio-economic development of the community and the nation. The institution has steadily built its reputation as a reliable provider of technical education in the coastal region.
institutions.short_description: Matuga Technical And Vocational College (MTVC) is a public TVET institution in Matuga, Kwale County, offering technical and vocational training. It aims to equip students with practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship, contributing to national development through quality education and training.

institutions.name: Mawego National Polytechnic
institutions.profile:
Mawego National Polytechnic, located in Mawego, Karachuonyo Constituency, Homa Bay County, stands as a prominent public technical and vocational education and training (TVET) institution in Kenya. Established in 1952 initially as a youth training center, it has evolved significantly over the decades, upgrading its status to a national polytechnic in 2016. This transformation reflects its growing role in providing practical, market-relevant skills to students from the surrounding region and beyond. The polytechnic is strategically situated near the shores of Lake Victoria, providing a conducive learning environment away from urban congestion. It operates under the guidance of the Ministry of Education, playing a crucial part in the government's initiative to enhance technical skills and employability among Kenyan youth.

The polytechnic offers a diverse portfolio of programs across various disciplines, including engineering technology, building and civil engineering, business studies, information communication technology, and hospitality management. These programs are designed to meet the demands of the modern job market, emphasizing hands-on training and industry exposure. Mawego National Polytechnic is recognized for its commitment to producing competent graduates equipped with practical skills. Its reputation is built on providing accessible and quality TVET education, contributing significantly to the socio-economic development of the Homa Bay region and Kenya as a whole by empowering individuals with the skills needed for employment and entrepreneurship.
institutions.short_description: Mawego National Polytechnic is a leading public TVET institution in Homa Bay County, Kenya, established in 1952 and upgraded to a national polytechnic. It offers a wide range of practical, market-driven diploma and certificate programs across various technical and business disciplines.

institutions.name: Mbeere North Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Mbeere North Technical And Vocational College (MNTVC) is a public Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) institution located in Ishiara Town, Mbeere North Constituency, Embu County, Kenya. Established under the Ministry of Education, the college plays a crucial role in expanding access to technical and vocational training in the region. Its mission is centered on equipping students with practical, industry-relevant skills that are essential for employability and entrepreneurship, thereby contributing to national development and poverty alleviation.

Situated in a semi-rural setting, MNTVC serves the local community and surrounding areas by offering a diverse range of programs across various disciplines including electrical engineering, building technology, information communication technology (ICT), business studies, fashion design, and more. The college is committed to providing quality training through qualified instructors and relevant facilities, aiming to produce competent graduates capable of meeting the demands of the labor market and contributing positively to society. Its establishment reflects the government's commitment to strengthening TVET as a pathway to skills development and economic empowerment.
institutions.short_description: Mbeere North Technical And Vocational College is a public TVET institution in Ishiara, Embu County, offering diverse technical and vocational programs to empower youth with job-ready skills for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Mbita Technical And Vocational College
programs: Food and Beverage Production and Service
institutions.profile:
Mbita Technical And Vocational College (Mbita TVC) is a public TVET institution located in Mbita town, Homa Bay County, Kenya. Established to provide accessible technical and vocational training, the college plays a vital role in equipping students from the local community and beyond with practical skills and knowledge relevant to the job market. Situated in the rural yet significant lakeside town of Mbita, the college addresses the regional need for skilled manpower in various sectors, contributing to socio-economic development.

Mbita TVC offers a diverse range of programs at Artisan, Certificate, and Diploma levels across disciplines such as Engineering Technology, Building & Construction, Business Studies, Information Communication Technology, Hospitality, and Agriculture. The curriculum is designed to be hands-on and industry-relevant, aiming to produce graduates who are readily employable or capable of starting their own enterprises. The college is committed to fostering a conducive learning environment, despite potential resource constraints often faced by rural institutions, and strives to maintain standards set by the TVET Authority (TVETA) and align with national educational goals.
institutions.short_description: Mbita Technical And Vocational College (Mbita TVC) is a public TVET institution in Mbita, Homa Bay County. It offers a range of technical and business courses at Artisan, Certificate, and Diploma levels, aiming to provide practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Merti Technical And Vocational College
programs: Fashion Design and Garment Making Technology
institutions.profile:
Merti Technical And Vocational College is a public TVET institution located in Merti Town, Isiolo County, Kenya. Established as part of the government's initiative to expand access to technical education across all constituencies, the college aims to provide relevant skills training to the youth in the arid and semi-arid northern region. It plays a crucial role in equipping students with practical competencies needed for employment and entrepreneurship, contributing to local economic development.

The college offers a variety of courses accredited by TVETA, covering diverse fields such as building technology, electrical engineering, information communication technology, business studies, hospitality, and fashion design. These programs are designed to address the specific needs of the region and the national job market, providing opportunities for students who have completed secondary education to acquire marketable skills. Merti TVC is committed to fostering a learning environment that promotes innovation, practical application of knowledge, and personal development.
institutions.short_description: Merti Technical And Vocational College is a public TVET institution in Merti Town, Isiolo County. It provides accessible, quality technical and vocational training programs across various disciplines like building technology, ICT, business, and hospitality, empowering local youth with essential skills for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Meru University Of Science And Technology Tvet Directorate
institutions.profile:
The TVET Directorate at Meru University of Science and Technology (MUST) functions as a key component within the university structure, dedicated to providing technical and vocational education and training. MUST itself was established as a constituent college of Jomo Kenyatta University of Agriculture and Technology (JKUAT) in 2008 and was chartered as a fully-fledged university in 2013. Located at the main campus in Kiegoi, Meru, the TVET programs leverage the existing infrastructure and expertise of the university, offering students practical, hands-on training designed to meet the demands of various industries.

The directorate focuses on delivering market-driven courses at diploma and certificate levels, complementing the degree programs offered by the university. These programs cover a range of technical fields, equipping students with essential skills for employment or entrepreneurship. As part of a public university, the TVET Directorate adheres to national standards for technical training and plays a role in expanding access to quality education and training in the Mount Kenya region and beyond.
institutions.short_description: The TVET Directorate at Meru University of Science and Technology offers practical technical and vocational education programs at diploma and certificate levels, providing students with essential skills for various industries within the robust framework of a public university.

institutions.name: Meru National Polytechnic
institutions.profile:
Meru National Polytechnic is a premier public TVET institution located in Meru Town, Meru County, Kenya. Established in 1956 as a technical and trade school, it has undergone significant transformation and upgrading over the years, eventually attaining the status of a National Polytechnic. The institution is strategically situated along the Meru-Nanyuki highway, making it easily accessible. It falls under the purview of the Ministry of Education and is a key player in providing technical and vocational training in the region and nationally.

The polytechnic offers a wide array of programs ranging from artisan and certificate courses to diploma levels across various disciplines, including engineering, business, information and communication technology, health sciences, hospitality, agriculture, and applied sciences. Known for its practical-oriented training, the institution plays a crucial role in equipping students with the technical skills and knowledge required for the job market and self-employment. It has built a reputation as a reputable centre for TVET excellence, contributing significantly to Kenya's workforce development.
institutions.short_description: Meru National Polytechnic is a public TVET institution in Meru, Kenya, offering practical artisan, certificate, and diploma programs in diverse fields like engineering, business, and IT. It is a key centre for skills development.

institutions.name: Meru Teachers Training College
institutions.profile: Meru Teachers Training College is a public teacher training institution located in Meru County, Kenya. It is dedicated to training qualified and competent teachers for primary and secondary schools in Kenya. The college offers various teacher education programs, focusing on academic excellence, pedagogical skills, and character development. It plays a crucial role in the Kenyan education system by producing well-equipped educators.
institutions.short_description: A public teacher training college in Meru, Kenya, offering quality education programs to train competent teachers.

other_info.location: Located within Meru Municipality, along the Meru-Maua road, near Meru National Polytechnic.

institutions.name: Meru University Of Science And Technology
institutions.profile:
Meru University of Science and Technology (MUST) is a public institution of higher learning located in Meru County, Kenya, specifically situated in Nchiru along the Meru-Maua Road. Established as a Constituent College of Jomo Kenyatta University of Agriculture and Technology (JKUAT) in 2008, under the name Meru University College of Science and Technology (MUCST), it was granted its charter as a full-fledged university in 2013. This elevation marked a significant milestone, allowing MUST to independently pursue its mandate of providing education, research, and innovation, particularly in the fields of science and technology, to contribute to national development.

MUST is renowned for its strong emphasis on Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics (STEM) programs, offering a wide array of courses from diploma to doctorate levels across various faculties. The university is committed to academic excellence, practical training, and fostering a culture of research and innovation. It has built a reputation for producing graduates who are well-equipped with the knowledge and skills required by both the local and international job markets. The university's focus on practical learning and industry linkages helps ensure its programs remain relevant and its graduates competitive.
institutions.short_description: Meru University of Science and Technology (MUST) is a public university located in Meru, Kenya, chartered in 2013. It focuses primarily on providing high-quality education in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM) fields.

institutions.name: Michuki National Polytechnic
institutions.profile:
Michuki National Polytechnic (MNP) is a public TVET institution located in Kangema, Murang'a County, Kenya. It was established in 1987 as Kangema Youth Polytechnic with the primary goal of providing technical skills to local youth. Over the years, the institution grew significantly and was upgraded to a National Polytechnic, reflecting its expanded mandate and enhanced capacity to offer a wide range of technical and vocational training programs. MNP is committed to providing quality, industry-relevant education and training to equip students with the practical skills necessary for employment, entrepreneurship, and national development.

The polytechnic is situated in a serene environment conducive to learning and offers diverse courses across various disciplines including engineering, business, ICT, hospitality, and applied sciences. As a National Polytechnic, it plays a crucial role in Kenya's TVET sector, aiming to produce competent graduates who can contribute effectively to the economy. MNP strives to foster innovation, research, and community engagement while upholding values of excellence, integrity, and social responsibility, serving students from across the country.
institutions.short_description: Michuki National Polytechnic (MNP) is a public TVET institution in Kangema, Murang'a County, Kenya, offering diverse technical and vocational training programs across various disciplines to equip students with practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Migori Teachers Training College
programs: Diploma in Early Childhood Development Education (DECE)
institutions.profile:
Migori Teachers Training College (TTC) is a renowned public institution located in Migori Town, Migori County, along the Migori-Kisii Road within Suna East Constituency. Established after Kenya gained independence, the college was founded with the primary objective of training qualified primary school teachers to serve the growing educational needs of the nation. As a government-sponsored institution operating under the Ministry of Education, Migori TTC plays a vital role in implementing national education policies and training frameworks, particularly those related to teacher preparation for the Competency-Based Curriculum (CBC).

The college offers accredited diploma programs including the Diploma in Primary Teacher Education (DPTE) and the Diploma in Early Childhood Development Education (DECE), which are aligned with the current curriculum requirements. Additionally, it facilitates upgrade programs for teachers holding P1 certificates who wish to advance their qualifications to the diploma level. Migori TTC is equipped with essential facilities such as well-furnished classrooms, libraries, science and computer laboratories, and student accommodation, providing a conducive environment for learning and professional development. Its commitment to producing competent, professional, and dedicated educators has solidified its position as a respected institution in teacher training in Kenya.
institutions.short_description: Migori Teachers Training College is a prominent public institution situated in Migori Town, Kenya, specializing in the training of primary and early childhood education teachers. It offers accredited diploma programs focused on the Competency-Based Curriculum.

institutions.name: Mitunguu Technical Training Institute
institutions.profile:
Mitunguu Technical Training Institute (MITTU) is a burgeoning public Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) institution situated in Mitunguu area, South Imenti Constituency, Meru County. Established with the core mandate of providing accessible and quality technical education, MITTU serves the surrounding community and beyond by offering a diverse array of programs across various disciplines, including engineering technologies, information communication technology, business studies, and hospitality. The institute plays a crucial role in bridging the skills gap in the region and contributing to national development goals by producing competent and skilled graduates ready for the workforce or entrepreneurial ventures.

The institute is committed to fostering a conducive learning environment equipped with necessary infrastructure and resources to facilitate hands-on training and theoretical knowledge acquisition. Through its practical-oriented curriculum and dedicated faculty, MITTU strives to instill not only technical proficiency but also critical thinking and innovation among its students. Its strategic location makes it a vital educational hub for individuals seeking to acquire marketable skills without having to travel to larger urban centers, thereby promoting local development and empowering the youth within Meru County and neighboring areas.
institutions.short_description: Mitunguu Technical Training Institute (MITTU) is a public TVET institution in Mitunguu, South Imenti Constituency, Meru County. It offers a range of technical, business, and hospitality courses to equip trainees with practical skills for employment and self-employment.

institutions.name: Mochongoi Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Mochongoi Technical And Vocational College (Mochongoi TVC) is a public technical training institution located in Mochongoi, Baringo County, Kenya. Established in 2014, the college falls under the Ministry of Education and is dedicated to providing quality technical and vocational education and training (TVET) to equip students with practical skills for various industries. Situated in a rural setting, the college serves the local community and beyond, contributing significantly to human resource development in the region. As a recognized institution listed by KUCCPS, it upholds national standards in TVET provision.

The college offers a diverse range of programs across various departments, including Electrical & Electronics Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Building & Civil Engineering, Business Studies, Information Communication Technology (ICT), Agriculture, and Hospitality. These programs are designed to meet the demands of the job market and empower graduates with competencies for employment and entrepreneurship. Mochongoi TVC aims to foster innovation, creativity, and excellence among its students, preparing them to be skilled professionals and contributing members of society, thereby playing a vital role in national development through technical training.
institutions.short_description: Mochongoi Technical And Vocational College, located in Mochongoi, Baringo County, is a public TVET institution offering practical skills training in engineering, business, ICT, agriculture, and hospitality since 2014, preparing students for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Moi Teachers College - Baringo
institutions.profile:
Moi Teachers' College, Baringo (MTC Baringo) is a prominent public teachers training college situated in Baringo County, Kenya. Established with the aim of training competent and professional teachers for the nation, the college offers various P1 Certificate and Diploma in Primary Teacher Education programs. It serves as a key institution in the region, contributing significantly to the quality of basic education by producing qualified teaching personnel. The college is located in a conducive learning environment, providing adequate facilities for academic and co-curricular activities.

MTC Baringo is recognized by the Ministry of Education and is a KUCCPS-accredited institution, allowing students to apply for admission through the national placement service. While a dedicated official website is not readily available through standard searches, information about its programs, admission requirements, and contact details can often be found on educational portals and government sites. The college plays a vital role in addressing the demand for qualified teachers in Kenya, upholding standards set by the Teachers Service Commission (TSC) and the Kenya National Examinations Council (KNEC) for teacher education.
institutions.short_description: Moi Teachers College Baringo is a public institution in Baringo County, Kenya, dedicated to training primary school teachers. It offers P1 Certificate and Diploma in Primary Teacher Education programs under the Ministry of Education.

institutions.name: Moi University
institutions.profile:
Moi University, located in Kesses, Uasin Gishu County, is Kenya's second public university, established by an Act of Parliament in 1984 and officially opened in 1985. Situated approximately 35 kilometers southeast of Eldoret town, the university's main campus is built on the site of a former textile complex, transforming the area into a vibrant academic hub. From its inception, Moi University has grown significantly, adding numerous faculties, schools, and research centers, and developing satellite campuses to expand its reach and impact across various regions of Kenya.

The university offers a comprehensive range of academic programs across diverse disciplines, from certificates and diplomas to undergraduate and postgraduate degrees. Key faculties include Health Sciences, Engineering, Arts and Social Sciences, Education, Business, Law, Agriculture, and Information Sciences. Moi University is known for its commitment to academic excellence, fostering research, innovation, and community engagement. It plays a crucial role in higher education in Kenya and the East African region, producing graduates equipped with relevant skills for national development and contributing to knowledge through research.
institutions.short_description: Moi University is Kenya's second public university, established in 1984 in Uasin Gishu. It offers a broad spectrum of academic programs focusing on excellence, research, and community service.

institutions.name: Moi University TVET Institute
institutions.profile:
Moi University TVET Institute is a constituent college of Moi University, located in Eldoret, Uasin Gishu County, Kenya. Established to complement the university's academic offerings, the institute focuses on providing technical and vocational education and training programs. It aims to equip students with practical skills and knowledge relevant to various industries, contributing to the country's skilled workforce development. The institute leverages the resources and reputation of Moi University while offering specialized TVET courses designed to meet market demands.

The institute offers a range of diploma and certificate programs across different fields, including business, information technology, hospitality, engineering, and applied sciences. It maintains a commitment to quality training, often incorporating practical attachments and industry linkages into its curriculum. Situated within or near the main Moi University campus, students benefit from a conducive learning environment and access to university facilities, contributing to a well-rounded educational experience. The institute plays a crucial role in providing accessible higher education pathways, particularly for students seeking technical and vocational skills.
institutions.short_description: Moi University TVET Institute, a public institution under Moi University in Eldoret, offers technical and vocational training. It provides practical-oriented diploma and certificate programs across various disciplines, aiming to equip students with relevant skills for the job market.

institutions.name: Moiben Technical Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Moiben Technical Vocational College (Moiben TVC) is a public TVET institution located in Moiben, Uasin Gishu County, Kenya. Established to offer accessible technical and vocational training, the college plays a crucial role in equipping students with practical skills and knowledge relevant to the demands of the job market. It is situated along the Moiben-Kipsinende Road, near Moiben Town, providing a conducive learning environment for students from the surrounding region and beyond. The college operates under the Ministry of Education and is a registered institution by the Technical and Vocational Education and Training Authority (TVETA).

Moiben TVC offers a diverse range of programs across various departments, including Engineering Technology, Building and Civil Engineering, Business Studies, Information Communication Technology (ICT), Agriculture, and Health Sciences. These courses are designed to cater to different academic levels, from artisan and certificate to diploma programs, ensuring pathways for continuous learning and skill development. The college emphasizes hands-on training and industrial attachments to prepare graduates for employment or entrepreneurship, contributing significantly to the local economy and national development by producing skilled manpower.
institutions.short_description: Moiben Technical Vocational College is a public TVET institution in Moiben, Uasin Gishu County, offering a variety of technical and vocational programs. It provides practical skills training across multiple disciplines to prepare students for the workforce and entrepreneurship.

other_info.location: Along Moiben-Kipsinende Road, near Moiben Town

institutions.name: Molo Technical & Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Molo Technical & Vocational College (Molo TVC) is a public technical institution situated in Molo Town, Nakuru County, Kenya. Established to address the growing need for skilled technical and vocational manpower in the region and the country at large, the college offers a wide range of courses aligned with industry demands. Its strategic location in Molo, a key agricultural and commercial hub, positions it well to serve students from the surrounding areas and contribute to local development through practical training and skill transfer.

The college operates under the Ministry of Education and is registered and regulated by the Technical and Vocational Education and Training Authority (TVETA). Molo TVC is committed to providing quality technical education and training, equipping students with practical skills, knowledge, and attitudes necessary for employment, entrepreneurship, and further education. The institution strives to foster innovation, creativity, and ethical values among its trainees, aiming to produce competent professionals capable of contributing effectively to national development and the global economy.
institutions.short_description: Molo Technical & Vocational College is a public TVET institution in Molo, Nakuru County, offering a diverse range of technical and vocational training programs under the Ministry of Education to equip students with practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Morendat Institute Of Oil And Gas
institutions.profile:
The Morendat Institute of Oil and Gas (MIOG) is a public technical and vocational education and training (TVET) institution in Kenya, operating under the Ministry of Energy and Petroleum. Located in Naivasha, Nakuru County, the institute was established to build local capacity and provide specialized training for the nascent oil and gas industry in Kenya and the East African region. It plays a crucial role in equipping Kenyans with the necessary skills and knowledge to participate effectively in the upstream, midstream, and downstream segments of the petroleum sector, aligning with the government's vision for local content development.

MIOG offers a range of programs designed to meet the specific demands of the oil and gas industry, including technical courses, safety certifications, and operational training. The curriculum covers areas such as basic oil and gas operations, health, safety, and environment (HSE), pipeline operations, and international certifications like OPITO. By providing practical, industry-relevant education, MIOG aims to produce competent professionals and technicians ready for employment in both national and international oil and gas companies, contributing significantly to job creation and expertise development within the country.
institutions.short_description: Morendat Institute of Oil and Gas (MIOG) is Kenya's premier public TVET institute for specialized training in the oil and gas sector. Located in Naivasha, it provides industry-relevant technical, operational, and safety programs under the Ministry of Energy and Petroleum.

institutions.name: Mosoriot Teachers Training College
programs: Diploma in Early Childhood Development Education (DECTE)
institutions.profile:
Mosoriot Teachers Training College, situated in Mosoriot town, Nandi County, is a prominent public institution under the Ministry of Education dedicated to the training of primary school teachers in Kenya. Established in 1978, the college has a rich history and plays a crucial role in shaping the country's education sector by producing qualified educators. It is one of the well-regarded public TTCs in the Rift Valley region, known for its commitment to academic excellence and teacher professional development.

The college offers various teacher training programs, including the certificate-level Primary Teacher Education (PTE), and more recently, the Diploma in Primary Teacher Education (DPTE) and Diploma in Early Childhood Development Education (DECTE), aligning with the new curriculum requirements in Kenya. Mosoriot TTC is known for its structured academic environment, practical teaching methodologies, and commitment to producing graduates who are not only academically proficient but also ethically grounded and prepared for the challenges of the modern classroom. Its location provides a conducive learning environment away from urban distractions, fostering focused learning.
institutions.short_description: Mosoriot Teachers Training College is a leading public institution located in Mosoriot, Nandi County, Kenya. Established in 1978, it is dedicated to training competent and professional teachers for primary schools in Kenya, offering P1, DPTE, and DECTE programs.

institutions.name: Mount Kenya University
institutions.profile:
Mount Kenya University (MKU) is a fully chartered private university in Kenya, established from the groundwork laid by the Thika Institute of Technology. Founded by Prof. Simon Gicharu, MKU received its Letter of Interim Authority in 2005 and was granted its Charter in 2011, marking its full transition into a recognized university. It is also ISO 9001:2015 certified, signifying its commitment to maintaining high standards of quality in its operations and education delivery. The main campus is strategically located in Thika, Kiambu County, but the university has significantly expanded its reach with campuses and learning centres across Kenya and in neighboring countries, making higher education accessible to a wider population.

MKU offers a vast array of academic programs across various disciplines, including Health Sciences, Business and Economics, Education, Pure and Applied Sciences, Engineering, Technology, Social Sciences, and Hospitality. This diverse portfolio caters to a wide range of student interests and career aspirations. The university is known for its practical-oriented training, research initiatives, and efforts to integrate technology into learning. Its reputation is built on rapid growth, accessibility, and its role as a major provider of tertiary education in Kenya, attracting a large student body and contributing significantly to the development of skilled professionals in the region.
institutions.short_description: Mount Kenya University (MKU) is a leading chartered private university located in Thika, Kiambu County. It offers a wide range of academic programs and is recognized for its expansive network and commitment to quality education and research.

institutions.name: Msambweni Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Msambweni Technical And Vocational College (MSTAVC) is a public technical and vocational training institution situated in Msambweni, Kwale County, along the Kenyan coast. Established to address the growing demand for skilled labour in the region and nationally, MSTAVC is under the Ministry of Education and is a recognized institution by the Kenya Universities and Colleges Central Placement Service (KUCCPS). The college plays a crucial role in providing accessible technical education to students from diverse backgrounds, focusing on practical, hands-on training to prepare them for employment or entrepreneurship.

The college offers various courses in fields such as building and civil engineering, electrical engineering, mechanical engineering, business studies, ICT, and applied sciences. MSTAVC is committed to fostering a learning environment that promotes innovation, creativity, and ethical conduct. Its location in Msambweni makes it a key educational hub for the South Coast region, contributing significantly to local economic development by producing competent graduates who can meet the demands of the job market. The institution aims to be a leading TVET center known for excellence in training and contributing to sustainable development.
institutions.short_description: Msambweni Technical And Vocational College is a public TVC located in Msambweni, Kwale County. It offers a range of technical and vocational training programs aimed at equipping students with practical skills for various industries, contributing to national development.

institutions.name: Mukiria Technical Training Institute
institutions.profile:
Mukiria Technical Training Institute (MTTI) is a public Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) institution located in Meru, Meru County, Kenya. Established in 2007, the institute is registered and accredited by the Technical and Vocational Education and Training Authority (TVETA). MTTI is dedicated to providing accessible and quality technical and business training programs to equip students with the skills and knowledge necessary for self-reliance and employment in various sectors of the economy. The institute operates under the Ministry of Education, positioning it as a key player in the government's efforts to enhance technical skills development in the country.

MTTI offers a diverse range of courses at diploma, certificate, and craft levels across various departments, including Business Studies, Information Communication Technology, Electrical Engineering, Hospitality and Tourism, and Applied Sciences. The curriculum is designed to be industry-relevant, combining theoretical knowledge with practical, hands-on training. Located strategically in the rich agricultural region of Meru, the institute serves students from the local community and beyond, fostering a conducive learning environment with modern facilities. Mukiria Technical Training Institute is recognized for its commitment to producing competent graduates ready to meet the demands of the dynamic job market and contribute to national development.
institutions.short_description: Mukiria Technical Training Institute is a public TVET institution in Meru, Meru County, established in 2007. It offers diverse diploma, certificate, and craft programs aimed at equipping students with market-ready technical and business skills for employment and self-reliance.

institutions.name: Mukurweini Technical Training Institute
institutions.profile:
Mukurweini Technical Training Institute (Mukurweini TTI) is a public TVET institution located in Mukurweini Town, Nyeri County, Kenya. It officially opened its doors to students in the 2014/2015 academic year. Established under the Ministry of Education, the institute plays a vital role in expanding access to technical and vocational education and training in the region. Its mission is to provide quality, demand-driven training that equips trainees with relevant skills and knowledge for both formal employment and entrepreneurial ventures, contributing to the socio-economic development goals outlined in Kenya's Vision 2030. Situated along the Mukurweini-Kieini road, it serves as a key educational hub for students from Mukurweini Constituency and surrounding areas.

The institute offers a diverse range of programs at Artisan, Certificate, and Diploma levels across various disciplines. Key areas of study include Building & Civil Engineering, Information Communication Technology (ICT), Electrical & Electronics Engineering, Business Studies, Hospitality & Institutional Management, Mechanical & Automotive Engineering, Applied Sciences, and Textiles & Fashion Design. Mukurweini TTI is recognized for its focus on practical, hands-on training delivered through well-equipped workshops and experienced faculty. It aims to foster innovation, creativity, and self-reliance among its graduates, preparing them to meet the evolving demands of the job market and contribute effectively to national development.
institutions.short_description: Mukurweini Technical Training Institute is a public TVET college in Mukurweini, Nyeri County, offering practical technical, vocational, and entrepreneurial training programs at Artisan, Certificate, and Diploma levels to empower students for employment and self-reliance.

institutions.name: Mulango Technical & Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Mulango Technical & Vocational College (MTVC) is a public TVET institution located in Mulango Ward, Kitui Central Constituency, Kitui County, Kenya. Established in 2014 under the Ministry of Education, the college is dedicated to providing quality technical, vocational, and entrepreneurial training aimed at equipping youth with practical skills for employment and self-reliance. Situated approximately 5 kilometers from Kitui town, MTVC plays a vital role in addressing the demand for skilled manpower in the region and contributing to national development goals.

The college offers a diverse range of market-driven programs across various disciplines, including engineering technologies, business studies, information technology, hospitality, and creative arts. These programs are structured to provide hands-on training and theoretical knowledge, supported by qualified instructors and well-equipped workshops. MTVC is committed to fostering a conducive learning environment that promotes innovation, creativity, and ethical values, preparing graduates to meet the challenges of the contemporary job market and contribute meaningfully to society.
institutions.short_description: Mulango Technical & Vocational College (MTVC) is a public TVET institution in Kitui, Kenya, established in 2014. It offers a variety of technical, vocational, and business programs designed to equip youth with practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Multimedia University Of Kenya
institutions.location: Magadi Road, near Bomas of Kenya, Nairobi
institutions.profile:
Multimedia University of Kenya (MMU) is a public university situated along Magadi Road in Nairobi, Kenya, near the renowned Bomas of Kenya. Its origins trace back to the Kenya Post and Telecommunications Corporation (KPTC) Training School established in 1948. Over the years, it evolved, becoming a constituent college of the University of Nairobi in 2008 before being granted its Charter on March 1, 2013, by then-President Mwai Kibaki, officially establishing it as a fully-fledged university. MMU is dedicated to pioneering technological and media education, offering a wide range of programs across disciplines like Information Technology, Engineering, Business, Mass Communication, and Applied Sciences.

MMU is recognized for its commitment to innovation and research, particularly in fields relevant to the digital economy and media landscape. It plays a crucial role in training professionals for Kenya's growing ICT and media sectors. Located outside the immediate city center but still within Nairobi County, it provides a conducive learning environment while being accessible. The university continues to expand its academic offerings and infrastructure, aiming to be a leader in technology and media-driven education in East Africa.
institutions.short_description: Multimedia University of Kenya (MMU), located on Magadi Road in Nairobi near Bomas of Kenya, is a leading public institution chartered in 2013, specializing prominently in technology, media, and communication programs, preparing students for the digital age.

other_info.location: Magadi Road, near Bomas of Kenya, Nairobi

institutions.name: Multimedia University Of Kenya Tvet Centre
institutions.profile:
Multimedia University of Kenya (MMU) is a public university situated along Magadi Road in Nairobi. Originally a branch campus of Kenya Institute of Mass Communication and later a constituent college of Moi University, it was chartered as a full-fledged university in 2013. MMU specializes in various disciplines, with a strong emphasis on technology, including Information and Communication Technology, Engineering, Business, Media and Communication, and Applied Sciences. The university is located on a spacious campus providing a conducive environment for learning and research, contributing significantly to higher education in Kenya.

The MMU TVET Centre operates as a specialized unit within the university, dedicated to providing technical and vocational education and training. It utilizes the extensive facilities and experienced faculty of the main university to offer practical, skill-based programs designed to meet industry demands. The centre plays a crucial role in enhancing workforce readiness by offering certifications and diplomas in various technical fields, complementing the degree programs offered by the university and contributing to Kenya's technical skills base. Its integration within a university setting provides unique advantages, offering students access to broader resources and potential pathways for further education.
institutions.short_description: Multimedia University of Kenya TVET Centre offers diverse technical and vocational courses, leveraging the university's infrastructure and expertise. Located in Nairobi along Magadi Road, it provides hands-on training for practical skills development in various fields.

institutions.name: Mumias West Technical Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Mumias West Technical Vocational College (MWTVC) is a public TVET institution situated in Mumias West Constituency, Kakamega County, Kenya. Established as part of the Kenyan government's initiative to expand access to technical and vocational training, MWTVC plays a crucial role in providing relevant skills and knowledge to the youth in the Western region and beyond. The college is located along the Mumias-Busia Road, making it accessible to students from various areas. It is under the Ministry of Education and is dedicated to contributing to national development through competency-based education and training. The college admits students through the Kenya Universities and Colleges Central Placement Service (KUCCPS) and directly.

MWTVC offers a wide range of programs across various disciplines, including engineering technologies (e.g., Building, Electrical, Automotive), Information Communication Technology, Business Studies, Hospitality, and Cosmetology. These courses are designed to meet the demands of the industry and labor market, emphasizing practical skills and hands-on training conducted in well-equipped workshops and laboratories. The institution is committed to fostering a learning environment that promotes innovation, creativity, and ethical values, preparing graduates for successful careers, self-employment, and further education. Its focus on practical, industry-aligned training makes it a key institution for youth empowerment and skill development in the region.
institutions.short_description: Mumias West Technical Vocational College is a public TVET institution located in Mumias West Constituency, Kakamega County. It offers diverse technical, vocational, and business programs aimed at equipping students with practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Mungatsi Technical & Vocational College
programs: Fashion Design and Garment Making Technology
institutions.profile:
Mungatsi Technical & Vocational College (TVC) is a public institution located in Mungatsi, Funyula Constituency, Busia County, Kenya. Established under the Ministry of Education, the college is dedicated to providing quality technical and vocational education and training. Situated strategically in a rural setting, Mungatsi TVC plays a crucial role in increasing access to TVET programs for students in the Western Kenya region and beyond, contributing to the government's agenda of skills development and capacity building.

The college offers a diverse range of market-driven diploma and certificate courses across various disciplines, including engineering, building technology, business, hospitality, fashion design, and information communication technology. These programs are designed to equip students with practical skills and knowledge essential for employment, entrepreneurship, and further education. Mungatsi TVC aims to empower its graduates to become competent professionals and innovators capable of contributing effectively to the economy and their communities.
institutions.short_description: Mungatsi Technical & Vocational College is a public TVC in Mungatsi, Busia County, Kenya, offering diverse technical, vocational, and business programs focused on practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Muraga Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Muraga Technical And Vocational College is a public TVET institution situated in Kamanyaki, Tharaka Constituency, Tharaka Nithi County, Kenya. Established to address the growing need for skilled manpower in the region and nationally, the college plays a vital role in empowering youth and the local community through accessible and relevant technical education. Its location in a relatively rural area makes it a crucial educational hub for students who might otherwise face challenges accessing such training opportunities. The college is committed to fostering practical skills and knowledge essential for employability and entrepreneurship, aligning its curriculum with industry demands and national development goals.

The college offers a diverse range of programs across various disciplines, including engineering, business studies, information communication technology (ICT), agriculture, and building technology, among others. These courses are designed to provide hands-on training and theoretical knowledge, preparing graduates for successful careers or further education. Muraga TVC is under the Ministry of Education and is regulated by TVETA, ensuring the quality and relevance of its training programs. Its reputation is built on its dedication to providing quality, affordable technical education and contributing positively to the socio-economic development of the Tharaka Nithi region and Kenya as a whole.
institutions.short_description: Muraga Technical And Vocational College is a public institution in Tharaka Nithi County, Kenya, dedicated to providing quality technical and vocational training. It equips students with practical skills for various industries.

institutions.name: Murang'A Teachers Training College
programs: Diploma in Early Childhood Development Education (DECTE)
institutions.profile:
Murang'a Teachers Training College (TTC) is a prominent public institution situated in Murang'a Town, Murang'a County, Kenya. Established to address the growing demand for qualified teachers in the country, the college operates under the mandate of the Ministry of Education. Over the years, Murang'a TTC has built a strong reputation for its commitment to producing competent and dedicated educators who are well-prepared to serve in primary schools and early childhood education centers across Kenya, particularly within the Mount Kenya region. Its strategic location in Murang'a makes it an accessible hub for students from the surrounding counties.

The college primarily focuses on offering diploma programs aligned with the current national curriculum and teacher education standards. Key programs include the Diploma in Primary Teacher Education (DPTE) and the Diploma in Early Childhood Development Education (DECTE). These programs are designed to equip trainees with the necessary pedagogical skills, subject knowledge, and professional values required for effective teaching and childcare. Murang'a TTC is accredited by relevant bodies, ensuring that its graduates meet the required standards for employment by the Teachers Service Commission (TSC). The institution strives to provide a conducive learning environment, fostering both academic excellence and character development among its trainees, contributing significantly to the quality of basic education in Kenya.
institutions.short_description: Murang'a Teachers Training College (TTC) is a leading public teacher training institution in Murang'a Town, Kenya, dedicated to training qualified primary school teachers and early childhood development educators.

institutions.name: Murang'A Technical Training Institute
institutions.profile:
Murang'a Technical Training Institute (MTTI) is a prominent public TVET institution situated in Murang'a Town, Murang'a County, Kenya. Established to contribute to the national agenda of skills development, MTTI plays a crucial role in equipping students with practical knowledge and technical expertise across various disciplines. The institute offers diverse programs including engineering, building technology, business studies, information communication technology (ICT), and hospitality, catering to students pursuing Artisan, Certificate, and Diploma qualifications. Its strategic location makes it accessible to students from Murang'a and surrounding counties.

MTTI is regulated by the Ministry of Education and is recognized by relevant bodies like TVETA (Technical and Vocational Education and Training Authority) and KNEC (Kenya National Examinations Council). The institution is known for its commitment to practical training, often featuring well-equipped workshops and laboratories. Over the years, MTTI has built a reputation for producing skilled graduates who contribute to various sectors of the Kenyan economy. It continues to adapt its curriculum to meet the evolving demands of the job market and the national development goals outlined in Kenya's Vision 2030.
institutions.short_description: Murang'a Technical Training Institute is a public TVET institution in Murang'a Town, offering a wide range of technical, vocational, business, and ICT programs at diploma, certificate, and artisan levels, committed to quality skills training.

institutions.name: Murang'A University Of Technology
institutions.profile:
Murang'a University of Technology (MUT) is a public chartered university situated in Murang'a Town, Murang'a County, Kenya. Its origins trace back to 1975 with the establishment of Murang'a College of Technology. The institution was upgraded to a constituent college of Jomo Kenyatta University of Agriculture and Technology (JKUAT) in 2011, significantly expanding its academic offerings and research capacity. In 2016, it achieved full university status upon receiving its charter, becoming Murang'a University of Technology. Located approximately 85 kilometers North-East of Nairobi, MUT plays a crucial role in providing higher education access to students from Murang'a and surrounding regions.

MUT is known for its diverse academic programs spread across various schools and departments, including Engineering and Technology, Pure and Applied Sciences, Business and Economics, Education, Computing and Informatics, and Health Sciences. The university emphasizes practical training and research relevant to national development needs, aiming to produce graduates equipped with skills demanded by the job market. Its reputation is growing as an institution committed to academic excellence, innovation, and community engagement, contributing to the socio-economic development of the region through education, research, and extension services.
institutions.short_description: Murang'a University of Technology (MUT) is a public university located in Murang'a Town, Murang'a County, Kenya. It offers a wide range of programs in technology, applied sciences, business, education, and health sciences, recognized for its focus on practical, market-oriented skills.

institutions.name: Murang'A University Of Technology Tvet Institute
institutions.profile:
Murang'a University Of Technology TVET Institute operates as a key component of Murang'a University of Technology (MUT), a public university located in Murang'a County, Kenya. Established to complement the university's mandate in higher education, the TVET Institute focuses specifically on providing accessible, high-quality technical and vocational education and training. Situated within or adjacent to the main university campus along the Murang'a-Sabasaba Road, the institute leverages the infrastructure and academic expertise of the university to deliver practical, hands-on courses designed to equip students with industry-relevant skills and knowledge. It plays a crucial role in expanding access to post-secondary education, particularly for students seeking technical careers.

As part of a reputable public university, the TVET Institute benefits from established academic standards and resources. It offers a variety of certificate and diploma programs across different technical and vocational fields, catering to diverse student interests and labor market needs. The institute is recognized by relevant government bodies like TVETA and facilitates admissions through platforms like KUCCPS. Its integration with MUT ensures a strong connection to advanced research and technology while maintaining its core focus on practical skills development, aiming to produce competent graduates ready for employment or entrepreneurship in various sectors of the Kenyan economy.
institutions.short_description: Murang'a University Of Technology TVET Institute is a public technical and vocational education and training provider located in Murang'a. It is a constituent part of Murang'a University of Technology, dedicated to offering skill-based courses to meet industry demands.

institutions.name: Musakasa Technical Training Institute
institutions.profile:
Musakasa Technical Training Institute (MTTI) is a public TVET institution located in Bungoma, Kenya. Established to provide technical and vocational education and training, MTTI aims to equip students with practical skills and knowledge relevant to industry demands. The institute offers a diverse range of programs across various disciplines, contributing significantly to workforce development in the region and the country at large. Its focus is on producing competent graduates ready for employment or entrepreneurship.

The institute is situated strategically in Bungoma County, making it accessible to students from the Western region and beyond. MTTI is committed to fostering a conducive learning environment, supported by qualified instructors and relevant training facilities. As a government institution under the Ministry of Education, it plays a crucial role in advancing the technical skills agenda in Kenya, aligning its curriculum with national development goals and industry needs. The institute strives for excellence in TVET provision, contributing to socio-economic growth through skilled labor.
institutions.short_description: Musakasa Technical Training Institute (MTTI) is a public TVET college in Bungoma, Kenya, providing technical and vocational training across various disciplines to equip students with essential skills for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Mwala Technical & Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Mwala Technical & Vocational College (Mwala TVC) is a public technical and vocational training institution located in Mwala, Machakos County, Kenya. Established to provide accessible and quality technical and vocational education and training (TVET), the college plays a crucial role in equipping young Kenyans with practical skills and knowledge required for various industries and self-employment. It offers a diverse range of programs at diploma, certificate, and artisan levels across various fields including engineering, information technology, business studies, building and construction, hospitality, and applied sciences, aligning its curriculum with the national development goals and labor market demands.

Situated in the Mwala sub-county, the college serves students from Machakos County and beyond, contributing significantly to the region's educational landscape and economic empowerment. As a public institution under the Ministry of Education, Mwala TVC is dedicated to fostering innovation, entrepreneurship, and ethical conduct among its students. It aims to produce competent graduates who can contribute effectively to the socio-economic development of Kenya, emphasizing hands-on training, industry linkages, and holistic student development.
institutions.short_description: Mwala Technical & Vocational College is a public TVET institution in Mwala, Machakos County, Kenya, offering diploma, certificate, and artisan programs to equip students with practical skills for various industries and self-employment.

other_info.address: Along Machakos - Makutano Road, Mwala, Machakos County

institutions.name: Mwatate Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Mwatate Technical And Vocational College (MTVC) is a public institution located in Mwatate town, Taita Taveta County, Kenya. Established to bridge the skills gap and contribute to national development, MTVC offers a wide range of technical, vocational, and business courses aligned with industry needs. The college is registered by the Technical and Vocational Education and Training Authority (TVETA) and admits students through the Kenya Universities and Colleges Central Placement Service (KUCCPS) and directly. It aims to empower students with practical skills and knowledge necessary for employment, entrepreneurship, and further education, contributing significantly to the socio-economic development of the region.

The college prides itself on providing quality education through qualified faculty and adequate training facilities. It focuses on nurturing talent and fostering innovation among its students. MTVC plays a crucial role in providing accessible technical education to the youth in Taita Taveta County and beyond, helping to improve livelihoods and reduce unemployment. The institution is committed to maintaining high standards of training and producing competent graduates ready to meet the demands of the modern job market and contribute positively to society.
institutions.short_description: Mwatate Technical And Vocational College (MTVC) is a public TVET institution in Taita Taveta County, offering technical, vocational, and business courses to equip students with practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship, fostering regional development.
