institutions.name: Mwea Technical & Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Mwea Technical & Vocational College (Mwea TVC) is a prominent public technical and vocational education and training institution situated in Wang'uru town, Mwea East Constituency, Kirinyaga County, Kenya. Established by the Kenyan government, Mwea TVC plays a vital role in addressing the demand for skilled labor by providing accessible and relevant training programs. The college is strategically located in the heart of the agricultural Mwea region, positioning it well to offer courses that align with local and national economic needs, including various engineering disciplines, building technology, information communication technology, business studies, hospitality, and institutional management.

The institution is committed to delivering quality technical education that empowers its graduates with practical competencies essential for the job market and self-employment. Mwea TVC boasts experienced faculty and aims to provide adequate workshops and laboratories to facilitate hands-on learning. Through its comprehensive curriculum and focus on practical skills, the college contributes significantly to the development of the region and the country by producing a competent workforce capable of driving innovation and productivity across diverse sectors. It serves as a key resource for young people seeking alternatives to university education, offering pathways to successful careers in technical fields.
institutions.short_description: Mwea Technical & Vocational College is a public TVET institution in Wang'uru, Kirinyaga County, Kenya. It offers diverse technical and business courses aimed at equipping students with practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship in various sectors.

institutions.name: Nachu Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Nachu Technical And Vocational College is a public technical training institution located in Nachu, Kikuyu Constituency, Kiambu County, Kenya. Established with the aim of providing accessible and quality technical and vocational education and training (TVET), the college plays a vital role in empowering Kenyan youth with market-ready skills. Situated in a serene environment, it offers a conducive atmosphere for learning practical trades and technologies. The institution operates under the guidance of the Ministry of Education, ensuring its programs align with national educational standards and workforce development needs.

The college offers a diverse range of courses across various technical and business disciplines. These programs are designed to equip students with hands-on expertise and theoretical knowledge necessary for successful careers in industries such as engineering, construction, information technology, and business. Nachu TVC is dedicated to fostering innovation, promoting entrepreneurship, and producing competent graduates who can contribute meaningfully to the socio-economic growth of Kenya. Its commitment to practical training and student welfare contributes to its growing reputation as a key TVET provider in the region.
institutions.short_description: Nachu Technical And Vocational College is a public TVET institution located in Nachu, Kikuyu, Kiambu County. It offers practical technical and vocational training across various fields, preparing students for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Nairobi Technical Training Institute
institutions.profile:
Nairobi Technical Training Institute (NTTI) is a premier public technical and vocational education and training (TVET) institution located in Nairobi, Kenya. Situated off Haile Selassie Avenue in the heart of the city, NTTI has a long-standing history as a key provider of technical skills in the country. Established to bridge the gap between academic knowledge and practical industry requirements, the institute offers a wide array of diploma and certificate programs designed to equip students with hands-on expertise and theoretical understanding in various fields. Its central location makes it accessible to students from across the Nairobi metropolitan area and beyond.

Renowned for its robust technical courses, NTTI focuses on areas like electrical engineering, building and civil engineering, mechanical engineering, and automotive engineering. Beyond traditional engineering, it also offers programs in applied sciences, business studies, ICT, and hospitality. The institute is recognized by the Ministry of Education and plays a crucial role in the national development agenda by producing skilled manpower. Its reputation is built on experienced faculty, practical workshops, and strong industry linkages, contributing significantly to the employability of its graduates. NTTI remains a sought-after institution for students seeking practical, career-oriented education.
institutions.short_description: Nairobi Technical Training Institute (NTTI) is a leading public TVET institution in Nairobi, Kenya, offering diverse diploma and certificate programs in engineering, business, and applied sciences to equip students with practical skills for the job market and national development.

other_info.location: Off Haile Selassie Avenue / Mogira Rd, Nairobi

institutions.name: Naivasha Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Naivasha Technical And Vocational College (NTVC) is a prominent public technical and vocational education and training institution located in Naivasha, Nakuru County, Kenya. Established to address the growing demand for skilled labour, NTVC plays a crucial role in providing accessible and quality TVET programs to students from the Rift Valley region and beyond. The college offers a broad spectrum of courses across various disciplines, including engineering (civil, electrical, mechanical, automotive), hospitality, business studies, information and communication technology (ICT), and liberal studies, leading to Artisan, Certificate, and Diploma qualifications.

Situated off the Naivasha-Elementaita Road, NTVC boasts a conducive learning environment with workshops, labs, and classrooms designed to facilitate hands-on training and theoretical instruction. The institution is committed to empowering its graduates with practical skills and knowledge relevant to current industry needs, enhancing their employability and contribution to national development. As a public institution under the Ministry of Education, NTVC adheres to national TVET standards and is a key player in the government's agenda to promote technical and vocational training as a pathway to economic growth and youth empowerment.
institutions.short_description: Naivasha Technical And Vocational College is a public TVET institution in Naivasha, Nakuru County, offering a diverse range of certificate, diploma, and artisan courses in technical, vocational, and business fields to equip students with practical skills for the job market.

institutions.name: Narok South Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Narok South Technical and Vocational College (NSTVC) is a public institution located in Ololulunga, Narok South Constituency, Narok County. Established by the Kenyan government, NSTVC aims to provide quality technical and vocational education and training to equip students with practical skills relevant to the job market and national development goals. The college is situated along the Bomet-Narok Highway, making it accessible to students from the surrounding areas and beyond. It operates under the Ministry of Education, contributing to the expansion of TVET opportunities in the Rift Valley region.

NSTVC offers a diverse range of programs across various disciplines, including building technology, electrical and electronic engineering, business studies, ICT, hospitality, and agriculture. The curriculum is designed to be competency-based, focusing on hands-on training and industry relevance. The college plays a significant role in empowering local youth and adults by providing them with valuable skills for self-employment or formal employment. It strives to be a center of excellence in technical training, contributing to the socio-economic development of Narok County and Kenya as a whole.
institutions.short_description: Narok South Technical and Vocational College is a public TVET institution in Ololulunga, Narok County, offering diverse technical, business, and vocational programs focused on practical skills training for employability and national development.

institutions.name: Narok Teachers Training College
programs: Diploma in Early Childhood Development Education (DECTE)
institutions.profile:
Narok Teachers Training College (Narok TTC) is a public institution located in Narok Town, Narok County, Kenya. Established with the primary mission of training qualified primary school teachers, it has been a significant contributor to the education sector in the region and nationally. Over the years, the college has adapted its curriculum to align with the evolving educational policies and needs in Kenya, including the transition to competence-based curriculum (CBC) training. Situated conveniently off Bomet Road, it serves as a key educational hub attracting students from various parts of the country.

The college offers recognized programs such as the Diploma in Primary Teacher Education (DPTE) and the Diploma in Early Childhood Development Education (DECTE), replacing the former P1 certificate program. Narok TTC is known for its commitment to producing competent educators equipped with the necessary pedagogical skills and subject knowledge. Its reputation is built on its history as a government-funded institution dedicated to teacher training, playing a crucial role in staffing primary schools across Kenya.
institutions.short_description: Narok Teachers Training College is a public institution in Narok County, Kenya, specializing in training primary and early childhood education teachers. It offers Diploma programs approved by the Ministry of Education, contributing significantly to the teaching workforce in Kenya.

institutions.name: Narok West Technical Training Institute
institutions.profile:
Narok West Technical Training Institute (NWTTI) is a public TVET institution located in Kilgoris town, Narok West Constituency, Narok County. Established in 2017, the institute operates under the Ministry of Education in Kenya. Its primary mission is to provide quality and relevant technical, industrial, vocational, and entrepreneurship training to equip students with marketable skills necessary for employment and self-reliance. Situated in a strategic location within the South Rift region, NWTTI serves as a key center for technical skills development, catering to students from Narok County and surrounding areas.

NWTTI offers a diverse range of programs aligned with national development goals, spanning various disciplines including Engineering, Business Studies, Information Communication Technology (ICT), Applied Sciences, and Hospitality. These courses are designed to meet industry demands and promote innovation among learners. The institute is committed to fostering a conducive learning environment with qualified instructors and appropriate facilities to support practical training and academic excellence, contributing significantly to the workforce development in Kenya.
institutions.short_description: Narok West Technical Training Institute (NWTTI) is a public TVET institution established in 2017 in Kilgoris, Narok County. It offers diverse programs in technical, business, and applied sciences fields, aiming to provide marketable skills and promote self-reliance among youth.

institutions.name: Navakholo Technical & Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Navakholo Technical & Vocational College (TVC) is a public technical institution located in Navakholo Constituency, Kakamega County, Kenya. Established by the Kenyan government, the college aims to provide technical, industrial, technological, and entrepreneurial training to equip students with relevant skills for national development and self-reliance. Situated in a rural setting, Navakholo TVC plays a crucial role in expanding access to TVET education in the Western Kenya region, contributing to the government's agenda of increasing skilled manpower.

The college offers a diverse range of programs aligned with market demands, spanning various disciplines such as Engineering (Building, Electrical, Automotive), Business Studies, Information Communication Technology (ICT), and Applied Sciences. These courses are designed to be practical and hands-on, preparing graduates for direct entry into the workforce or for further studies. Navakholo TVC is registered by TVETA (Technical and Vocational Education and Training Authority) and is a recognized institution by KUCCPS (Kenya Universities and Colleges Central Placement Service), allowing students to apply for admission and government funding. Its focus on practical skills makes it a vital institution for empowering youth and fostering economic growth in its catchment area and beyond.
institutions.short_description: Navakholo Technical & Vocational College is a public TVET institution in Kakamega County, offering practical, skills-based training in engineering, business, ICT, and applied sciences to empower youth for employment and entrepreneurship, contributing to national development.

institutions.name: Ndaragwa Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Ndaragwa Technical and Vocational College (Ndaragwa TVC) is a public technical and vocational training institution located in Ndaragwa Town, Nyandarua County, along the Nyahururu-Nyeri Highway. Established to provide accessible and quality technical education and training, the college plays a crucial role in equipping the youth with practical skills relevant to the job market and entrepreneurial pursuits. It operates under the Ministry of Education and is a key player in the government's initiative to promote technical education as a driver of economic development in Kenya.

The college offers a diverse range of programs across various departments, including engineering (civil, mechanical, electrical), ICT, business studies, hospitality, agriculture, and environmental studies. These programs are designed to meet the standards set by relevant accreditation bodies, ensuring graduates are competent and ready for industry demands. Ndaragwa TVC aims to foster innovation, research, and community engagement, contributing positively to the socio-economic development of the surrounding region and the nation by producing skilled and ethical professionals.
institutions.short_description: Ndaragwa Technical and Vocational College is a public institution in Ndaragwa Town, Nyandarua County, offering a wide array of technical, vocational, and business programs to equip students with essential skills for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Ndia Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Ndia Technical And Vocational College is a public institution registered under the Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) Act, 2013. Located in Kutus Town, Ndia Constituency, Kirinyaga County, the college is dedicated to providing accessible and high-quality technical and vocational education and training. It operates under the Ministry of Education and plays a crucial role in the government's initiative to increase the uptake of technical skills among Kenyan youth, aiming to contribute significantly to the country's industrial development goals by producing skilled manpower.

The college offers a diverse range of market-driven programs at diploma and certificate levels across various disciplines, including engineering, business, ICT, and applied sciences. Ndia TVC emphasizes practical, competence-based training methodologies designed to equip students with hands-on skills directly applicable to industry needs and entrepreneurial ventures. Its strategic location makes it a key training center for students from Kirinyaga and surrounding counties, fostering local development and providing pathways for students to gain relevant qualifications for employment or to start their own businesses.
institutions.short_description: Ndia Technical And Vocational College is a public TVET institution in Kutus, Kirinyaga County. institutions.name: Ngeria Technical Training Institituon

Ngeria Technical Training Institution (NTTI) is a public institution established in 2014 under the Ministry of Education, State Department for Vocational and Technical Training (TVET). Situated along the busy Eldoret-Nakuru Highway in Ngeria, Uasin Gishu County, the institution provides accessible technical and vocational education to students from the North Rift region and beyond. Its establishment was aimed at increasing access to technical training and producing skilled graduates to support Kenya's industrialization agenda by offering market-driven courses.

NTTI offers diverse programs across various departments, including Information and Communication Technology, Business Studies, Engineering Technology, Agriculture, Hospitality and Tourism, and Health Sciences. The institution is committed to providing quality training through practical-oriented learning, modern facilities, and qualified trainers. It is a recognized institution by the Kenya National Examinations Council (KNEC) and the Technical and Vocational Education and Training Authority (TVETA), ensuring its graduates meet national standards and are well-prepared for the workforce. NTTI plays a vital role in developing a skilled workforce for key sectors of the Kenyan economy.

Ngong Technical and Vocational College (TVC) is a public institution located in Ngong Town, Kajiado County, Kenya. Established with the aim of providing relevant and quality technical and vocational training, the college plays a crucial role in equipping the youth with practical skills necessary for industrial development and self-employment. Strategically situated along the Ngong-Suswa Road, the college provides an accessible learning environment for students from Kajiado and neighboring counties. institutions.name: Njoro Technical Training Institute
institutions.profile:
Njoro Technical Training Institute (NJOTTI) is a public TVET institution situated in Njoro Sub-County, Nakuru County, Kenya. Established under the Ministry of Education, the institute plays a crucial role in the country's technical and vocational training landscape. It is registered by the Technical and Vocational Education and Training Authority (TVETA) and is a recognised examination centre for various bodies, including KNEC, TVET CDACC, and NITA. Located conveniently within Njoro town, the institute serves students from Nakuru and surrounding regions, providing accessible technical education.

NJOTTI offers a diverse range of programs across different disciplines, including engineering, business studies, computing, and applied sciences. These courses are designed to equip students with practical skills and theoretical knowledge relevant to the current job market and entrepreneurial opportunities. With a focus on hands-on training and industry collaboration, Njoro TTI aims to produce competent graduates capable of contributing to national development. The institute is committed to fostering innovation, professionalism, and excellence in technical education, preparing students for successful careers and lifelong learning.
institutions.short_description: Njoro Technical Training Institute (NJOTTI) is a public TVET college in Njoro, Nakuru County. It offers diverse technical, vocational, and business programs aimed at equipping students with practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Nkabune Technical Training Institute
institutions.profile:
Nkabune Technical Training Institute (NTTI) is a public TVET institution located in Meru County, Kenya. It was established in 1987 as a community-based 'Harambee' institute and was officially handed over to the Government of Kenya in 1990. Situated along the Meru-Maua road, NTTI is recognized by the Ministry of Education and is listed by the Kenya Universities and Colleges Central Placement Service (KUCCPS) for student intake. The institute is dedicated to providing technical and vocational education and training (TVET) programs that equip learners with relevant skills and knowledge to meet the demands of various industries and contribute to national development.

Over the years, Nkabune TTI has grown significantly in terms of infrastructure, academic programs, and student enrollment. It offers a wide array of certificate and diploma courses in fields such as engineering, building and construction, business studies, hospitality, and information communication technology, among others. The institute emphasizes practical training and aims to produce competent graduates who are ready for employment or entrepreneurship. NTTI plays a crucial role in providing accessible and quality TVET to students primarily from Meru County and other parts of Kenya, fostering a skilled workforce essential for socio-economic growth.
institutions.short_description: Nkabune Technical Training Institute is a public TVET institution in Meru County, Kenya. Established in 1987, it offers diverse technical and business certificate and diploma programs focused on practical skills and industry relevance for career and entrepreneurial development.

institutions.name: North Eastern National Polytechnic
institutions.profile:
North Eastern National Polytechnic (NESPOLY) is a public TVET institution located in Garissa, Garissa County, Kenya. Established in 2016, the polytechnic is strategically situated approximately 3km from Garissa Town along Sankuri Road. Its establishment was part of the Kenyan government's initiative to expand access to technical and vocational training across the country, particularly in underserved regions. As a national polytechnic, NESPOLY plays a crucial role in providing relevant, practical skills and knowledge to students from the North Eastern region and beyond.

The institution is mandated to offer diploma and certificate programs aligned with national development goals and market demands. It houses various departments including Business Studies, Engineering disciplines (Electrical, Building & Civil), ICT, Applied Sciences, Agriculture, and Liberal Studies. These programs are designed to equip graduates with competencies for employment, entrepreneurship, and further education. NESPOLY contributes significantly to building a skilled workforce and promoting economic development in the North Eastern part of Kenya, serving as a key centre for technical education.
institutions.short_description: North Eastern National Polytechnic (NESPOLY) is a public TVET institution in Garissa, Kenya, established in 2016. It offers diverse diploma and certificate programs across various technical and business fields to provide essential skills for regional and national development.

other_info.address: 3km from Garissa Town, along Sankuri Road off Ngamia Road, after the former KEN GEN Power station. P.O.Box 329 -70100, Garissa

institutions.name: North Horr Technical & Vocational College
institutions.profile:
North Horr Technical & Vocational College is a public TVC located in the remote town of North Horr, within the arid and semi-arid region of Marsabit County, Kenya. Established as part of the Kenyan government's initiative to expand access to technical and vocational training across the country, the college plays a crucial role in providing essential skills and knowledge to the youth and local community in a region historically underserved by higher education institutions. Its strategic location in North Horr town makes it a vital resource for residents seeking to gain practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship.

The college offers a diverse range of technical and business programs designed to meet the needs of the local economy and broader national development goals. Courses typically include areas such as Building Technology, Electrical and Electronic Engineering, Automotive Engineering, Business Management, Information Communication Technology, and various trades. By focusing on hands-on training and practical application, North Horr TVC equips students with the competencies necessary to thrive in various sectors, contributing to the socio-economic growth of Marsabit County and empowering individuals with marketable skills. The institution operates under the Ministry of Education and is listed on the KUCCPS portal, allowing students to apply for placement and government funding.
institutions.short_description: North Horr Technical & Vocational College is a public institution in North Horr, Marsabit County, providing vital technical and vocational education and training programs to the local community, fostering skill development and economic empowerment in the arid region.

institutions.name: North Rift Technical & Vocational College
institutions.profile:
North Rift Technical & Vocational College (NRTVC) is a public TVC located in Eldama Ravine Town, Baringo County. Established to provide accessible and quality technical and vocational education and training, the college plays a crucial role in equipping the youth from the Rift Valley region and beyond with market-relevant skills. NRTVC is registered under the TVET Act and operates under the Ministry of Education, contributing significantly to Kenya's efforts in developing a skilled workforce.

The college offers a diverse range of certificate and diploma programs across various disciplines, including engineering, building and construction, business studies, hospitality, and information technology. These programs are designed to be practical-oriented, ensuring graduates are well-prepared for employment or entrepreneurship. NRTVC is situated in the Kapsoya Estate area of Eldama Ravine, making it a key educational institution within the community and the broader Baringo County.
institutions.short_description: North Rift Technical & Vocational College is a public TVET institution located in Eldama Ravine, Baringo County. It offers a variety of certificate and diploma programs aimed at providing technical and vocational skills to the youth.

institutions.name: Nuu Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Nuu Technical And Vocational College (Nuu TVC) is a public institution under the Ministry of Education, located in Nuu Town, Mwingi East Constituency, Kitui County, Kenya. Established to address the growing demand for skilled personnel, the college plays a crucial role in the region by offering accessible and relevant technical and vocational education. It aims to empower local youth and beyond with practical skills that are directly applicable to various industries, thereby contributing to economic development and reducing unemployment. The college is strategically situated to serve students from Kitui and surrounding areas, providing a conducive learning environment for hands-on training.

Nuu TVC offers a diverse range of programs across various disciplines, including engineering, business, and information technology. These courses are designed to meet the current market needs and are taught by qualified instructors using modern equipment and facilities. The institution is committed to producing competent and innovative graduates who can excel in their chosen careers or entrepreneurial ventures. By focusing on practical skills and theoretical knowledge, Nuu TVC prepares students for successful transitions into the workforce, enhancing their employability and potential for self-reliance. Its programs are aligned with national development goals, emphasizing the importance of technical training in building a skilled workforce for Kenya's future.
institutions.short_description: Nuu Technical And Vocational College is a public TVC located in Nuu Town, Mwingi East, Kitui County. It provides quality technical and vocational training to equip students with essential skills for employment and self-reliance in various fields.

other_info.address: Nuu Market, Nuu Town, P.O. Box 164 - 90200, Kitui

institutions.name: Nyakach Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Nyakach Technical and Vocational College (Nyakach TVC) is a public TVET institution located in Nyakach Constituency, Kisumu County, Kenya. Established to provide accessible and quality technical and vocational training, the college plays a crucial role in empowering the youth and community through practical skills development. It is registered and regulated by the Technical and Vocational Education and Training Authority (TVETA), ensuring its programs meet national standards and contribute to producing competent graduates for various industries. The college is situated in a serene environment, offering a conducive learning atmosphere.

Nyakach TVC offers a diverse range of courses at Diploma, Certificate, and Artisan levels across various fields, including engineering, business, information technology, building technology, and applied sciences. The curriculum is designed to be industry-relevant, equipping students with the hands-on skills and theoretical knowledge necessary for employment or entrepreneurship. As a public institution under the Ministry of Education, Nyakach TVC is committed to contributing to Kenya's Vision 2030 by enhancing technical capacity and fostering innovation among its students.
institutions.short_description: Nyakach Technical and Vocational College is a public TVET institution in Nyakach, Kisumu County, Kenya. It offers diverse Diploma, Certificate, and Artisan programs focused on technical and vocational skills development to empower youth for employment and entrepreneurship.

other_info.location: Along Pap Onditi-Katito Road, Nyakach, Kisumu County

institutions.name: Nyamira National Polytechnic
institutions.profile:
Nyamira National Polytechnic (NNP) is a leading public TVET institution located in Nyamira Town, Nyamira County, Kenya. It is one of the national polytechnics in the country, recognized for its commitment to providing quality technical, industrial, vocational, and entrepreneurship training. Established to meet the growing demand for skilled manpower, NNP plays a crucial role in the socio-economic development of the region and the nation by equipping students with practical skills and knowledge relevant to various industries. The polytechnic offers a wide array of programs ranging from certificate to diploma levels across diverse fields, including engineering, applied sciences, business studies, hospitality, and ICT.

Strategically situated near Nyamira Town along the Nyamira-Kisii Road, the polytechnic serves students from Nyamira and neighbouring counties. NNP boasts modern facilities, including workshops, laboratories, lecture halls, and a library, designed to facilitate effective learning and practical training. The institution is dedicated to fostering innovation, research, and community engagement. Its reputation is built on consistently producing competent graduates who are well-prepared for the job market and entrepreneurship, contributing significantly to various sectors of the economy. The polytechnic operates under the Ministry of Education and is a key player in the government's initiative to enhance technical and vocational training.
institutions.short_description: Nyamira National Polytechnic is a public TVET institution in Nyamira Town, Kenya, offering diverse certificate and diploma programs in technical, vocational, and entrepreneurial fields to equip students with practical skills for the job market.

institutions.name: Nyandarua National Polytechnic
institutions.profile:
Nyandarua National Polytechnic (formerly Ol Kalou Technical Training Institute) is a public TVET institution located in Ol Kalou Town, Nyandarua County, Kenya. Established as a technical institute, it was upgraded to a national polytechnic in 2016, signifying its expanded role and capacity in providing technical and vocational education and training. Situated along the Ol Kalou-Nyahururu Highway, it plays a crucial role in equipping students from Nyandarua and surrounding regions with practical skills and knowledge essential for various industries and entrepreneurship.

The polytechnic offers a diverse range of programs across various disciplines, including Engineering, Building Technology, Information Communication Technology (ICT), Business Studies, Hospitality and Tourism, Agriculture, and Applied Sciences. These programs are offered at different levels, including Artisan, Certificate, Diploma, and Higher Diploma, catering to varying educational backgrounds and career aspirations. Nyandarua National Polytechnic is committed to producing competent graduates who can contribute effectively to the economic development of Kenya, emphasizing hands-on training, innovation, and industry linkages.
institutions.short_description: Nyandarua National Polytechnic is a public TVET institution in Ol Kalou, Nyandarua County, Kenya. It offers diverse technical and vocational programs in engineering, business, ICT, hospitality, and agriculture, providing essential skills for the job market and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Nyando Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Nyando Technical And Vocational College (NYANDO TVC) is a public TVC located in Nyando Constituency, Kisumu County, Kenya, near Ahero Town. Established to provide accessible technical and vocational education and training, the college plays a crucial role in equipping the youth from the region and beyond with market-relevant skills. Its strategic location serves communities around Kisumu County, offering pathways to gainful employment and entrepreneurship.

The college offers a diverse range of programs at Diploma, Certificate, and Artisan levels across various fields including engineering (electrical, automotive, building), business studies, ICT, hospitality, and agriculture. Nyando TVC is committed to producing competent and skilled graduates who can contribute effectively to national development. Through practical training and industry linkages, the institution aims to be a centre of excellence in technical education in the Nyanza region.
institutions.short_description: Nyando Technical And Vocational College is a public institution in Kisumu County offering diverse technical and vocational training programs at Diploma, Certificate, and Artisan levels, near Ahero Town. It equips youth with practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship, contributing to national development.

institutions.name: Nyeri National Polytechnic
institutions.profile:
Nyeri National Polytechnic is a leading public TVET institution located in Nyeri County, Kenya. Established initially in 1956 as a Technical High School, it has undergone several transformations, evolving into a Technical Institute in 1967 and then the Nyeri Technical Training Institute in 1972. Its upgrade to a National Polytechnic in 2013 cemented its status as a key player in Kenya's technical education sector. Situated along the Nyeri-Karatina road, the polytechnic is easily accessible and serves students from the central region and beyond.

The polytechnic is dedicated to providing high-quality technical, industrial, and entrepreneurial training to produce competent manpower for national development. It offers a diverse range of certificate, diploma, and higher diploma programs across various fields including engineering, business studies, ICT, applied sciences, and liberal studies. With modern workshops, laboratories, and experienced faculty, Nyeri National Polytechnic has built a reputation for its practical-oriented training approach, making its graduates highly sought after in the job market and contributing significantly to the country's economic growth.
institutions.short_description: Nyeri National Polytechnic is a public TVET institution in Nyeri, Kenya, offering practical, market-driven certificate, diploma, and higher diploma courses across engineering, business, ICT, and applied sciences to train skilled personnel for national development.

institutions.name: Okame Technical And Vocational College
programs: Diploma in Electrical and Electronic Engineering (Power)
Diploma in Food Science and Technology
Diploma in Environmental Science and Technology
Diploma in Social Work and Community Development
Certificate in Electrical and Electronic Engineering (Power)
Certificate in Food Science and Technology
Certificate in Environmental Science and Technology
Certificate in Social Work and Community Development
institutions.profile:
Okame Technical And Vocational College is a public technical and vocational education and training (TVET) institution situated in the rural setting of Okame, within Teso South Constituency, Busia County, Kenya. Established to enhance skills development and contribute to national growth, the college provides accessible and relevant training opportunities for youth and adults in the region and beyond. Its location plays a crucial role in serving communities in Busia and neighboring areas, bridging the gap in technical education access.

The college offers diverse programs at diploma and certificate levels, covering fields such as engineering, business, information technology, agriculture, and health sciences. These courses are designed in line with industry needs, focusing on practical skills and hands-on experience to prepare graduates for the job market or to start their own ventures. While relatively young compared to older institutions, Okame TVC is building a reputation for its commitment to quality training and contributing to the socio-economic development of its catchment area through skilled manpower.
institutions.short_description: Okame Technical And Vocational College (OKAME TVC) is a public TVET institution located in Okame area, Teso South Constituency, Busia County, Kenya. It offers a wide range of market-driven diploma and certificate courses across various disciplines, aiming to equip students with practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Ol Kalou Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Ol Kalou Technical And Vocational College (OTVC) is a public TVET institution located in Ol Kalou town, Nyandarua County, Kenya. Established in 2014, the college falls under the Ministry of Education and plays a crucial role in providing accessible technical and vocational training to students from Nyandarua and surrounding regions. Situated along Ol Kalou-Rurii Road, it serves as a key centre for skills development in the area, aiming to equip learners with practical knowledge and competencies required for various industries and entrepreneurship.

The college offers a diverse range of diploma, certificate, and artisan courses across various disciplines including engineering technologies (electrical, building), business studies, information communication technology (ICT), agriculture, hospitality, and applied sciences. As a government institution, OTVC is mandated to contribute to the national agenda of producing a skilled workforce aligned with market demands. Its programs are designed to meet national standards and prepare graduates for direct employment or further education, fostering local economic growth and individual empowerment through practical, hands-on training.
institutions.short_description: Ol Kalou Technical And Vocational College is a public TVET institution in Ol Kalou, Nyandarua County. Established in 2014, it offers diverse technical, vocational, and business programs, equipping students with essential skills.

other_info.location: Along Ol Kalou-Rurii Road, Ol Kalou

institutions.name: Ol'Lessos Technical Training Institute
programs: Diploma in Electrical and Electronic Engineering
Certificate in Fashion Design and Garment Making
Certificate in Electrical and Electronic Engineering
institutions.profile:
Ol'Lessos Technical Training Institute (OLTIS) is a government-funded public technical and vocational education and training (TVET) institution situated in Ol'Lessos Town, Tinderet Constituency, Nandi County, Kenya. Established with the mandate of providing accessible and quality technical and business training, OLTIS plays a crucial role in developing a skilled workforce for the nation's industrial and economic growth. The institute is registered under the Ministry of Education and falls under the TVET Directorate, adhering to national standards for technical education.

The institute offers diverse programs at diploma, certificate, and artisan levels across various departments, including Engineering (Automotive, Electrical, Building), Information Communication Technology, Business Studies, Home Science, and Applied Sciences. These programs are designed to be hands-on, combining theoretical knowledge with practical application, often incorporating workshops, labs, and industrial attachments. OLTIS aims to produce graduates who are not only technically competent but also innovative and entrepreneurial, ready to meet the demands of the job market or create their own opportunities. Its location in Nandi County serves the local community and surrounding regions, providing essential post-secondary technical training opportunities.
institutions.short_description: Ol'Lessos Technical Training Institute is a public TVET institution located in Ol'Lessos, Nandi County, Kenya. It offers a wide range of technical and vocational training programs aimed at equipping students with practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship, contributing significantly to national development through quality technical education.

institutions.name: Omuga Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Omuga Technical And Vocational College (Omuga TVC) is a public technical and vocational training institution located in Oyugis town, Kasipul Constituency, Homa Bay County. Established under the Kenyan government's initiative to expand access to technical education, Omuga TVC plays a crucial role in providing skills-based training to the youth in the Nyanza region and beyond. The college is registered and regulated by the Technical and Vocational Education and Training Authority (TVETA) and is a key institution for students seeking technical courses through the Kenya Universities and Colleges Central Placement Service (KUCCPS).

The college offers a diverse range of diploma, certificate, and artisan programs across various disciplines including Engineering, Business, Information Communication Technology, and Applied Sciences. These programs are designed to meet the demands of the modern job market and equip students with practical skills and knowledge essential for employment or entrepreneurship. With its location along the Kisii - Homa Bay Highway, Omuga TVC is accessible and serves as a vital center for technical skill development, contributing significantly to the economic empowerment and development of the community.
institutions.short_description: Omuga Technical And Vocational College is a public TVC located in Oyugis, Homa Bay County, offering a variety of technical, vocational, and business programs to equip students with essential skills for the job market and self-employment.

other_info.address: Along Kisii - Homa Bay Highway, Oyugis

institutions.name: Open University Of Kenya
programs: Bachelor of Business and Management in Entrepreneurship
Bachelor of Agriculture and Food Security
Postgraduate Diploma in Leadership and Accountability
Postgraduate Diploma in Learning Design and Technology
institutions.profile:
The Open University of Kenya (OUK) marks a significant milestone in Kenya's higher education landscape, being the nation's first dedicated public open university. Chartered by President William Ruto in August 2023, OUK is strategically positioned to address the growing demand for flexible and accessible learning opportunities. Located within the ambitious Konza Technopolis in Machakos County, the university operates primarily on an online and blended learning model, removing geographical barriers and allowing students to study at their own pace and schedule. This approach is particularly beneficial for working professionals, lifelong learners, and individuals in remote areas who might face constraints with traditional campus-based education.

OUK offers a diverse range of market-driven programs designed to equip learners with skills relevant to the digital economy and national development goals. Its initial program offerings include Bachelor's degrees in fields like Data Science, Technology Education, Business and Management, Economics and Statistics, Cyber Security, and Agriculture, alongside Postgraduate Diplomas in areas such as Leadership and Learning Design. While a relatively new institution, OUK is rapidly building a reputation for innovation in pedagogy and its commitment to widening access to quality higher learning, aligning with global trends in online and distance education.
institutions.short_description: Open University of Kenya (OUK) is Kenya's pioneering public open university, offering flexible, accessible, and technology-driven higher education programs primarily through online and blended learning. Located in Konza City, Machakos County, OUK aims to democratize access to quality education for a diverse range of learners.

institutions.name: Orogare Technical And Vocational College
programs: Craft Certificate in Supply Chain Management
Craft Certificate in Information Communication Technology
Craft Certificate in Human Resource Management
institutions.profile:
Orogare Technical And Vocational College is a public technical and vocational training institution located in Orogare Town, Nyamache Constituency, Kisii County, Kenya. Established under the Ministry of Education, the college plays a vital role in providing accessible technical, industrial, and entrepreneurial skills training to the youth in the region and beyond. Its mission is to equip students with practical knowledge and competencies that are responsive to the needs of the job market and national development.

The college offers a range of programs at different levels, including Diploma, Craft Certificate, and Artisan Certificates, across various disciplines such as Business Management, Information Communication Technology, Supply Chain Management, and Human Resource Management. These programs are designed to foster innovation, self-reliance, and employability among graduates. As a key player in the TVET sector in Kisii County, Orogare TVC is committed to contributing to the socio-economic growth of the community by producing skilled and competent professionals.
institutions.short_description: Orogare Technical And Vocational College is a public TVET institution in Orogare, Nyamache Constituency, Kisii County, offering diverse Diploma, Craft, and Artisan programs in technical and business fields to equip students with market-ready skills.

institutions.name: Pan Africa Christian University
institutions.profile:
Pan Africa Christian University (PAC University) is a distinguished private Christian university situated in Nairobi, Kenya. Founded in 1978 as a Bible college, it has evolved significantly over the decades, gaining university status and expanding its academic offerings beyond theology. The university is chartered and accredited by the Commission for University Education (CUE) in Kenya, a testament to its commitment to maintaining high academic standards. PAC University is known for its focus on integrating Christian faith with academic learning, aiming to produce leaders grounded in integrity and service.

The university's main campus is located in the Roysambu area, off Thika Road, providing a conducive environment for learning and growth. PAC University offers a wide range of programs through its various schools, including the School of Theology, School of Business, School of Humanities and Social Sciences, and the School of Education, alongside a robust Graduate School. These programs cover diverse fields from business and communication to psychology and education, attracting a diverse student body seeking holistic development and professional competence. The university has built a reputation for fostering a vibrant community and equipping graduates with skills and values relevant for impacting society positively.
institutions.short_description: Pan Africa Christian University (PAC University) is a premier private Christian institution in Nairobi, Kenya, established in 1978. It offers a wide range of accredited programs, emphasizing academic excellence and Christian leadership development.

other_info.address: P.O. Box 56749 – 00200 Nairobi, Lumumba Drive, Off Thika Road, Roysambu

institutions.name: Pc Kinyanjui Technical Training Institute
institutions.profile:
PC Kinyanjui Technical Training Institute is a public tertiary institution located in Kikuyu Constituency, Nairobi County, Kenya. Established to provide technical and vocational training, the institute aims to equip students with practical skills and knowledge relevant to various industries. The institute offers a range of courses in fields such as engineering, automotive technology, building and construction, business studies, and information technology.

The institute is committed to fostering innovation, entrepreneurship, and ethical values among its students. Through hands-on training, workshops, and industrial attachments, students gain real-world experience that prepares them for the job market. PC Kinyanjui Technical Training Institute plays a vital role in contributing to the country's skilled workforce and economic development by providing quality technical education and training.
institutions.short_description: PC Kinyanjui Technical Training Institute, located in Kikuyu, Nairobi, is a public institution offering technical and vocational training in engineering, automotive, construction, business, and IT to foster innovation and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Presbyterian University Of East Africa
institutions.profile:
The Presbyterian University of East Africa (PUEA) is a private university located in Kikuyu, Kiambu County, Kenya. It was founded by the Presbyterian Church of East Africa in 2007. The university is committed to providing quality education that is both relevant and accessible to students from all backgrounds. PUEA offers a wide range of undergraduate and graduate programs in areas such as business, theology, education, and arts and social sciences. These programs are designed to equip students with the knowledge and skills they need to succeed in their chosen fields.

The university is known for its strong emphasis on Christian values and ethics. PUEA aims to produce graduates who are not only academically proficient but also morally upright and socially responsible. The university's main campus in Kikuyu provides a serene and conducive learning environment. PUEA also has several satellite campuses in other parts of Kenya. With its commitment to academic excellence and holistic development, PUEA has established itself as a reputable institution of higher learning in East Africa.
institutions.short_description: The Presbyterian University of East Africa (PUEA), located in Kikuyu, Kiambu, is a private university founded by the Presbyterian Church of East Africa, committed to quality, ethical education.

institutions.name: Pwani University
institutions.profile:
Pwani University (PU), located in Kilifi, Kenya, is a public institution committed to providing quality education, fostering research, and engaging in community outreach. Established as a constituent college of Kenyatta University, it gained full university status in 2007. The university's coastal location provides a unique environment for studying marine sciences and related fields, alongside a broad range of other academic disciplines.

Pwani University offers a wide array of undergraduate and postgraduate programs across various faculties, including Science, Applied Sciences, Humanities and Social Sciences, and Business. The university is particularly known for its programs in marine and aquatic sciences, tourism, and hospitality. Its commitment to research is evident through its various research centers and projects, focusing on areas such as coastal resource management, climate change, and sustainable development. Pwani University strives to produce well-rounded graduates who are equipped to contribute to national and global development.
institutions.short_description: Pwani University, a public university in Kilifi, Kenya, excels in marine sciences, tourism, and hospitality programs, fostering research and community development. It is committed to quality education.

institutions.name: Pwani University Tvet College
institutions.profile:
Pwani University TVET College is a constituent college of Pwani University, located in Kilifi County, Kenya. It offers a range of technical and vocational training programs designed to equip students with practical skills for various industries. The college aims to contribute to the socio-economic development of the region and the country by producing skilled graduates who are ready to enter the workforce or start their own businesses.

The college provides courses in fields such as engineering, agriculture, business, and hospitality. Its affiliation with Pwani University ensures access to resources and academic support, enhancing the quality of education. Pwani University TVET College is committed to providing quality training and fostering innovation to meet the changing demands of the Kenyan job market.
institutions.short_description: Pwani University TVET College, located in Kilifi, offers diverse technical and vocational programs as part of Pwani University, equipping students with practical skills for Kenya's industries.

institutions.name: Rachuonyo Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Rachuonyo Technical and Vocational College is a public institution located in Oyugis, Rachuonyo South Constituency, Homa Bay County. The college is dedicated to providing high-quality technical and vocational training to equip students with practical skills for the job market. It aims to contribute to the socio-economic development of the region and the country by producing competent and skilled graduates. The college offers a range of certificate and diploma courses in various fields such as building and construction, electrical engineering, mechanical engineering, agriculture, business, and ICT.

The institution focuses on hands-on training and practical experience. Rachuonyo Technical and Vocational College strives to create a conducive learning environment with modern workshops, laboratories, and experienced instructors. It plays a crucial role in empowering the youth with employable skills, fostering innovation, and promoting entrepreneurship. Through its programs, the college addresses the technical skills gap in the region and supports the government's agenda of industrialization and economic growth. The college is committed to providing accessible and affordable training to all, contributing to the overall development of Homa Bay County and beyond.
institutions.short_description: Rachuonyo Technical and Vocational College, located in Oyugis, Homa Bay County, is a public TVC offering diverse technical and vocational programs aimed at equipping students with practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Railway Training Institute
institutions.profile:
The Railway Training Institute (RTI), located in Nairobi, Kenya, is a premier institution dedicated to providing specialized training and education in railway operations, technology, and management. Established to support the growth and efficiency of the railway sector in Kenya and the broader East African region, RTI offers a range of courses designed to equip students with the skills and knowledge necessary for successful careers in the industry. These courses cover various aspects of railway engineering, signaling, rolling stock maintenance, and railway transport management.

Over the years, RTI has built a strong reputation for its commitment to excellence and its close collaboration with railway companies and other stakeholders. The institute's strategic location in Nairobi, a major transportation hub, allows it to provide practical, hands-on training that reflects real-world challenges and opportunities. RTI's mission is to produce highly competent professionals who can contribute to the safe, reliable, and sustainable operation of railway systems, playing a vital role in the economic development of the region. The institute continues to adapt its programs to meet the evolving needs of the railway industry, incorporating the latest technologies and best practices.
institutions.short_description: The Railway Training Institute (RTI) in Nairobi is a premier public college specializing in railway operations, engineering, and management. RTI equips students with skills for successful railway careers.

institutions.name: Ramogi Institute Of Advance Technology
institutions.profile:
Ramogi Institute of Advanced Technology (RIAT) is a public tertiary institution located in Kisumu, Kenya. Established to provide technical and vocational training, RIAT plays a crucial role in equipping students with practical skills relevant to various industries. The institute offers a range of programs, including diploma and certificate courses in fields such as engineering, business, information technology, and hospitality. institutions.name: Regional Centre For Mapping Of Resources For Development
institutions.parent_ministry: Ministry of Lands and Physical Planning
institutions.profile:
The Regional Centre for Mapping of Resources for Development (RCMRD) is an intergovernmental organization established in 1975 under the auspices of the United Nations Economic Commission for Africa (UNECA). Its mission is to promote sustainable development in its member States through the generation, application, and dissemination of geo-information and allied technologies. RCMRD provides training, research, and services in various fields, including remote sensing, Geographic Information Systems (GIS), surveying, and mapping. The center plays a crucial role in building capacity within its member states for natural resource management, disaster risk reduction, and environmental monitoring.

Located in Nairobi, Kenya, RCMRD serves as a hub for geospatial data and expertise for Eastern and Southern Africa. The institution offers a range of programs, workshops, and short courses designed to equip professionals with the skills necessary to utilize geospatial technologies effectively. RCMRD collaborates with international partners, research institutions, and member states to address regional challenges related to climate change, food security, and urbanization. Its reputation as a leading geospatial center in the region is built on its commitment to innovation, excellence, and service to its member states.
institutions.short_description: The Regional Centre for Mapping of Resources for Development (RCMRD) in Nairobi promotes sustainable development through geo-information technologies, offering training, research, and services in mapping, remote sensing, and GIS for Eastern and Southern Africa.

institutions.name: Rongo University
institutions.profile:
Rongo University (RU) is a public chartered university located in Rongo, Migori County, Kenya. Originally established as a constituent college of Moi University in 2011, it was granted its charter on January 19, 2016, becoming a fully-fledged university. Situated approximately 8 kilometers from Rongo town on the Kitere road, the university occupies a serene rural setting conducive to learning.

RU is committed to providing quality education, fostering research, and promoting community engagement. It offers a diverse range of academic programs across various faculties and schools, including Education, Business and Human Resource Development, Arts and Social Sciences, Information and Communication Technology, and Health Sciences. The university plays a significant role in the socio-economic development of the region through its academic offerings, research initiatives focused on local challenges, and community outreach programs.

Its strategic location in a rapidly developing industrial area allows for strong links with local industries, providing students with opportunities for industrial attachments and enhancing their employability upon graduation. The institution is equipped with workshops, laboratories, and learning facilities designed to provide hands-on experience. It serves students from Kiambu County and beyond, contributing significantly to the development of a skilled workforce essential for the country's economic growth. The college strives to foster innovation, entrepreneurship, and ethical values among its trainees, preparing them not just for employment but also for creating their own ventures.

institutions.name: Siruti Technical & Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Siruti Technical and Vocational College is a public institution located in Siruti, Suna West constituency, Migori County, Kenya. The college is dedicated to providing quality technical and vocational training to equip students with the skills and knowledge necessary for success in various industries. It aims to address the growing demand for skilled workforce in the region and contribute to the economic development of Kenya. The college provides a range of courses including building technology, automotive engineering, electrical and electronics engineering, and ICT, tailored to meet the needs of the local and national job markets. 

Siruti Technical and Vocational College is committed to fostering a conducive learning environment that promotes innovation, creativity, and entrepreneurship. The institution focuses on practical, hands-on training to ensure that graduates are well-prepared to enter the workforce or start their own businesses. With experienced faculty and modern facilities, Siruti TVC strives to be a leading center for technical and vocational education in Migori County and beyond. The college also emphasizes community engagement and offers programs to support local development initiatives.
institutions.short_description: Siruti Technical & Vocational College is a public TVC in Migori County, offering quality technical training in fields like engineering and ICT. It focuses on practical skills and community development.

institutions.name: Sot Technical Training Institute
institutions.profile:
Sot Technical Training Institute (Sot TTI) is a public TVET institution situated in Sot Town, Chepalungu Constituency, Bomet County, Kenya. Established with the aim of providing quality technical and vocational education, the institute serves students from Bomet and the wider region, equipping them with essential skills for employment and entrepreneurship. It is registered and regulated by the Technical and Vocational Education and Training Authority (TVETA) and offers a diverse array of courses at Certificate and Diploma levels across various faculties.

The institution is dedicated to producing competent and innovative graduates who can contribute significantly to the socio-economic development of the nation. Sot TTI focuses on practical, hands-on training alongside theoretical knowledge in fields such as engineering, business studies, information communication technology, agriculture, and hospitality. Its location makes it a vital educational hub in Bomet County, providing accessible technical training opportunities and playing a key role in empowering the youth through skills development aligned with national development goals and industry needs.
institutions.short_description: Sot Technical Training Institute is a public TVET institution in Sot Town, Bomet County, Kenya. It offers a wide range of certificate and diploma programs in technical, business, and vocational fields, aiming to equip students with practical skills for the job market.

other_info.location: Along Mulot-Sot Road, near Sot Town

institutions.name: Sotik Technical Training Institute
institutions.profile:
Sotik Technical Training Institute (STTI) is a reputable public Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) institution situated in Sotik Town, Bomet County, along the busy Sotik-Kericho Highway. Established to provide accessible and quality technical training in the South Rift region, STTI operates under the Ministry of Education, Science and Technology. The institute is committed to producing skilled and competent graduates ready to contribute to national development through various technical, business, and service sectors. It serves a diverse student body from Bomet and surrounding counties, playing a crucial role in enhancing vocational skills in the area.

STTI offers a variety of programs at diploma, certificate, and artisan levels across disciplines such as Engineering, ICT, Business Studies, Hospitality & Tourism, Agriculture, and Liberal Studies. The curriculum is designed to be industry-relevant, incorporating practical sessions and workshops to ensure students gain hands-on experience. With a focus on academic excellence, innovation, and student welfare, Sotik TTI aims to be a leading TVET institution in Kenya, fostering a learning environment that promotes creativity, discipline, and entrepreneurial spirit among its students. The institute also collaborates with various industry partners to provide attachment opportunities and ensure curriculum responsiveness to market needs.
institutions.short_description: Sotik Technical Training Institute is a public TVET institution located in Sotik, Bomet County, Kenya. It offers a wide range of technical, business, and hospitality programs aimed at equipping students with practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: South Eastern Kenya University
institutions.profile:
In the heart of Kitui County lies a vibrant institution dedicated to nurturing academic excellence, community engagement, and sustainable development: South Eastern Kenya University (SEKU). SEKU traces its origins to 2008 when it was established as a Constituent College of the University of Nairobi, then known as South Eastern University College (SEUC). It attained its status as a fully-fledged public university through a Charter granted in 2013. Located in Kwa Vonza, along the Kitui-Mutomo Highway, SEKU serves the Eastern region and beyond, making quality higher education accessible.

As one of the growing public universities in Kenya, SEKU is quickly earning a name for itself among learners seeking affordable, quality education, especially in regions outside Nairobi. The university offers a wide range of degree and diploma programs across various faculties, including Pure and Applied Sciences, Education, Humanities and Social Sciences, Environment and Natural Resource Management, and Agriculture, Water and Environmental Engineering. SEKU is committed to producing graduates who are not only academically proficient but also socially responsible and equipped to contribute to national development and address global challenges.
institutions.short_description: A public university in Kitui County, established in 2013, offering diverse academic programs in sciences, education, humanities, and agriculture, focused on academic excellence and sustainable development.

institutions.name: South Eastern Kenya University Tvet - Migwani
institutions.profile:
South Eastern Kenya University (SEKU) operates a TVET Institute with various training centres, one of which is located in Migwani town. The SEKU TVET Institute is dedicated to providing quality technical and vocational education and training aimed at equipping students with practical skills relevant to the job market and entrepreneurial pursuits. As part of a public university, the TVET centre in Migwani adheres to national standards for TVET education, offering programs designed to foster innovation and empower individuals for socio-economic development in the region and beyond.

The Migwani TVET centre extends the reach of South Eastern Kenya University's technical training mandate to the Mwingi West constituency in Kitui County. It serves local communities by providing accessible TVET programs, contributing to capacity building and skill development in various trades and technical areas. While specific historical details of the Migwani centre might be integrated within the broader history of SEKU's TVET expansion, its establishment aligns with the university's commitment to decentralized education and training opportunities. The centre leverages the resources and academic foundation of SEKU to offer impactful TVET education.
institutions.short_description: SEKU TVET - Migwani is a technical and vocational training centre operated by South Eastern Kenya University, offering practical skills development programs in Migwani town, Kitui County, to empower local communities.

institutions.name: South Eastern Kenya University TVET Centre, Mtito Andei
programs: Diploma in Electrical and Electronics Engineering
Craft Certificate in Electrical and Electronic Technology
Craft Certificate in Fashion Design and Garment Making
institutions.profile:
South Eastern Kenya University (SEKU) is a public university located in Kitui County, Kenya. Established as a constituent college of the University of Nairobi in 2008 and awarded a charter in 2013, SEKU has grown to include satellite campuses and centres across the region, extending its reach and educational mandate. The university is dedicated to providing quality education, research, and innovation, contributing significantly to national development through various programs, including those focused on technical and vocational education and training (TVET). The Mtito Andei TVET Centre serves as a key component of SEKU's commitment to technical skills development in the lower Eastern region.

The SEKU TVET Centre in Mtito Andei is strategically located along the busy Mombasa-Nairobi highway, making it accessible to students from different areas. As part of the larger SEKU institution, the centre benefits from the university's resources and academic standards. It offers various Diploma and Craft Certificate programs designed to equip students with practical skills and knowledge relevant to the job market, contributing to the skilled workforce needed for Kenya's economic growth. The centre aims to provide accessible and quality TVET education to students in Makueni County and surrounding areas, fostering local talent and opportunities.
institutions.short_description: South Eastern Kenya University (SEKU) TVET Centre, Mtito Andei, is a public TVET institution located in Makueni County. It offers various technical and vocational Diploma and Craft Certificate programs, providing essential practical skills training as part of SEKU's mission to expand accessible quality education in the region.

institutions.name: St Joseph'S Technical Institute For The Deaf Nyang'Oma
institutions.profile:
St Joseph's Technical Institute for the Deaf Nyang'Oma is a specialized public technical and vocational education and training (TVET) institution situated in the Nyang'oma area of Siaya County, near Bondo town. Established under the Ministry of Education, the institute plays a crucial role in Kenya's inclusive education landscape by focusing exclusively on providing practical skills and vocational training for deaf students. Its curriculum is designed to accommodate the unique learning needs of deaf individuals, offering pathways to various technical trades and empowering them to overcome communication barriers and participate effectively in the workforce.

The institute is committed to equipping its students with marketable skills that promote economic independence and social integration. By offering tailored programs, specialized instruction, and a supportive learning environment, St Joseph's aims to build the capacity of deaf youth in Siaya County and beyond. Its existence underscores the government's effort to ensure that technical training is accessible to all segments of the population, contributing significantly to the development of skilled personnel within the country's TVET sector while championing the rights and potential of people with disabilities.
institutions.short_description: St Joseph's Technical Institute for the Deaf Nyang'Oma is a public TVET institution in Nyang'oma, Siaya County, near Bondo town. It specializes in technical and vocational training tailored for deaf students, empowering them with practical skills for employment and self-reliance.

institutions.name: St Pauls University
institutions.profile:
St. Paul's University (SPU) is a premier ecumenical Christian university located in Limuru, Kiambu County, Kenya. Its roots trace back to 1903 when it was founded by the Church Missionary Society (CMS) as a divinity school, primarily for training clergy. Over the decades, the institution grew and diversified its offerings, achieving the status of a constituent college of the University of Nairobi in 1992. This progression culminated in the award of its Charter in 2007, formally recognizing it as a fully-fledged university and marking a significant milestone in its history of providing higher education in Kenya.

SPU is renowned for its commitment to integrating Christian faith with academic learning, fostering holistic development among its students. While historically strong in theology, the university has expanded its academic portfolio significantly, offering a wide array of programs across faculties such as Business, Computing and Informatics, Education, Health Sciences, Humanities and Social Sciences, and Theology. The main campus is situated in the serene environment of Limuru, with additional campuses in Nairobi, Nakuru, and Machakos, making its educational opportunities accessible to a wider population across Kenya. The university aims to produce graduates who are not only academically competent but also ethically grounded and socially responsible leaders prepared to address contemporary challenges.
institutions.short_description: St. Paul's University is an ecumenical Christian institution in Limuru, Kiambu, Kenya, founded in 1903. It offers diverse programs integrating faith and learning across its main and branch campuses, aiming to develop ethical and competent leaders for society.

institutions.name: St. Augustine Teachers Training College - Eregi
programs: Diploma in Early Childhood Teacher Education (DECTE)
institutions.profile:
St. Augustine Teachers Training College (TTC) - Eregi is a public teacher training institution located in Eregi, Ikolomani Constituency, Kakamega County, Western Kenya. It is one of the prominent TTCs in the region, established to train qualified teachers for basic education levels in Kenya. The college falls under the Ministry of Education and admits students through the Kenya Universities and Colleges Central Placement Service (KUCCPS) for diploma programs, as well as direct entry for certificate courses.

The college offers various programs aligned with the current education curriculum in Kenya, including the Diploma in Primary Teacher Education (DPTE) and Diploma in Early Childhood Teacher Education (DECTE), which are the new standards for teacher training replacing the former PTE and ECDE certificates. St. Augustine TTC is dedicated to producing competent and professional educators equipped with the necessary pedagogical skills and knowledge to contribute effectively to the education sector in Kenya.
institutions.short_description: St. Augustine TTC Eregi is a public teacher training college in Kakamega County, Kenya. It offers Diploma and Certificate programs in Primary and Early Childhood Education, training teachers for the Kenyan education system.

institutions.name: St. John'S Teachers Training College - Kilimambogo
institutions.profile:
St. John's Teachers Training College (TTC) Kilimambogo is a public institution located in the Kilimambogo area near Thika Town, Kiambu County, Kenya. Established to train competent and dedicated primary school teachers, the college has been a significant contributor to the education sector in Kenya for many years. It focuses on providing quality training in Pedagogy, Subject Content, and professional ethics, preparing trainees to meet the demands of the Kenyan primary school curriculum, including the new Competency-Based Curriculum (CBC).

The college offers various teacher training programs, primarily focusing on the Diploma in Primary Teacher Education (DPTE) and the Diploma in Early Childhood Development and Education (DECDE). St. John's TTC Kilimambogo is known for its commitment to holistic formation, emphasizing not only academic excellence but also character development and community engagement. The college boasts adequate infrastructure, including lecture halls, a library, laboratories, hostels, and recreational facilities, creating a conducive learning environment. It attracts students from across the country, contributing to the diversity of its community and its reputation as a reputable teacher training institution in the region.
institutions.short_description: St. John's Teachers Training College (TTC) Kilimambogo is a public college near Thika, Kiambu County, Kenya, specializing in training primary school teachers for Diploma in Primary Teacher Education (DPTE) and Early Childhood Development and Education (DECDE).

institutions.name: St. Marks Teachers Training College-Kigari
programs: Diploma in Early Childhood Development and Education (ECDAE)
institutions.profile:
St. Marks Teachers Training College, located in Kigari, Embu County, is a renowned public institution with a rich history dating back to 1946. It was initially founded by the Catholic Church and later transitioned to government management, becoming a key player in teacher education in Kenya. Situated in a serene and conducive environment along the Embu-Meru Highway, the college provides an ideal setting for academic pursuit and professional development.

The college is dedicated to training competent and disciplined teachers for primary and early childhood development education sectors. Historically known for its P1 program, it now offers the Diploma in Primary Teacher Education (DPT) and Diploma in Early Childhood Development and Education (ECDAE) programs under the CBC curriculum framework. St. Marks TTC Kigari is recognized for its commitment to academic excellence and holistic development, producing educators who contribute significantly to the nation's education system.
institutions.short_description: St. Marks Teachers Training College, Kigari, is a public institution in Embu County, training teachers since 1946. Offering Diploma in Primary Teacher Education and Early Childhood Development Education programs, it is a reputable college known for its conducive learning environment and production of qualified educators.

institutions.name: Subukia Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Subukia Technical And Vocational College is a public institution situated in Subukia Town, Nakuru County, Kenya. Established to address the growing need for skilled manpower, the college operates under the Ministry of Education and is a KUCCPS-accredited institution. It is strategically located approximately 2 kilometers off the Nyahururu-Nakuru road, making it accessible to students from the surrounding areas and beyond.
