The college is dedicated to providing quality technical and vocational training that is responsive to industry needs and national development goals.

The college offers diverse certificate and diploma programs across various disciplines, including engineering, business, information technology, building technology, hospitality, and agriculture. These programs are designed with a strong practical component, ensuring that graduates are well-equipped with hands-on skills highly valued in the job market. Subukia TVC is committed to fostering a conducive learning environment, promoting innovation, and producing competent professionals and entrepreneurs who can contribute significantly to the socio-economic growth of Kenya. Its reputation is built on providing accessible, relevant, and quality TVET education.
institutions.short_description: Subukia Technical And Vocational College is a public TVC in Subukia Town, Nakuru County, Kenya, offering various technical, vocational, and business courses aimed at equipping students with practical skills for the job market and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Taita Taveta National Polytechnic
institutions.profile:
Taita Taveta National Polytechnic (TTNP) is a premier public TVET institution situated in Voi Town, Taita Taveta County, Kenya. Established under the TVET Act of 2013, the polytechnic plays a crucial role in the national development agenda by providing relevant and quality technical and vocational training. It aims to produce skilled and competent graduates capable of meeting the demands of the industry and contributing to socio-economic growth. The institution is committed to excellence in training, innovation, and research.

The polytechnic offers a diverse portfolio of programs across various disciplines, including engineering, building & civil engineering, applied sciences, business studies, hospitality, and information communication technology. These programs are offered at different levels, from artisan and certificate to diploma, catering to a wide range of learners' needs and career aspirations. TTNP emphasizes practical learning and industry linkages to ensure that its curriculum remains current and aligned with market demands. The institution is located strategically in Voi, making it accessible to students from the surrounding regions and beyond.
institutions.short_description: Taita Taveta National Polytechnic (TTNP) is a public TVET institution located in Voi, Taita Taveta County, Kenya. It offers a wide range of technical, vocational, and business programs aimed at equipping students with practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Taita Taveta University
institutions.profile:
Established in 2007 as a constituent college of Jomo Kenyatta University of Agriculture and Technology (JKUAT), Taita Taveta University (TTU) became a fully-fledged public university in 2013. Nestled in Voi, Taita Taveta County, its location near the vast Tsavo National Park provides a unique backdrop, integrating environmental and wildlife studies into its curriculum. The university was strategically founded to focus on harnessing and developing human capacity relevant to the arid and semi-arid lands (ASALs) and the mineral-rich Taita Taveta region, with a strong emphasis on mining, mineral processing, and environmental resource management.

Taita Taveta University offers a diverse range of academic programs across various fields, including Engineering, Science, Environmental Sciences, Education, Business, and Humanities. It is particularly renowned for its specialized programs aligned with the region's economic potential, such as Mining and Mineral Processing Engineering and Environmental Studies. TTU is committed to providing quality education that is both academically rigorous and practically relevant, fostering innovation and research aimed at addressing local and national development challenges. The university plays a vital role in the educational landscape of the Coast region and is recognized for its focus on sustainability and community engagement.
institutions.short_description: Taita Taveta University (TTU) is a public institution located in Voi, Taita Taveta County, Kenya. Established in 2013, it specializes in programs relevant to arid and semi-arid lands, mining, mineral processing, and environmental resource management, leveraging its proximity to Tsavo National Park for practical learning. It offers diverse programs and aims to contribute to regional development through quality education and research.

institutions.name: Tambach Teachers Training College
programs: Diploma in Early Childhood Development Education
institutions.profile:
Tambach Teachers Training College is a public institution located in Tambach town, Keiyo North Constituency, Elgeyo Marakwet County. Established under the Ministry of Education, it plays a crucial role in Kenya's education sector, primarily by training competent and qualified teachers for primary and early childhood levels. Situated in the scenic North Rift region, the college serves students from Elgeyo Marakwet and other parts of the country, contributing significantly to regional educational capacity and community development.

As a key player in teacher education, Tambach TTC offers programs such as the Diploma in Primary Teacher Education (DPTE) and Diploma in Early Childhood Development Education (DECDE). These programs are designed to equip trainees with essential pedagogical skills, subject knowledge, and professional ethics required for effective teaching in the contemporary educational landscape. The college is committed to fostering the holistic development of its students, preparing them not only as proficient educators but also as responsible citizens and positive role models within their communities.
institutions.short_description: Tambach Teachers Training College is a public institution in Tambach, Elgeyo Marakwet County, dedicated to training primary and early childhood educators through Diploma programs like DPTE and DECDE under the Ministry of Education.

institutions.name: Tana River Technical & Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Tana River Technical & Vocational College (TRTVC) is a public Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) institution located in Hola Town, Tana River County, Kenya. Established under the Ministry of Education, TRTVC plays a crucial role in providing accessible and relevant skills training to the youth and residents of the Tana River region and beyond. The college was founded as part of the government's initiative to expand TVET opportunities across the country, aiming to equip learners with practical skills necessary for employment, entrepreneurship, and national development, particularly focusing on the unique socio-economic context of arid and semi-arid lands (ASALs) like Tana River.

The college offers a diverse range of courses across various disciplines, including Information Communication Technology, Building & Civil Engineering, Electrical & Electronics Engineering, Business Studies, Agriculture, and Hospitality. These programs are designed to meet the current labor market demands and foster innovation. TRTVC is situated to serve a community that historically had limited access to technical training, making it a vital educational hub for local development and empowerment. Its location in Hola, the county headquarters, ensures accessibility for students from different parts of the county, contributing significantly to human capital development in the coastal region.
institutions.short_description: Tana River Technical & Vocational College (TRTVC) in Hola offers accessible TVET programs under the Ministry of Education. It provides skills training in technology, engineering, business, agriculture, and hospitality, empowering youth for employment and entrepreneurship in Tana River County.

institutions.name: Tangaza University
institutions.profile:
Tangaza University, located in Karen, Nairobi, is a leading private Catholic university founded by nine religious congregations in 1986. It transitioned from Tangaza College to a full university in 2015. The university is dedicated to providing holistic education grounded in faith and reason, aiming to form ethical servant leaders for church and society. It offers a diverse range of academic programs spanning theology, social sciences, education, and business, attracting a vibrant community of students from across East Africa and beyond.

Tangaza University is renowned for its commitment to academic excellence, spiritual formation, and social responsibility. The curriculum integrates rigorous academic study with practical application and community engagement, encouraging students to critically analyze contemporary issues and contribute positively to societal development. The university fosters a supportive and inclusive environment, promoting interdisciplinary dialogue and intellectual growth while upholding its core values rooted in the Catholic intellectual tradition. Its reputation is built on producing graduates who are not only academically competent but also possess strong ethical values and a deep commitment to service.
institutions.short_description: Tangaza University, located in Karen, Nairobi, is a private Catholic university focused on nurturing ethical servant leaders through a blend of academic rigor, spiritual formation, and social engagement.

institutions.name: Tarbaj Teachers Training College
institutions.profile:
Tarbaj Teachers Training College (TTC) is a public institution located in Tarbaj, Wajir County, in the North Eastern region of Kenya. Established under the Ministry of Education, the college plays a crucial role in training qualified primary school teachers to serve within Wajir County and the broader region. Its establishment is part of the government's effort to enhance access to teacher training opportunities in underserved areas and address the shortage of trained educators, particularly those familiar with the local context and culture.

The college primarily offers programs aimed at equipping individuals with the necessary skills and knowledge for teaching at the primary school level. These programs typically include Certificate and Diploma in Primary Teacher Education, covering various pedagogical approaches, subject content knowledge, and practical teaching experience. By focusing on local needs and contexts, Tarbaj TTC contributes significantly to building the capacity of the education sector in Wajir County, empowering local youth and improving the quality of primary education in the area.
institutions.short_description: Tarbaj Teachers Training College is a public institution located in Tarbaj, Wajir County, focusing on training primary school teachers. It offers Certificate and Diploma programs vital for regional educational development.

institutions.name: Tarbaj Technical & Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Tarbaj Technical & Vocational College (TVC) is a public technical institution located in Tarbaj, Wajir County, Kenya. Established to address the growing demand for technical skills in the region, the college plays a crucial role in providing accessible and relevant vocational training to the youth and local community. It operates under the Ministry of Education and is registered by the Technical and Vocational Education and Training Authority (TVETA).

The college offers a range of certificate and diploma courses across various disciplines, including business, information technology, electrical engineering, building technology, and more, tailored to meet local economic needs and national development goals. Tarbaj TVC aims to empower students with practical skills and knowledge, preparing them for employment or entrepreneurship. Its establishment signifies a commitment to expanding TVET opportunities in arid and semi-arid lands (ASALs), contributing to socio-economic development and reducing youth unemployment in Wajir County and surrounding areas.
institutions.short_description: Tarbaj Technical & Vocational College is a public TVET institution in Tarbaj, Wajir County, offering certificate and diploma programs in various technical and business fields to empower youth with practical skills.

institutions.name: Taveta Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Taveta Technical and Vocational College (TAVETA TVC) is a government-owned institution located in Taveta town, Taita Taveta County, Kenya. Established to bridge the skills gap and promote technical and vocational education and training (TVET) in the region, TAVETA TVC offers a diverse range of courses aligned with industry demands. The college provides training in various fields including engineering, building technology, information communication technology (ICT), business studies, and hospitality, catering to students pursuing certificate and diploma qualifications. Its establishment reflects the government's commitment to increasing access to quality post-secondary education and producing a skilled workforce capable of contributing to national development.

The college is situated strategically in Taveta, making it accessible to students from the surrounding areas of Taita Taveta and neighbouring counties. TAVETA TVC is equipped with workshops, laboratories, and classrooms designed to facilitate hands-on learning and practical application of theoretical knowledge. Beyond academic pursuits, the institution aims to foster holistic student development through various extracurricular activities and mentorship programs. By focusing on practical skills and entrepreneurship, TAVETA TVC endeavors to empower its graduates for successful careers and self-reliance in a competitive job market, contributing significantly to the socio-economic growth of the Taveta region and Kenya as a whole.
institutions.short_description: Taveta Technical and Vocational College (TAVETA TVC) is a public TVET institution in Taita Taveta County, offering technical and business training programs to equip students with practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship in various fields.

institutions.name: Technical University Of Kenya
institutions.profile:
The Technical University of Kenya (TUK) is a premier public university located in Nairobi, Kenya. It was established in 2013 following the dissolution of the Kenya Polytechnic University College (a constituent college of the University of Nairobi). TUK traces its roots back to 1961 with the founding of The Kenya Polytechnic, which for decades served as a leading technical training institution in East Africa. The transition to a fully-fledged university marked a significant milestone, aimed at providing higher education with a strong emphasis on technical and applied sciences.

TUK is situated along Haile Selassie Avenue in the heart of Nairobi Central Business District, making it easily accessible. The university is organized into several faculties and schools offering a wide range of programs from certificate and diploma levels to undergraduate and postgraduate degrees. It is known for its focus on engineering, applied sciences, architecture, built environment, and technology-related fields, aligning its curriculum with the demands of industry and national development goals. TUK plays a crucial role in producing graduates equipped with practical skills and theoretical knowledge essential for Kenya's technological advancement.
institutions.short_description: The Technical University of Kenya (TUK) is a public university in Nairobi, specializing in technical education, applied sciences, engineering, and technology. It evolved from the Kenya Polytechnic, offering practical skills and academic excellence.

institutions.name: Technical University Of Mombasa
programs: Bachelor of Technology in Civil Engineering
Bachelor of Science in Mechanical Engineering
Bachelor of Science in Applied Statistics with Computing
Diploma in Electrical and Electronic Engineering
Certificate in Tourism and Travel Operations
institutions.profile:
The Technical University of Mombasa (TUM) traces its origins back to 1948, initially established as a technical institute to provide crucial skilled manpower. It progressively grew, becoming a polytechnic before finally achieving full university status in 2013. Situated on the vibrant coastal city of Mombasa, TUM provides a unique learning environment that blends rigorous academic and technical training with the rich cultural tapestry of the region. Its strategic location makes it a key educational hub for the coast and beyond, attracting students from diverse backgrounds seeking quality higher education.

As a public university, TUM is committed to innovation, research, and community service. It offers a wide array of programs across various disciplines including engineering, applied and health sciences, business, and technology education, designed to equip students with practical skills and theoretical knowledge relevant to the modern job market and societal needs. TUM emphasizes hands-on training and applied learning, fostering innovation and entrepreneurship among its graduates. The university plays a significant role in regional development through research initiatives and community outreach programs, solidifying its reputation as a leading institution for technical and higher education in Kenya.
institutions.short_description: Technical University of Mombasa (TUM) is a public university in Kenya's coastal city, offering a wide range of technical and academic programs with a strong focus on practical skills, research, innovation, and community engagement, contributing significantly to regional and national development.

institutions.name: Technical University Of Mombasa Tvet Institute
institutions.profile:
The Technical University of Mombasa TVET Institute operates as a key constituent part of the Technical University of Mombasa (TUM), a prominent public university located in the coastal city of Mombasa, Kenya. TUM itself evolved from the former Mombasa Polytechnic, which was established in 1948. This historical lineage imbues the TVET institute with a long-standing tradition of technical and vocational education. Situated within the vibrant and strategically important Mombasa County, the institute benefits from the region's coastal economy and its position as a major transport and industrial hub.

The TVET Institute is dedicated to offering demand-driven technical and vocational education and training programs, primarily at Diploma and Certificate levels, as listed through admissions bodies like KUCCPS. Its curriculum focuses on providing students with practical skills and theoretical knowledge essential for direct entry into various industries. As a public institution under the University TVET category, it plays a crucial role in national development by producing a skilled workforce ready to contribute to sectors such as engineering, business, hospitality, and applied sciences, thereby enhancing Kenya's technical capacity and supporting economic growth.
institutions.short_description: The Technical University of Mombasa TVET Institute is a public technical and vocational training institution located in Mombasa County. Part of the Technical University of Mombasa, it offers practical-oriented Diploma and Certificate programs focusing on various technical fields to equip students with industry-relevant skills for the job market.

institutions.name: Tetu Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Tetu Technical and Vocational College (TETU TVC) is a public institution situated in Tetu Constituency, Nyeri County, Kenya. Established with the goal of providing accessible and quality technical and vocational training, the college plays a crucial role in empowering youth and adults with practical skills relevant to the job market and entrepreneurship. It is registered and accredited by TVETA (Technical and Vocational Education and Training Authority), ensuring that its programs meet national standards and are recognized by employers.

TETU TVC offers a diverse range of diploma, certificate, and artisan courses across various disciplines, including Building Technology, Business Studies, Information and Communication Technology, Electrical and Electronics Engineering, and Hospitality, among others. The college is committed to providing hands-on training, often incorporating workshops and practical sessions into the curriculum. Located in a rural setting, it serves as a key center for skill development in the region, contributing to local economic growth and providing opportunities for those seeking alternatives to university education. Its focus on practical skills makes it a valuable institution for students aiming for direct employment or starting their own businesses.
institutions.short_description: Tetu Technical And Vocational College is a public TVET institution in Tetu, Nyeri County, Kenya, offering accredited diploma, certificate, and artisan courses focused on practical skills and vocational training for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Tharaka Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Tharaka Technical and Vocational College is a key public institution within the technical and vocational education and training (TVET) sector in Kenya, strategically located near Marimanti town in Tharaka Constituency, Tharaka Nithi County. Established under the Ministry of Education, its core mandate is to provide relevant, accessible, and quality technical and business skills training to young Kenyans, particularly those from the surrounding region. The college is registered by the Technical and Vocational Education and Training Authority (TVETA) and adheres to national standards for curriculum delivery and assessment. It serves as a crucial hub for skill development, aiming to empower trainees with practical knowledge and competencies needed for employment, self-reliance, and contributing to the socio-economic development of the nation.

The college offers a wide array of programs at certificate and diploma levels across various disciplines, including but not limited to electrical engineering, building technology, business management, information communication technology (ICT), agriculture, and hospitality. These programs are designed to be market-driven, incorporating practical training and industry linkages where possible. By focusing on hands-on learning and relevant theoretical knowledge, Tharaka TVC strives to produce graduates who are readily employable or capable of starting their own ventures. Its presence in Tharaka Nithi is significant, addressing the local demand for technical skills and providing post-secondary education opportunities closer to home for many students.
institutions.short_description: Tharaka Technical and Vocational College is a public TVETA-registered institution located near Marimanti, Tharaka Nithi County. It offers diverse certificate and diploma programs across various technical and business fields, equipping students with essential skills for the job market and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Tharaka University
institutions.profile:
Tharaka University is a public university situated in Marimanti Town, Tharaka Constituency, Tharaka Nithi County, Kenya. It was established as a constituent college of Chuka University and was granted a full charter to operate as an independent public university by His Excellency Hon. Uhuru Kenyatta, C.G.H., former President of the Republic of Kenya, on 18th October 2017. Located in an area with unique environmental challenges, the university positions itself as a centre of excellence particularly in research and development relevant to arid and semi-arid lands (ASALs), while also offering a broad spectrum of academic programs.

The university's mission is to provide quality education, training, research, and innovation for development, focusing on nurturing graduates who can contribute effectively to society. Its campus offers a serene learning environment away from the bustle of larger cities. Tharaka University offers diverse programs across faculties like Agriculture, Education, Business and Economics, Science and Technology, and Humanities and Social Sciences. Despite being relatively young, it is gaining recognition for its commitment to academic standards and its strategic focus on addressing the specific needs and potential of its regional setting.
institutions.short_description: Tharaka University is a public institution located in Marimanti, Tharaka Nithi County. Established by charter in 2017, it offers diverse programs and specializes in research relevant to arid and semi-arid lands, aiming to contribute to regional and national development.

institutions.name: Tharaka University Tvet Institute
institutions.profile:
Tharaka University, which encompasses the TVET programs referenced as "Tharaka University Tvet Institute," is a public institution situated in Gatunga, Tharaka Nithi County, Kenya. It began its journey in 2013 as a constituent college of Chuka University and achieved full university status in 2017. Located in a rural setting, the university plays a crucial role in expanding access to higher education and technical training in the Tharaka region and beyond. Its establishment marked a significant step in bringing university-level education closer to the local community and fostering development.

The university offers a diverse range of academic and technical programs, including degrees, diplomas, and certificates, covering fields such as agriculture, education, sciences, business, and technology. Its TVET offerings are integral to its mission, providing practical skills and vocational training essential for employability and local economic development. Tharaka University is recognized for its focus on community engagement, research relevant to regional challenges, and its commitment to nurturing skilled graduates prepared for various sectors. It aims to contribute significantly to the socio-economic transformation of the semi-arid Tharaka region.
institutions.short_description: Tharaka University Tvet Institute refers to the vocational training programs offered by Tharaka University, a public institution in Gatunga, Tharaka Nithi County. It provides diverse degree, diploma, and certificate courses, focusing on practical skills and community development.

institutions.name: The Bungoma National Polytechnic
institutions.profile:
The Bungoma National Polytechnic is a premier public technical and vocational education and training (TVET) institution located in Bungoma Town, Bungoma County, Kenya. Originally established as Bungoma Technical Training Institute, it was elevated to the status of a National Polytechnic, signifying its significant role in providing high-quality technical skills and knowledge. The institution is mandated to train competent human resources in various technical and business disciplines, contributing directly to the country's economic growth and industrialization agenda. Situated along the Bungoma-Chwele Road, the Polytechnic serves students from the surrounding Western region and across Kenya, attracting individuals seeking practical, hands-on training relevant to industry needs.

The Polytechnic offers a wide array of courses across several departments, including Engineering (Civil, Electrical, Mechanical), Information Communication Technology, Applied Sciences, Business Studies, Liberal Studies, and Hospitality & Tourism. These programs range from certificate to diploma levels, designed to meet the demands of the labor market. The institution is recognized for its commitment to excellence, providing a conducive learning environment with modern facilities and experienced faculty. It plays a vital role in empowering youth with employable skills, fostering innovation, and promoting entrepreneurship, thereby improving livelihoods and contributing to the socio-economic development of the region and the nation. Its reputation is built on a foundation of producing skilled, work-ready graduates who are capable of making meaningful contributions in their respective fields.
institutions.short_description: The Bungoma National Polytechnic is a leading public TVET institution in Kenya, offering diverse technical and vocational programs to equip students with practical skills for employment and national development, located in Bungoma Town.

institutions.name: The Cuk Nairobi Cbd Training Institute
institutions.profile:
The CUK Nairobi CBD Training Institute is widely understood to refer to the Nairobi CBD Campus of the Cooperative University of Kenya (CUK). The Cooperative University of Kenya is a public institution dedicated to providing quality education, training, research, and innovation, with a special focus on cooperative development and enterprise. Its main campus is situated in Karen, Nairobi, but it operates several satellite campuses to enhance accessibility, including the strategic location in Nairobi's Central Business District.

The Nairobi CBD Campus serves as a convenient hub for students and professionals in the city center, offering flexible learning options such as evening and weekend classes. Situated in Uchumi House, along Aga Khan Walk, the campus provides a range of programs, typically including diplomas and certificates in various fields relevant to business, finance, and cooperative management. This campus plays a crucial role in extending CUK's educational outreach, making its programs more accessible to a broader urban population seeking to advance their skills and knowledge without the constraints of travelling to the main campus.
institutions.short_description: The CUK Nairobi CBD Training Institute is the Nairobi CBD Campus of the Cooperative University of Kenya, offering flexible diploma and certificate programs in a convenient downtown location for working professionals.

other_info.address: Uchumi House, Along Aga Khan Walk

institutions.name: The East African University
institutions.profile:
The East African University (TEAU) is a private, not-for-profit university located in Kitengela, Kajiado County, Kenya. Established in 2010, the university was founded by the Tanzania-based Mt. Meru Group of companies. TEAU was granted a Letter of Interim Authority by the Commission for University Education (CUE) in 2010, allowing it to operate and admit students. It received its Charter in 2015, becoming a fully accredited university. Situated on a spacious campus off the Nairobi-Namanga Road, TEAU offers a serene environment conducive to learning, research, and innovation, approximately 35 kilometers from Nairobi's central business district.

TEAU offers a wide range of academic programs across various disciplines, including Business, Information Technology, Health Sciences, Education, Social Sciences, and Engineering. The university is committed to providing quality education that prepares students for the job market and entrepreneurial endeavors, emphasizing practical skills and ethical values. With modern facilities, qualified faculty, and a focus on student support services, TEAU aims to be a leading institution of higher learning in the East African region, contributing to socio-economic development through education and research.
institutions.short_description: The East African University (TEAU) is a private university in Kitengela, Kajiado County, Kenya, founded in 2010. It offers diverse programs in business, IT, health sciences, education, and more, focusing on quality education and practical skills development.

institutions.name: The University Of Embu Tvet Institute
institutions.profile:
The University of Embu TVET Institute operates under the auspices of the University of Embu, leveraging the university's established infrastructure and academic standards to offer technical and vocational education and training (TVET) programs. Established to address the growing need for skilled manpower in various sectors of the economy, the institute provides accessible and practical training opportunities for students seeking alternative pathways to career development. Located within the main University of Embu campus in Embu Town, the institute benefits from the university environment while maintaining a distinct focus on hands-on learning and industry relevance.

The institute is dedicated to equipping students with practical skills and knowledge that are directly applicable in the job market. It offers a range of certificate and diploma programs designed in line with national TVET standards and industry requirements. By providing quality technical education, the University of Embu TVET Institute plays a crucial role in contributing to Kenya's economic growth and development by producing competent and work-ready graduates prepared to meet the demands of various technical fields. Its integration within a public university structure enhances its credibility and access to resources.
institutions.short_description: The University of Embu TVET Institute offers technical and vocational training programs under the University of Embu. It provides practical skills education leading to certificates and diplomas, aiming to produce job-ready graduates for various industries.

other_info.address: University of Embu, Along Embu-Meru Highway (A2), Embu

institutions.name: Thika Technical Training Institute
institutions.profile:
Thika Technical Training Institute (TTTI) is a renowned public Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) institution located in Thika Town, Kiambu County, Kenya. Established in 1949 as a technical and trade school, TTTI has a long history of providing practical and relevant skills training to Kenyans. Over the decades, it has evolved significantly, expanding its course offerings and upgrading its facilities to meet the changing demands of the job market and national development goals. The institute operates under the Ministry of Education and is a key player in the government's efforts to promote technical education and vocational training.

TTTI offers a wide array of programs across various disciplines at diploma, certificate, and artisan levels. These include courses in engineering technology, building technology, business and economics, information communication technology, health sciences, hospitality and tourism, fashion and design, and liberal studies, among others. The institution is strategically located in Thika, a major industrial hub, which provides students with opportunities for industrial attachments and exposure. Known for its strong focus on hands-on training and industry partnerships, Thika TTI has built a solid reputation for producing competent graduates ready for employment and entrepreneurship, contributing significantly to the socio-economic development of the region and the country.
institutions.short_description: Thika Technical Training Institute (TTTI) is a leading public TVET institution in Kenya, located in Thika Town, Kiambu County. Established in 1949, it offers a diverse range of technical, vocational, and business programs focused on providing practical skills relevant to industry needs.

institutions.name: Thogoto Teachers Training College
institutions.profile:
Thogoto Teachers Training College, located in Thogoto, Kikuyu, Kiambu County, is a prominent public institution dedicated to the training of teachers in Kenya. Established in 1949 by the Church of Scotland Mission (CSM), the college has a long and rich history in contributing to the nation's education sector. Situated approximately 23 kilometers from Nairobi along Dagoretti Road, the college has evolved over the decades, initially training P1 teachers and later adapting its curriculum to train P1 (PTE) and Early Childhood Development Education (ECDE) teachers, and now includes CBC programs to align with the current education system.

As a public institution operating under the Ministry of Education, Thogoto TTC's core mandate is to produce competent and qualified teachers for both primary and pre-primary levels. The college is recognized for its commitment to academic excellence and practical training, equipping trainees with the pedagogical skills and knowledge required to effectively shape the future generation. Its serene environment provides a conducive atmosphere for learning and personal development, attracting students from across the country seeking quality teacher education programs.
institutions.short_description: Thogoto Teachers Training College, located in Thogoto, Kikuyu, Kiambu County, is a public institution established in 1949. It specializes in training teachers for primary and early childhood education, offering programs aligned with the national curriculum like PTE, ECDE, and CBC, contributing significantly to Kenya's education sector.

institutions.name: Tigania East Technical & Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Tigania East Technical & Vocational College is a public TVET institution located in Kangeta Town, Igembe Central Constituency, Meru County, Kenya. Established under the Kenyan government's initiative to expand access to technical and vocational training, the college plays a crucial role in equipping young Kenyans with practical skills essential for the modern workforce and entrepreneurship. Its strategic location in Meru County allows it to serve students from the surrounding agricultural and commercial regions, contributing to local and national development through skills development.

The college offers a diverse range of programs across various disciplines, including Information Communication Technology, Business Studies, Engineering (Building & Civil, Electrical & Electronics), Hospitality & Tourism, Agriculture, and Liberal Studies. These programs are designed to be market-driven, focusing on hands-on training and industry relevance. By providing competency-based education and training (CBET), Tigania East TVC aims to produce graduates who are job creators and readily employable, addressing the skills gap in various sectors of the economy. The institution is committed to fostering innovation, quality education, and holistic student development.
institutions.short_description: Tigania East Technical & Vocational College is a public TVET institution in Kangeta, Meru County, offering practical skills training in diverse fields like engineering, business, ICT, and hospitality to empower youth for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Tindiret Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Tindiret Technical And Vocational College (Tindiret TVC) is a public technical institution located in Tindiret, Nandi County, Kenya. Established under the Ministry of Education, the college plays a crucial role in providing accessible and relevant technical and vocational education and training (TVET) to the youth in the region and beyond. Its strategic location makes it a vital center for skills development, contributing to the economic empowerment of individuals and the overall development of the community.

Tindiret TVC offers a diverse range of market-driven programs across various disciplines, including Business Studies, Information Communication Technology (ICT), Electrical & Electronic Engineering, Building & Civil Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Institutional Management (Hospitality), and Applied Sciences. The college is committed to equipping students with practical skills, theoretical knowledge, and entrepreneurial competencies essential for employment or self-employment. By focusing on hands-on training and industry linkages, Tindiret TVC aims to produce highly competent graduates capable of meeting the demands of the evolving job market and contributing meaningfully to national development goals.
institutions.short_description: Tindiret Technical And Vocational College is a public TVET institution in Nandi County, Kenya, offering diverse technical and vocational programs to equip students with practical skills for employment and self-reliance.

institutions.name: Tom Mboya Labour College
programs: Diploma in Social Work and Community Development
institutions.profile:
Tom Mboya Labour College (TMLC) is a public national college located in Kisumu, Kenya. Established in 1963 by the late Hon. Tom Mboya, the then Secretary-General of the Kenya Federation of Labour (KFL), its initial purpose was to train trade unionists and equip them with essential skills in labour relations, negotiation, and advocacy. Over the years, TMLC has evolved into a reputable institution offering a diverse range of programs beyond its traditional labour studies, including business management, human resource management, social work, and project management.

The college maintains a strong affiliation with the Central Organization of Trade Unions (COTU) in Kenya, reflecting its historical roots and continued commitment to labour education. Located in Kisumu along Tom Mboya Drive, Milimani, the college serves students from across the country and the East African region. It is recognized for its specialized programs and its role in shaping labour leadership and industrial relations in Kenya, providing both academic qualifications and practical skills relevant to various sectors.
institutions.short_description: Tom Mboya Labour College is a public institution in Kisumu, Kenya, founded in 1963 to train trade unionists. It now offers a range of business, social science, and labour-focused programs, maintaining strong ties with COTU (K).

institutions.name: Tom Mboya University
institutions.profile:
Tom Mboya University (TMU) is a public university located in Homa Bay Town, Homa Bay County, Kenya. It began its operations in 2016 as Tom Mboya University College, a constituent college of Maseno University. On May 11, 2020, it was granted a charter to become a fully-fledged university, marking a significant milestone in expanding access to higher education in the region. Named in honor of the late Tom Mboya, a prominent Kenyan statesman and advocate for education, the university is strategically positioned to serve the academic needs of Western Kenya and beyond. It sits along the Homa Bay - Kisumu Highway, making it easily accessible.
TMU is committed to academic excellence, research, and community engagement. The university offers a diverse range of programs across various disciplines, including Arts, Social Sciences, Education, Business, and Science & Technology, through its different schools and departments. It aims to foster critical thinking, innovation, and leadership skills among its students, preparing them for the challenges of the modern world. By focusing on relevant academic programs and promoting research addressing local and national issues, TMU plays a vital role in the socio-economic development of the Homa Bay region and contributes to the national goal of producing skilled human capital.
institutions.short_description: Tom Mboya University (TMU) is a public university in Homa Bay Town, Homa Bay County, Kenya, established in 2020 after transitioning from a constituent college. It offers diverse programs in arts, sciences, education, and business, focusing on academic excellence, research, and community development.

institutions.name: Total Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Total Technical And Vocational College is a public TVC located in the Total area of Nakuru County, Kenya. Established to provide technical and vocational education and training, the college aims to equip students with practical skills relevant to various industries and the job market. It offers a range of courses certified by examination bodies like KNEC and CDACC, catering to the growing demand for skilled labor in Kenya.

The institution is situated in the Bahati Constituency, Nakuru County, making it accessible to students from the surrounding regions and beyond. Total TVC is under the Ministry of Education and plays a crucial role in advancing the government's agenda of promoting TVET education. It focuses on practical learning, workshops, and industry linkages to ensure graduates are well-prepared for employment or entrepreneurship, contributing significantly to socio-economic development.
institutions.short_description: Total Technical And Vocational College is a public TVC located in Nakuru County, Kenya, offering a variety of technical and vocational courses. It provides practical skills training aimed at preparing students for employment and entrepreneurship in various sectors, contributing to national development.

institutions.name: Tseikuru Technical Training Institute
institutions.profile:
Tseikuru Technical Training Institute is a public TVET institution situated in Tseikuru, Kitui East Constituency, Kitui County, Kenya. The institute was established to address the growing need for technical and vocational skills development in the region and across the country. It operates under the Ministry of Education and is registered by TVETA (Technical and Vocational Education and Training Authority). The institute is dedicated to providing quality training that is market-driven and relevant to the needs of both individuals and industries.

Tseikuru TTI offers a range of courses across various departments, including building and civil engineering, electrical and electronics engineering, business studies, and information communication technology, among others. These programs are designed to provide students with hands-on experience and theoretical knowledge, preparing them for employment or entrepreneurship. The institute aims to contribute to national development by producing skilled graduates capable of contributing to economic growth and societal well-being. Its strategic location in Kitui County makes it an important center for TVET education in the Eastern region of Kenya.
institutions.short_description: Tseikuru Technical Training Institute is a public TVET institution located in Tseikuru, Kitui East Constituency, Kitui County. It offers technical and vocational education and training programs aimed at equipping students with practical skills for various industries.

institutions.name: The Technical University Of Kenya (TUK) TVET Institute
institutions.profile:
The Technical University of Kenya (TUK) TVET Institute serves as the technical and vocational education arm of the prestigious Technical University of Kenya. Located within the main TUK campus in the bustling Nairobi Central Business District, the institute benefits from the university's extensive infrastructure, experienced faculty, and rich academic environment. It is mandated to offer Certificate and Diploma programs aligned with national development goals, providing practical, hands-on training in various technical, engineering, business, and hospitality disciplines. This focus ensures graduates are equipped with relevant skills for the job market or for pursuing further education.

The institute plays a crucial role in expanding access to technical and vocational training, complementing the degree programs offered by the main university. By providing specialized training pathways, TUK TVET Institute contributes significantly to Kenya's workforce development by producing competent technicians and technologists. Its programs are designed in line with industry needs, often incorporating practical attachments and real-world projects to enhance student employability. The institute's integration within a university setting allows for a unique blend of academic rigor and practical skills development, fostering well-rounded graduates prepared to tackle contemporary challenges.
institutions.short_description: Part of The Technical University of Kenya, TUK TVET Institute offers practical Certificate and Diploma programs in various technical, business, and hospitality fields. Located in Nairobi CBD, it leverages university resources to provide quality technical and vocational training, preparing graduates for industry and further studies.

institutions.name: Turbo Technical & Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Turbo Technical & Vocational College (Turbo TVC) is a public institution located in Turbo, Uasin Gishu County, Kenya. Established with the aim of providing accessible and quality technical and vocational training, the college plays a crucial role in equipping students with practical skills relevant to the job market and promoting self-employment. It operates under the Ministry of Education and is accredited by the Technical and Vocational Education and Training Authority (TVETA).

Turbo TVC offers a diverse range of programs across various disciplines, including engineering technology, building technology, business studies, hospitality, and information technology. These programs are designed to cater to the needs of both school leavers and individuals seeking to upgrade their skills. The college is committed to fostering a conducive learning environment, emphasizing hands-on training, and contributing to national development through skilled manpower.
institutions.short_description: Turbo Technical & Vocational College is a public TVET institution in Turbo, Uasin Gishu County, Kenya, offering practical skills training in various technical and vocational disciplines to equip students for employment and entrepreneurship.

other_info.address: Off Eldoret - Webuye Highway, 1 Km from Turbo Town

institutions.name: Turkana East Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Turkana East Technical And Vocational College is a public institution located in Lokori, Turkana East Constituency, Turkana County, Kenya. Established as part of the Kenyan government's initiative to expand access to technical and vocational education and training (TVET) across the country, especially in underserved regions, the college plays a crucial role in providing essential skills to the youth of Turkana East and surrounding areas. It operates under the Ministry of Education and is a key component in addressing the skills gap and promoting economic empowerment through practical, hands-on training. The college aims to equip students with competencies relevant to the local and national job markets, covering various technical and business disciplines.

The institution is committed to fostering a learning environment that promotes innovation, entrepreneurship, and self-reliance among its students. By offering accessible and affordable training programs, Turkana East TVC contributes significantly to the social and economic development of the arid and semi-arid Turkana region. Its curriculum is designed to meet the standards set by regulatory bodies, ensuring graduates are well-prepared for employment or further education. As a public TVC, it serves as a vital resource for local communities, providing pathways to skilled labor and contributing to the overall transformation of the region through education.
institutions.short_description: Turkana East Technical And Vocational College in Lokori offers practical, skills-based training under the Ministry of Education. It provides accessible TVET programs to youth in Turkana East, aiming to empower them with competencies for employment and entrepreneurship, contributing to regional development.

institutions.name: Turkana North Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Turkana North Technical And Vocational College is a public institution located in Lokichogio town, Turkana County, in the northern region of Kenya. Established under the Ministry of Education, it is part of the Kenyan government's initiative to expand access to technical and vocational education and training (TVET) across the country, particularly in underserved areas. The college plays a crucial role in the educational landscape of Turkana North Constituency, aiming to equip local youth with practical skills and knowledge relevant to the current job market and local economic needs.

By offering various technical and business-related courses, Turkana North TVC seeks to enhance employability and foster entrepreneurship among its students. The college's presence in Lokichogio is significant, providing a accessible pathway to post-secondary education and training for students in a region that traditionally faces challenges in accessing such opportunities. It contributes to regional development by building a skilled workforce capable of participating in and contributing to the local economy.
institutions.short_description: Turkana North Technical And Vocational College is a public TVET institution in Lokichogio, Turkana County, offering technical and vocational training to local youth. It aims to impart practical skills, enhance employability, and contribute to regional development.

institutions.name: Turkana University College
institutions.profile:
Turkana University College (TUC) is a public institution of higher learning located in Lodwar, Turkana County, Kenya. It is a constituent college of Moi University, established with the mandate to provide accessible and quality university education, conduct research, and engage in community service. The college is strategically positioned to address the unique development challenges and opportunities present in the arid and semi-arid lands (ASALs) of Northern Kenya, particularly Turkana.

Since its establishment, TUC has played a crucial role in expanding access to higher education in a region that historically had limited opportunities. It offers various academic programs relevant to the local context and national development goals, focusing on areas like education, business, social sciences, and environmental studies. TUC is committed to fostering research that contributes to sustainable development, environmental conservation, and community empowerment in Turkana and beyond, aiming to be a catalyst for positive change in the region.
institutions.short_description: Turkana University College is a public constituent college of Moi University located in Lodwar, Turkana County, Kenya. It is dedicated to providing quality higher education, conducting research, and engaging in community service to foster sustainable development in Northern Kenya.

institutions.name: Turkana University Tvet Institute
institutions.profile:
Turkana University TVET Institute is a public technical and vocational education and training institution located in Lodwar town, Turkana Central Constituency, Turkana County. Established in 2017 and registered in 2018, the institute is situated along the Lodwar - Kitale highway. As a TVET provider, its primary mission is to equip students with practical, industry-relevant skills and knowledge essential for employment, entrepreneurship, and national development, particularly focusing on the specific needs and opportunities within the arid and semi-arid lands (ASALs) of Kenya.

The institute offers a diverse range of programs across various fields, including business, information technology, engineering, building technology, hospitality, and agriculture. These courses are designed to address the growing demand for skilled labor in both the formal and informal sectors. Turkana University TVET Institute plays a crucial role in increasing access to post-secondary education in a region that has historically faced educational challenges, contributing significantly to human capacity development and socio-economic transformation in Turkana County and beyond.
institutions.short_description: Turkana University TVET Institute is a public technical and vocational education and training institution located in Lodwar, Turkana County. It offers a wide array of practical, industry-focused courses to equip students with skills for employment and regional development.

institutions.name: Ugenya Teachers College
institutions.profile:
Ugenya Teachers College is a significant public teacher training institution located in Ugenya, Siaya County, Kenya. Situated along the busy Busia-Kisumu Road, the college plays a crucial role in preparing qualified educators for the nation's primary schools and early childhood development centers. Its strategic location in the western part of Kenya allows it to serve students from Siaya and surrounding counties, contributing to the development of the education workforce in the region. The college focuses on imparting pedagogical skills, subject knowledge, and professional ethics necessary for effective teaching in foundational education levels.

The college is known for its programs such as the Primary Teacher Education (PTE), commonly known as P1, and the Early Childhood Development Education (ECDE) certificate courses. These programs are designed to meet the standards set by the Ministry of Education and the Teachers Service Commission (TSC) in Kenya. By offering these vital training courses, Ugenya Teachers College helps to bridge the gap in teacher supply, especially in rural and semi-urban areas. The institution strives to provide a conducive learning environment and practical teaching experiences to ensure its graduates are well-prepared to face the challenges and opportunities in the dynamic field of education.
institutions.short_description: Ugenya Teachers College is a public institution in Ugenya, Siaya County, Kenya, specializing in training teachers for primary and early childhood education, offering programs like P1 and ECDE.

institutions.name: Ugenya Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Ugenya Technical And Vocational College (UTVC) is a public Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) institution located in Ukwala, Ugenya Constituency, Siaya County, Kenya. Established under the Ministry of Education, the college plays a crucial role in providing accessible technical and vocational training to the local community and beyond. UTVC is dedicated to empowering learners with relevant, practical skills and knowledge necessary for both formal employment and self-employment, contributing to the socio-economic development of the region.

UTVC offers diverse programs across various disciplines, including engineering technologies, building and civil engineering, computing and informatics, business studies, and hospitality. The college is committed to maintaining high standards of training, utilizing modern facilities and equipment, and employing qualified instructors. By focusing on competency-based education and training (CBET), UTVC aims to produce graduates who are not only skilled but also innovative and ready to meet the demands of the dynamic industry and entrepreneurial landscape. The institution is listed by KUCCPS, facilitating student placement into its accredited courses.
institutions.short_description: Ugenya Technical And Vocational College is a public TVET institution in Ukwala, Siaya County, offering a wide range of technical, vocational, and business programs to equip students with practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Ugunja Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Ugunja Technical And Vocational College (TVC) is a public institution situated in Ugunja Town, Siaya County, Kenya. Operating under the Ministry of Education, the college is dedicated to providing quality technical and vocational education and training (TVET) to empower the youth with market-relevant skills. The college aims to contribute significantly to the socio-economic development of the region and the nation by producing competent, innovative, and entrepreneurial graduates prepared for employment or self-employment.

The institution offers a diverse portfolio of courses across various technical, vocational, and business disciplines, including engineering, ICT, building trades, business studies, and creative arts. Ugunja TVC is equipped with training facilities designed to provide practical, hands-on learning experiences guided by qualified instructors. It strives to maintain a reputation for academic excellence and practical training, fostering a learning environment that encourages critical thinking, creativity, and ethical conduct among its students.
institutions.short_description: Ugunja Technical And Vocational College is a public TVET institution in Ugunja, Siaya County, offering diverse technical, vocational, and business programs designed to equip students with practical skills for gainful employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: University Of Eastern Africa, Baraton
programs: Bachelor of Science in Computer Science
Bachelor of Science in Public Health
institutions.profile:
The University of Eastern Africa, Baraton (UEAB) is a private Christian university located in Baraton, Nandi County, Kenya. Established in 1978 and granted its charter in 1991, it holds the distinction of being the first private university in Kenya to receive a charter. Owned and operated by the Seventh-day Adventist Church, UEAB is committed to providing holistic education that integrates faith and learning, aiming to develop students intellectually, spiritually, socially, and physically. Situated on a large, serene campus, the university offers a conducive environment away from urban distractions, fostering focused academic pursuits and personal growth.

UEAB offers a diverse range of academic programs through its various schools and faculties, including Education, Business, Health Sciences, Science & Technology, Humanities & Social Sciences, and Post Graduate Studies. Notable programs include Nursing, Public Health, Business Administration, Theology, and various education degrees. The university is recognized within the Kenyan higher education landscape for its adherence to Christian principles, its focus on character development alongside academic excellence, and its contribution to training professionals in various fields, particularly in healthcare and education. Its charter status signifies its accreditation and recognition by the Kenyan government.
institutions.short_description: The University of Eastern Africa, Baraton (UEAB) is Kenya's first chartered private university, founded in 1978 by the Seventh-day Adventist Church in Baraton, Nandi County. It offers holistic, Christian-based education across diverse fields, emphasizing character development and academic excellence.

institutions.name: University Of Eldoret
institutions.profile:
The University of Eldoret (UoE) is a distinguished public institution in Kenya, renowned for its commitment to academic excellence, research, and community engagement. Originally established in 1946 as a Farmers Training Centre, it evolved significantly over the years. In 1984, it became a satellite campus of Moi University, known as Chepkoilel Campus, before being elevated to a university college status in 2010.

Located along Chepkoilel Road in Eldoret, Uasin Gishu County, UoE was granted its charter as a full-fledged university in 2013. It offers a diverse range of programs across various faculties including Agriculture, Education, Arts and Social Sciences, Science, Engineering, Environmental Studies, Human Resource Development, and Business. The university is particularly recognized for its strong focus on agriculture and environmental science, contributing significantly to research and development in the Rift Valley region and Kenya as a whole.
institutions.short_description: The University of Eldoret (UoE) is a prominent public university in Uasin Gishu, Kenya. Formerly a campus of Moi University, it attained full university status in 2013. UoE is recognized for its diverse academic offerings, particularly in agriculture and environmental studies.

institutions.name: University Of Embu
institutions.location: Along Embu-Meru Highway, near Embu Town
institutions.profile:
The University of Embu (UoEm) traces its origins back to 1947 as the Embu Agricultural Staff Training (EAST) Centre. It later transformed into Embu University College, a constituent college of the University of Nairobi, in 2011. This marked a significant step towards becoming a fully-fledged university. On February 11, 2016, UoEm was awarded its Charter by the President of Kenya, officially elevating it to an independent public university. Situated along the Embu-Meru Highway, about 3 kilometers from Embu Town, the university benefits from a serene and conducive environment ideal for academic pursuits and research.

UoEm has quickly established itself as a prominent institution of higher learning in Kenya, known for its strong focus on agriculture, environmental sciences, education, business, and computing. The university is committed to providing quality, affordable education and conducting research that addresses societal needs, particularly in the arid and semi-arid regions. With growing infrastructure and dedicated faculty, UoEm strives to produce graduates who are not only academically proficient but also equipped with practical skills and innovative thinking, contributing positively to national development.
institutions.short_description: The University of Embu (UoEm) is a dynamic public university situated in Embu, Kenya. It offers a wide array of affordable degree, diploma, and certificate programs with a strong emphasis on research, particularly in agriculture and environmental sciences.

institutions.name: University Of Kabianga
institutions.profile:
The University of Kabianga (UoK) is a public university located in Kabianga, Kericho County, Kenya. It traces its origins to the former Kabianga Farmers Training Centre established in 1959, which later became a campus of Moi University in 2007. The institution was elevated to a constituent college of Moi University in 2009 and finally received its Charter to become a fully-fledged university in 2013 from then-President Mwai Kibaki. Situated about 20 kilometers from Kericho town along the Kericho-Kisii highway, UoK benefits from a serene rural environment that is conducive to academic pursuits and research, offering a peaceful setting away from urban distractions.

UoK offers a wide array of academic programs across various disciplines, including Education, Arts and Social Sciences, Science and Technology, Agriculture and Environmental Sciences, and Business and Economics. The university is committed to providing quality education, fostering research, and engaging in community service. Its programs are designed to equip students with the knowledge and skills necessary for the job market and for contributing to national development. UoK has grown steadily since its chartering, attracting students from across Kenya and East Africa, and continues to expand its infrastructure and academic offerings. It is recognized as a key institution of higher learning in the Rift Valley region.
institutions.short_description: The University of Kabianga (UoK) is a public university chartered in 2013, located in the serene Kabianga area of Kericho County, Kenya. It offers a diverse range of undergraduate and postgraduate programs across multiple faculties, committed to quality education, research, and community engagement.

institutions.name: University Of Nairobi
institutions.profile:
The University of Nairobi (UoN), founded in 1956 as the Royal Technical College and chartered in 1970, stands as Kenya's oldest and largest public university. Located in the vibrant capital city of Nairobi, UoN is recognized as a premier institution of higher learning, offering a comprehensive array of academic programs spanning diverse disciplines such as Medicine, Law, Engineering, Arts & Humanities, Social Sciences, and pure Sciences. Its main campus is strategically situated along University Way, providing easy access within the city.

Renowned for its robust research output and extensive global partnerships, the University of Nairobi is a leading hub for innovation and intellectual leadership in East Africa. The university is committed to excellence in teaching, research, and community service, aiming to produce highly skilled professionals and contribute to addressing national and global challenges through knowledge creation and dissemination. With multiple campuses across Nairobi, UoN provides a dynamic academic environment fostering critical thinking, creativity, and leadership among its diverse student body and faculty.
institutions.short_description: The University of Nairobi is Kenya's largest and oldest public university, established in 1956. It offers a wide range of programs and is a leading institution for research and higher education in East Africa.

institutions.name: Uriri Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Uriri Technical and Vocational College (URIRI TVC) is a public TVC located in Uriri Constituency, Migori County, Kenya. Established with the aim of providing accessible and quality technical and vocational education and training (TVET), the institution plays a crucial role in equipping young Kenyans with practical skills relevant to the current and future job market. It is registered and regulated by the Technical and Vocational Education and Training Authority (TVETA) in Kenya.

Strategically situated in the heart of Uriri, the college serves students from Migori County and beyond, contributing to regional development through skill development and innovation. URIRI TVC offers a diverse range of programs across various disciplines, including engineering, business, ICT, agriculture, and hospitality. The college is committed to fostering a conducive learning environment and preparing graduates who are competent, innovative, and ready for employment or entrepreneurship, thereby contributing to the nation's economic growth.
institutions.short_description: Uriri Technical and Vocational College (URIRI TVC) is a public TVET institution in Migori County, offering a wide range of technical and vocational programs. It is committed to providing quality skills for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Uzima University
institutions.profile:
Uzima University, founded in 2012 and sponsored by the Archdiocese of Kisumu, is a private institution of higher learning located in the vibrant city of Kisumu, Kenya. The university was established with a clear mission to address the critical shortage of healthcare professionals in the region and beyond, focusing primarily on health sciences education and research. Its academic programs are designed to equip students with not only strong clinical skills but also a deep sense of ethical responsibility and compassion, grounded in the Catholic intellectual tradition.

The university offers a range of accredited programs, including Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery (MBChB), Bachelor of Science in Nursing (BScN), Bachelor of Science in Public Health, and Bachelor of Science in Clinical Medicine, among others. Uzima University is recognized by the Commission for University Education (CUE) in Kenya and maintains affiliations with relevant professional bodies. Situated on Oginga Odinga Street, its campus provides a focused environment for learning and aims to produce graduates who are competent, ethical, and ready to contribute significantly to the healthcare sector.
institutions.short_description: Uzima University is a private Catholic-sponsored university in Kisumu, Kenya, specializing in health sciences education and research, offering programs like Medicine, Nursing, and Public Health.

institutions.name: Wajir East Technical & Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Wajir East Technical & Vocational College (WETVC) is a public TVET institution located in Wajir County, Kenya. Established to enhance technical and vocational skills in the region, the college plays a crucial role in providing accessible and relevant education to the youth and residents of Wajir and neighboring areas. It offers a range of technical and business courses designed to equip students with practical skills needed for employment and entrepreneurship in various sectors. The college is under the Ministry of Education and participates in the KUCCPS placement process.

Situated in Wajir Town, Wajir East TVC serves as a key training hub in a region where such opportunities are vital for socio-economic development. The college is committed to fostering a learning environment that promotes innovation, skill development, and career readiness. While specific program details can vary, typical offerings in TVCs include courses in business studies, ICT, building technology, electrical engineering, and other technical trades. Its establishment reflects the government's effort to decentralize technical education and make it available to all parts of the country, addressing regional skill gaps and contributing to local development.
institutions.short_description: Wajir East Technical & Vocational College is a public TVET institution in Wajir Town, Wajir County. It offers various technical and business courses aimed at equipping students with practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship, serving the educational needs of the North Eastern region.

institutions.name: Wanga Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Wanga Technical And Vocational College (Wanga TVC) is a public TVET institution located in Musanda area of Mumias West Constituency, Kakamega County, Kenya. Established with the aim of providing accessible and quality technical and vocational training, the college plays a crucial role in empowering local youth and the wider community with practical skills essential for employment and entrepreneurship. It offers a range of courses aligned with national development goals, focusing on technical trades, business studies, information technology, and other vocational areas.

The college is committed to fostering a conducive learning environment equipped with workshops and facilities to support hands-on training. By emphasizing competency-based education and training (CBET), Wanga TVC prepares graduates who are market-ready and capable of contributing to various sectors of the economy. Its location in a rural setting highlights its mission to bring technical education opportunities closer to communities that might otherwise have limited access, thereby contributing to regional development and poverty reduction.
institutions.short_description: Wanga Technical And Vocational College is a public TVET institution in Kakamega County, Kenya, providing quality technical and vocational training to empower youth with practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship through competency-based education.

institutions.name: Webuye West Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Webuye West Technical and Vocational College (WWTVC) is a public TVC situated in Webuye West Constituency, Bungoma County, Kenya. Established in 2017 under the Ministry of Education, the college is dedicated to providing quality technical and vocational education and training. Its primary goal is to equip students with relevant practical skills and knowledge that meet the current and future demands of the job market and promote entrepreneurship. Located in Webuye Town, the college serves students from the local community and beyond, contributing significantly to human capital development in the region.

The college offers a wide array of courses in various technical and business disciplines, ranging from certificate to diploma levels. These programs are structured to provide hands-on training, ensuring graduates are well-prepared for employment or creating their own ventures. WWTVC is committed to academic excellence, fostering innovation, and providing a conducive learning environment. As a recognized institution by KUCCPS, it plays a vital role in the national effort to increase access to technical and vocational training and bridge the skills gap in key economic sectors.
institutions.short_description: Webuye West Technical and Vocational College is a public TVC in Webuye, Bungoma County, established in 2017. It offers practical technical and business programs aimed at equipping students with job-ready skills for employment and entrepreneurship, contributing to regional development.

institutions.name: Weru Technical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
Weru Technical And Vocational College is a prominent public technical and vocational training institution situated in Gede, Malindi Constituency, Kilifi County, Kenya. Established under the Kenyan government's initiative to expand access to quality technical education, the college plays a crucial role in providing essential skills development opportunities to students in the coastal region and beyond. Its strategic location, close to the historical Gede Ruins, makes it an accessible center for learners seeking practical training. The college operates under the Ministry of Education and is committed to bridging the skills gap in various sectors.

The institution offers diverse programs accredited by relevant bodies, covering fields such as Engineering (Electrical, Mechanical, Civil), Information and Communication Technology (ICT), Business Studies, Agriculture, Hospitality, and Applied Sciences. These courses are designed to be industry-relevant, combining theoretical knowledge with hands-on practical training to ensure graduates are well-prepared for the job market or self-employment. Weru TVC is dedicated to fostering innovation, research, and community engagement, contributing to the socio-economic development of the region by producing competent and skilled professionals.
institutions.short_description: Weru Technical And Vocational College is a public TVC located in Gede, Kilifi County, offering a wide range of technical, vocational, and business programs aimed at equipping students with practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: West Mugirango Techchnical And Vocational College
institutions.profile:
West Mugirango Technical and Vocational College (WMTVC) is a public TVET institution located in Nyamira County, Kenya. Situated within the West Mugirango Constituency near Nyamira town, the college plays a crucial role in providing accessible technical and vocational education and training to the youth in the region and beyond. Established as part of the Kenyan government's initiative to expand TVET opportunities across the country, WMTVC aims to equip students with practical skills and knowledge relevant to the current job market, contributing to socio-economic development by training a skilled workforce.

The college offers a diverse range of programs across various disciplines, including business studies, information communication technology (ICT), engineering technologies, hospitality, agriculture, and fashion and design, among others. These courses are designed to cater to different academic backgrounds and career aspirations, leading to certificates and diplomas. With a focus on hands-on training and industry relevance, West Mugirango TVC strives to produce competent graduates prepared for employment, entrepreneurship, and further education, thereby addressing the demand for skilled labor and promoting self-reliance among its students and contributing to regional growth.
institutions.short_description: West Mugirango Technical and Vocational College in Nyamira County is a public TVET institution offering diverse programs like business, ICT, and engineering. It focuses on providing practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship to support regional development.

institutions.name: Wote Technical Training Institute
institutions.profile:
Wote Technical Training Institute (Wote TTI) is a public Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) institution situated in Wote town, Makueni County. Established in 2011, the institute operates under the Ministry of Education and is dedicated to providing accessible and quality technical training to students in the region and beyond.
