Located strategically along the Wote-Makindu Road, it serves as a key center for skills development in the lower Eastern part of Kenya, contributing to the national agenda of increasing technical expertise.

The institute offers a wide array of market-driven programs at various levels, including diploma, certificate, and artisan courses. These programs span diverse fields such as Building & Civil Engineering, Electrical & Electronic Engineering, Information Communication Technology (ICT), Business Studies, Hospitality, and Agriculture. Wote TTI is committed to equipping its graduates with practical competencies and entrepreneurial skills necessary for employment, self-reliance, and contributing effectively to socio-economic development.
institutions.short_description: Wote Technical Training Institute is a public TVET institution in Wote, Makueni County, established in 2011. It offers diverse technical, vocational, and business programs at diploma, certificate, and artisan levels, aiming to equip students with practical, market-relevant skills for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Wumingu Technical & Vocational College
programs: Fashion Design and Garment Making Technology
Food and Beverage Production, Sales and Service
institutions.profile:
Wumingu Technical & Vocational College (WUMINGU TVC) is a public institution located in the serene Wumingu area of Wundanyi Constituency, Taita Taveta County, Kenya. Established to address the growing need for skilled professionals in various technical fields, the college plays a crucial role in providing accessible and relevant technical and vocational education and training (TVET) to youth and adults in the region and beyond. It operates under the Ministry of Education, aligning its curriculum with national development goals and industry standards.

The college offers a diverse range of programs at certificate and diploma levels across disciplines such as engineering (Automotive, Building, Electrical), hospitality (Food and Beverage), ICT, business studies, and fashion design. Wumingu TVC is committed to equipping its graduates with practical skills, theoretical knowledge, and entrepreneurial competencies necessary for employment, self-employment, and further education. Its location in Taita Taveta makes it a vital resource for local community development, contributing to the region's economic growth through skill development and innovation.
institutions.short_description: Wumingu Technical & Vocational College is a public TVET institution in Wumingu, Wundanyi, Taita Taveta County. It provides a range of certificate and diploma programs in technical, vocational, and business fields to empower youth with essential skills for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Yatta Technical And Vocational College
institutions.logo: Yatta Technical And Vocational College Logo
institutions.profile:
Yatta Technical And Vocational College (Yatta TVC) is a public TVET institution located in Kinyaata, Ndalani Ward, Yatta Constituency, Machakos County, Kenya. Established through the government's initiative to expand access to technical and vocational education, the college officially opened its doors in 2017. It operates under the Ministry of Education and is dedicated to providing market-driven skills and training to young Kenyans and adults alike. The college serves the local community and the broader region, aiming to bridge the gap between academic knowledge and practical skills necessary for employment and entrepreneurship.

Yatta TVC offers a diverse range of certificate and diploma programs across various technical and business disciplines. These programs are designed to meet the current industry demands and equip students with hands-on experience and theoretical knowledge. The college is committed to fostering a conducive learning environment with qualified instructors and relevant infrastructure. While relatively new, Yatta TVC is steadily building its reputation as a key player in TVET provision in Machakos County, contributing to the socio-economic development of the region through skills development.
institutions.short_description: Yatta Technical And Vocational College is a public TVET institution in Machakos County, Kenya, offering market-driven certificate and diploma programs across various technical and business fields to equip students with essential practical skills for employment and entrepreneurship.

institutions.name: Zetech University
institutions.profile:
Zetech University is a leading private university in Kenya, situated in Ruiru, Kiambu County. It began as Zetech College in 1999 and was granted its Letter of Interim Authority by the Commission for University Education (CUE) in 2008, eventually receiving a full charter in 2014, marking its transition to university status. Zetech University is well-regarded for its strong emphasis on technology, business, and communication studies, offering a diverse portfolio of accredited programs from diploma to postgraduate levels.

The university is committed to providing accessible and quality education that equips students with both theoretical knowledge and practical, market-relevant skills. Zetech University fosters a culture of innovation, research, and entrepreneurship, actively seeking industry collaborations to enhance its curriculum and improve graduate employability. With its main campus conveniently located along Thika Road, the university provides modern learning facilities, a supportive environment, and extracurricular activities aimed at holistic student development, preparing graduates to become impactful professionals and leaders in their fields.
institutions.short_description: Zetech University is a reputable private university in Ruiru, Kiambu County, known for offering market-driven programs in technology, business, and media, focusing on practical skills and innovation.

other_info.address: Along Thika Road, Mang'u Road Junction, Ruiru

institutions.name: Ziwa Technical Training Institute
institutions.profile:
Ziwa Technical Training Institute (Ziwa TTI) is a public TVET institution located in Ziwa, near Eldoret town, in Soy Constituency, Uasin Gishu County. Established to address the growing demand for technical and vocational skills in the region and across Kenya, Ziwa TTI operates under the Ministry of Education. The institute is dedicated to providing quality, market-driven technical and vocational education and training to empower youth and contribute to national development goals. It offers a diverse range of certificate, diploma, and artisan courses across various fields, including engineering, business studies, ICT, and hospitality, among others.

Strategically located in the agriculturally rich North Rift region, Ziwa TTI plays a crucial role in equipping students with practical skills and knowledge relevant to local industries and the broader economy. The institution is committed to fostering a conducive learning environment, equipped with workshops, laboratories, and qualified instructors to facilitate effective training. Ziwa TTI aims to produce competent, innovative, and entrepreneurial graduates capable of contributing significantly to the workforce and national growth, upholding its mission to provide holistic education and training.
institutions.short_description: Ziwa Technical Training Institute (Ziwa TTI) is a public TVET institution in Ziwa, Uasin Gishu County, Kenya. It offers market-driven certificate, diploma, and artisan courses across various technical and vocational fields, empowering youth with practical skills for national development.

other_info.address: Ziwa Technical Training Institute P.O. BOX 1055-30100 Eldoret
